data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw ‚Äî roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation ‚Äî but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps, make substantial changes in the global strategy. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance, you should not defer and postpone hard nodes, you should commit to them and build the infrastructure needed to make progress.

Make concrete progress on the ASSIGNED task's real objective ‚Äî do not preemptively stop or pivot to easy unrelated wins because the objective is large. A node being big, multi-session, or blocked on missing mathlib infrastructure is NOT a reason to skip it: start it, build the missing lemma/definition yourself as project-local infrastructure, and push it as far as you genuinely can.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.
- Use your own tools as you want.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` ‚Äî a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions ‚Äî The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style ‚Äî environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit ‚Äî Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` ‚Äî small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox ‚Äî Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox ‚Äî list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting ‚Äî How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session ‚Äî block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check ‚Äî Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors ‚Äî use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag ‚Äî Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) ‚Äî declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects ‚Äî via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch ‚Äî Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving ‚Äî the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- mathlib-conventions ‚Äî Mathlib house style for the Lean you write ‚Äî naming (snake_case props / UpperCamelCase types / lowerCamelCase defs, Is-prefix, le/lt), meaningful discoverable names, preferred abstractions, and how to add a missing lemma as reusable infrastructure instead of a local hack.
- project-git ‚Äî How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace ‚Äî one out-of-tree workspace ledger (no per-project repos); read a project's history by pathspec or by a session's commit trailers.
- references ‚Äî The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml ‚Äî how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents ‚Äî How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work ‚Äî spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.
- task-status ‚Äî What the task status labels mean and how you (the agent) set them ‚Äî you OWN your task's terminal status via `horizon task set`; the machine only ever leaves it queued/running, so `done` is never inferred from your report or a clean exit.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope ‚Äî you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0017/sessions/0002-horizon-T16

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint ‚Äî Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end ‚Äî keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug ‚Äî Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run ‚Äî missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors ‚Äî applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor ‚Äî Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier ‚Äî keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber ‚Äî Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever ‚Äî Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] ‚Äî Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task ‚Äî starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper ‚Äî then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits ‚Äî use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` ‚Äî it recompiles on the next run.

# Task focus
Task: T16
Title: Conclude the Jacobian challenge ‚Äî genus, Jacobian, Abel‚ÄìJacobi, universal property
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/**/*.lean,MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex

Discharge every open obligation of references/challenge.lean so the eight mathematician-owned declarations become sorry-free and axiom-clean ([propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]): AlgebraicGeometry.genus; Jacobian + its GrpObj / smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus / IsProper / GeometricallyIrreducible instances; Jacobian.ofCurve; comp_ofCurve; exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (the Albanese universal property). These signatures are archon-protected ‚Äî do NOT modify them; close them by proving picardJacobianWitness (Jacobian.lean) and its upstream cone. Build any missing infrastructure rather than deferring it. The 2026-07-07/08 fleet session took the tree from 30 to ~24 sorries, proved the substantive Abel map, and stood up a NEW adelic Riemann‚ÄìRoch lane whose keystone (H^1(C,O_C) finite-dimensional via a finite map to P^1) is proved and now ONE gate from unconditional ‚Äî this reframes the endgame away from full-generality FGA toward the curve-specialised route. Full ordered plan in the pinned comment below (a recommendation, not a mandate ‚Äî revise freely). Cross-refs: inbox I-0136 (session handoff / state), I-0134 (DG post-mortem: why the concrete/keystone-funnel route beats full-generality FGA), I-0118 (QuotScheme statement trap), and the design doc referenced in I-0136.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) ƒåech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **ƒåech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC ‚Äî axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [done/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), ƒåech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` ‚Äî **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact ‚Äî **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman ¬ß5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley‚ÄìRosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (K√§hler, rigidity, Abel‚ÄìJacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** K√§hler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel‚ÄìJacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk‚Ä¶
- I-0135 hint (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready): 2026-07-07 ultracode fleet session: state + ordered next actions for the Picard-representability milestone STATE: 30->25 sorries, tree green (8677 jobs), all work kernel-verified + committed. Closed: IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible, Stacks 02KE (pullback_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv_of_quasiCompact), Pic0.bundle (honest splice), rationalMap_order_finite_support (after the [IsNoetherian X] re-pin - was FALSE as pinned, counterexample recorded in the blueprint), pullback_kernel_isLocallyTrivial (+ iso form isIso_pullbackKernelComparison = the Abel-map feeder). I-0118 QuotScheme restated faithfully (QuotRepresentability.lean). NEW adelic RR lane (RiemannRoch/Adelic/: Subst‚Ä¶
- I-0136 hint (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready): HANDOFF for a fresh Fable-5 session (supersedes I-0135): AJC Jacobian challenge state after waves 6-9 + PR#1 merge GOAL: conclude the Jacobian challenge (challenge.lean: genus, Jacobian, ofCurve, exists_unique_ofCurve_comp); build missing infra rather than wait. Session-Stop goal is active. STATE (2026-07-08): tree green ~8685 jobs, 24 committed sorries; wave 9 in flight (agents commit as they go ‚Äî reconcile before next wave). Committed this run: substantive Abel map (instHasAbelMap CLOSED, data-carrying class, [D]|->-[ker q]); Stacks 02KE; pullback_kernel_isLocallyTrivial (+iso feeder); isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible; Pic0.bundle; rationalMap_order_finite_support (after honest [IsNoe‚Ä¶

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable ‚Äî critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `‚ü®sorry‚ü©` `Nonempty` ‚Äî a fully opaque functor. No H¬π comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) √©tale sheafification target ‚Äî Mathlib v4.31 has NO √©tale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman ¬ß2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^‚ôØ(T) ‚Üí Pic^‚ôØ_√©t(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor ‚Äî universe bump `Type u ‚Üí Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_Œµ) ‚âÖ H¬π(C_Œµ, O^√ó)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 ‚Üí O_C ‚Üí O^√ó_{C_Œµ} ‚Üí O^√ó_C ‚Üí 1` for the kernel = `H¬π(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) ‚Äî none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C√óT)` is not representable ‚Äî it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4‚Ä≤ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try ‚Äî no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ‚áí extension to a complete target" is FALSE ‚Äî never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-‚â•2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ‚áí $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum depth-‚â•2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ‚â´ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ‚â´ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ‚ä§` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ‚ü®‚ä§, ‚Ä¶‚ü©` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.Œπ_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_Œπ` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg ‚Äî the parser rejects `(Œπ := ‚Ä¶)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict ‚Ä¶).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements ‚Äî Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ‚ãô forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman ¬ß4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` ‚Äî no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) ‚Äî `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 ‚Äî pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/√©tale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now ‚Äî record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 ‚Äî `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ‚Ñ§/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, √©tale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ‚ü®sorry‚ü©` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win ‚Äî Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" ‚Äî **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ‚äá g(U')} M(U) ‚äó_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat ‚Äî flatness lives on stalks). - DG-vs-AG Jacobian post-mortem: why DG closed in 2 days vs AG 2 months, + the transferable steals

Analysis of rkirov/jacobian-fable@lean-eval (2026-07-07). Ranked reasons: (1) INTRINSIC/dominant: the DG contract asks for a CONCRETE object (complex torus C^g/Lambda + injective Abel map; NO universal property, NO representability) while challenge.lean demands the Albanese UP = representability-grade mathematics over arbitrary k. (2) INTRINSIC: over C every homological input has an elementary analytic substitute (Serre duality -> Miranda Laurent-tail residue pairing; sheaf cohomology -> one directed-colimit Cech H^1; derived functors -> one dbar-on-a-disk lemma); over arbitrary k these substitutes do not exist. (3) mathlib asymmetry: Analysis/Manifolds mature; AG pillars ABSENT (P^n coherent cohomology, EnoughInjectives for SheafOfModules, ample vocabulary, Altman-Kleiman, EGA IV 8.11.5). (4) elaboration friction ~100x lower in DG (one ULift wall + ~50 heartbeat bumps vs our instance diamonds / defeq walls / 6.4M-heartbeat chases). (5) ACTIONABLE: DAG geometry - DG funneled to ONE keystone (h^1 <= g) with a wide parallel base and NAMED-HYPOTHESIS GATING (unproven pillars as Prop classes, downstream builds now, ungates by inferInstance later). STEALS for AJC: (a) adelic/Laurent-tail Riemann-Roch (Weil repartitions; field-agnostic, no EnoughInjectives/derived functors/P^n cohomology) - would unlock FiniteDimensional H^1(C,O_C), finrank_eq_genus, tangentSpaceIso dual flip, and deg>2g-2 vanishing replacing Castelnuovo-Mumford FOR CURVES; design doc commissioned. (b) extend the HasPicScheme/HasAbelMap Prop-class gating to all remaining pillars. (c) acceptance-gist: pin challenge.lean signatures as examples + print-axioms audit in a ChallengeAcceptance.lean. Next action: review the adelic-RR lane design, then blueprint + fleet it.

Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session ‚Äî run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action ‚Äî never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Progress` and `## Why I stopped`. In `## Progress`, use only inline `-` bullets, one per relevant file or target, e.g. `- FileA.lean: 4 sorries -> 3 sorries; closed the base case.` or `- FileB.lean: No change because dependency X is blocked.` Do not use nested bullets or wrapped multi-line bullets there. In `## Why I stopped`, state plainly whether the assigned objective is fully complete, partly advanced, or blocked, and why. You OWN your task's status: the machine only ever leaves it queued/running, so nothing marks it done for you and the report is NOT parsed for completion. Only when the assigned objective is FULLY complete (not merely mostly), run `"$HORIZON_BIN" task set <task_id> --status done`; use `--status blocked` or `--status failed` if you are genuinely stuck. If it is only partly advanced, set nothing ‚Äî the task returns to the queue for the next round. Leave a `"$HORIZON_BIN" task comment <task_id> --body ‚Ä¶` whenever you hit a significant step or change the status. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this Horizon session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

/-

Authors: Christian Merten

import Mathlib -- compiles with commit b80f227 (20th April 2026)

/-
# Jacobians in algebraic geometry

An AI challenge to construct the Jacobian of an algebraic curve, inspired by Kevin Buzzard's
version for Riemann surfaces.

Current version: v0.1

## Main missing definitions

In the following, by a smooth curve we mean a geometrically irreducible, smooth scheme of relative
dimension one over a field.

* `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` -- genus of a proper, smooth curve
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` -- the Jacobian of a proper, smooth curve
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.ofCurve` -- the Abel-Jacobi map from a proper smooth curve to its
  Jacobian

## Main missing theorems

* `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` -- The Jacobian of a proper, smooth
  curve `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g`, where `g` is the genus of `C`.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` -- the universal property of the Jacobian
  of a proper, smooth curve
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
  -- smooth curve
  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
  -- proper
  [IsProper C.hom]
  -- geometrically irreducible
  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]

-- data
/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
def genus (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ‚Ñï :=
  sorry

-- data
/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`. -/
def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
  sorry

namespace Jacobian

/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/

-- data
/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
instance : GrpObj (Jacobian C) :=
  sorry

/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
genus of `C`. -/
instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
  sorry

/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
instance : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom :=
  sorry

/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
instance : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
  sorry

variable {C} in
-- data
/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
to a `k`-rational point of `C`. -/
def ofCurve (P : ùüô_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ‚ü∂ C) : C ‚ü∂ Jacobian C :=
  sorry

/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `Œ∑` below) is
the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. -/
lemma comp_ofCurve (P : ùüô_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ‚ü∂ C) :
    P ‚â´ ofCurve P = Œ∑[Jacobian C] :=
  sorry

/--
The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
any morphism `f : C ‚ü∂ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
Jacobian of `C`.

input.description: Read project-git skill and list memory files

data.content:
---
name: project-git
description: How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace ‚Äî root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
---

Read this before running `git diff`/`git log` on a project.

## The model

- **Workspace repo is out-of-tree too** ‚Äî its git dir is
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` with the workspace **root** as its work tree
  (driven via `--git-dir`/`--work-tree`). **`<root>/.git`** is deliberately not touched, 
  because it is reserved for a user repo. It commits `config.yaml`, scoped project files, and `.archon-horizon/`
  shared state at integration boundaries; never the binary git dirs under
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/`, locks, or other volatile internals.
- **Project repos live out-of-tree.** Every project should have an out-of-tree
  git directory at `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`, with the project
  directory as its work tree. Project directories do not contain nested `.git/`.
- Expected cadence: both workspace and project repos should be commited at the end of each session, 
  but note that in the case of parallel runs, the commits order might not be sequential, 
  and some files might be modified by other runs, mostly for the workspace repo. However, 
  this should not be a problem in general. 

So a plain `git diff` at the root sees only a user repo (if any), never Archon's.
To inspect Archon's workspace or a project's history, drive its out-of-tree repo
explicitly.

```bash
# Workspace (manifest) history:
git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. log --oneline -n 20
```

## Reading a project's history / diff

Run from the workspace root:

```bash
GD=.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git

import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.Adelic.FinitenessP1

/-!
# Genus finiteness of the curve (node `N12`, the adelic lane's consumable)

This file is part of the **adelic Riemann‚ÄìRoch lane**.  It composes the two halves
of the lane that are now proved:

* the **keystone** `Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finite_map`
  (`FinitenessP1.lean`, node `N11`): under the gates `HasFiniteMapToP1 C` and
  `P1HasLaurentChartData k`, *some* 2-affine cover `S` of `C` (the pullback of the
  standard ‚Ñô¬π charts along the finite map) has finite-dimensional ƒåech cokernel
  `»û¬π = S.H1Cok`, so any comparison `H¬π(C, ùí™_C) ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] S.H1Cok` transports the
  finiteness to the genus carrier;
* the **comparison** `AffineCoverMVSquare.hModuleOneEquivH1Cok`
  (`Cokernel.lean`, nodes `N5` + `N6`): for *every* 2-affine cover `S`, under the
  ƒåech-to-derived gate `HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily`,
  `HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] S.H1Cok (toModuleKSheaf C)` ‚Äî the N5 half
  (`cechCohomology 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó H1Cok`) is unconditional, only the N6 half is gated.

## The honest gate set

The keystone produces an *existential* cover (`D.pullbackSquare œÄ` for a chosen
finite `œÄ` and chart data `D`), which cannot be named in a theorem signature; the
comparison must therefore be available for **all** 2-affine covers, i.e. the honest
ƒåech-to-derived hypothesis is the `‚àÄ`-quantified instance gate
`[‚àÄ S, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily]` (a `Prop`-class
hypothesis with no sorried instance, in the `HasPicScheme`/`HasFiniteMapToP1`
gating pattern).  The full gate set of the consumable is

* `HasFiniteMapToP1 C` ‚Äî node `N9`; derived in `FiniteMapToP1.lean` from the single
  strictly weaker gate `ExistsNonconstantMapToP1 C` under the ambient curve
  hypotheses;
* `P1HasLaurentChartData k` ‚Äî node `N11b`, the ‚Ñô¬π chart-ring computation
  (`P1ChartData.lean` is building towards its instance);
* `HasExt.{u}`/`HasExt.{u+1}` ‚Äî the ambient `Ext`-theory instances the `HModule`
  machinery runs on;
* `‚àÄ S, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily` ‚Äî the ƒåech-to-derived
  comparison (Leray/Cartan) for 2-affine covers of the curve.

Under these, the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C) = HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` is a
finite-dimensional `k`-vector space, so `genus C = finrank k H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` is an
honest natural number.
-/

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

variable {k : Type u} [Field k]

/-- **Genus finiteness of the curve (node `N12`, gated).**  For a curve `C` over `k`
with a finite morphism to ‚Ñô¬π and the ‚Ñô¬π chart data, and with the ƒåech-to-derived
comparison available on 2-affine covers, the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` is a finite
`k`-module.  Composes the `N11` keystone `module_finite_hModule_one_of_finite_map`
with the `N5`+`N6` comparison `hModuleOneEquivH1Cok` of `Cokernel.lean`. -/
theorem module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [HasFiniteMapToP1 C] [P1HasLaurentChartData k]
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
      HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily] :
    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
  module_finite_hModule_one_of_finite_map C
    (fun S => ‚ü®S.hModuleOneEquivH1Cok (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)‚ü©)

end AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.Adelic.CechComparisonGate

/-!
# Gate 4: the ƒåech-to-derived comparison on 2-affine covers, reduced to affine vanishing

This file attacks **gate 4** of the unconditional genus-finiteness consumable
`Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`
(`CechComparisonGate.lean`), namely the instance

```
‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily.
```

`HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞` (`MayerVietorisCover.lean`) packages, for the cover `ùí∞`, a
degreewise `k`-linear comparison
`‚àÄ n, cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F n (‚®Ü ùí∞)` between unnormalised ƒåech
cohomology and the `Ext`-based derived cohomology `HModule'`.

## The mathematical reduction (Leray on a 2-affine cover)

For the 2-affine cover `S.coverFamily` of a separated `k`-curve `C`, with the three
pieces `U‚ÇÅ`, `U‚ÇÇ`, `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ` affine (the `AffineCoverMVSquare` fields), the comparison
is the classical statement "an acyclic cover computes derived cohomology", which for a
**two**-element cover is the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris long exact sequence
(`HModule'_sequence_exact`, in-tree) fed by **affine Serre vanishing in the constant-`k`
sheaf apparatus** `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`, i.e.
`Subsingleton (HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) i U) = 0` for every affine open `U` and
every `i > 0`.  Given that vanishing the comparison assembles degreewise:

* `n = 0`: sheaf axiom (`H‚Å∞` on both sides is the group of global sections);
* `n = 1`: `AffineCoverMVSquare.cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok` (node N5, in-tree,
  unconditional) `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] H1Cok S F`, chained with the
  MV cokernel identification `H1Cok S F ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F 1 ‚ä§` (which uses the
  degree-`0`/degree-`1` slice of the MV LES together with the affine `H¬π`-vanishing on
  `U‚ÇÅ`, `U‚ÇÇ`);
* `n ‚â• 2`: both sides vanish ‚Äî the ƒåech complex of a 2-element cover is `3`-term, and the
  derived side vanishes by the MV LES together with the affine vanishing on
  `U‚ÇÅ`, `U‚ÇÇ`, `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ`.

## What this file establishes

* `AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_coverFamily` / `isAffineOpen_overlapOpen`: the three
  pieces of the cover, in their `coverFamily`/`overlapOpen` typed forms, are affine
  (bridging the structure fields to the family-indexed forms the degreewise assembly
  consumes).
* `AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_coverFamily` /
  `subsingleton_hModule'_overlapOpen`: the **per-piece derived vanishing** ‚Äî under
  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F`, `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i (piece))` for every
  `i > 0`.  These are the affine-vanishing inputs the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris degreewise assembly
  reads off (the `H¬π` cokernel identification and the `n ‚â• 2` whole-space vanishing are
  both immediate consequences of the MV LES fed by exactly these).
* `module_finite_hModule_one_unconditional`: the capstone, stated under the honest gate
  set (finite map to ‚Ñô¬π, ‚Ñô¬π chart data, and the gate-4 comparison), delivering
  `Module.Finite k (H¬π(C, ùí™_C))`.  This is `module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1`
  with the `HasExt` gates synthesised (via `CechComparisonGate`); once the gate-4 instance
  is supplied ‚Äî i.e. once `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` is proved and
  the degreewise assembly built ‚Äî the genus of the curve is honest.

## The precise residual

The single named residual for the **unconditional** gate 4 is

> `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` ‚Äî affine Serre vanishing
> `H‚Å±(U, ùí™_C) = 0` (`i > 0`, `U` affine) in the `Sheaf (Opens.gT ‚Ä¶) (ModuleCat k)`
> apparatus ‚Äî

together with the degreewise assembly (the degree-`0`/degree-`1` MV cokernel
identification and the `n ‚â• 2` whole-space vanishing) built from it.  No `sorry`/axiom or
sorried instance is introduced here; the residual is the existing `Prop`-class
`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`, whose producer for the structure sheaf is the genuine open
target (Mathlib supplies no scheme-cohomology Serre vanishing in this apparatus, and the
in-tree `X.Modules` vanishing is gated on the un-instantiable
`[EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules]`).

**Universe note for the assembly (probe-confirmed here).**  The affine vanishing lives at
`HModule' = Abelian.Ext.{u}` (`Type u`), but the in-tree Mayer‚ÄìVietoris LES
`AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence` has its `HasExt` baked at `Abelian.Ext.{u+1}`
(`Type u+1`): the two carriers are *not* definitionally equal, so the degreewise assembly
must transport the per-piece vanishing across the universe bridge
`Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` (`MayerVietorisCore.lean`) before feeding it into the LES.
This is the same `Ext.{u}` ‚Üî `Ext.{u+1}` bridge the `HModule'`/`HModule` interface already
uses (`HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv`), and is the one non-obvious wrinkle a future
author of the assembly must account for.
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

section Affineness

variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)

/-- Each piece of the 2-element cover family is affine: `S.coverFamily i` is `U‚ÇÅ` or
`U‚ÇÇ`, both affine by the `AffineCoverMVSquare` fields. -/
lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_coverFamily (i : ULift.{u} (Fin 2)) :
    IsAffineOpen (S.coverFamily i) := by
  rcases i with ‚ü®i‚ü©
  fin_cases i
  ¬∑ exact S.isAffineOpen_U‚ÇÅ
  ¬∑ exact S.isAffineOpen_U‚ÇÇ

/-- The overlap `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ` of the 2-element cover, in the `overlapOpen` typed form used by
the degreewise ƒåech assembly, is affine. -/
lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_overlapOpen :
    IsAffineOpen S.overlapOpen :=
  S.isAffineOpen_inf

end Affineness

/-! ## Per-piece derived-cohomology vanishing (the acyclicity inputs of the assembly)

Under affine Serre vanishing `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F`, each affine piece of the
2-affine cover has vanishing higher derived cohomology `HModule'`.  These are the exact
inputs the degreewise ƒåech comparison consumes (the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris LES on the cover reads
off `H¬π(‚ä§)` and the higher vanishing from precisely these). -/

section PieceVanishing

variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
  (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
  (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
  [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
  [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]

/-- Higher derived cohomology of `F` vanishes on each piece `S.coverFamily j` of the
2-affine cover (both pieces are affine). -/
lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_coverFamily
    (j : ULift.{u} (Fin 2)) (i : ‚Ñï) (hi : 0 < i) :
    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i (S.coverFamily j)) :=
  IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) (S.isAffineOpen_coverFamily j) i hi

/-- Higher derived cohomology of `F` vanishes on the overlap `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ` of the 2-affine
cover (the overlap is affine). -/
lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_overlapOpen
    (i : ‚Ñï) (hi : 0 < i) :
    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i S.overlapOpen) :=
  IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) S.isAffineOpen_overlapOpen i hi

end PieceVanishing

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

/-! ## The capstone: genus finiteness under the honest gate set

Once the gate-4 comparison instance
`‚àÄ S, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily` is available (its unconditional
production is the residual `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` plus the
degreewise assembly), the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` is a finite `k`-module. -/

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

variable {k : Type u} [Field k]

/-- **Genus finiteness of the curve, honest gate-set form.**  For a curve `C` over `k`
with a finite morphism to ‚Ñô¬π (`HasFiniteMapToP1`), the ‚Ñô¬π chart data
(`P1HasLaurentChartData`), and the ƒåech-to-derived comparison on 2-affine covers (gate 4),
the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C) = HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` is a finite `k`-module.

This is `module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate` (the `HasExt`-free
restatement, `CechComparisonGate.lean`) named as the lane capstone.  It becomes
**unconditional** exactly when gate 2 (`P1HasLaurentChartData k`, lane A) and gate 4
(this file's target instance, whose residual is affine Serre vanishing
`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`) are both discharged. -/
theorem module_finite_hModule_one_unconditional
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [HasFiniteMapToP1 C] [P1HasLaurentChartData k]
    [‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
      HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily] :
    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
  module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate C

/-- **Standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing for quasi-coherent coefficients, unconditional**
(Stacks 02KG, condition (3)). For a quasi-coherent `ùí™_{Spec R}`-module `F`, the positive-degree
{\v C}ech cohomology over every standard cover of a distinguished open vanishes:
`HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystem R) F`. Obtained by discharging the `htilde` hypothesis of
`affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing` with the now-proved residual
`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`. Project-local: the unconditional Lane-1 seed
feeding the basis-comparison criterion. -/
theorem affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
    HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystem R) F :=
  affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing F (affine_tildeVanishing F)

/-- **Serre vanishing on affines, unconditional** (Stacks 02KG,
`lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero`). For a quasi-coherent `ùí™_{Spec R}`-module `F`, the
absolute cohomology `H·µñ(Spec R, F) = Ext·µñ(jShriekOU ‚ä§, F)` vanishes for all `p > 0`. Obtained by
discharging the `htilde` hypothesis of `affine_serre_vanishing_of_tildeVanishing` with the
now-proved residual `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`. Project-local: the Serre
vanishing top, instantiating `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` at `affineCoverSystem R`. -/
theorem affine_serre_vanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
    (p : ‚Ñï) (hp : 0 < p)
    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU (‚ä§ : (Spec R).Opens)) F p) : e = 0 :=
  affine_serre_vanishing_of_tildeVanishing F (affine_tildeVanishing F) p hp e

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî general-affine-open Serre vanishing (Need #2)

The declarations below **enlarge** the affine cover system from the distinguished-open basis
`{D f}` to the basis of *all* affine opens of `Spec R`, so that the basis-comparison criterion
`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` yields `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, H) = 0` for any affine open `V` (not just a
distinguished `D f`), entirely inside `(Spec R).Modules` (no restriction functor). The route is the
ambient `enlarge-B` of `analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md`: the only structurally new pieces
are the generalisations of `standard_cover_cofinal` and `affine_surj_of_vanishing` from `D f` to a
general affine open (swapping `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen` for `IsAffineOpen.isCompact`); the
`faces_mem`/`injective_acyclic` fields are unchanged (faces are distinguished ‚äÜ affine; injective
acyclicity is cover-agnostic). The positive-degree quasi-coherent ƒåech-vanishing *seed* over covers
of a general affine open is the single remaining geometric residual; it is carried as an explicit
hypothesis `hseed` (the general analogue of `affine_tildeVanishing`, needing a change-of-base to
`Œì(V)` rather than to a single `R_f`). -/

/-- **Distinguished opens of `Spec R` are affine opens.**  `D(r) ‚äÜ Spec R` is affine, being
isomorphic to `Spec R[1/r]` via `basicOpenIsoSpecAway`.  Project-local: places each distinguished
open into the enlarged affine-open basis, discharging `faces_mem` for `affineCoverSystemGeneral`. -/
theorem isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (r : R) :
    IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) :=
  IsAffine.of_isIso (basicOpenIsoSpecAway r).hom

/-- **Standard covers are cofinal among open covers of a general affine open** (Stacks 02KG,
Tag 009L), generalising `standard_cover_cofinal` from a distinguished `D(f)` to an *arbitrary affine
open* `V` of `Spec R`.  Given an arbitrary open cover `W : Œ± ‚Üí (Spec R).Opens` of `V`, there is a
finite standard subcover refining it: finitely many `g : Fin n ‚Üí R` with `V = ‚®Ü·µ¢ D(g·µ¢)` and each
`D(g·µ¢) ‚â§ W (œÜ i)`.  The only change from `standard_cover_cofinal` is the source of
quasi-compactness: `IsAffineOpen.isCompact` in place of `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen`.  The
refinement step for `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` over a general affine open. -/
theorem standard_cover_cofinal_affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (V : (Spec R).Opens)
    (hV : IsAffineOpen V) {Œ± : Type u} (W : Œ± ‚Üí (Spec R).Opens) (hcov : V ‚â§ ‚®Ü a, W a) :
    ‚àÉ (n : ‚Ñï) (g : Fin n ‚Üí R) (œÜ : Fin n ‚Üí Œ±),
===GENERAL OPEN===
    (p : ‚Ñï) (hp : 0 < p) :
    IsZero (cechCohomology
      (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
        (tilde (moduleSpecŒìFunctor.obj F))) p) :=
  sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen (moduleSpecŒìFunctor.obj F)
    (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g i.down) haff p hp

/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, unconditional** (Stacks 02KG, generalised from
`D(f)` to an arbitrary affine open; closes Need #2). For a quasi-coherent `ùí™_{Spec R}`-module `F`
and **any** affine open `V` of `Spec R`, the absolute cohomology `H·µñ(V, F) = Ext·µñ(jShriekOU V, F)`
vanishes for `p > 0`. Obtained by discharging the tilde-{\v C}ech-vanishing leaf `htilde` of
`affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing` with the now-proved residual
`affine_tildeVanishing_general` (in turn `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen`). Project-local:
the general-affine 02KG top, discharging the Need #2 general-affine cone end-to-end. -/
theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_open {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ‚Ñï) (hp : 0 < p)
    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
  affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing F
    (affine_tildeVanishing_general F) V hV p hp e

end AlgebraicGeometry
===GATE CLASS===
morally `Module.Finite k (Œì(X_i, O_C))` for affine $X_i$ ‚Äî to the H‚Å∞ cohomology
piece `HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0 X_i`. -/
theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero_curve
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
    [Module.Finite k
      ((presheafToSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
        ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj U) ‚ü∂
          Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0 U) :=
  Scheme.module_finite_HModule'_zero k _ U

/-- Iter-040 affine cohomology vanishing carrier predicate. Packages the
geometric statement that for every affine open `U` of `C.left.toTopCat` and
every degree `i > 0`, the open-evaluation cohomology `Scheme.HModule' k F i U`
is the zero `k`-module (formulated as `Subsingleton`, since `HModule'` returns
a `Type u` rather than a `ModuleCat` object ‚Äî see iter-014 abbrev). The class
is the affine-vanishing input the cover-evaluation chain consumes once the
producer instance is supplied (queued for iter-041+; multi-iteration
project-local construction expected since Mathlib does not yet provide
scheme-level Serre vanishing on affines for the `ModuleCat k`-flavour). -/
class IsAffineHModuleVanishing
    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
    Prop where
  subsingleton_HModule' : ‚àÄ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat},
    AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U ‚Üí ‚àÄ i, 0 < i ‚Üí
      Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U)

/-- Iter-040 immediate consumer of `IsAffineHModuleVanishing`: given the
class hypothesis, the open-evaluation cohomology `HModule' k F i U` is
`Module.Finite k` for any affine open `U` and any `i > 0`. The proof unfolds
the class field to `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i U)` and then invokes
Mathlib's auto-derived `Subsingleton M ‚Üí Module.Finite R M` instance (any
subsingleton module is finitely generated by the empty set, hence finite).
This consumer is the building block for the cover-evaluation chain: combined
with iter-039's `H^0` curve specialisations and iter-037's general-degree
corner-bridge transport, it closes the algebraic side of `Module.Finite k
(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` once the producer instance for
`IsAffineHModuleVanishing` is supplied. -/
theorem module_finite_HModule'_of_isAffineHModuleVanishing
    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
    [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]
    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
    (hU : AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U) (i : ‚Ñï) (hi : 0 < i) :
    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
  have : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
    IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) hU i hi
  inferInstance

/-- Iter-043 wholespace H‚Å∞ Hom-finiteness carrier predicate. Packages the
algebraic statement that the global Hom group
`((constantSheaf _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F)` is finite over `k`. Morally
`Œì(C.left, F)` (the **global** sections of `F`) being finite over `k`. On a
proper geometrically integral $k$-curve $C$ with $F = O_C$, this is
$\Gamma(C, O_C) \simeq k$ (Stein factorization on a proper geometrically
connected curve), so this class admits a producer instance from the
geometric content of $C$ (supplied at iter-046).

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK

/-!
# Genus of a smooth proper curve

The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.

## Status (closed iteration 011 ‚Äî `genus`)

The iter-011 refactor added the `noncomputable` modifier (user-authorised
`2026-05-07`) and an import of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`
so the body uses the project's `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology
`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009) of the structure sheaf
`Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (iter-006). The body is the probe-confirmed
one-liner

    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)

which is the honest mathematical definition `dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.

See `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` for the informal proof sketch and
`.archon/STRATEGY.md` for the multi-phase build-out plan.
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ‚Ñï :=
  Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)

end AlgebraicGeometry

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechBridge
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AbsoluteCohomology

/-!
# ƒåech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis (Stacks 01EO) ‚Äî L1/L2 chain

Project-local: builds the section-ƒåech short-exact-sequence (L1) and the
quotient-vanishing step (L2) of the 01EO dimension-shift argument.
-/

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits CategoryTheory.Abelian

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

-- Re-activate the (file-local) `HasExt` instance from `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` so that the
-- `Ext`-based absolute cohomology resolves here without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom` search.
attribute [local instance] hasExtModules

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî functoriality of the section ƒåech complex -/

/-- The cosimplicial morphism of section ƒåech objects induced by a morphism `œÜ` of
presheaves of modules, acting coordinatewise by the underlying presheaf morphism on each
basic-open section group. Project-local: `sectionCechCosimplicial` has no functoriality in
Mathlib. -/
noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicialMap {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (œÜ : F ‚ü∂ G) :
    sectionCechCosimplicial U F ‚ü∂ sectionCechCosimplicial U G where
  app n := Limits.Pi.map (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
    ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œÜ).app (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
  naturality {m n} f := by
    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
    intro œÉ
    simp only [sectionCechCosimplicial, Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.map_œÄ, Limits.Pi.lift_œÄ,
      Limits.Pi.map_œÄ_assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_œÄ_assoc]
    congr 1
    exact ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œÜ).naturality _

/-- The section ƒåech cosimplicial object as a functor in the coefficient presheaf of modules.
Project-local: packages `sectionCechCosimplicialMap` with functoriality so the induced
short-complex maps compose and respect zero. -/
noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor {Œπ : Type u}
    (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.PresheafOfModules ‚•§ CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
  obj F := sectionCechCosimplicial U F
  map œÜ := sectionCechCosimplicialMap U œÜ
  map_id F := by
    apply NatTrans.ext
    funext n
    show (Limits.Pi.map fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (ùüô F)).app
        (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) = ùüô _
    rw [show (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (ùüô F)).app
          (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
      = (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
        ùüô ((PresheafOfModules.presheaf F).obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))) from by
        funext œÉ; rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]; rfl]
    exact Limits.Pi.map_id
  map_comp œÜ œà := by
    apply NatTrans.ext
    funext n
    show (Limits.Pi.map fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (œÜ ‚â´ œà)).app
        (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) = _
    rw [show (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (œÜ ‚â´ œà)).app
          (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
      = (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œÜ).app (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))) ‚â´
          ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œà).app
            (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) from by funext œÉ; rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]; rfl]
    exact (Limits.Pi.map_comp_map _ _).symm

/-- The section ƒåech cochain complex as a functor in the coefficient presheaf of modules.
Project-local: functoriality of `sectionCechComplex`, used to form the short exact sequence of
ƒåech complexes. -/
noncomputable def sectionCechComplexFunctor {Œπ : Type u}
    (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.PresheafOfModules ‚•§ CochainComplex Ab.{u} ‚Ñï :=
  sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor U ‚ãô AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}

/-- The chain map of section ƒåech complexes induced by a morphism `œÜ` of presheaves of
modules. Project-local: functoriality of `sectionCechComplex` in the coefficient presheaf. -/
noncomputable def sectionCechComplexMap {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (œÜ : F ‚ü∂ G) :
    sectionCechComplex U F ‚ü∂ sectionCechComplex U G :=
  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).map (sectionCechCosimplicialMap U œÜ)

/-- **{\v C}ech cohomology accessor** `»û^p(ùí∞, F)`: the degree-`p` homology of the section
ƒåech complex, packaged as a named `Ab`-object. Project-local thin wrapper so the 01EO chain
can refer to `»û^p` uniformly. -/
noncomputable def cechCohomology {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ‚Ñï) : Ab.{u} :=

173 /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/SubProjects/Cech-Cohomology/analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md
# Analogy: transporting an Ext / right-derived cohomology computation across a scheme isomorphism

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
change-of-scheme-cohomology

## Iteration
056

## Question
Find Mathlib's idiom (decl names + imports) for transporting the right-derived / `Ext` cohomology
computation `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, H)` across the canonical iso of an affine scheme with `Spec` of its
global sections, so the ‚ä§-case Serre vanishing `affine_serre_vanishing` over `Spec R` discharges the
residual `IsZero ((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU (j ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W)))).rightDerived q).obj H)` for
`j‚Åª¬πW` a *general affine open* of the affine scheme `U`. Is the transport route sound and cheaper
than building a `BasisCovSystem U` over arbitrary affine opens?

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/OpenImmersionPushforward.lean:296-306` ‚Äî the residual `sorry`
  (`hSec` leaf inside `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic`); already reduced through
  `rightDerivedNatIso (sectionsFunctorCorepIso (j ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W))` to
  `IsZero ((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU (j ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W)))).rightDerived q).obj H)`.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/AffineSerreVanishing.lean:521` ‚Äî `affine_serre_vanishing`, the
  available ‚ä§-case (`Ext^p (jShriekOU ‚ä§) F = 0` over `Spec R`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechToCohomology.lean:323,429` ‚Äî `BasisCovSystem` and
  `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (the comparison consuming a cover system on a basis `B`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/AffineSerreVanishing.lean:373` ‚Äî `affineCoverSystem R`
  (`BasisCovSystem (Spec R)` with `B = {D f}`).
- `analogies/restriction-preserves-injectives.md` (iter-026) ‚Äî the project's recorded decision to
  adopt **Form B** *precisely to avoid* restriction-preserves-injectives (a 200‚Äì500 LOC Mathlib gap).

## Headline finding (read this first)

The directive's chosen route ‚Äî **make `V = j‚Åª¬πW` the whole space via `V ‚âÖ Spec Œì(V,ùí™)`** ‚Äî is a
**WALL, not a shortcut.** It secretly re-introduces **restriction-preserves-injectives**, the exact
Mathlib gap the project deliberately side-stepped in iter-026 by adopting Form B
(`H^q(V,‚àí) := Ext^q_{U.Modules}(jShriekOU V, ‚àí)`, ambient, no restriction). The transport is *not*
circular, but its cost is the 200‚Äì500 LOC `j_!`/restriction-injectives infrastructure, with the same
risk profile that already forced a pivot once.

Reason (the crux): `jShriekOU_U V` and `H` both live in `U.Modules`, and the only available iso is
`V ‚âÖ Spec Œì(V)` ‚Äî an iso of the **open subscheme** `V`, **not** of `U`. To use it you must first
**restrict** `U.Modules ‚Üí V.Modules` (`H ‚Ü¶ H|_V`); the derived comparison
`Ext^q_{U.Modules}(j_!ùí™_V, H) ‚âÖ H^q(V, H|_V)` *is* restriction-preserves-injectives (an injective
resolution in `U.Modules` must stay injective after `(‚àí)|_V` to compute the intrinsic `H^q(V,‚àí)`).
`V ‚Ü™ U` is an **open immersion, not an iso**, so there is no equivalence `U.Modules ‚âå V.Modules` to
transport `Ext` along ‚Äî only the non-exact-inverse restriction functor.

**Contrast:** `U ‚âÖ Spec Œì(U)` (the *whole* affine `U`) IS a genuine iso, giving a genuine
equivalence `U.Modules ‚âå (Spec ŒìU).Modules`. That transport ("need #1", abstract-affine ‚Üí `Spec`)
is sound. But it sends the proper open `V ‚äÜ U` to a **general (non-distinguished) affine open**
`œÜ(V) ‚äÜ Spec ŒìU`, *not* to `‚ä§`. So even after the sound whole-affine transport, you still face
general-affine-open vanishing, which `affine_serre_vanishing` (‚ä§ only) does not cover.

**The general-affine-open gap is unavoidable** (no basis dodge): `Q.obj(op W) = H^q(j‚Åª¬πW, H)` and any
affine open `W ‚äÜ X` straddling the boundary of `j(U)` forces `j‚Åª¬πW = U ‚äì W` to be a general affine
open of `U`; points of `W` on the boundary of `j(U)` cannot be covered by opens whose `j`-preimage is
distinguished-or-empty. So the residual genuinely is "Serre vanishing for **every** affine open of an
affine scheme".

**The sound, Form-B-native discharge** is to stay ambient and get general-affine-open vanishing from
`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` by **enlarging the cover system's basis `B` from `{D f}` to all affine
opens** ‚Äî `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis s ‚Ä¶ V hV` already gives `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, H)=0` for *any*
`V ‚àà s.B`, entirely inside `(Spec R).Modules`, with no restriction functor anywhere in its proof.

## Verified Lean names

### Q1 ‚Äî affine scheme ‚âÖ Spec(Œì)
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isoSpec (X) [IsAffine X] : X ‚âÖ Spec (X.presheaf.obj (op ‚ä§))`
  ‚Äî `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme`. **[verified]**
- `Scheme.isoSpec_hom : X.isoSpec.hom = X.toSpecŒì` **[verified]**;
  `Scheme.toSpecŒì_isoSpec_inv` **[verified]**;
  `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_top : hV.fromSpec = X.isoSpec.inv` **[verified]**.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isCompact (hU : IsAffineOpen U) : IsCompact ‚ÜëU`
  ‚Äî `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme`. **[verified]** (this is what generalizes
  `standard_cover_cofinal` from `D f` to a general affine open).

### Q2 ‚Äî module-category functoriality from a scheme morphism / iso

Module.finrank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` (iter-011) lives at
universe `u+1`, but the iter-048 acyclicity carrier and consumer live at
universe `u`. Iter-049 bridges. -/
theorem subsingleton_HModule_of_isCechAcyclicCover_top
    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
    {Œπ : Type u} {ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [IsCechAcyclicCover F ùí∞] (h : ‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i = ‚ä§)
    (compIso : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï),
      cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
        HModule' k F n (‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i))
    (n : ‚Ñï) (hn : 0 < n) :
    Subsingleton (HModule k F n) := by
  haveI : Subsingleton (HModule' k F n (‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i)) :=
    subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover compIso n hn
  haveI : Subsingleton (HModule' k F n ‚ä§) := h ‚ñ∏ this
  exact (HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv k F n
    (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton

/-- Iter-049: curve specialisation at `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.

Mirrors the iter-039 / iter-042 / iter-043 / iter-048 `_curve` pattern: a thin
dot-notation wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is
the structure sheaf) from re-typing `toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-049
consumer is chained through. Used by the iter-053 genus carrier producer
`Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)`. -/
theorem subsingleton_HModule_of_isCechAcyclicCover_top_curve
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    {Œπ : Type u} {ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞] (h : ‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i = ‚ä§)
    (compIso : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï),
      cechCohomology C (toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
        HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) n (‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i))
    (n : ‚Ñï) (hn : 0 < n) :
    Subsingleton (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n) :=
  subsingleton_HModule_of_isCechAcyclicCover_top (ùí∞ := ùí∞) h compIso n hn

/-- Iter-050: ƒåech-vs-derived comparison-iso typeclass carrier. Packages the
existence of a `LinearEquiv`-valued comparison `‚àÄ n, cechCohomology n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
HModule' n (‚®Ü ùí∞)` as a `Prop`-valued class via `Nonempty`-wrapping. The
`Nonempty` wrapper is required because the `‚àÄ n, ...` is data (a
`LinearEquiv`-valued function), and `Prop`-valued classes can only have `Prop`
fields.

Once iter-051+ proves the substantive comparison theorem
`‚àÄ n, cechCohomology n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' n (‚®Ü ùí∞)` under acyclicity hypotheses,
an `instance` for `HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞` wraps it via `‚ü®‚ü®the_iso‚ü©‚ü©`.
Downstream producers (iter-052+ basic-open cover acyclicity + iter-053+
`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`) then chain through both `IsCechAcyclicCover` and
`HasCechToHModuleIso` via instance synthesis without re-passing the iso
explicitly at every call site. Mirrors the iter-046 instance-driven
`IsHModuleHomFinite` design.

Mirrors the iter-040 / iter-043 / iter-048 carrier-class pattern: a
`Prop`-valued class with a single field. The class is parameterised over the
sheaf `F` and cover `ùí∞` (matches the iter-048 `IsCechAcyclicCover` signature). -/
class HasCechToHModuleIso
    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))] :
    Prop where
  nonempty_compIso : Nonempty
    (‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï), cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
        HModule' k F n (‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i))

/-- Iter-050: comparison-iso extractor. Given the iter-050 typeclass instance
`[HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞]`, recover the comparison iso as a function via
`Classical.choice` on the class field. The `noncomputable` modifier is required
because `Classical.choice` is non-constructive (already in the kernel-only
axiom set `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` since iter-048; no new
axiom introduced). -/
noncomputable def cechToHModuleIso
    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
    {Œπ : Type u} {ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞] :
    ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï), cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
      HModule' k F n (‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i) :=
  Classical.choice HasCechToHModuleIso.nonempty_compIso

/-- Iter-050: instance-driven top-supremum HModule transport. Combines iter-048's
`IsCechAcyclicCover` carrier with iter-050's `HasCechToHModuleIso` carrier:
under both class hypotheses (and the cover-totality hypothesis `‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i = ‚ä§`),
conclude `Subsingleton (HModule k F n)` for `n > 0`.

Body: applies iter-049's `subsingleton_HModule_of_isCechAcyclicCover_top`
directly, with the comparison iso supplied via the iter-050 extractor
`cechToHModuleIso`. The extractor fires automatically on the
`[HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞]` instance argument; no explicit `compIso` needs to
be passed at the call site.

This is the iter-050 instance-driven counterpart to iter-049's explicit-argument
form. Iter-053+ producers chain through this consumer via typeclass synthesis on
both `[IsCechAcyclicCover]` and `[HasCechToHModuleIso]` instances. -/
theorem subsingleton_HModule_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_top
    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
    {Œπ : Type u} {ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [IsCechAcyclicCover F ùí∞] [HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞]
    (h : ‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i = ‚ä§) (n : ‚Ñï) (hn : 0 < n) :
    Subsingleton (HModule k F n) :=
  subsingleton_HModule_of_isCechAcyclicCover_top h cechToHModuleIso n hn

/-- Iter-050: curve specialisation at `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.

Mirrors the iter-039 / iter-042 / iter-043 / iter-048 / iter-049 `_curve`
pattern: a thin dot-notation wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting
(where `F` is the structure sheaf) from re-typing `toModuleKSheaf C` whenever
the iter-050 instance-driven consumer is chained through. Used by the iter-053+
genus carrier producer `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)`. -/
theorem subsingleton_HModule_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_top_curve
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    {Œπ : Type u} {ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞]
    [HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞]
    (h : ‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i = ‚ä§) (n : ‚Ñï) (hn : 0 < n) :
    Subsingleton (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n) :=
  subsingleton_HModule_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_top (ùí∞ := ùí∞) h n hn

/-- Iter-051: instance-driven supremum HModule' consumer (abstract sheaf form).
Combines iter-048's `IsCechAcyclicCover` carrier with iter-050's `HasCechToHModuleIso`
