open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

/-- Phase A step 5/6 bridge (iter-009 scaffold): the parallel `Sheaf.H` for
`ModuleCat k`-valued sheaves. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is
parameterised over `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` only, so closing `genus` honestly
requires this `ModuleCat k`-flavoured version. The result carries `Module k`
automatically via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and
`Module.finrank k` is therefore well-defined on it. The declaration is a
`noncomputable abbrev` (rather than `def`) so that instance synthesis sees
through the wrapper to find `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances. -/
noncomputable abbrev HModule
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ‚Ñï) : Type (u+1) :=
  Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n

/-- Phase A step 6 algebraic bridge (iter-010 scaffold): the $k$-linear
identification of `HModule k F 0` with the Hom group from the constant
sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ : Ext X Y 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (X ‚ü∂ Y)` in any
`Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to the `Linear k`
enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ`;
probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
identify `H‚Å∞(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Œì(C, O_C)` viewed as a
`k`-module on a connected proper `k`-curve. -/
noncomputable def HModule_zero_linearEquiv
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
    HModule k F 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
      ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F) :=
  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ

/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-013 scaffold): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
cohomology of an open `X : C` with values in a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`.
Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X = (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (op X)`
(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L105) for
`AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, with `AddCommGrpCat.free ‚Üí ModuleCat.free k`.

The codomain is `Type u` (not `ModuleCat.{u} k`): `Abelian.Ext` returns a bare
`Type u` carrying `Module k` via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` through the `Linear k`
enrichment. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) is required so
instance synthesis sees through the wrapper to find `Module k (HModule' k F n X)`
and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` ‚Äî under `def`, `Module.finrank` would fail
to typecheck (cf. iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`).

This is the prerequisite for the iter-014+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris
LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`).
The iter-014+ work will state and prove the LES on a `MayerVietorisSquare`,
specialising to a 2-affine cover of a proper `k`-curve in iter-015+. The
comparison theorem `cechCohomology_OC C ùí∞ n ‚âÖ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`
for an acyclic cover (the technical heart of Path-2) is also queued for
iter-015+. -/
noncomputable abbrev HModule' (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ‚Ñï) (X : C) : Type u :=
  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
    ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n

/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-015): the `H‚Å∞` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`,
mirroring iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 `HModule'`. The
$k$-linear identification of `HModule' k F 0 X` with the Hom group from the
sheafified representable `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k).obj X)`.
Mathlib provides `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ : Ext X Y 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (X ‚ü∂ Y)`
in any `Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to `R = k` and to the
`Linear k`-enriched abelian category `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (the `Linear k`
enrichment is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's
`Sheaf.linear`), this collapses `HModule' k F 0 X` to a `k`-linear Hom group. The
closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ`; probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-015
plan-agent). Used downstream as the algebraic prerequisite for Stein-factorization-
derived `H‚Å∞(C, O_C) ‚âÉ k` on a connected proper `k`-curve (multi-iteration; queued
iter-017+ alongside the Mayer-Vietoris LES of iter-016+). -/
noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv

1137	  rw [res_p1XSection k, res_p1YSection k, ‚Üê map_mul, ‚Üê map_mul,
1138	    awayMap_coord_mul_eq_one, map_one, map_one]
1139	
1140	/-- **The `‚Ñô¬π` Laurent chart datum (route step 4, assembly).**  On the standard 2-chart
1141	geometry `p1CoverSquare` of `‚Ñô¬π = ‚Ñô(ULift (Fin 2); Spec k)` (charts `V‚ÇÄ = p1Chart ‚ü®0‚ü©`,
1142	`V‚ÇÅ = p1Chart ‚ü®1‚ü©`, coordinates `x = p1XSection`, `y = p1YSection`), the chart-ring
1143	computation assembles into the `Adelic.LaurentChartData` consumed by the keystone:
1144	the overlap is the basic open of either coordinate (`p1Chart_inf_eq_basicOpen_coordSection`),
1145	the coordinates are mutually inverse (`p1_res_x_mul_res_y`), and each chart ring is the
1146	`k`-span of the powers of its coordinate (`span_pow_p1XSection_scaffold`,
1147	`span_pow_p1YSection_scaffold`). -/
1148	noncomputable def p1LaurentChartData (k : Type u) [Field k] :
1149	    LaurentChartData
1150	      (Over.mk (‚Ñô(ULift.{u} (Fin 2); Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) ‚Üò Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) where
1151	  V‚ÇÄ := p1Chart k ‚ü®0‚ü©
1152	  V‚ÇÅ := p1Chart k ‚ü®1‚ü©
1153	  isAffineOpen_V‚ÇÄ := isAffineOpen_p1Chart k ‚ü®0‚ü©
1154	  isAffineOpen_V‚ÇÅ := isAffineOpen_p1Chart k ‚ü®1‚ü©
1155	  cover := p1Chart_sup_eq_top k
1156	  x := p1XSection k
1157	  y := p1YSection k
1158	  inf_eq_basicOpen_x := p1Chart_inf_eq_basicOpen_coordSection k ‚ü®0‚ü© ‚ü®1‚ü©
1159	  inf_eq_basicOpen_y := by
1160	    rw [inf_comm]
1161	    exact p1Chart_inf_eq_basicOpen_coordSection k ‚ü®1‚ü© ‚ü®0‚ü©
1162	  res_x_mul_res_y := p1_res_x_mul_res_y k
1163	  span_pow_x := span_pow_p1XSection_scaffold k
1164	  span_pow_y := span_pow_p1YSection_scaffold k
1165	
1166	/-- **Gate #1 of the genus theorem discharged.**  The concrete projective line
1167	`‚Ñô(ULift (Fin 2); Spec k)` carries Laurent chart data (`p1LaurentChartData`), so the
1168	`Adelic.P1HasLaurentChartData` gate holds for every field `k` ‚Äî the char-free Route B
1169	of the module header, closing node `N11b`. -/

    hS.comp_extClass, Ext.comp_zero]

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî cover system on a basis (01EO conditions 1‚Äì2) -/

/-- A **covering datum** on `X`: an index family of opens (the covered open is `‚®Ü i, c.2 i`).
Project-local: the lightweight cover representation matching the cover-local ƒåech lemmas
(`cechCohomology`, `faceShortComplex`), which are indexed by a family `Œπ ‚Üí Opens X`. -/
abbrev CovDatum (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type (u + 1) :=
  Œ£ Œπ : Type u, Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X

/-- **Cover system on a basis** (Stacks 01EO conditions (1)‚Äì(2)). Records a basis `B`, a set of
admissible coverings `Cov`, the faces-in-basis datum (every finite intersection of a covering's
opens lies in `B`, condition (1)), and the cofinality datum in the form it is consumed ‚Äî
condition (2) packaged as the section-surjectivity it produces through `ses_cech_h1`: for every
short exact sequence `S` of `O_X`-modules whose left term has vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology,
the section map of `S.g` is surjective over every basic open. The auxiliary `injective_acyclic`
field records the standard-cover injective ƒåech-acyclicity (Stacks
`lemma-injective-trivial-cech`, i.e. `injective_cech_acyclic`) for the system's coverings.
Project-local: bespoke encoding; the two sheaf-theoretic fields are discharged at the affine
(02KG) instantiation by `ses_cech_h1` + standard-cover cofinality and by `injective_cech_acyclic`
respectively. -/
structure BasisCovSystem (X : Scheme.{u}) where
  /-- The basis of opens. -/
  B : Set (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
  /-- The set of admissible coverings. -/
  Cov : Set (CovDatum X)
  /-- Condition (1): every finite intersection of a covering's opens lies in the basis. -/
  faces_mem : ‚àÄ c ‚àà Cov, ‚àÄ (p : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí c.1), (‚®Ö k, c.2 (œÉ k)) ‚àà B
  /-- Condition (2), in the section-surjectivity shape produced by `ses_cech_h1` + cofinality. -/
  surj_of_vanishing : ‚àÄ (S : ShortComplex X.Modules), S.ShortExact ‚Üí
    (‚àÄ c ‚àà Cov, ‚àÄ q, 0 < q ‚Üí
      IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj S.X‚ÇÅ) q)) ‚Üí
    ‚àÄ V ‚àà B, Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map S.g)).app (Opposite.op V)))
  /-- Injective ƒåech-acyclicity for the system's coverings (`injective_cech_acyclic`). -/
  injective_acyclic : ‚àÄ (I : X.Modules), Injective I ‚Üí ‚àÄ c ‚àà Cov, ‚àÄ q, 0 < q ‚Üí
    IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I) q)

/-- **Vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology for a cover system** (Stacks 01EO condition (3)). An
`O_X`-module `F` satisfies this when `»û·µñ(ùí∞, F) = 0` for every covering `ùí∞ ‚àà s.Cov` and every
`p > 0`. Stated for an arbitrary module (not necessarily quasi-coherent): the dimension-shift
induction feeds the quotient `Q = I/F` back as coefficient, and `Q` need not be quasi-coherent.
Project-local: the inductive predicate of the 01EO argument. -/
@[reducible] def HasVanishingHigherCech (s : BasisCovSystem X) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
  ‚àÄ c ‚àà s.Cov, ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí
    IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) p)

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî L4 dimension-shift induction -/

-- The dimension-shift induction embeds modules into injectives, which needs
-- `EnoughInjectives X.Modules`. That instance is NOT available in Mathlib for sheaves of modules
-- (it would follow from `IsGrothendieckAbelian (SheafOfModules R)`, which is absent ‚Äî see
-- `analogies/p5a.md`), so ‚Äî following the project convention for the P5a derived-functor lane ‚Äî
-- it is carried as an explicit hypothesis on `injSES`/L4 rather than synthesized.

/-- The injective-embedding short exact sequence `0 ‚Üí F ‚Üí I ‚Üí I/F ‚Üí 0` of an `O_X`-module `F`,
with `I = Injective.under F` the chosen injective and `I/F` its cokernel. Project-local helper for
the 01EO dimension-shift induction; carries `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` as a hypothesis. -/
@[reducible] private noncomputable def injSES [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (F : X.Modules) :
    ShortComplex X.Modules :=
  ShortComplex.mk (Injective.Œπ F) (cokernel.œÄ (Injective.Œπ F)) (cokernel.condition _)

private lemma injSES_shortExact [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (F : X.Modules) :
    (injSES F).ShortExact :=
  ShortComplex.ShortExact.mk (ShortComplex.exact_cokernel _)

/-- **Dimension-shift induction `H·µñ(U, F) = 0` for `p > 0` (Stacks 01EO L4).** For a cover system
`s` on a basis and any `O_X`-module `F` with vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology for `s`, the
absolute cohomology `H·µñ(U, F) = Ext·µñ(jShriekOU U, F)` vanishes for every basic open `U ‚àà s.B` and
`p > 0`. Induction on `p`, quantified over all `F` in the inductive class: the quotient
`Q = I/F` re-enters the class (via the section ƒåech SES of L1 from the per-face SES, plus L2
quotient-vanishing and the cover system's injective acyclicity), the base case is L3, and the
inductive step is the covariant Ext long exact sequence. Project-local: the 01EO inductive core. -/
theorem absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (s : BasisCovSystem X)
    {F : X.Modules} (hF : HasVanishingHigherCech s F)
    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X} (hU : U ‚àà s.B) {p : ‚Ñï} (hp : 0 < p)
    (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F p) : e = 0 := by
  have key : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (F : X.Modules), HasVanishingHigherCech s F ‚Üí
      ‚àÄ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X), U ‚àà s.B ‚Üí
        ‚àÄ e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F (n + 1), e = 0 := by
    intro n
    induction n with
    | zero =>
      intro F hF U hU e
      have hS := injSES_shortExact F
      haveI : Injective (injSES F).X‚ÇÇ := inferInstance
      exact absoluteCohomology_one_eq_zero_of_basis U hS
        (s.surj_of_vanishing (injSES F) hS hF U hU) e
    | succ m ih =>
      intro F hF U hU e
      have hS := injSES_shortExact F
      haveI : Injective (injSES F).X‚ÇÇ := inferInstance
      -- The quotient `Q = (injSES F).X‚ÇÉ` again has vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology.
      have hQ : HasVanishingHigherCech s (injSES F).X‚ÇÉ := by
        intro c hc q hq
        set P := (injSES F).map (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X) with hP
        have hface : ‚àÄ (pp : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (pp + 1) ‚Üí c.1),
            (faceShortComplex c.2 P œÉ).ShortExact := by
          intro pp œÉ
          refine faceShortComplex_shortExact_of_sheaf_ses c.2 (injSES F) hS ?_ pp œÉ
          intro pp' œÉ'
          exact s.surj_of_vanishing (injSES F) hS hF _ (s.faces_mem c hc pp' œÉ')
        have hSES := cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis c.2 P hface
        exact quotient_cech_vanishing_of_basis c.2 P hSES
          (s.injective_acyclic (injSES F).X‚ÇÇ inferInstance c hc) (hF c hc) q hq
      -- Ext long exact sequence step: `H^{m+2}(F)` is squeezed by `H^{m+1}(Q) = 0` (IH) and
      -- `H^{m+2}(I) = 0` (injective vanishing).
      have hef : e.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ (injSES F).f) (add_zero (m + 1 + 1)) = 0 :=
        absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective (m + 1) U (injSES F).X‚ÇÇ _
      obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÉ, hx‚ÇÉ‚ü© :=
        absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact‚ÇÅ U hS e hef (rfl : (m + 1) + 1 = m + 1 + 1)
      have hx‚ÇÉzero : x‚ÇÉ = 0 := ih (injSES F).X‚ÇÉ hQ U hU x‚ÇÉ
      rw [‚Üê hx‚ÇÉ, hx‚ÇÉzero, Ext.zero_comp]
  obtain ‚ü®n, rfl‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hp.ne'
  exact key n F hF U hU e

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî ƒåech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis (01EO top) -/

/-- **ƒåech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis (Stacks 01EO).** Let `s` be a cover system on a
basis for `X` and `F` an `O_X`-module with vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology for `s` (conditions
(1)‚Äì(3) of 01EO, packaged in `s` and `hF`). Then the absolute cohomology `H·µñ(U, F) =
Ext·µñ(jShriekOU U, F)` vanishes for every basic open `U ‚àà s.B` and every `p > 0`. The thin assembly
of the dimension-shift induction `absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis` (L4), which itself chains
the section-ƒåech short exact sequence (L1), quotient vanishing (L2), and the base case (L3).
Project-local: the named 01EO target, carrying `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` as a hypothesis (the
instance is absent in Mathlib for sheaves of modules; see `injSES`). -/
theorem cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (s : BasisCovSystem X)
    {F : X.Modules} (hF : HasVanishingHigherCech s F) :
    ‚àÄ U ‚àà s.B, ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí ‚àÄ e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F p, e = 0 :=
  fun _ hU _ hp e => absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis s hF hU hp e

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- `ùí™_X(U)`-linearity of the local graded action in the module slot, `ModuleCat`
-carrier spelling: over the objectwise ring the action `twistedSMul U r` is the
composite of the two objectwise module maps (sheafification unit, action
comparison) with `r ‚äó‚Çú ¬∑`, so linearity is `TensorProduct.tmul_smul` followed by
`_root_.map_smul` of the two `ModuleCat` morphisms.  (The qualification matters:
inside this namespace the bare name `map_smul` is `Scheme.Modules.map_smul`, whose
`M.presheaf.map`-headed LHS sends `erw`'s defeq matcher into a `whnf` blowup
against the categorical composite.) -/
private lemma twistedSMul_smul_right_aux (F L : X.Modules) (i j : ‚Ñï) (U : X.Opens)
    (a : ‚Ü•((X.sheaf.obj ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (op U)))
    (r : ‚Ü•(((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (tensorPow L i)).obj (op U)))
    (x : ‚Ü•(((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (moduleTensorPow F L j)).obj (op U))) :
    twistedSMul F L i j U r (a ‚Ä¢ x)
      = a ‚Ä¢ (twistedSMul F L i j U r x :
          ‚Ü•(((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (moduleTensorPow F L (i + j))).obj (op U))) := by
  unfold twistedSMul
  erw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul, _root_.map_smul, _root_.map_smul]
  rfl

/-- The local graded action is `Œì(X, U)`-linear in the module slot. -/
lemma twistedSMul_smul_right (F L : X.Modules) (i j : ‚Ñï) (U : X.Opens)
    (a : Œì(X, U)) (r : Œì(tensorPow L i, U)) (x : Œì(moduleTensorPow F L j, U)) :
    twistedSMul F L i j U r (a ‚Ä¢ x)
      = a ‚Ä¢ twistedSMul F L i j U r x :=
  twistedSMul_smul_right_aux F L i j U a r x

/-- `ùí™_X(U)`-linearity of the local graded action in the line-bundle slot,
`ModuleCat`-carrier spelling.  (Same `_root_.map_smul` qualification as in
`twistedSMul_smul_right_aux`.) -/
private lemma twistedSMul_smul_left_aux (F L : X.Modules) (i j : ‚Ñï) (U : X.Opens)
    (a : ‚Ü•((X.sheaf.obj ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (op U)))
    (r : ‚Ü•(((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (tensorPow L i)).obj (op U)))
    (x : ‚Ü•(((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (moduleTensorPow F L j)).obj (op U))) :
    twistedSMul F L i j U (a ‚Ä¢ r) x
      = a ‚Ä¢ (twistedSMul F L i j U r x :
          ‚Ü•(((toPresheafOfModules X).obj (moduleTensorPow F L (i + j))).obj (op U))) := by
  unfold twistedSMul
  erw [‚Üê TensorProduct.smul_tmul', _root_.map_smul, _root_.map_smul]
  rfl

/-- The local graded action is `Œì(X, U)`-linear in the line-bundle slot. -/
lemma twistedSMul_smul_left (F L : X.Modules) (i j : ‚Ñï) (U : X.Opens)
    (a : Œì(X, U)) (r : Œì(tensorPow L i, U)) (x : Œì(moduleTensorPow F L j, U)) :
    twistedSMul F L i j U (a ‚Ä¢ r) x
      = a ‚Ä¢ twistedSMul F L i j U r x :=
  twistedSMul_smul_left_aux F L i j U a r x

end SMulLinearity

import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.Adelic.GenusFiniteness

/-!
# The last genus-finiteness gates: scoping the ƒåech-to-derived comparison

This file scopes the **final** gates of the adelic genus-finiteness consumable
`Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1` (`GenusFiniteness.lean`, node `N12`).
That consumable is stated under four gates:

1. `HasFiniteMapToP1 C`               ‚Äî node `N9`  (curve ‚ü∂ ‚Ñô¬π, in progress elsewhere);
2. `P1HasLaurentChartData k`          ‚Äî node `N11b` (‚Ñô¬π chart rings, `P1ChartData.lean`);
3. `HasExt.{u}` **and** `HasExt.{u+1}` for the sheaf category
   `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`
   ‚Äî the ambient `Ext`-theory the `HModule` genus carrier runs on;
4. `‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
      HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily`
   ‚Äî the ƒåech-to-derived comparison (Leray/Cartan) on 2-affine covers.

The question this file answers: **are gates 3 and 4 instantiable today?**

## Gate 3 (the `HasExt` pair): INSTANTIABLE ‚Äî proved here

The genus carrier lives in the sheaf category `ùíÆ := Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology
C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`, i.e. sheaves of `k`-modules for the *constant* base
field `k`.  This is the crucial point that dissolves the historical blocker: `ùíÆ` is a
**Grothendieck abelian category** (Mathlib `IsGrothendieckAbelian.{v} (Sheaf J A)` for a
small site `J` and Grothendieck-abelian `A = ModuleCat k`), so Mathlib supplies
`EnoughInjectives ùíÆ` (`079H`) and both `HasExt.{u} ùíÆ`, `HasExt.{u+1} ùíÆ` (`HasExt.standard`)
**for free**.  See `hasExt_moduleKSheaf` / `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf` /
`enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf` / `isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf` below.

This is *not* the situation of the `SheafOfModules R = X.Modules` apparatus (`Ext` over the
structure sheaf `ùí™_X` as a sheaf of rings), which the historical ƒåech capstone
(`CechToCohomology.cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`, `AffineSerreVanishing.affine_serre_vanishing`)
runs on: there Mathlib v4.31 provides *no* Grothendieck-abelian / enough-injectives instance
for `SheafOfModules`, so every derived-functor statement in that lane carries
`[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` / `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` as an un-instantiable
hypothesis.  The genus carrier deliberately uses the constant-`k` sheaf category precisely to
avoid that trap ‚Äî and the trap is genuinely avoided.

Consequence (`module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`): gate 3 can simply be
dropped from the `N12` consumable ‚Äî Mathlib synthesises it ‚Äî leaving only gates 1, 2, 4.

## Gate 4 (the comparison `‚àÄ S, HasCechToHModuleIso ‚Ä¶`): NOT instantiable today

`HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞` (`MayerVietorisCover.lean`) is the `Prop`-class packaging of the
comparison data `‚àÄ n, cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F n (‚®Ü ùí∞)`, i.e. "for the cover
`ùí∞`, unnormalised ƒåech cohomology equals derived (`Ext`) cohomology in every degree".

For the 2-affine cover `S.coverFamily` this comparison is *mathematically* the Leray statement
"an affine cover is acyclic ‚üπ ƒåech computes cohomology", which for a **two**-element cover is
exactly the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris long exact sequence (`HModule'_sequence_exact`, in-tree) together
with **affine Serre vanishing in the constant-`k` sheaf apparatus**:
`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`, i.e. `Subsingleton (HModule' k
(toModuleKSheaf C) i U) = 0` for every affine open `U` and every `i > 0`.  Given that vanishing,
the comparison assembles degreewise: `n = 0` (sheaf axiom), `n = 1` (`cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok`
= node `N5`, in-tree, plus the MV cokernel identification), `n ‚â• 2` (both sides vanish).

The **precise gap** is therefore this single named input:

> **MISSING:** `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` ‚Äî Serre vanishing
> `H‚Å±(U, ùí™_C) = 0` (`i > 0`, `U` affine) in the `Sheaf (Opens.gT ‚Ä¶) (ModuleCat k)` apparatus,
> **plus** the degreewise assembly of the 2-cover comparison from it.

Neither piece is a one-line consequence of an existing lemma:

* Mathlib has no scheme-cohomology Serre vanishing at all, in any apparatus.
* The in-tree Serre vanishing `AffineSerreVanishing.affine_serre_vanishing` lives in the *wrong*
  category (`X.Modules`, `Ext (jShriekOU U)`) and is itself gated on the un-instantiable
  `[EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules]`; bridging it to the constant-`k` apparatus would need a
  "cohomology is independent of the linear structure" comparison that is likewise absent.
* The ƒåech-to-derived carrier `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` is currently *produced* in-tree only
  from `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` ‚Äî which itself bundles `HasCechToHModuleIso` on the affine
  sub-covers, i.e. the very comparison we are trying to instantiate (a circular producer).

So gate 4 is a genuine multi-lemma target, **but the historical infrastructure blocker is gone**:
because `EnoughInjectives ùíÆ` is now available (proved below), the honest way to close gate 4 is
to build affine Serre vanishing + the ƒåech comparison *directly in the constant-`k` sheaf
category* `ùíÆ` (where injective resolutions exist), rather than attempting to route through the
blocked `SheafOfModules` lane.  That is the recommended roadmap for the next wave.

## Deliverable of this file

* the gate-3 infrastructure, proved and named for citation
  (`isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf`, `enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf`,
  `hasExt_moduleKSheaf`, `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf`);
* the gate-3-free restatement of the `N12` consumable
  (`module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`): under gates 1, 2, 4 alone,
  `H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` is a finite `k`-module.  Once `P1ChartData` lands (gate 2) and the gate-4
  comparison is built, `Module.Finite k H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` ‚Äî hence the honesty of `genus C` ‚Äî is
  unconditional on a curve with a finite map to ‚Ñô¬π.
-/

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

variable {k : Type u} [Field k]

/-! ## Gate 3: the `Ext` infrastructure of the constant-`k` sheaf category is present -/

/-- The genus-carrier sheaf category `Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat k)` is **Grothendieck
abelian**: sheaves valued in the Grothendieck-abelian `ModuleCat.{u} k` on the small site of
opens of `X`.  Mathlib's `IsGrothendieckAbelian.{v} (Sheaf J A)` fires here.  This is the
structural fact that makes the derived-functor genus apparatus well-founded (contrast the
`SheafOfModules ùí™_X` apparatus, for which Mathlib v4.31 supplies no such instance). -/
theorem isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
    IsGrothendieckAbelian.{u}
      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
  inferInstance

/-- The genus-carrier sheaf category has **enough injectives** (Stacks `079H`, from
Grothendieck-abelianness).  This is the input the entire `Ext`/derived-functor comparison
needs, and the exact instance the `SheafOfModules ùí™_X` lane cannot supply.  Its availability
here is what dissolves the historical ƒåech-capstone blocker for the *constant-`k`* apparatus. -/
theorem enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
    EnoughInjectives
      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
  inferInstance

/-- Gate 3, lower universe: `HasExt.{u}` for the genus-carrier sheaf category is synthesisable
(from `HasExt.standard`, itself from Grothendieck-abelianness). -/
theorem hasExt_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
    HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
  inferInstance

/-- Gate 3, upper universe: `HasExt.{u+1}` for the genus-carrier sheaf category is
synthesisable (the ambient `Ext`-theory the universe-bridged `HModule` genus carrier runs on). -/
theorem hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
    HasExt.{u+1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
  inferInstance

/-! ## Gate-3-free restatement of the genus-finiteness consumable (node `N12`)

Because both `HasExt` gates are now synthesised (gate 3 above), the `N12` consumable of
`GenusFiniteness.lean` can be restated without them: the only remaining gates are the geometric
inputs (`HasFiniteMapToP1 C`, `P1HasLaurentChartData k`) and the ƒåech-to-derived comparison
(gate 4).  This is the honest, tightest current form of adelic genus finiteness. -/

/-- **Genus finiteness of the curve, gate-3-free form (node `N12`).**  For a curve `C` over `k`
with a finite morphism to ‚Ñô¬π and the ‚Ñô¬π chart data, and with the ƒåech-to-derived comparison on
2-affine covers (gate 4), the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C) = HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` is a
finite `k`-module.  Identical to `Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1` but with the
two `HasExt` hypotheses dropped ‚Äî Mathlib synthesises them from the Grothendieck-abelianness of
the constant-`k` sheaf category (`hasExt_moduleKSheaf`, `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf`).  Once gates
2 and 4 are discharged, `Module.Finite k H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` ‚Äî and hence the honesty of `genus C` ‚Äî is
unconditional on any curve with a finite map to ‚Ñô¬π. -/
theorem module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate
    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [HasFiniteMapToP1 C] [P1HasLaurentChartData k]
    [‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
      HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily] :
    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
  module_finite_hModule_one_of_finite_map C
    (fun S => ‚ü®S.hModuleOneEquivH1Cok (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)‚ü©)

end AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

data.content:
{Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ‚Ñï :=
  (cechComplexFunctor ùí∞).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))

/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the `n`-th ƒåech cohomology
of the structure sheaf for an arbitrary indexed open cover. Defined as the
`n`-th homology of the ƒåech cochain complex `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`. The
result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k` and therefore carries a `Module k`
structure for free; the iter-013+ comparison theorem will identify it
with `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n` when the cover is
acyclic. -/
noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ‚Ñï) :
    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C ùí∞).homology n

/-- Iter-047: parameterised ƒåech cochain complex generalising iter-012's
`Scheme.cechCochain_OC` to any sheaf of `k`-modules `F`, not just the structure
sheaf. Built from the same Mathlib `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor`
(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
underlying presheaf of `F`. The result is a cochain complex valued in
`ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `‚Ñï`. Iter-012's specialisation
`Scheme.cechCochain_OC C ùí∞` is recovered by setting `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`
(see `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` below).

This generalisation is the foundational scaffolding the iter-048+ ƒåech-vs-derived
comparison theorem will build on: the comparison map
`Scheme.cechCohomology k C F ùí∞ n ‚Üí‚Çó[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (‚®Ü·µ¢ ùí∞ i)`
(queued for iter-048) is naturally parameterised over the sheaf `F`, not just
the structure sheaf, so the parameterised carrier is the right level of
generality. -/
noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ‚Ñï :=
  (cechComplexFunctor ùí∞).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)

/-- Iter-047: parameterised ƒåech cohomology generalising iter-012's
`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`. The `n`-th cohomology of the parameterised ƒåech
cochain complex. The result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k`. Iter-012's
specialisation is recovered by `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` below. -/
noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ‚Ñï) :
    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
  (Scheme.cechCochain C F ùí∞).homology n

/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCochain_OC` is definitionally the
`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCochain`. The
proof is `rfl` since iter-012's body is the same `(cechComplexFunctor ùí∞).obj
((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))` term. Used by downstream
consumers (iter-048+) to switch between the iter-012 structure-sheaf-specific
form and the iter-047 parameterised form without semantic loss. -/
theorem Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
    Scheme.cechCochain_OC C ùí∞ = Scheme.cechCochain C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞ :=
  rfl

/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` is definitionally the
`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCohomology`. -/
theorem Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ‚Ñï) :
    Scheme.cechCohomology_OC C ùí∞ n =
      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞ n :=
  rfl

===N5/N6===
    have h := congrArg (fun t => t.hom p) (S.pairLift_comp_cechD01 F)
    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, ModuleCat.hom_neg,
      LinearMap.neg_apply] at h
    rw [S.cechToCyclesD01_coe F, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, S.overlapKerEquiv_coe F,
      map_neg, map_neg, h, neg_neg]
  ¬∑ rintro n ‚ü®m, rfl‚ü©
    refine Submodule.mem_map.mpr
      ‚ü®(S.pairDiff F).hom (-((S.pairProj F).hom m)), ‚ü®-((S.pairProj F).hom m), rfl‚ü©,
        Subtype.ext ?_‚ü©
    have hfact := congrArg (fun t => t.hom m) (S.pairProj_comp_pairLift F)
    have h := congrArg (fun t => t.hom ((S.pairProj F).hom m)) (S.pairLift_comp_cechD01 F)
    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, ModuleCat.hom_neg,
      LinearMap.neg_apply, ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq] at hfact h
    rw [hfact] at h
    rw [LinearEquiv.coe_coe, S.overlapKerEquiv_coe F, S.cechToCyclesD01_coe F,
      map_neg, map_neg]
    exact h.symm

/-- The concrete `toCycles` is definitionally the `moduleCatToCycles` of the short
complex `sc' 0 1 2` of the ƒåech cochain complex. -/
lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.cechToCyclesD01_eq :
    cechToCyclesD01 S.coverFamily F
      = ((Scheme.cechCochain C F S.coverFamily).sc' 0 1 2).moduleCatToCycles :=
  rfl

/-- **Node N5 ‚Äî the degree-`1` ƒåech cohomology of the 2-affine cover is the concrete
cokernel `»û¬π = Œì(U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ, F) ‚ß∏ im (sectionDiff)`.**  Unconditional: this is a pure
homological-algebra fact about the 2-cover complex ‚Äî no acyclicity or comparison
hypothesis.  The chain is: homology of the complex at `1` ‚âÖ homology of the short
complex `(d‚Å∞, d¬π)` ‚âÖ `ker d¬π ‚ß∏ im (toCycles)` (the concrete `ModuleCat` homology),
and the kernel equivalence `overlapKerEquiv` matches `im (sectionDiff)` with
`im (toCycles)` (`map_range_sectionDiff`), so the quotients agree. -/
noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok :
    cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] S.H1Cok F :=
  ((ShortComplex.homologyMapIso
      ((Scheme.cechCochain C F S.coverFamily).isoSc' 0 1 2
        ((ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï).prev_eq' rfl) ((ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï).next_eq' rfl))).trans
    ((Scheme.cechCochain C F S.coverFamily).sc' 0 1 2).moduleCatHomologyIso).toLinearEquiv.trans
  ((Submodule.Quotient.equiv (LinearMap.range (S.sectionDiff F))
      (LinearMap.range
        ((Scheme.cechCochain C F S.coverFamily).sc' 0 1 2).moduleCatToCycles)
      (S.overlapKerEquiv F)
      ((S.map_range_pairDiff F).trans
        (congrArg LinearMap.range (S.cechToCyclesD01_eq F)))).symm)

/-- **The lane's consumable corollary (nodes N5 + N6)**: under the ƒåech-to-derived
comparison gate on the 2-affine cover, the genus-degree cohomology `H¬π(C, F)` is the
concrete two-chart cokernel `»û¬π = Œì(U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ, F) ‚ß∏ (Œì(U‚ÇÅ, F) + Œì(U‚ÇÇ, F))`. -/
noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.hModuleOneEquivH1Cok
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [HasCechToHModuleIso F S.coverFamily] :
    HModule k F 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] S.H1Cok F :=
  (S.hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology_coverFamily F 1).trans
    (S.cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok F)

end OverlapCocycle

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

1	/-

4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
7	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Preorder
8	
9	/-!
10	# Mayer-Vietoris LES core for `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology
11	
12	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 1) contains the MV LES core for
13	`Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`-valued cohomology: the Mathlib gap-fill
14	`Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` plus the iter-016 ‚Üí iter-026 abstract
15	Mayer-Vietoris infrastructure (`HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor`,
16	`HModule'_toBiprod`, `HModule'_fromBiprod`, `HModule'_Œ¥`, `HModule'_sequence`,
17	`HModule'_sequence_exact`, and the `Œ¥`-zero simp companions).
18	
19	This is cohort 1 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean` (this file): MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	
24	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	
29	universe u v w w'
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
32	
33	/-! ## Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill: `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` as a `LinearEquiv`
34	
35	Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ‚âÉ Ext.{w'} X Y n`
36	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540) is a bare
37	`Equiv` only. The iter-034 universe-bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv`
38	(below in `section CoverTotality`) requires it as a `LinearEquiv` over the
39	`Linear R C`-enriched abelian category. The upgrade is mechanical: the
40	`AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` and `Module R (Ext X Y n)` instances are themselves
41	transferred from the standard derived category through `homEquiv`, and
42	Mathlib's load-bearing intermediate lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv` (Basic.lean
43	L543‚Äì545) shows that `chgUniv` preserves the underlying derived-category
44	morphism. So `chgUniv` automatically commutes with `+` and `‚Ä¢`. Two short
45	helper lemmas (`chgUniv_add` and `chgUniv_smul`) plus a 7-line `LinearEquiv`
46	structure literal give the upgrade.
47	
48	This cohort lives outside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` because the
49	helpers belong to the `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext` namespace. We use the
50	qualified-name pattern `Abelian.Ext.foo` rather than wrapping the entire file
51	in a second namespace block. -/
52	
53	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (a): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is additive. The
54	underlying `homEquiv` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean`
55	L162) is preserved by `chgUniv` (Mathlib lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv`,
56	L543‚Äì545), and the `AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` instance (L241‚Äì243) is defined by
57	transferring the `AddCommGroup` from the standard derived category through
58	`homEquiv`. Combining these via the `Ext.ext` extensionality (L177‚Äì179) gives
59	additivity. -/
60	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add
61	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
62	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ‚Ñï} (a b : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
63	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b) =
64	      Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a + Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b := by
65	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
66	  ext
67	  rw [Abelian.Ext.add_hom, ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
68	      ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply, ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
69	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b)) =
70	    Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a) +
71	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b)
72	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
73	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.add_hom]
74	
75	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (b): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is `R`-linear when `C`
76	is `Linear R`-enriched. Same proof shape as `chgUniv_add`, but using
77	`smul_hom` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Linear.lean` L52‚Äì55)
78	in place of `add_hom`. -/
79	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul
80	    {R : Type*} [Ring R] {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [Linear R C]
81	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ‚Ñï}
82	    (r : R) (a : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
83	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r ‚Ä¢ a) = r ‚Ä¢ Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a := by
84	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
85	  ext
86	  rw [Abelian.Ext.smul_hom, ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
87	      ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
88	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r ‚Ä¢ a)) =
89	    r ‚Ä¢ Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a)
90	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
91	      ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.smul_hom]
92	
93	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (c): the `R`-linear upgrade of Mathlib's bare-`Equiv`
94	`Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ‚âÉ Ext.{w'} X Y n` to a `LinearEquiv`,
95	when `C` is `Linear R`-enriched abelian. Combines (a) and (b). The
96	`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540 declaration is
97	universe-polymorphic but only proves the underlying bijection; this wrapper
98	adds the algebraic structure preservation needed for downstream `LinearEquiv`
99	chaining (specifically, the iter-034 universe bridge
100	`HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` below). -/
101	noncomputable def Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv
102	    {R : Type*} [Ring R] {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [Linear R C]
103	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ‚Ñï} :
104	    Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] Abelian.Ext.{w'} X Y n where
105	  toFun a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a
106	  invFun a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w} a
107	  left_inv a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'}.left_inv a
108	  right_inv a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'}.right_inv a
109	  map_add' a b := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add a b
110	  map_smul' r a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul r a
111	
112	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
113	
114	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the substantive functor mirroring
115	Mathlib's `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheafFunctor` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Sends
116	a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` to the presheaf
117	`X ‚Ü¶ Ext^n((presheafToSheaf J _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k).obj X), F)`,
118	i.e.\ to a presheaf `C·µí·µñ ‚•§ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is
119	`HModule' k F n X` (definitionally, see `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` below).
120	
121	The codomain is `C·µí·µñ ‚•§ AddCommGrpCat` (not `C·µí·µñ ‚•§ ModuleCat k`) because Mathlib's
122	`Abelian.extFunctor n : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ C ‚•§ AddCommGrpCat` always lands in `AddCommGrpCat`
123	regardless of the source category's `Linear`-enrichment; the per-fiber `Module k`
124	structure on `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op X)` is preserved
125	through the iter-014 `HModule'` reducibility chain. Probe-confirmed body
126	(iter-016 plan-agent). Used downstream as the prerequisite for the queued
127	iter-017+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
128	noncomputable def HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor
129	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
130	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
131	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
132	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
133	    (n : ‚Ñï) :
134	    Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k) ‚•§ C·µí·µñ ‚•§ AddCommGrpCat :=
135	  Functor.flip
136	    ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ‚ãô
137	      presheafToSheaf _ _).op ‚ãô Abelian.extFunctor n)
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the abbrev wrapper mirroring Mathlib's
140	`Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Evaluates the
141	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` at a sheaf `F`, giving a presheaf
142	`C·µí·µñ ‚•§ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is `HModule' k F n X` definitionally
143	(probe-confirmed `rfl` by the iter-016 plan-agent). The `noncomputable abbrev`
144	form is required for the per-fiber definitional identification with `HModule'`;
145	under `def` the wrapper would block the `rfl`-level reduction that downstream
146	Mayer-Vietoris and Stein-factorization arguments rely on. -/
147	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf
148	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
149	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
150	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
151	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
152	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ‚Ñï) :
153	    C·µí·µñ ‚•§ AddCommGrpCat :=
154	  (HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor k J n).obj F
155	
156	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the first Mayer-Vietoris LES building
157	block on the `ModuleCat k` side ‚Äî the sum of the two restriction maps
158	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f‚ÇÇ‚ÇÑ.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f‚ÇÉ‚ÇÑ.op`,
159	as a single map into the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
160	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod` (file
161	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L43‚Äì46) for
162	the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `F.cohomologyPresheaf ‚Üí HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F`.
163	
164	The codomain `(... ).obj (op S.X‚ÇÇ) ‚äû (...).obj (op S.X‚ÇÉ)` is the biproduct in
165	`AddCommGrpCat` (Mathlib `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Biproducts.lean`); since
166	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n` is `C·µí·µñ ‚•§ AddCommGrpCat`, the biproduct
167	auto-resolves. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). Used downstream as the
168	first of three Mayer-Vietoris LES building blocks; iter-018 will add the
169	composition-is-zero lemma and the connecting hom `Œ¥`, iter-019 the LES sequence
170	and exactness theorem. -/
171	noncomputable def HModule'_toBiprod
172	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
173	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
174	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
175	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
176	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ‚Ñï) :
177	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X‚ÇÑ) ‚ü∂
178	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X‚ÇÇ) ‚äû
179	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X‚ÇÉ) :=

282	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
283	short complex of free sheaves underlying the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
284	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.shortComplex`
285	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L234‚Äì249) for the
286	`ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free ‚Üí ModuleCat.free k`.

289	vertices of the Mayer-Vietoris square (with the middle pair `S.X‚ÇÇ` and `S.X‚ÇÉ`
290	biproduct-summed). The two morphisms are: `f` (the difference `(yoneda.map S.f‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ)
291	‚Äì (yoneda.map S.f‚ÇÅ‚ÇÉ)` lifted through the biproduct), and `g` (the sum

303	noncomputable def HModule'_shortComplex
304	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
305	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
306	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
307	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
308	    ShortComplex (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) where
309	  X‚ÇÅ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X‚ÇÅ ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k)
310	  X‚ÇÇ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X‚ÇÇ ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k) ‚äû
311	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X‚ÇÉ ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k)
312	  X‚ÇÉ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X‚ÇÑ ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k)
313	  f :=
314	    biprod.lift
315	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ) _))
316	      (-(presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f‚ÇÅ‚ÇÉ) _))

320	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f‚ÇÉ‚ÇÑ) _))
321	  zero :=

323	        presheafToSheaf J _)).cokernelCofork.condition

484	    {n : ‚Ñï} (y : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X‚ÇÑ)) :
485	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n y =
486	      (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv
487	        ‚ü®(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f‚ÇÇ‚ÇÑ.op y,
488	          (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f‚ÇÉ‚ÇÑ.op y‚ü© := by
489	  apply (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.injective
490	  dsimp [HModule'_toBiprod]
491	  ext
492	  ¬∑ rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
493	      ‚Üê AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_fst_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
494	      ‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_fst, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
495	  ¬∑ rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
496	      ‚Üê AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_snd_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
497	      ‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_snd, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
498	
499	/-- Iter-023 aux lemma 2: explicit elementwise formula for `HModule'_fromBiprod`
500	on the inverse of `biprodIsoProd` (mirror Mathlib L77‚Äì83). -/
501	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply
502	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
503	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
504	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
505	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
506	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
507	    {n : ‚Ñï}
508	    (y‚ÇÅ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X‚ÇÇ))
509	    (y‚ÇÇ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X‚ÇÉ)) :
510	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n
511	        ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv ‚ü®y‚ÇÅ, y‚ÇÇ‚ü©) =
512	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ.op y‚ÇÅ -
513	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f‚ÇÅ‚ÇÉ.op y‚ÇÇ := by
514	  dsimp [HModule'_fromBiprod]
515	  rw [‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
516	  simp [AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_desc, sub_eq_add_neg]
517	
518	-- Iter-023 aux lemma 3: bridges `AddCommGrpCat`-side `biprodIsoProd` and
519	-- `Ext`-side `Ext.biprodAddEquiv` for the `toBiprod` morphism
520	-- (mirror Mathlib L85‚Äì91). The `set_option ... in` and `attribute ... in`
521	-- wrappers match Mathlib L85, L86 verbatim.
522	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
523	attribute [local simp] HModule'_toBiprod_apply in
524	lemma HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply
525	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
526	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
527	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
528	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
529	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
530	    {n : ‚Ñï} (x : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X‚ÇÑ)) :
531	    Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm
532	        ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).hom (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n x)) =
533	      (Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ (HModule'_shortComplex k S).g).comp x (zero_add n) :=
534	  Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.injective (by cat_disch)
535	
536	-- Iter-023 aux lemma 4: bridges the same machinery for the `fromBiprod`
537	-- morphism (mirror Mathlib L93‚Äì106).
538	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
539	attribute [local simp] sub_eq_add_neg in
540	lemma HModule'_mk‚ÇÄ_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom
541	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
542	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
543	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
544	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
545	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
546	    {n : ‚Ñï}
547	    (x : ‚Üë((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X‚ÇÇ) ‚äû
548	           (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X‚ÇÉ))) :
549	    (Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ (HModule'_shortComplex k S).f).comp
550	        (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm
551	          ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).hom x)) (zero_add n) =
552	      (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n x) := by
553	  obtain ‚ü®‚ü®x‚ÇÇ, x‚ÇÉ‚ü©, rfl‚ü© :=
554	    (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.symm.surjective x
555	  dsimp
556	  rw [Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv_symm_apply,
557	    Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_symm_apply,
558	    HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply]
559	  cat_disch
560	
561	-- Iter-023 main: comparison iso from the iter-022 LES sequence to
562	-- `Ext.contravariantSequence` (mirror Mathlib L124‚Äì138). The technical heart
563	-- of the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
564	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
565	noncomputable def HModule'_sequenceIso
566	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
567	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
568	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
569	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
570	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
571	    (n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñï) (h : n‚ÇÄ + 1 = n‚ÇÅ) :
572	    HModule'_sequence k S F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h ‚âÖ
573	      Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence (HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S)
574	        F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ (by omega) :=
575	  ComposableArrows.isoMk‚ÇÖ (Iso.refl _)
576	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
577	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
578	    (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _)
579	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
580	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
581	    (Iso.refl _)
582	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
583	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk‚ÇÄ_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
584	    (by simp [ComposableArrows.Precomp.map]; rfl)
585	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
586	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk‚ÇÄ_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
587	
588	/-- Iter-026: Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness theorem (mirror Mathlib
589	`MayerVietoris.lean` L140‚Äì141). The iter-022 sequence is exact via the iter-023
590	comparison iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence`. -/
591	lemma HModule'_sequence_exact
592	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
593	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
594	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
595	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
596	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
597	    (n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñï) (h : n‚ÇÄ + 1 = n‚ÇÅ) :
598	    (HModule'_sequence k S F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h).Exact :=
599	  ComposableArrows.exact_of_iso (HModule'_sequenceIso k S F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h).symm
600	    (Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence_exact _ _ _ _ _)
601	
602	/-- Iter-026: `Œ¥ ‚â´ toBiprod = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L143‚Äì145). -/
603	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
604	lemma HModule'_Œ¥_toBiprod
605	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
606	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
607	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
608	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
609	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
610	    (n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñï) (h : n‚ÇÄ + 1 = n‚ÇÅ) :
611	    HModule'_Œ¥ k S F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h ‚â´ HModule'_toBiprod k S F n‚ÇÅ = 0 :=
612	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h).zero 2
613	
614	/-- Iter-026: `fromBiprod ‚â´ Œ¥ = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L147‚Äì149). -/
615	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
616	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_Œ¥
617	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
618	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
619	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
620	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
621	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
622	    (n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñï) (h : n‚ÇÄ + 1 = n‚ÇÅ) :
623	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n‚ÇÄ ‚â´ HModule'_Œ¥ k S F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h = 0 :=
624	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h).zero 1
625	
626	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietorisCore
7	
8	/-!
9	# Mayer-Vietoris on a 2-affine cover and ƒåech-acyclic cover infrastructure
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 2) contains the 2-affine cover
12	Mayer-Vietoris theorems, the cover-totality bridges (universe-shift
13	`LinearEquiv`s, `finrank` / `Module.Finite` transports), the
14	`IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers, the comparison-iso typeclass
15	`HasCechToHModuleIso`, and the existence-bundled
16	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` predicate together with the producer that
17	fires `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` from a `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover`
18	hypothesis.
19	
20	This is cohort 2 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
21	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
22	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean` (this file): 2-affine cover MV +
23	  cover-totality bridges + `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers +
24	  `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v w w'
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	section AffineCoverMVSquare
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): a bundled 2-affine cover of a scheme
40	together with the affineness of the pairwise intersection. The geometric input
41	to the abstract Mayer-Vietoris LES `HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes ‚Äî packaged
42	to record the affineness conditions and the cover-totality hypothesis that the
43	abstract MV-square does not capture. The accessor `toMayerVietorisSquare`
44	produces the underlying Mathlib `MayerVietorisSquare`.
45	
46	Mathematically: a 2-affine open cover of `X` whose pairwise intersection is also
47	affine. The cover hypothesis `cover : U‚ÇÅ ‚äî U‚ÇÇ = ‚ä§` is load-bearing for downstream
48	Serre-finiteness use (it identifies the `MayerVietorisSquare` corner `X‚ÇÑ` with
49	the whole scheme `X`). -/
50	structure AffineCoverMVSquare (X : Scheme.{u}) where
51	  /-- First affine open of the cover. -/
52	  U‚ÇÅ : X.Opens
53	  /-- Second affine open of the cover. -/
54	  U‚ÇÇ : X.Opens
55	  /-- `U‚ÇÅ` is affine. -/
56	  isAffineOpen_U‚ÇÅ : IsAffineOpen U‚ÇÅ
57	  /-- `U‚ÇÇ` is affine. -/
58	  isAffineOpen_U‚ÇÇ : IsAffineOpen U‚ÇÇ
59	  /-- The intersection `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ` is affine. -/
60	  isAffineOpen_inf : IsAffineOpen (U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ)
61	  /-- Total cover: `U‚ÇÅ ‚äî U‚ÇÇ = ‚ä§`. -/
62	  cover : U‚ÇÅ ‚äî U‚ÇÇ = ‚ä§
63	
64	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): the underlying abstract Mayer-Vietoris
65	square produced by a 2-affine cover. Bridge from the geometric input (an
66	`AffineCoverMVSquare`) to the categorical input (a `MayerVietorisSquare` for
67	the Grothendieck topology on `Opens X.toTopCat`) the abstract MV-LES theorem
68	`HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes. The four corners collapse definitionally:
69	`X‚ÇÅ = U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ`, `X‚ÇÇ = U‚ÇÅ`, `X‚ÇÉ = U‚ÇÇ`, `X‚ÇÑ = U‚ÇÅ ‚äî U‚ÇÇ` (each by `rfl`,
70	plan-agent probe-verified). -/
71	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare {X : Scheme.{u}}
72	    (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
73	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat).MayerVietorisSquare :=
74	  Opens.mayerVietorisSquare S.U‚ÇÅ S.U‚ÇÇ
75	
76	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X‚ÇÅ = U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ`. The first three corners are
77	`rfl`-equal because `Opens.mayerVietorisSquare` defines them definitionally. -/
78	@[simp]
79	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X‚ÇÅ
80	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
81	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X‚ÇÅ = S.U‚ÇÅ ‚äì S.U‚ÇÇ := rfl
82	
83	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X‚ÇÇ = U‚ÇÅ`. -/
84	@[simp]
85	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X‚ÇÇ
86	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
87	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X‚ÇÇ = S.U‚ÇÅ := rfl
88	
89	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X‚ÇÉ = U‚ÇÇ`. -/
90	@[simp]
91	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X‚ÇÉ
92	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
93	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X‚ÇÉ = S.U‚ÇÇ := rfl
94	
95	/-- Iter-029 cover-totality identification: `X‚ÇÑ = ‚ä§`. The substantive corner
96	identification ‚Äî uses the `cover` field, which is the totality hypothesis
97	`U‚ÇÅ ‚äî U‚ÇÇ = ‚ä§` recorded in `AffineCoverMVSquare`. -/
98	@[simp]
99	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X‚ÇÑ
100	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
101	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X‚ÇÑ = ‚ä§ := S.cover
102	
103	/-- Iter-029 Mayer-Vietoris LES sequence on a 2-affine cover: a routine
104	specialisation of the iter-022 abstract sequence `HModule'_sequence` to the
105	bundled `AffineCoverMVSquare` data. Parameterised over a generic sheaf `F`
106	for reusability; the `toModuleKSheaf C` application is iter-030+ work. The
107	fully-qualified `_root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence` reference
108	is required because `def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence` auto-opens the
109	`AffineCoverMVSquare` namespace inside the body, causing bare
110	`HModule'_sequence` to recursively resolve to itself. -/
111	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence
112	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
113	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
114	    (n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñï) (h : n‚ÇÄ + 1 = n‚ÇÅ) :
115	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
116	  _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence k S.toMayerVietorisSquare F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h
117	
118	/-- Iter-029 Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness on a 2-affine cover: a routine
119	specialisation of the iter-026 abstract exactness theorem
120	`HModule'_sequence_exact` to the bundled `AffineCoverMVSquare` data. -/
121	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence_exact
122	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
123	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
124	    (n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñï) (h : n‚ÇÄ + 1 = n‚ÇÅ) :
125	    (S.HModule'_sequence k F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h).Exact :=
126	  _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence_exact k S.toMayerVietorisSquare F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h
127	
128	/-- Iter-030 Mayer-Vietoris LES on a 2-affine cover of a curve: a routine
129	binding of the iter-029 sheaf-parameterised `HModule'_sequence` to the
130	structure sheaf `toModuleKSheaf C` for `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`.
131	The dot-notation `S.HModule'_sequence` resolves cleanly via method-call
132	against `S : AffineCoverMVSquare`, sidestepping the iter-029 sub-namespace
133	shadowing trap that required `_root_` disambiguation in the abstract
134	specialisation's body. -/
135	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence_curve
136	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
137	    (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
138	    (n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñï) (h : n‚ÇÄ + 1 = n‚ÇÅ) :
139	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
140	  S.HModule'_sequence k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h
141	
142	/-- Iter-030 Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness on a 2-affine cover of a curve:
143	the exactness companion of `HModule'_sequence_curve`, by direct application
144	of the iter-029 abstract `HModule'_sequence_exact` to the structure sheaf
145	`toModuleKSheaf C`. -/
146	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence_curve_exact
147	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
148	    (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
149	    (n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñï) (h : n‚ÇÄ + 1 = n‚ÇÅ) :
150	    (S.HModule'_sequence_curve k n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h).Exact :=
151	  S.HModule'_sequence_exact k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h
152	
153	end AffineCoverMVSquare
154	
155	/-- Iter-031 cover-totality source-object identification. For a category `C`
156	with terminal object `T`, the sheafification of the free `k`-module on the
157	representable presheaf at `T` is canonically isomorphic to the constant sheaf
158	at `ModuleCat.of k k`. This is the natural-iso prerequisite for identifying
159	`HModule' k F n T` (open-evaluation cohomology at the terminal `T`, iter-014)
160	with `HModule k F n` (global cohomology, iter-009): both are `Ext` groups
161	whose source objects are made canonically isomorphic by this iso.
162	
163	Assembled from three Mathlib pieces, mirroring the structure
164	`constantSheaf J D = Functor.const C·µí·µñ ‚ãô presheafToSheaf J D`:
165	1. `yoneda.obj T ‚âÖ (Functor.const C·µí·µñ).obj PUnit` ‚Äî the terminal-collapse
166	   of the representable, by `IsTerminal.from`/`IsTerminal.hom_ext`.
167	2. `Functor.constComp _ PUnit (ModuleCat.free k)` ‚Äî the identity
168	   `(Functor.const C·µí·µñ).obj PUnit ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k =
169	    (Functor.const C·µí·µñ).obj ((ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit)`
170	   packaged as an iso.
171	3. `(Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso` ‚Äî
172	   `(ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit ‚âÖ ModuleCat.of k k`. -/
173	noncomputable def HModule'_top_sourceIso
174	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
175	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
176	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
177	    {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
178	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj
179	        ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj T)
180	      ‚âÖ (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) :=
181	  (presheafToSheaf J _).mapIso
182	    (Functor.isoWhiskerRight
183	        (NatIso.ofComponents
184	          (fun _ => Equiv.toIso { toFun := fun _ => PUnit.unit
185	                                  invFun := fun _ => hT.from _
186	                                  left_inv := fun _ => hT.hom_ext _ _
187	                                  right_inv := fun _ => rfl })
188	          (fun _ => by ext; rfl))
189	        (ModuleCat.free k) ‚â™‚â´
190	      Functor.constComp _ PUnit.{u+1} (ModuleCat.free k) ‚â™‚â´
191	      (Functor.const C·µí·µñ).mapIso

14	abbrevs, the H‚Å∞ `LinearEquiv` transport companions, the constant-sheaf-Œì adjunction
15	upgrade), and the curve-side producer instances. Upstream sub-files
16	`Presheaf.lean` and `SheafProperty.lean` provide the structure-sheaf object the
17	carriers depend on.
18	
19	Downstream finite-length carriers in this file:
20	
21	* H>0 affine cohomology vanishing is carried by iter-040's
22	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` (the producer instance for the curve case
23	  lives at iter-051).
24	* H‚Å∞ Hom-finiteness is carried by iter-043's **wholespace**
25	  `IsHModuleHomFinite`; the producer instance on a proper geometrically
26	  integral $k$-curve is supplied at iter-046 from Stein finiteness
27	  (`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper`).
28	* The iter-041 per-affine-open variant `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite` was an
29	  abandoned attempt (dead scaffolding ‚Äî $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is not finite
30	  over $k$ on a proper affine open of a non-trivial curve) and has been
31	  removed from this file.
32	
33	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
34	-/
