40	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
41	
42	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
43	
44	/-- Phase A step 5/6 bridge (iter-009 scaffold): the parallel `Sheaf.H` for
45	`ModuleCat k`-valued sheaves. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is
46	parameterised over `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` only, so closing `genus` honestly
47	requires this `ModuleCat k`-flavoured version. The result carries `Module k`
48	automatically via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and
49	`Module.finrank k` is therefore well-defined on it. The declaration is a
50	`noncomputable abbrev` (rather than `def`) so that instance synthesis sees
51	through the wrapper to find `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances. -/
52	noncomputable abbrev HModule
53	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
54	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
55	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
56	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ‚Ñï) : Type (u+1) :=
57	  Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n
58	
59	/-- Phase A step 6 algebraic bridge (iter-010 scaffold): the $k$-linear
60	identification of `HModule k F 0` with the Hom group from the constant
61	sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
62	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ : Ext X Y 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (X ‚ü∂ Y)` in any
63	`Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to the `Linear k`
64	enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
65	`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
66	`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ`;
67	probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
68	identify `H‚Å∞(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Œì(C, O_C)` viewed as a
69	`k`-module on a connected proper `k`-curve. -/
70	noncomputable def HModule_zero_linearEquiv
71	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
72	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
73	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
74	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
75	    HModule k F 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
76	      ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F) :=
77	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ
78	
79	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-013 scaffold): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
80	cohomology of an open `X : C` with values in a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`.
81	Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X = (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (op X)`
82	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L105) for
83	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, with `AddCommGrpCat.free ‚Üí ModuleCat.free k`.
84	
85	The codomain is `Type u` (not `ModuleCat.{u} k`): `Abelian.Ext` returns a bare
86	`Type u` carrying `Module k` via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` through the `Linear k`
87	enrichment. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) is required so
88	instance synthesis sees through the wrapper to find `Module k (HModule' k F n X)`
89	and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` ‚Äî under `def`, `Module.finrank` would fail
90	to typecheck (cf. iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`).
91	
92	This is the prerequisite for the iter-014+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris
93	LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`).
94	The iter-014+ work will state and prove the LES on a `MayerVietorisSquare`,
95	specialising to a 2-affine cover of a proper `k`-curve in iter-015+. The
96	comparison theorem `cechCohomology_OC C ùí∞ n ‚âÖ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`
97	for an acyclic cover (the technical heart of Path-2) is also queued for
98	iter-015+. -/
99	noncomputable abbrev HModule' (k : Type u) [Field k]
100	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
101	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
102	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
103	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ‚Ñï) (X : C) : Type u :=
104	  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
105	    ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n
106	
107	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-015): the `H‚Å∞` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`,
108	mirroring iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 `HModule'`. The
109	$k$-linear identification of `HModule' k F 0 X` with the Hom group from the
110	sheafified representable `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k).obj X)`.
111	Mathlib provides `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ : Ext X Y 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (X ‚ü∂ Y)`
112	in any `Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to `R = k` and to the
113	`Linear k`-enriched abelian category `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (the `Linear k`
114	enrichment is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's
115	`Sheaf.linear`), this collapses `HModule' k F 0 X` to a `k`-linear Hom group. The
116	closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ`; probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-015
117	plan-agent). Used downstream as the algebraic prerequisite for Stein-factorization-
118	derived `H‚Å∞(C, O_C) ‚âÉ k` on a connected proper `k`-curve (multi-iteration; queued
119	iter-017+ alongside the Mayer-Vietoris LES of iter-016+). -/
120	noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv
121	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
122	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
123	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
124	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
125	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) :
126	    HModule' k F 0 X ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
127	      ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
128	        ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ‚ü∂ F) :=
129	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ
130	
131	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H‚Å∞ transport companion for `HModule k F 0`. The iter-010
132	H‚Å∞ bridge `HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] ((constantSheaf J _).obj
133	(ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F)` identifies the degree-zero cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom
134	group; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across.
135	The `.symm` is required: iter-010's bridge has `HModule k F 0` on the LHS and the
136	Hom group on the RHS, but we want the Hom hypothesis on the LHS and the
137	`HModule` conclusion on the RHS, so we apply `.symm` first. Mirrors iter-037's
138	`module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X‚ÇÑ` pattern at degree $0$, with no Mayer-Vietoris
139	machinery required. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k
140	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness
141	input is supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves. -/
142	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero
143	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
144	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
145	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
146	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
147	    [Module.Finite k ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F)] :
148	    Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0) :=
149	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule_zero_linearEquiv k F).symm
150	
151	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H‚Å∞ transport companion for `HModule' k F 0 X`. Parallel
152	of `Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised carrier
153	`HModule'`. The iter-015 H‚Å∞ bridge `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv : HModule' k F 0 X ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
154	((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k).obj X) ‚ü∂ F)` identifies the
155	degree-zero open-evaluation cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom group from the sheafified
156	representable; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness
157	across. The `.symm` is required for the same orientation reason as in
158	`Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero`. -/
159	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero
160	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
161	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
162	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
163	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
164	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C)
165	    [Module.Finite k ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
166	        ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ‚ü∂ F)] :
167	    Module.Finite k (HModule' k F 0 X) :=
168	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule'_zero_linearEquiv k F X).symm
169	
170	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero` to
171	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` of a `Spec k`-scheme `C`. The
172	Grothendieck topology `Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat` is auto-inferred
173	via the iter-005 instances `instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK` and
174	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK`. The sheaf argument is inferred from the
175	result type. Mirrors iter-030 / iter-035 / iter-036 / iter-037's `_curve`
176	patterns. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k (HModule k
177	(toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness input is
178	supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves (typically the morally
179	trivial `H^0(C, O_C) ‚âÉ k` from Stein factorization on a connected proper
180	curve). -/
181	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_curve
182	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
183	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
184	    [Module.Finite k
185	      ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
186	        (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
187	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) :=
188	  Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero k _
189	
190	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule'_zero` to
191	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` of a `Spec k`-scheme `C`,
192	evaluated at an arbitrary open `U` of the underlying topological space. Parallel
193	of `module_finite_HModule_zero_curve` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised
194	carrier `HModule'`.  Same auto-inferred topology / instance setup. The open `U`
195	is an explicit parameter (unlike the implicit topology); the sheaf argument is
196	inferred. Used downstream in the cover-evaluation chain for proper geometrically
197	integral $k$-curves: for each affine corner $X_i$ of an `AffineCoverMVSquare`,
198	the iter-039 transport propagates `Module.Finite k`-ness from the Hom group
199	`((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô free_k).obj X_i) ‚ü∂ toModuleKSheaf C)` ‚Äî
200	morally `Module.Finite k (Œì(X_i, O_C))` for affine $X_i$ ‚Äî to the H‚Å∞ cohomology
201	piece `HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0 X_i`. -/
202	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero_curve
203	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
204	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
205	    (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
206	    [Module.Finite k
207	      ((presheafToSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
208	        ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj U) ‚ü∂
209	          Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
210	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0 U) :=
211	  Scheme.module_finite_HModule'_zero k _ U
212	
213	/-- Iter-040 affine cohomology vanishing carrier predicate. Packages the
214	geometric statement that for every affine open `U` of `C.left.toTopCat` and
215	every degree `i > 0`, the open-evaluation cohomology `Scheme.HModule' k F i U`
216	is the zero `k`-module (formulated as `Subsingleton`, since `HModule'` returns
217	a `Type u` rather than a `ModuleCat` object ‚Äî see iter-014 abbrev). The class
218	is the affine-vanishing input the cover-evaluation chain consumes once the
219	producer instance is supplied (queued for iter-041+; multi-iteration
220	project-local construction expected since Mathlib does not yet provide
221	scheme-level Serre vanishing on affines for the `ModuleCat k`-flavour). -/
222	class IsAffineHModuleVanishing
223	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
224	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
225	    Prop where
226	  subsingleton_HModule' : ‚àÄ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat},
227	    AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U ‚Üí ‚àÄ i, 0 < i ‚Üí
228	      Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U)
229	
230	/-- Iter-040 immediate consumer of `IsAffineHModuleVanishing`: given the
231	class hypothesis, the open-evaluation cohomology `HModule' k F i U` is
232	`Module.Finite k` for any affine open `U` and any `i > 0`. The proof unfolds
233	the class field to `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i U)` and then invokes
234	Mathlib's auto-derived `Subsingleton M ‚Üí Module.Finite R M` instance (any
235	subsingleton module is finitely generated by the empty set, hence finite).
236	This consumer is the building block for the cover-evaluation chain: combined
237	with iter-039's `H^0` curve specialisations and iter-037's general-degree
238	corner-bridge transport, it closes the algebraic side of `Module.Finite k
239	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` once the producer instance for
240	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing` is supplied. -/
241	theorem module_finite_HModule'_of_isAffineHModuleVanishing
242	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
243	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
244	    [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]
245	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
246	    (hU : AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U) (i : ‚Ñï) (hi : 0 < i) :
247	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
248	  have : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
249	    IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) hU i hi
250	  inferInstance
251	
252	/-- Iter-043 wholespace H‚Å∞ Hom-finiteness carrier predicate. Packages the
253	algebraic statement that the global Hom group
254	`((constantSheaf _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F)` is finite over `k`. Morally
255	`Œì(C.left, F)` (the **global** sections of `F`) being finite over `k`. On a
256	proper geometrically integral $k$-curve $C$ with $F = O_C$, this is
257	$\Gamma(C, O_C) \simeq k$ (Stein factorization on a proper geometrically
258	connected curve), so this class admits a producer instance from the
259	geometric content of $C$ (supplied at iter-046).
260	
261	**Historical note on the abandoned per-affine-open variant.** An earlier
262	iter-041 attempt packaged Hom-finiteness of
263	`((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô free k).obj U) ‚ü∂ F)` **for every
264	affine open** `U`. By Yoneda + free-functor + sheafify adjunctions this
265	Hom group is `‚âÉ‚Çó[k] Œì(U, F)`, and on a proper smooth $k$-curve $C$ with
266	$F = O_C$, $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is **NOT finite over $k$** for $U$ a proper
267	affine open ‚Äî e.g. for the standard cover of $\mathbb{P}^1_k$ by
268	$U_0 = U_1 = \mathbb{A}^1_k$, $\Gamma(U_i, O_{\mathbb{P}^1}) = k[t]$ is
269	infinite over $k$. That per-open class therefore admits no producer
270	instance on a non-trivial proper curve and has been deleted as dead
271	scaffolding.
272	
273	This wholespace version captures only the **global** Hom group, which on
274	a proper curve is finite via Stein. Iter-044+ LES finite-length transport
275	uses this wholespace class (in conjunction with iter-040's H>0 affine
276	vanishing). -/
277	class IsHModuleHomFinite
278	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
279	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) : Prop where
280	  module_finite_hom : Module.Finite k
281	    ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
282	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F)
283	
284	/-- Iter-043 immediate consumer: with the wholespace H‚Å∞ Hom-finiteness class
285	hypothesis in scope, transport via iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero`
286	(which transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across iter-010's H‚Å∞ algebraic bridge
287	`HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] ((constantSheaf _).obj _ ‚ü∂ F)`)
288	to obtain `Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0)`. Works at the global level
289	(one Hom-finiteness instance, not one per affine open). -/
290	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite
291	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
292	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
293	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C F] :
294	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k F 0) :=
295	  have := IsHModuleHomFinite.module_finite_hom (k := k) (C := C) (F := F)
296	  module_finite_HModule_zero k F
297	
298	/-- Iter-043 curve specialisation: direct dot-notation wrapper for
299	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`. Saves call sites in the curve setting from
300	re-typing the sheaf argument when chaining into the LES finite-length
301	transport (queued for iter-044+). Mirrors the iter-039/iter-042 `_curve`
302	patterns. -/
303	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve
304	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
305	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
306	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) :=
307	  module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite k C _
308	
309	/-- Iter-044 geometric Stein input for the iter-043 wholespace H‚Å∞
310	Hom-finiteness carrier. For `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` an
311	integral $k$-scheme with proper structure morphism, the global sections
312	$\Gamma(C, O_C)$ form a finite-dimensional $k$-vector space.
313	
314	This is Stein's classical statement, packaged via Mathlib's
315	`AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`
316	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean`):
317	for $X$ integral and $f \colon X \to \Spec K$ universally closed and
318	locally of finite type, the structure-morphism ring map `f.appTop`
319	is module-finite. `IsProper f` packages both `UniversallyClosed f` and
320	`LocallyOfFiniteType f`.
321	
322	The bridge from `RingHom.Finite (C.hom.appTop.hom)` to
323	`Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ‚ä§))` (where the algebra
324	structure on `Œì(C, ‚ä§)` is iter-006's `kToSection`-derived) uses
325	`RingHom.finite_algebraMap` plus `Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv` to
326	transport the base ring from intermediate `Œì(Spec k, ‚ä§)` to `k` along
327	the ring iso `Scheme.ŒìSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)`. The compatibility
328	of algebra maps reduces to showing
329	`kToSection C (op ‚ä§).hom = (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp (Scheme.ŒìSpecIso _).inv.hom`,
330	which collapses via `Subsingleton.elim` on the `‚ä§ ‚ü∂ ‚ä§` hom-set +
331	`Functor.map_id`.
332	
333	Iter-045+ consumes this input to assemble the producer instance
334	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` via lifting Mathlib's
335	`constantSheafŒìAdj.homEquiv` to a `LinearEquiv` + identification of
336	`Sheaf.Œì.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)` with the underlying global sections
337	+ transport via `Module.Finite.equiv`. -/
338	theorem module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper
339	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
340	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
341	    Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)) := by
342	  have hf : (C.hom.appTop.hom).Finite :=
343	    AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k C.hom
344	  letI alg2 : Algebra ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))
345	               (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))
346	    := RingHom.toAlgebra C.hom.appTop.hom
347	  have hM_inter :
348	      Module.Finite ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))
349	        (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)) := by
350	    rw [‚Üê RingHom.finite_algebraMap]; exact hf
351	  refine Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv
352	    (Scheme.ŒìSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
353	    (RingEquiv.refl _) ?_
354	  ext x
355	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, RingEquiv.refl_apply,

In ùíÆ, the cohomology carrier is `Scheme.HModule' k F n U := Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj U)) F n` (AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK/Carriers.lean:99) ‚Äî the analogue of Ext(jShriekOU U, F). The ƒåech side in ùíÆ is `Scheme.cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n` (Carriers.lean:573), built from Mathlib's CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor.

DELIVERABLE: a port plan. Specifically:

A. TEMPLATE INVENTORY ‚Äî for CechBridge.lean, AbsoluteCohomology.lean, CechToCohomology.lean: list every major declaration in dependency order with name, file:line, one-line role in the 01EO argument (L1 = section-ƒåech SES from a sheaf SES; L2 = quotient ƒåech-vanishing; L3 = base case H¬π=0; L4 = dimension-shift induction; plus helpers: jShriekOU, sectionsFunctorCorepIso, injective_cech_acyclic, faceShortComplex, cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis, quotient_cech_vanishing_of_basis, absoluteCohomology_one_eq_zero_of_basis, absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective, absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact‚ÇÅ, ses_cech_h1, injective_cech_acyclic...). For each, note which X.Modules-SPECIFIC ingredients its statement/proof uses (jShriekOU, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf, X.ringCatSheaf, sectionCechCosimplicial, toPresheafOfModules, Scheme.Modules.*), i.e. what must be replaced in a constant-k port.
B. THE ANALOGUE DICTIONARY ‚Äî for each X.Modules ingredient, identify the constant-k analogue and whether it EXISTS already: jShriekOU U ‚Ü¶ freeSheafOn U (the presheafToSheaf‚àòfree‚àòyoneda above ‚Äî is there a named def?); Hom(jShriekOU U, F) ‚âÉ F(U)-sections (in X.Modules: sectionsFunctorCorepIso?) ‚Ü¶ in ùíÆ: find the adjunction chain (Sheaf.adjunction/sheafificationAdjunction + ModuleCat free-forget adjunction whiskering + yoneda) and any EXISTING project lemma computing Hom_{ùíÆ}(freeSheafOn U, F) ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] F(U) ‚Äî search Carriers.lean, StructureSheafModuleK.lean, StructureSheafModuleK/ subfolder (Presheaf.lean, SheafProperty.lean, Carriers.lean), MayerVietorisCore.lean for such Hom computations; the ƒåech complex functoriality in ùíÆ (Mathlib cechComplexFunctor is functorial ‚Äî check what natural-transformation/short-complex API exists on it in Mathlib and in the project); homology LES of a SES of cochain complexes in ModuleCat k (Mathlib: HomologySequence / ShortComplex.ShortExact.homology stuff ‚Äî exact names).
C. MATHLIB BRICKS for the port: (1) Ext vanishing on injective target: exact name of the Mathlib lemma giving Ext X I (n+1) = 0 (or subsingleton) for injective I in an abelian category with HasExt (search Abelian.Ext, e.g. Ext.eq_zero_of_injective / Injective-related in Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/); (2) covariant Ext long exact sequence for a ShortExact short complex: exact names (Ext.covariantSequence / ShortComplex.ShortExact.ext_exact... find what v4.31 has, the project's AbsoluteCohomology.lean already uses some ‚Äî list what it uses); (3) EnoughInjectives + HasExt instances for Sheaf J (ModuleCat k): confirmed present via IsGrothendieckAbelian ‚Äî the project already proved them (RiemannRoch/Adelic/CechComparisonGate.lean) ‚Äî just confirm names; (4) presheafToSheaf preservesFiniteColimits/epi facts available (the project's RiemannRoch/Adelic files proved toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits, toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms in AffineSerreVanishing.lean:119,151 ‚Äî check statements: are they generic over any site/category? report exact signatures).
D. STRUCTURE RECOMMENDATION: propose the constant-k port file split with an estimated LOC per piece, and flag the 3 hardest lemmas of the port (e.g. injective-ƒåech-acyclicity: how does the X.Modules proof work ‚Äî via Hom(-,I) of an exact complex of frees? summarize its actual proof strategy from CechBridge.lean in 10 lines so a port agent can mirror it).

Format: structured markdown; exact names and file:line everywhere. This report will be pasted verbatim into implementation-agent briefs, so precision matters more than brevity.

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietorisCover
7	
8	/-!
9	# The 2-cover ƒåech cokernel `»û¬π(D)` for adelic Riemann‚ÄìRoch (nodes N5‚ÄìN7)
10	
11	This file is part of the **adelic Riemann‚ÄìRoch lane** (see the lane design document).
12	It realises the degree-`1` cohomology of a curve as the concrete cokernel of a
13	two-chart cover ‚Äî the algebraic incarnation of Weil's repartition quotient
14	`A_K / (A_K(D) + K)`.
15	
16	## The bridge from genus `H¬π` to the ƒåech `H¬π` (node N6)
17	
18	The project's genus carrier is `H¬π(C, ùí™_C) = HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1`.
19	For a cover `ùí∞` of `C` with `‚®Ü ùí∞ = ‚ä§` that is a **good** (Leray) cover ‚Äî i.e. one
20	for which the ƒåech-to-derived comparison `HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞` holds ‚Äî there is a
21	`k`-linear identification
22	```
23	HModule k F 1  ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]  cechCohomology C F ùí∞ 1,
24	```
25	obtained by chaining the comparison iso `cechToHModuleIso` (iter-050) with the
26	universe bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` (iter-034) at the terminal open
27	`‚ä§ = ‚®Ü ùí∞`.  This is `hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology`.
28	
29	The hypothesis `HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞` is the existing project **gate** (a
30	single-field `Prop` class with no unconditional instance for a 2-affine cover of a
31	positive-genus curve): the equivalence is delivered *conditional* on that class,
32	exactly as the design intends.  Note that `IsCechAcyclicCover F ùí∞` for the whole
33	curve is **not** available (nor true) here: for a genus-`g` curve the degree-`1`
34	ƒåech cohomology of a 2-affine cover is `k^g ‚âÝ 0`, so it is deliberately *not*
35	assumed ‚Äî the comparison `HasCechToHModuleIso` (which does *not* force the
36	cohomology to vanish) is the correct and honest hypothesis.
37	
38	## The concrete section-level cokernel (node N5 target, definition)
39	
40	For a bundled 2-affine cover `S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare` and a sheaf `F` of
41	`k`-modules, the **difference-of-restrictions** map
42	```
43	Œ¥ : Œì(U‚ÇÅ, F) √ó Œì(U‚ÇÇ, F) ‚Üí‚Çó[k] Œì(U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ, F),   (a, b) ‚Ü¶ a|_{U‚ÇÅ‚à©U‚ÇÇ} ‚àí b|_{U‚ÇÅ‚à©U‚ÇÇ}
44	```
45	is `AffineCoverMVSquare.sectionDiff`.  Its cokernel `AffineCoverMVSquare.H1Cok`
46	is the concrete `»û¬π`, and its kernel `AffineCoverMVSquare.sectionGlue` is the
47	`H‚Å∞ = L(D)` sheaf-gluing space (global sections `= { f : agree on the overlap }`).
48	
49	Because `F` is an arbitrary sheaf of `k`-modules, `H1Cok S F` is *already* the
50	divisor-twisted `»û¬π(D)`: specialising `F := ùí™_C(D)` once N3's `sectionOfDivisor`
51	lands recovers the `H¬π(D)` of the œá-ledger.
52	
53	## The concrete 2-cover family and the N6 bridge on it (this wave)
54	
55	`AffineCoverMVSquare.coverFamily : ULift (Fin 2) ‚Üí Opens X` packages `U‚ÇÅ, U‚ÇÇ` as the
56	`Type u`-indexed family that `Scheme.cechCohomology` / the N6 bridge consume, with
57	`iSup_coverFamily : ‚®Ü i, S.coverFamily i = ‚ä§` the family-form of the cover totality.
58	`hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology_coverFamily` then lands the N6 bridge on this
59	concrete family: `HModule k F n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily n` (gated on
60	`HasCechToHModuleIso F S.coverFamily`).  Chaining node N5 (below) into this at `n = 1`
61	gives the lane's consumable corollary `HModule k F 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] H1Cok S F`.
62	
63	## The N5 identification `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] H1Cok S F` (PROVED)
64	
65	The in-tree `cechCohomology` is the homology of Mathlib's **unnormalized**
66	`cechComplexFunctor` (`FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor` of the ƒåech nerve): in
67	degree `n` the product runs over **all** `Fin (n+1) ‚Üí Œπ` (not just increasing
68	multi-indices), and the differential `d‚Åø = ‚àë_{i} (-1)^i Œ¥‚Å±` is the alternating sum of
69	the cosimplicial cofaces `Œ¥‚Å± = evalOp(P)(mapPower Œ¥_i)` (a `Pi.lift` of projections to
70	`x ‚àò Œ¥_i` followed by the restriction `P(‚®Ö U‚àò(x‚àòŒ¥_i)) ‚Üí P(‚®Ö U‚àòx)`).  For the 2-element
71	cover (`Œπ = ULift (Fin 2)`) this is
72	```
73	  M‚Å∞ = Œì(U‚ÇÄ) √ó Œì(U‚ÇÅ)                         (indices Fin 1 ‚Üí Œπ)
74	  M¬π = Œì(U‚ÇÄ) √ó Œì(U‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ) √ó Œì(U‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ) √ó Œì(U‚ÇÅ)       (indices (0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1))
75	  M¬≤ = ‚àè over the 8 indices Fin 3 ‚Üí Œπ         (incl. the degenerate diagonals)
76	```
77	where `Œì(U‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ) = Œì(U‚ÇÄ ‚äì U‚ÇÅ)`, and the two diagonal factors of `M¬π` are the degenerate
78	cofaces `U_i ‚äì U_i = U_i`.  Writing a degree-1 cochain as `(p, q, r, s)`:
79	
80	* `d‚Å∞(a, b) = (0, b|‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ ‚àí a|‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ, a|‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ ‚àí b|‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ, 0)`, so
81	  `im d‚Å∞ = { (0, w, ‚àíw, 0) : w ‚àà range (S.sectionDiff F) }` (note `w = b|‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ ‚àí a|‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ` and
82	  `sectionDiff (a,b) = a|‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ ‚àí b|‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ = ‚àíw`, whence the images coincide as subgroups);
83	* `(d¬π n)_{(x‚ÇÄ,x‚ÇÅ,x‚ÇÇ)} = n_{(x‚ÇÅ,x‚ÇÇ)}| ‚àí n_{(x‚ÇÄ,x‚ÇÇ)}| + n_{(x‚ÇÄ,x‚ÇÅ)}|`.  The eight
84	  components force, after restriction (each triple intersection collapses to `U‚ÇÄ`, `U‚ÇÅ`
85	  or `U‚ÇÄ ‚äì U‚ÇÅ`): the diagonals `p = s = 0` (from `x = (0,0,0)` and `(1,1,1)`) and
86	  `r = ‚àíq` (from `x = (0,1,0)` / `(1,0,1)`), the remaining four being automatic.  Hence
87	  `ker d¬π = { (0, q, ‚àíq, 0) : q ‚àà Œì(U‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ) } ‚âÖ Œì(U‚ÇÄ‚ÇÅ)` via `q`.
88	
89	Therefore `H¬π = ker d¬π / im d‚Å∞ ‚âÖ Œì(U‚ÇÄ ‚äì U‚ÇÅ) / range (S.sectionDiff F) = H1Cok S F`, the
90	iso being `[(0, q, ‚àíq, 0)] ‚Ü¶ [q]`.  The identification is **unconditional** (no
91	acyclicity hypothesis ‚Äî it is a pure homological-algebra fact about this specific
92	2-cover complex), and it is carried out in full in this file:
93	
94	* `sectionRestrict_trans`/`sectionRestrict_self`/`restrict_map_comp`/
95	  `restrict_map_self`/`isIso_restrict_map` ‚Äî the poset restriction calculus every
96	  cancellation step reduces to;
97	* `cechD01`/`cechD12` with `cechD01_œÄ`/`cechD12_œÄ` ‚Äî the low-degree differentials in
98	  product-typed, componentwise form;
99	* `overlapCocycle` with `overlapCocycle_comp_d` ‚Äî the cocycle `q ‚Ü¶ (0, q, ‚àíq, 0)`
100	  and the 8-index cancellation;
101	* `ker_cechD12_œÄ_diag_zero‚ÇÄ`/`‚ÇÅ`, `ker_cechD12_œÄ_off_diag`, `overlapKerEquiv` ‚Äî the
102	  kernel description `ker d¬π = {(0, q, ‚àíq, 0)} ‚âÉ‚Çó Œì(U‚ÇÄ ‚äì U‚ÇÅ)`;
103	* `pairLift`/`pairProj`/`pairLift_comp_cechD01`/`map_range_pairDiff` ‚Äî the image
104	  identification `im d‚Å∞ = Œ∫(im sectionDiff)`;
105	* `cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok` ‚Äî node N5 proper, assembled through
106	  `isoSc' 0 1 2` and `ShortComplex.moduleCatHomologyIso`;
107	* `hModuleOneEquivH1Cok` ‚Äî the N5+N6 consumable
108	  `HModule k F 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] H1Cok S F` under the `HasCechToHModuleIso` gate.
109	-/
110	
111	set_option autoImplicit false
112	
113	universe u v
114	
115	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
116	
117	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
118	
119	/-! ## Node N6 ‚Äî the `k`-linear bridge `HModule k F 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] cechCohomology C F ùí∞ 1` -/
120	
121	/-- **Node N6 (bridge), abstract sheaf form.** For a sheaf `F` of `k`-modules on a
122	`Spec k`-scheme `C` and a cover `ùí∞` with `‚®Ü ùí∞ = ‚ä§` satisfying the ƒåech-to-derived
123	comparison gate `HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞`, the genus-degree cohomology
124	`HModule k F n` is `k`-linearly identified with the ƒåech cohomology
125	`cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n` of the cover.
126	
127	The equivalence chains the iter-050 comparison
128	`cechToHModuleIso n : cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F n (‚®Ü ùí∞)` with the
129	iter-034 universe bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` at the terminal open
130	`‚ä§ = ‚®Ü ùí∞` (using `Preorder.isTerminalTop` transported along `h`, exactly the
131	iter-035 `HModule'_X‚ÇÑ_linearEquiv` pattern), then symmetrises.
132	
133	This is stated *conditional* on the `HasCechToHModuleIso` gate ‚Äî no new `sorry` or
134	gate is introduced.  Downstream (`N5`, the finiteness keystone `N11`/`N12`) the
135	useful degree is `n = 1`: it reduces `H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` to the concrete ƒåech `H¬π`. -/
136	noncomputable def hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology
137	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
138	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
139	    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
140	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
141	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
142	    [HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞]
143	    (h : ‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i = ‚ä§) (n : ‚Ñï) :
144	    HModule k F n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n :=
145	  ((cechToHModuleIso n).trans
146	    (HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv k F n
147	      (h.symm ‚ñ∏ Preorder.isTerminalTop (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)))).symm
148	
149	/-- **Node N6 (bridge), curve specialisation.** Direct application of
150	`hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology` to the structure sheaf
151	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.  Mirrors the iter-039/‚Ä¶/iter-050 `_curve` pattern
152	(dot-notation resolution against the structure sheaf), giving
153	`HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] cechCohomology C (toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞ n`. -/
154	noncomputable def hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology_curve
155	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
156	    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
157	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
158	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
159	    [HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞]
160	    (h : ‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i = ‚ä§) (n : ‚Ñï) :
161	    HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n
162	      ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞ n :=
163	  hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞ h n
164	
165	/-! ## Node N5 ‚Äî the concrete 2-element cover family of an `AffineCoverMVSquare` -/
166	
167	/-- **The 2-element open cover family `ùí∞ : ULift (Fin 2) ‚Üí Opens X` of an
168	`AffineCoverMVSquare`.** Indexed by `ULift.{u} (Fin 2)` (the `Type u` two-element
169	index that `Scheme.cechCohomology`/`hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology` consume),
170	it sends the first index to `U‚ÇÅ` and the second to `U‚ÇÇ`.  This is the family whose
171	ƒåech cohomology `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily 1` is identified with the concrete
172	cokernel `H1Cok S F` (node N5 proper) and fed into the N6 bridge. -/
173	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.coverFamily {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
174	    ULift.{u} (Fin 2) ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat :=
175	  fun i => if i.down = 0 then S.U‚ÇÅ else S.U‚ÇÇ
176	
177	@[simp] lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.coverFamily_zero {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
178	    S.coverFamily ‚ü®0‚ü© = S.U‚ÇÅ := rfl
179	
180	@[simp] lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.coverFamily_one {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
181	    S.coverFamily ‚ü®1‚ü© = S.U‚ÇÇ := rfl
182	
183	/-- **Cover totality of `coverFamily`.** The 2-element family covers `X`:
184	`‚®Ü i, S.coverFamily i = ‚ä§`.  This is the `Œπ ‚Üí Opens`-family incarnation of the
185	`AffineCoverMVSquare.cover` field `U‚ÇÅ ‚äî U‚ÇÇ = ‚ä§`, and is the hypothesis
186	`hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology` requires to land the N6 bridge on the whole
187	curve. -/
188	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.iSup_coverFamily {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
189	    ‚®Ü i, S.coverFamily i = ‚ä§ := by
190	  refine le_antisymm le_top ?_
191	  have h : S.U‚ÇÅ ‚äî S.U‚ÇÇ ‚â§ ‚®Ü i, S.coverFamily i :=
192	    sup_le (le_iSup_of_le ‚ü®0‚ü© (le_of_eq S.coverFamily_zero.symm))
193	      (le_iSup_of_le ‚ü®1‚ü© (le_of_eq S.coverFamily_one.symm))
194	  exact S.cover ‚ñ∏ h
195	
196	/-- **Node N6 bridge on the concrete 2-cover family.** The specialisation of
197	`hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology` to `S.coverFamily`, discharging the totality
198	hypothesis with `iSup_coverFamily`: for a sheaf `F` of `k`-modules on the curve `C`
199	satisfying the ƒåech-to-derived comparison gate on the 2-affine cover, the
200	genus-degree cohomology `HModule k F n` is `k`-linearly identified with the ƒåech
201	cohomology `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily n` of the concrete 2-element cover.
202	Chaining node N5's `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] H1Cok S F` into this (at
203	`n = 1`) delivers the lane's consumable `HModule k F 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] H1Cok S F`. -/
204	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology_coverFamily
205	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
206	    (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
207	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
208	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
209	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
210	    [HasCechToHModuleIso F S.coverFamily]
211	    (n : ‚Ñï) :
212	    HModule k F n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily n :=
213	  hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology F S.coverFamily S.iSup_coverFamily n
214	
215	/-! ## Node N5 target ‚Äî the concrete section-level difference map and cokernel -/

222	/-- The restriction `k`-linear map `Œì(V, F) ‚Üí‚Çó[k] Œì(U, F)` for an inclusion of opens
223	`U ‚â§ V`, extracted from the underlying presheaf of the sheaf of `k`-modules `F`. -/
224	noncomputable def sectionRestrict {U V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat} (h : U ‚â§ V) :
225	    F.obj.obj (Opposite.op V) ‚Üí‚Çó[k] F.obj.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
226	  (F.obj.map (homOfLE h).op).hom
227	
228	/-- **Node N5 target ‚Äî the difference-of-restrictions map** of a 2-affine cover.
229	For `S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare`, this is the `k`-linear map
230	`Œì(U‚ÇÅ, F) √ó Œì(U‚ÇÇ, F) ‚Üí‚Çó[k] Œì(U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ, F)` sending `(a, b)` to `a|_{U‚ÇÅ‚à©U‚ÇÇ} ‚àí b|_{U‚ÇÅ‚à©U‚ÇÇ}`.
231	Its image is the "coboundary" subspace `Œì(U‚ÇÅ) + Œì(U‚ÇÇ)` inside `Œì(U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ)`. -/
232	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.sectionDiff (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
233	    (F.obj.obj (Opposite.op S.U‚ÇÅ) √ó F.obj.obj (Opposite.op S.U‚ÇÇ)) ‚Üí‚Çó[k]
234	      F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (S.U‚ÇÅ ‚äì S.U‚ÇÇ)) :=

236	    - (sectionRestrict F (inf_le_right)).comp (LinearMap.snd k _ _)
237	
238	/-- **The concrete ƒåech `»û¬π` of a 2-affine cover** as the cokernel of the
239	difference-of-restrictions map:
240	`»û¬π = Œì(U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ, F) ‚ß∏ (Œì(U‚ÇÅ, F) + Œì(U‚ÇÇ, F))`.
241	For `F` an arbitrary sheaf of `k`-modules this is the divisor-twisted `»û¬π(D)`;
242	specialising `F := ùí™_C(D)` recovers Weil's `A_K/(A_K(D)+K)`. -/

251	    (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) : Module k (S.H1Cok F) :=
252	  inferInstanceAs (Module k (_ ‚ß∏ LinearMap.range (S.sectionDiff F)))
253	
254	/-- **The `H‚Å∞ = L(D)` gluing space** as the kernel of the difference-of-restrictions
255	map: a pair `(a, b)` lies in the kernel iff `a` and `b` agree on the overlap
256	`U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ`, i.e. glue to a single global section (`= { f ‚àà K : div f + D ‚â• 0 }` in the
257	twisted case).  This is the `Submodule` incarnation of `L(D)`. -/
258	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.sectionGlue (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
259	    Submodule k (F.obj.obj (Opposite.op S.U‚ÇÅ) √ó F.obj.obj (Opposite.op S.U‚ÇÇ)) :=
260	  LinearMap.ker (S.sectionDiff F)
261	
262	/-- **The gluing/`L(D)` condition is agreement on the overlap.** A pair of sections
263	`(a, b) ‚àà Œì(U‚ÇÅ, F) √ó Œì(U‚ÇÇ, F)` lies in the `H‚Å∞`-gluing space `sectionGlue` exactly
264	when the two restrictions to `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ` coincide.  In the twisted case `F = ùí™_C(D)`
265	this is `L(D) = { f ‚àà K : div f + D ‚â• 0 }`. -/
266	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.mem_sectionGlue (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
267	    (p : F.obj.obj (Opposite.op S.U‚ÇÅ) √ó F.obj.obj (Opposite.op S.U‚ÇÇ)) :
268	    p ‚àà S.sectionGlue F ‚Üî
269	      sectionRestrict F (inf_le_left) p.1 = sectionRestrict F (inf_le_right) p.2 := by
270	  rw [sectionGlue, LinearMap.mem_ker, AffineCoverMVSquare.sectionDiff,
271	    LinearMap.sub_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply,
272	    LinearMap.fst_apply, LinearMap.snd_apply, sub_eq_zero]
273	
274	end ConcreteCokernel

276	/-! ## Node N5 ‚Äî concrete component formulas for the unnormalized ƒåech differential
277	
278	The in-tree `cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n = (cechCochain C F ùí∞).homology n` is the homology of
279	Mathlib's unnormalized `cechComplexFunctor`, routed through
280	`FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor = cosimplicialObjectFunctor ‚ãô alternatingCofaceMapComplex`.
281	There is no cosimplicial dual of `alternatingFaceMapComplex_obj_d` in Mathlib, so the concrete
282	`Pi.œÄ`-component formulas for the differential are derived here directly by unfolding the
283	alternating coface sum.  These are the reusable foundation the degree-`1` identification
284	(`cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] H1Cok S F`) is built on:
285	
286	* `cechCosimplicial_Œ¥_œÄ` ‚Äî the single-coface component
287	  `YŒ¥·µ¢ ‚â´ œÄ‚±º = œÄ_{j‚àòŒ¥·µ¢} ‚â´ (reindex restriction)`;
288	* `prodOpens_eq_iInf` ‚Äî the abstract ƒåech product object `‚àè·∂ú (ùí∞‚àòj)` is the concrete infimum
289	  `‚®Ö‚Çê ùí∞(j a)` of opens (equality in the poset `Opens X`), the bridge to `Œì(‚®Ö‚Çê U‚Çê)`;
290	* `alternatingCofaceMapComplex_objD` ‚Äî the differential is the alternating coface sum (the
291	  missing Mathlib dual);
292	* `cechCochain_d01_œÄ` / `cechCochain_d12_œÄ` ‚Äî the degree-`0`/degree-`1` differentials,
293	  componentwise.
294	-/
295	
296	section CechDifferential
297	
298	open AlgebraicTopology CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits
299	
300	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
301	  {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
302	  (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
303	
304	/-- **The alternating coface differential (Mathlib dual, project-local).** The degree-`n`
305	differential of `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` is the alternating sum of coface maps
306	`objD Y n = ‚àë·µ¢ (-1)‚Å± ‚Ä¢ YŒ¥·µ¢`.  Mathlib has `alternatingFaceMapComplex_obj_d` but no cosimplicial
307	dual; this recreates it (the same one-line `CochainComplex.of` unfolding). -/
308	theorem alternatingCofaceMapComplex_objD {D : Type*} [Category D] [Preadditive D]
309	    (Y : CosimplicialObject D) (n : ‚Ñï) :
310	    ((alternatingCofaceMapComplex D).obj Y).d n (n + 1)
311	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD Y n := by
312	  simp only [alternatingCofaceMapComplex, AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj, CochainComplex.of_d]
313	
314	/-- The cosimplicial object underlying the ƒåech complex `cechCochain C F ùí∞`: it sends `‚¶ãn‚¶å` to
315	`‚àè_{i : Fin (n+1) ‚Üí Œπ} Œì(‚®Ö‚Çê ùí∞(i a), F)`.  `cechCochain C F ùí∞` is by definition
316	`alternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj (cechCosimplicial ùí∞ F)`. -/
317	noncomputable def cechCosimplicial : CosimplicialObject (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
318	  (FormalCoproduct.cosimplicialObjectFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ ùí∞).cech).obj
319	    ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)
320	
321	lemma cechCochain_eq :
322	    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cechCochain C F ùí∞
323	      = (alternatingCofaceMapComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (cechCosimplicial ùí∞ F) :=
324	  rfl
325	
326	/-- **Single-coface component of the ƒåech cosimplicial object.** The `i‚ÇÄ`-th coface map
327	`YŒ¥_{i‚ÇÄ} : ƒå^n ‚ü∂ ƒå^{n+1}`, projected to the factor indexed by `j : Fin (n+2) ‚Üí Œπ`, is the factor
328	`j ‚àò Œ¥_{i‚ÇÄ}` of the source followed by the reindexing restriction
329	`Œì(‚®Ö‚Çê ùí∞(j(Œ¥_{i‚ÇÄ} a))) ‚ü∂ Œì(‚®Ö‚Çê ùí∞(j a))`.  Derived by unfolding
330	`cosimplicialObjectFunctor` (`evalOp` of the ƒåech nerve) and `Pi.lift ‚â´ Pi.œÄ`. -/
331	theorem cechCosimplicial_Œ¥_œÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (i‚ÇÄ : Fin (n + 2)) (j : Fin (n + 2) ‚Üí Œπ) :
332	    (cechCosimplicial ùí∞ F).Œ¥ i‚ÇÄ ‚â´ Pi.œÄ _ j
333	      = Pi.œÄ _ (j ‚àò (Fin.succAboveOrderEmb i‚ÇÄ)) ‚â´
334	          ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F).map
335	            (Pi.lift (fun a => Pi.œÄ ((FormalCoproduct.mk _ ùí∞).obj ‚àò j)
336	              ((Fin.succAboveOrderEmb i‚ÇÄ) a))).op := by
337	  simp only [cechCosimplicial, CosimplicialObject.Œ¥,
338	    FormalCoproduct.cosimplicialObjectFunctor_obj_map]
339	  erw [Limits.Pi.lift_œÄ]
340	  rfl
341	
342	/-- **The abstract ƒåech product is the concrete infimum of opens.** In the poset `Opens X` the
343	categorical product `‚àè·∂ú (ùí∞‚àòj)` of the family `a ‚Ü¶ ùí∞ (j a)` equals its infimum `‚®Ö‚Çê ùí∞ (j a)`.
344	This is the bridge identifying the ƒåech section groups `Œì(‚àè·∂ú ùí∞‚àòj, F)` with the concrete
345	`Œì(‚®Ö‚Çê ùí∞(j a), F)` (and, for the 2-cover, with `Œì(U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ)`). -/
346	theorem prodOpens_eq_iInf {m : ‚Ñï} (j : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ) :
347	    (‚àè·∂ú ((FormalCoproduct.mk _ ùí∞).obj ‚àò j) : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
348	      = ‚®Ö a, ùí∞ (j a) :=
349	  le_antisymm (le_iInf fun a => (Limits.Pi.œÄ ((FormalCoproduct.mk _ ùí∞).obj ‚àò j) a).le)
350	    (Limits.Pi.lift (fun a => homOfLE (iInf_le (fun a => ùí∞ (j a)) a))).le
351	
352	/-- The ƒåech face inclusion: the full intersection `‚àè·∂ú (ùí∞‚àòj) = ‚®Ö_{Fin (n+2)} ùí∞(j b)` is contained
353	in the face intersection `‚àè·∂ú (ùí∞‚àò(j‚àòŒ¥_{i‚ÇÄ})) = ‚®Ö_{Fin (n+1)} ùí∞(j(Œ¥_{i‚ÇÄ} a))` (dropping one index),
354	the `‚â§` of opens underlying the restriction that the coface map applies. -/
355	theorem prodOpens_Œ¥_le {n : ‚Ñï} (i‚ÇÄ : Fin (n + 2)) (j : Fin (n + 2) ‚Üí Œπ) :
356	    (‚àè·∂ú ((FormalCoproduct.mk _ ùí∞).obj ‚àò j) : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
357	      ‚â§ ‚àè·∂ú ((FormalCoproduct.mk _ ùí∞).obj ‚àò (j ‚àò Fin.succAboveOrderEmb i‚ÇÄ)) := by
358	  rw [prodOpens_eq_iInf, prodOpens_eq_iInf]
359	  exact le_iInf fun a => iInf_le (fun b => ùí∞ (j b)) (Fin.succAboveOrderEmb i‚ÇÄ a)

12	/-!
13	# ƒåech bridge ‚Äî assembly layer
14	
15	This file is the downstream assembly layer that consumes both:
16	
17	- `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplex` (from `PresheafCech.lean`): the section ƒåech
18	  cochain complex `ƒå‚Ä¢(ùí∞, F) : CochainComplex Ab ‚Ñï` built cosimplicially from
19	  `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`.
20	
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex` (from `FreePresheafComplex.lean`): the
22	  free-presheaf chain complex `K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢ : ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ‚Ñï` whose
23	  degree-`p` term is `‚àê_{œÉ : Fin(p+1) ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ)`.
24	
25	The reason this file must sit downstream of both is that `FreePresheafComplex.lean`
26	already imports `PresheafCech.lean`, so any file needing both must import
27	`FreePresheafComplex.lean` (which transitively brings in `PresheafCech.lean`).
28	
29	## Declarations
30	
31	- `cechComplex_hom_identification` (**proved**, line ~241): the per-degree `Ab`-isomorphism
32	  `Hom(K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢, F) ‚âÖ ƒå‚Ä¢(ùí∞, F)` intertwining the differentials, assembled cosimplicially
33	  via `homCechSectionCosimplicialIso` and `(alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab).mapIso`.
34	
35	- `homCechComplexMapOpIso` / `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso` (**proved**, project-local Mathlib
36	  supplement below): the contravariant-transport bridge. `homCechComplexMapOpIso` identifies
37	  the alternating-coface hom-complex `homCechComplex ùí∞ F` with the mapped opposite
38	  `Hom(-, F)((K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢)·µí·µñ)` of the free ƒåech complex (same degreewise terms; the differential
39	  squares are the degreewise identity `homCechComplex_d_eq`).  `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso`
40	  composes it with `cechComplex_hom_identification` to identify `ƒå‚Ä¢(coverOpen ùí∞, F)` with the
41	  mapped opposite of the free complex.  This is the precise bridge that lets
42	  `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` turn Lane-1's free resolution into ƒåech
43	  acyclicity in a single step.
44	
45	- `preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective` /
46	  `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` (**proved**, project-local Mathlib
47	  supplement below): the categorical bridge step. For an injective object `I` of an abelian
48	  category, the contravariant `Hom(-, I) = preadditiveYoneda.obj I` is exact (preserves
49	  finite colimits, hence homology), so it carries a quasi-isomorphism of chain complexes to
50	  a quasi-isomorphism of the `Hom`-cochain complex. This is the ingredient that turns the
51	  free-complex resolution `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` (Lane 1) and
52	  `cechComplex_hom_identification` into the positive-degree vanishing below.
53	
54	- `injective_cech_acyclic` (**proved**, project-local Mathlib supplement below): ƒåech
55	  cohomology vanishes in positive degrees for injective sheaves.  Built as the one-step
56	  op-transport assembly of `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` (Part 1, via
57	  `injective_toPresheafOfModules`) with the free resolution `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
58	  (Lane 1) transported onto the section ƒåech complex through `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso`.
59	
60	- `ses_cech_h1` (**proved**, project-local Mathlib supplement below): the surjectivity on
61	  sections induced by a short exact sequence of sheaves with ƒåech-H¬π-vanishing kernel
62	  (Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`), via the cocycle-to-coboundary heart
63	  `sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology` and sheaf gluing.
64	
65	- `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` / `affine_serre_vanishing` (planned): the comparison
66	  isomorphism `»û‚Ä¢(ùí∞, F) ‚âÖ H‚Ä¢(X, F)` on affine schemes / for acyclic covers, leading to
67	  Serre's vanishing theorem.
68	-/

76	/-
77	Planner strategy for `cechComplex_hom_identification`:
78	‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
79	
80	Goal: construct a cochain-complex isomorphism
81	  `Hom(K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢, F) ‚âÖ ƒå‚Ä¢(ùí∞, F)`
82	where:
83	  ‚Ä¢ `K(ùí∞)_p = ‚àê_{œÉ : Fin(p+1) ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ)`
84	    (the degree-`p` term of `cechFreePresheafComplex ùí∞`)
85	  ‚Ä¢ `ƒå^p(ùí∞, F) = ‚àè_{œÉ : Fin(p+1) ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ} F.obj (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ)`
86	    (the degree-`p` term of `sectionCechComplex ùí∞ F`)
87	  Both complexes live in `Ab` after applying the global-sections functor.
88	
89	Step 1 ‚Äî per-degree hom-coproduct duality:
90	  For each `p`, the Yoneda/free-presheaf adjunction gives
91	    `Hom(freeYoneda.obj V, F) ‚âÖ F.obj V`  (natural in `V`).
92	  This is exactly `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` from `PresheafCech.lean`.
93	  Combined with the coproduct-hom identity
94	    `Hom(‚àê_œÉ A_œÉ, F) ‚âÖ ‚àè_œÉ Hom(A_œÉ, F)`
95	  (via `preadditiveYoneda` preservation of limits, or hand-rolled from
96	  `Limits.Sigma.desc` / `Limits.Sigma.Œπ`), we get a degree-wise `Ab`-iso
97	    `Hom(K(ùí∞)_p, F) ‚âÖ ‚àè_œÉ F.obj (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ) = ƒå^p(ùí∞, F)`.
98	
99	Step 2 ‚Äî differential intertwining:
100	  Show that the degree-wise isos commute with the differentials.  The differential on
101	  `Hom(K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢, F)` is precomposition with the ƒåech boundary `d_p : K(ùí∞)_{p+1} ‚Üí K(ùí∞)_p`
102	  (the alternating sum of face maps of `cechFreeSimplicial`).  The differential on
103	  `ƒå‚Ä¢(ùí∞, F)` is the alternating sum of restriction maps built by `sectionCechComplex`.
104	  These match under the per-degree iso by naturality of `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`.
105	
106	Step 3 ‚Äî assemble:
107	  Use `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents` (or equivalent) to combine the per-degree
108	  isos into a full cochain-complex iso, supplying the naturality squares from Step 2.
109	
110	Key API:
111	  ‚Ä¢ `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` (PresheafCech.lean): the per-degree iso.
112	  ‚Ä¢ `Limits.Sigma.desc`, `Limits.Sigma.Œπ`: coproduct universal property in
113	    `X.PresheafOfModules`.
114	  ‚Ä¢ `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`: assemble component-wise isos into a
115	    full complex iso.
116	  ‚Ä¢ `sectionCechComplex` (PresheafCech.lean): the target complex.
117	  ‚Ä¢ `cechFreePresheafComplex` / `cechFreePresheafComplex_X` (FreePresheafComplex.lean):
118	    the source complex and its degreewise unfolding lemma.
119	-/
120	
121	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî ƒåech hom-identification -/
122	
123	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
124	
125	/-- **The hom cosimplicial abelian group `Hom(K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢, F)`.**
126	
127	The cosimplicial object in `Ab` obtained by applying the contravariant additive functor
128	`Hom_{PMod}(-, F) = preadditiveYoneda.obj F` to the free ƒåech simplicial object
129	`cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞`.  Its value in degree `p` is the hom-group
130	`Hom_{PMod}(K(ùí∞)_p, F)`, and its alternating-coface-map cochain complex is the left-hand
131	side of the ƒåech hom-identification.  Defining it this way makes the differential of the
132	hom-complex literally the alternating sum of `Hom(face·µ¢, F)`, so the identification with
133	`sectionCechComplex` reduces to a cosimplicial natural isomorphism.
134	
135	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `cechFreeSimplicial` nor this contravariant transport. -/
136	noncomputable def homCechCosimplicial (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) [Finite ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ]
137	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} :=
138	  (cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞).rightOp ‚ãô preadditiveYoneda.obj F
139	
140	/-- **The hom cochain complex `Hom(K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢, F)`** ‚Äî the left-hand side of the ƒåech
141	hom-identification.
142	
143	The alternating-coface-map cochain complex of `homCechCosimplicial ùí∞ F`. Built the same way as
144	`sectionCechComplex` (PresheafCech.lean) so that the planned identification
145	`cechComplex_hom_identification : homCechComplex ùí∞ F ‚âÖ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen ùí∞) F` is just
146	`(alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab).mapIso` of the cosimplicial natural isomorphism.
147	
148	Project-local: the hom-complex of the free ƒåech resolution has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
149	noncomputable def homCechComplex (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) [Finite ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ]
150	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ‚Ñï :=
151	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (homCechCosimplicial ùí∞ F)
152	
153	/-- **Per-degree component of the ƒåech hom-identification.**
154	
155	For each simplicial degree `n`, the abelian-group isomorphism
156	`Hom_{PMod}(K(ùí∞)_n, F) ‚âÖ ‚àè_œÉ F(‚®Ö‚Çñ U(œÉ k))` assembled from (i) the coproduct‚Äìhom duality
157	`Hom(‚àê_œÉ AœÉ, F) ‚âÖ ‚àè_œÉ Hom(AœÉ, F)` (the additive functor `Hom(-, F)` preserves the limit,
158	so `piComparison` is an iso, combined with `opCoproductIsoProduct`), and (ii) the per-index
159	free‚ÄìYoneda section identification `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`.
160	
161	Project-local: this is the degreewise core of `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`. -/
162	noncomputable def homCechSectionIsoApp (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) [Finite ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ]
163	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (n : SimplexCategory) :
164	    (homCechCosimplicial ùí∞ F).obj n ‚âÖ (sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen ùí∞) F).obj n :=
165	  (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapIso
166	      (opCoproductIsoProduct
167	        (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ)))
168	    ‚â™‚â´ asIso (piComparison (preadditiveYoneda.obj F)
169	        (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ => Opposite.op (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ))))
170	    ‚â™‚â´ Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ =>
171	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ) F).toAddCommGrpIso)
172	
173	/-- `Pi.mapIso e` is by definition `Pi.map (fun b => (e b).hom)`. -/
174	private lemma pi_mapIso_hom_eq {Œ≤ : Type*} {C : Type*} [Category C] {f g : Œ≤ ‚Üí C}
175	    [HasProductsOfShape Œ≤ C] (e : ‚àÄ b, f b ‚âÖ g b) :

180	Reduces the naturality of the assembled iso to the naturality of `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`. -/
181	private lemma homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_œÄ (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) [Finite ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ]
182	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (n : SimplexCategory) (œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ) :
183	    (homCechSectionIsoApp ùí∞ F n).hom ‚â´
184	        Pi.œÄ (fun œÑ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ =>
185	          F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÑ))) œÉ =
186	      (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map
187	          (Limits.Sigma.Œπ (fun œÑ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ =>
188	            freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÑ)) œÉ).op ‚â´
189	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (coverInterOpen ùí∞ œÉ) F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom := by
190	  rw [homCechSectionIsoApp, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom,
191	    pi_mapIso_hom_eq]
192	  simp only [Category.assoc]
193	  erw [Pi.map_œÄ, piComparison_comp_œÄ_assoc]
194	  rw [‚Üê Category.assoc]
195	  congr 1
196	  exact ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
197	    (congrArg _ (opCoproductIsoProduct_hom_comp_œÄ œÉ))

199	/-- **Naturality of the free‚ÄìYoneda section identification in the open.**
200	
201	For an inclusion of opens `h : V ‚ü∂ W`, precomposition with `freeYoneda.map h` on the hom-side
202	corresponds to restriction `F.presheaf.map h.op` on the section side.  This is the single
203	naturality square that powers the cosimplicial naturality of the ƒåech hom-identification. -/
204	private lemma freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality {V W : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•X}
205	    (h : V ‚ü∂ W) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
206	    (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map (freeYoneda.map h).op ‚â´
207	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv V F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom
208	      = (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv W F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom ‚â´ F.presheaf.map h.op := by
209	  ext œà
210	  show freeYonedaHomEquiv V F (freeYoneda.map h ‚â´ œà)
211	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.presheaf.map h.op)) (freeYonedaHomEquiv W F œà)
212	  show yonedaEquiv (PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv (freeYoneda.map h ‚â´ œà))
213	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.presheaf.map h.op))
214	          (yonedaEquiv (PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv œà))
215	  rw [show PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv (freeYoneda.map h ‚â´ œà)
216	        = yoneda.map h ‚â´ PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv œà from
217	      (PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction _).homEquiv_naturality_left (yoneda.map h) œà,
218	    ‚Üê yonedaEquiv_naturality]

221	/-- **The cosimplicial natural isomorphism `Hom(K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢, F) ‚âÖ ƒå‚Ä¢(ùí∞, F)`.**
222	
223	Assembles the per-degree isomorphisms `homCechSectionIsoApp` into a natural isomorphism of
224	cosimplicial abelian groups. The naturality square reduces, via `homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_œÄ`
225	in both degrees, to the single naturality square `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality` of the
226	free‚ÄìYoneda section identification.

228	Project-local: the cosimplicial comparison of the hom-complex with the section complex has no
229	Mathlib counterpart. -/
230	noncomputable def homCechSectionCosimplicialIso (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) [Finite ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ]
231	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
232	    homCechCosimplicial ùí∞ F ‚âÖ sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen ùí∞) F :=
233	  NatIso.ofComponents (homCechSectionIsoApp ùí∞ F) (by
234	    intro n m f
235	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
236	    intro œÉ
237	    dsimp only [sectionCechCosimplicial]
238	    rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, Pi.lift_œÄ]
239	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_œÄ ùí∞ F m œÉ]
240	    conv_rhs => rw [‚Üê Category.assoc]
241	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_œÄ ùí∞ F n (œÉ ‚àò ‚áë(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f))]
242	    erw [Category.assoc]
243	    erw [‚Üê freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality
244	        (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le ùí∞ ‚áë(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f) œÉ)) F]
245	    dsimp only [homCechCosimplicial, Functor.comp_map, Functor.rightOp_map]
246	    erw [‚Üê Category.assoc, ‚Üê Functor.map_comp, ‚Üê Category.assoc, ‚Üê Functor.map_comp]
247	    congr 1
248	    congr 1
249	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
250	    simp only [unop_comp]
251	    dsimp only [cechFreeSimplicial]
252	    erw [Limits.Sigma.Œπ_desc]
253	    rfl
254	    )

256	/-- **The ƒåech hom-identification** (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`).
257	
258	The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom_{PMod}(K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢, F) ‚âÖ ƒå‚Ä¢(ùí∞, F)` identifying the
259	hom-complex of the free ƒåech resolution with the section ƒåech complex of `F`. Obtained by
260	applying the alternating-coface-map complex functor to the cosimplicial natural isomorphism
261	`homCechSectionCosimplicialIso`, so the differential intertwining is automatic from the
262	cosimplicial naturality.

265	no Mathlib counterpart. -/
266	noncomputable def cechComplex_hom_identification (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) [Finite ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ]
267	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
268	    homCechComplex ùí∞ F ‚âÖ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen ùí∞) F :=
269	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).mapIso (homCechSectionCosimplicialIso ùí∞ F)

271	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî hom-complex as contravariant transport
272	
273	The injective-acyclicity assembly (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, gated on Lane-1's
274	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`) needs `homCechComplex ùí∞ F` ‚Äî the alternating *coface* complex of
275	the hom-cosimplicial object `homCechCosimplicial ùí∞ F` ‚Äî to be identified with the
276	contravariant transport `Hom(-, F) = preadditiveYoneda.obj F` of the *opposite* of the free
277	ƒåech chain complex `cechFreePresheafComplex ùí∞`.  Once this identification is in hand,
278	`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` (applied to `(cechFreeComplexAug ùí∞).op`) and
279	`cechComplex_hom_identification` combine in a single step to turn the free resolution into
280	ƒåech acyclicity of injective sheaves.
281	
282	Project-local: the comparison of the alternating-coface hom-complex with the mapped opposite
283	of the alternating-face free complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
284	
285	/-- The `i`-th coface of `homCechCosimplicial ùí∞ F` is `Hom(-, F)` applied to the opposite of
286	the `i`-th face of `cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞`.  Holds definitionally (both unfold to
287	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞).map (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).op).op`);
288	isolated as a `rfl` lemma to drive the differential identification `homCechComplex_d_eq`. -/
289	private lemma homCechCosimplicial_Œ¥ (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) [Finite ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ]
290	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {n : ‚Ñï} (i : Fin (n + 2)) :
291	    (homCechCosimplicial ùí∞ F).Œ¥ i
292	      = (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞).Œ¥ i).op :=
293	  rfl
294	
295	/-- **Degreewise differential identification** of the hom-complex with the mapped opposite of
296	the free ƒåech complex.  Both differentials are alternating sums of (co)faces; pushing the
297	opposite and the additive functor `Hom(-, F)` through the alternating sum on the right and
298	using `homCechCosimplicial_Œ¥` matches them term by term.  This is the naturality input for the
299	cochain-complex isomorphism `homCechComplexMapOpIso`. -/
300	private lemma homCechComplex_d_eq (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) [Finite ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ]
301	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ‚Ñï) :
302	    (homCechComplex ùí∞ F).d p (p + 1)
303	      = (((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï)).obj
304	          (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex ùí∞))).d p (p + 1) := by
305	  have hL : (homCechComplex ùí∞ F).d p (p + 1)
306	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (homCechCosimplicial ùí∞ F) p :=
307	    CochainComplex.of_d (fun n => (homCechCosimplicial ùí∞ F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk n)) (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (homCechCosimplicial ùí∞ F)) p
308	  have hR : (cechFreePresheafComplex ùí∞).d (p + 1) p
309	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞) p :=
310	    ChainComplex.of_d (fun n => (cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk n))) (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞)) p
311	  rw [hL, AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD,
312	    Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d, HomologicalComplex.op_d, hR,
313	    AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD]
314	  have hop := CategoryTheory.op_sum
315	    ((cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk (p + 1))))
316	    ((cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
317	    (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (p + 2)))
318	    (fun i => (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (i : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢ (cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞).Œ¥ i)

320	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)

322	  erw [Functor.map_zsmul]

324	
325	/-- **Hom-complex as contravariant transport of the free ƒåech complex.**
326	
327	The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom(K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢, F) ‚âÖ Hom(-, F)((K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢)·µí·µñ)` identifying the
328	alternating-coface hom-complex `homCechComplex ùí∞ F` with the contravariant transport
329	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex` of the opposite of the free ƒåech chain

334	This is the bridge that turns Lane-1's free resolution into ƒåech acyclicity: applying
335	`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` to `(cechFreeComplexAug ùí∞).op` produces a

338	
339	Project-local: the identification of the alternating-coface hom-complex with the mapped
340	opposite of the alternating-face free complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
341	noncomputable def homCechComplexMapOpIso (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) [Finite ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ]
342	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
343	    homCechComplex ùí∞ F ‚âÖ
344	      ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï)).obj
345	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex ùí∞)) :=
346	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun _ => Iso.refl _) (by
347	    rintro i j (rfl : i + 1 = j)

349	      ((homCechComplex_d_eq ùí∞ F i).symm.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm))

351	/-- **Section ƒåech complex as contravariant transport of the free ƒåech complex.**
352

354	`cechComplex_hom_identification` gives a cochain-complex isomorphism between the mapped

356	the single isomorphism needed for the injective-acyclicity assembly: once Lane 1 provides

362	Project-local: assembled from two project-local isomorphisms. -/
363	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexMapOpIso (ùí∞ : X.OpenCover) [Finite ùí∞.I‚ÇÄ]
364	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
365	    ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï)).obj
366	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex ùí∞))

368	  (homCechComplexMapOpIso ùí∞ F).symm ‚â™‚â´ cechComplex_hom_identification ùí∞ F
