370	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî `Hom(-, I)` is exact for injective `I`
371	
372	The presheaf-level ƒåech-acyclicity argument (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`) needs the single
373	categorical fact that, for an injective object `I` of an abelian category, the contravariant
374	hom functor `Hom(-, I)` is exact ‚Äî equivalently, the covariant functor
375	`preadditiveYoneda.obj I : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ Ab` preserves homology. Mathlib supplies the two pieces:
376	`preadditiveYoneda.obj I` always preserves finite limits (it is left exact), and injectivity
377	is equivalent to it preserving epimorphisms; we assemble these into preservation of finite
378	colimits (hence of homology), and feed the result through
379	`HomologicalComplex.quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology` to obtain quasi-isomorphism
380	preservation. These are stated for a general abelian category so they apply verbatim to
381	`X.PresheafOfModules` once the injectivity of `I` as a presheaf of modules is in hand.
382	
383	Project-local: Mathlib has the ingredients but not the packaged exactness of `Hom(-, I)` as a
384	homology-preserving functor. -/
385	
386	section InjectiveHomExact
387	
388	open CategoryTheory Limits
389	
390	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
391	
392	/-- **`Hom(-, I)` preserves finite colimits for injective `I`.** For an injective object `I`
393	of an abelian category, the covariant functor `preadditiveYoneda.obj I : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ Ab`
394	preserves finite colimits: it is left exact (preserves finite limits) automatically, and
395	injectivity makes it preserve epimorphisms, which together with left exactness gives
396	exactness. Combined with the automatic `PreservesFiniteLimits` instance this yields
397	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesHomology` by `Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact`.
398	
399	Project-local: the exactness of `Hom(-, I)` for injective `I` is the categorical engine of
400	`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`. -/
401	instance preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective
402	    (I : C) [Injective I] : PreservesFiniteColimits (preadditiveYoneda.obj I) := by
403	  have hepi : (preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesEpimorphisms :=
404	    (Injective.injective_iff_preservesEpimorphisms_preadditiveYoneda_obj I).mp inferInstance
405	  rw [Functor.preservesFiniteColimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_epi]
406	  intro S hS
407	  have hg := hS.epi_g
408	  exact ‚ü®hS.exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel _ hS.mono_f inferInstance,
409	    hepi.preserves S.g‚ü©
410	
411	/-- **`Hom(-, I)` carries quasi-isomorphisms to quasi-isomorphisms for injective `I`.**
412	Since `preadditiveYoneda.obj I` preserves homology
413	(`preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective`), applying it degreewise to a
414	quasi-isomorphism `œÜ` of chain complexes in `C·µí·µñ` produces a quasi-isomorphism of the
415	`Hom`-cochain complexes. This is the form consumed by `injective_cech_acyclic`: the
416	augmentation `cechFreeComplexAug ùí∞` (a quasi-isomorphism once Lane 1 lands), taken opposite
417	and mapped through `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`, stays a quasi-isomorphism.
418	
419	Project-local: packages `quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology` against the injective-hom
420	exactness instance above. -/
421	lemma quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective (I : C) [Injective I]
422	    {Œπ : Type*} {c : ComplexShape Œπ} {K L : HomologicalComplex C·µí·µñ c}
423	    (œÜ : K ‚ü∂ L) [QuasiIso œÜ] :
424	    QuasiIso (((preadditiveYoneda.obj I).mapHomologicalComplex c).map œÜ) := by
425	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
426	  intro i
427	  exact HomologicalComplex.quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology œÜ (preadditiveYoneda.obj I) i
428	    (hœÜ := QuasiIso.quasiIsoAt (f := œÜ) i)
429	
430	end InjectiveHomExact
431	
432	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî ƒåech `H¬π` vanishing ‚üπ cocycles are coboundaries
433	
434	The surjectivity-on-sections step `ses_cech_h1` (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`, Stacks
435	`lemma-ses-cech-h1`) consumes the {\v C}ech-algebra fact that a covering with
436	`»û¬π(ùí∞, F) = 0` has *every* {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle equal to a coboundary. This is the
437	`\uses{def:cech_complex}` content of that lemma, isolated here as a self-contained
438	statement about the section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (no sheaf gluing or
439	local-surjectivity input). It is the converse direction of
440	`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact` (CechAcyclic), packaged in section
441	coordinates via `sectionCechProductEquiv` / `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
442	
443	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules, so
444	neither the homology-to-exactness reduction nor its coordinate form exist there. -/
445	
446	section CechH1Coboundary
447	
448	open AlgebraicTopology
449	
450	/-- **Homology vanishing ‚üπ coface-differential exactness** (converse of
451	`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact`). If the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the
452	section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, the underlying group homomorphisms of the two
453	consecutive coface differentials `objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence. Pure
454	homological algebra: the same `exactAt_iff_isZero_homology` / `exactAt_iff'` /
455	`ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact` chain, run in the extraction direction.
456	
457	Project-local: extraction form of the `Ab`-side homology bridge of
458	`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`, needed by the `ses_cech_h1` coboundary step. -/
459	theorem sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology {Œπ : Type u}
460	    (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ‚Ñï)
461	    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1))) :
462	    Function.Exact
463	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
464	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
465	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1))) := by
466	  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
467	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
468	    CochainComplex.of_d (fun n => (sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk n)) (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)) q
469	  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
470	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
471	    CochainComplex.of_d (fun n => (sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk n)) (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)) (q + 1)
472	  have key : Function.Exact
473	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
474	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2))) := by
475	    have hh := h
476	    rw [‚Üê HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
477	        (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
478	        ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hh
479	    exact hh
480	  rw [hf, hg] at key
481	  exact key
482	
483	/-- **ƒåech `1`-cocycles are coboundaries when `»û¬π(ùí∞, F) = 0`** ‚Äî the
484	`\uses{def:cech_complex}` heart of `ses_cech_h1`.
485	
486	Working in the section coordinates of `sectionCechCosimplicial` (a family of sections
487	`c œÉ ‚àà F(‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k))` indexed by double-indices `œÉ : Fin 2 ‚Üí Œπ`), if the family
488	satisfies the {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle identity (the alternating sum of its restrictions
489	over each triple-index `œÉ : Fin 3 ‚Üí Œπ` vanishes) and the degree-`1` homology of the
490	section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, then `c` is a coboundary: there is a `0`-cochain
491	`t` (sections `t œÉ ‚àà F(U (œÉ 0))` over single-indices) with `c œÉ` the alternating sum
492	of the face restrictions of `t`, i.e. `c œÉ = t_{œÉ 1}|_{œÉ} - t_{œÉ 0}|_{œÉ}` once the
493	two-term sum is expanded. The restriction maps are `sectionCechFaceRestr`, exactly the
494	{\v C}ech coface restrictions appearing in `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
495	
496	Project-local: the section-coordinate coboundary extraction for the section {\v C}ech
497	complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
498	theorem sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology {Œπ : Type u}
499	    (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
500	    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
501	    (c : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin 2 ‚Üí Œπ, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
502	    (hcoc : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin 3 ‚Üí Œπ,
503	      ‚àë i : Fin 3, (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (i : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢
504	        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F œÉ i)
505	          (c (œÉ ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom)) = 0) :
506	    ‚àÉ t : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin 1 ‚Üí Œπ, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))),
507	      ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin 2 ‚Üí Œπ, c œÉ = ‚àë i : Fin 2, (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (i : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢
508	        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F œÉ i)
509	          (t (œÉ ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom)) := by
510	  have he := sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology U F 0 h
511	  set c' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 1)) :=
512	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).symm c with hc'
513	  have hpe1 : sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1 c' = c :=
514	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).apply_symm_apply c
515	  have hzero : ConcreteCategory.hom
516	      (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) 1) c' = 0 := by
517	    apply (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2).injective
518	    funext œÉ
519	    rw [sectionCech_objD_apply U F 1 c' œÉ, hpe1]
520	    rw [show sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2 0 œÉ = 0 from by
521	      rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]; exact map_zero _]
522	    exact hcoc œÉ
523	  obtain ‚ü®b, hb‚ü© := (he c').mp hzero
524	  refine ‚ü®sectionCechProductEquiv U F 0 b, fun œÉ => ?_‚ü©
525	  rw [‚Üê hpe1, ‚Üê hb, sectionCech_objD_apply U F 0 b œÉ]
526	
527	end CechH1Coboundary
528	
529	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¬π`
530	vanishing (`ses_cech_h1`)
531	
532	This section assembles the {\v C}ech-`H¬π` vanishing into the surjectivity statement
533	`ses_cech_h1` (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`, Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).  The
534	{\v C}ech-algebra heart is `sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology` (the
535	cocycle-to-coboundary extraction proven above); the remaining content is the standard
536	sheaf theory ‚Äî local lifting, left-exactness on sections, and gluing ‚Äî which here is
537	supplied through Mathlib's `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` and a handful of
538	restriction-bookkeeping helpers.
539	
540	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules, so
541	the surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is new here. -/
542	
543	section SesCechH1
544	
545	open AlgebraicTopology TopologicalSpace Opposite
546	
547	/-- **Composite of two presheaf restrictions is the direct restriction.**
548	For `A ‚â§ B ‚â§ C` the restriction `P(C) ‚Üí P(B)` followed by `P(B) ‚Üí P(A)` equals the direct
549	restriction `P(C) ‚Üí P(A)`.  Holds definitionally for `Ab`-valued presheaves on opens
550	because the composite of the opposite `homOfLE`s is the opposite `homOfLE` of the
551	composite.  Used pervasively to collapse restriction chains in `ses_cech_h1`. -/
552	private lemma restr_trans (P : (Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ ‚•§ Ab.{u}) {A B C : Opens ‚Ü•X}
553	    (h1 : A ‚â§ B) (h2 : B ‚â§ C) (x : ToType (P.obj (op C))) :
554	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h1).op)
555	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h2).op) x)
556	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE (h1.trans h2)).op) x := by
557	  rw [‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ‚Üê P.map_comp, ‚Üê op_comp]
558	  rfl
559	
560	/-- **Restriction between two equal opens is injective.**  When `V ‚â§ W` and `W ‚â§ V`
561	(i.e. `V = W`) the restriction `P(W) ‚Üí P(V)` is injective: the reverse restriction is a
562	two-sided inverse (`restr_trans` collapses the round trip to the identity, by uniqueness of
563	poset morphisms).  Used to transport the {\v C}ech compatibility equation between the binary
564	intersection `U i ‚äì U j` and the {\v C}ech double-overlap `‚®Ö‚Çñ U (![i,j] k)`. -/
565	private lemma restr_inj_of_eq (P : (Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ ‚•§ Ab.{u}) {V W : Opens ‚Ü•X}
566	    (h : V ‚â§ W) (h' : W ‚â§ V) :
567	    Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h).op)) := by
568	  intro a b hab
569	  have h2 := congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h').op)) hab
570	  rw [restr_trans, restr_trans] at h2
571	  rwa [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (h'.trans h)) (ùüô _), op_id, P.map_id,
572	    ConcreteCategory.id_apply, ConcreteCategory.id_apply] at h2
573	
574	/-- **Two parallel restrictions agree.**  Any two restriction maps `P(A) ‚Üí P(C)` (opposite
575	opens morphisms) act identically, by uniqueness of poset morphisms.  Used to discharge the
576	final defeq-up-to-proof-irrelevance step after collapsing restriction chains with
577	`‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ‚Üê Functor.map_comp`. -/
578	private lemma restr_op_unique (P : (Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ ‚•§ Ab.{u}) {A C : Opens ‚Ü•X}
579	    (f g : Opposite.op C ‚ü∂ Opposite.op A) (x : ToType (P.obj (Opposite.op C))) :
580	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map f) x = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map g) x := by
581	  rw [show f = g from Quiver.Hom.unop_inj (Subsingleton.elim _ _)]
582	
583	/-- **Restriction of a single-index {\v C}ech section is invariant under reindexing the
584	tuple.**  For two equal `Fin 1`-tuples `œÅ = œÅ'` (e.g. `fun _ => i` and `![i,j] ‚àò Œ¥`), the
585	restrictions of `g' œÅ` and `g' œÅ'` to a common open `W` agree.  Proved by `subst` (so the
586	propositional-but-not-definitional tuple equality is discharged without a heterogeneous
587	rewrite), then `restr_op_unique`.  This is the bridge between the `U`-indexed glue family
588	(`fun _ => i`) and the {\v C}ech double-overlap agreement (`![i,j] ‚àò Œ¥`). -/
589	private lemma restr_g'_transport {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí Opens ‚Ü•X) (G : X.PresheafOfModules)
590	    (g' : ‚àÄ œÅ : Fin 1 ‚Üí Œπ, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÅ k)))))
591	    {W : Opens ‚Ü•X} {œÅ œÅ' : Fin 1 ‚Üí Œπ} (hœÅ : œÅ = œÅ')
592	    (f : Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÅ k)) ‚ü∂ Opposite.op W)
593	    (f' : Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÅ' k)) ‚ü∂ Opposite.op W) :
594	    ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map f) (g' œÅ)
595	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map f') (g' œÅ') := by
596	  subst hœÅ
597	  exact restr_op_unique G.presheaf f f' (g' œÅ)
598	
599	/-- **A morphism of presheaves of modules commutes with the {\v C}ech face restrictions.**
600	For `fŒπ : F.presheaf ‚ü∂ G.presheaf` the section-level map `fŒπ.app` intertwines the
601	{\v C}ech face restriction `sectionCechFaceRestr` of `F` with that of `G`; this is just
602	the naturality of `fŒπ`.  It is the single naturality input that pushes the cocycle and
603	coboundary identities of the heart between `F` and `G`. -/
604	private lemma fŒπ_sectionCechFaceRestr {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí Opens ‚Ü•X)
605	    (F G : X.PresheafOfModules) (fŒπ : F.presheaf ‚ü∂ G.presheaf)
606	    {q : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin (q + 2) ‚Üí Œπ) (i : Fin (q + 2))
607	    (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj
608	      (op (‚®Ö l, U ((œÉ ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom) l))))) :
609	    ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G œÉ i)
610	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (fŒπ.app _) x)
611	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (fŒπ.app _)
612	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F œÉ i) x) := by
613	  unfold sectionCechFaceRestr
614	  rw [‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, fŒπ.naturality]
615	
616	/-- The single-index {\v C}ech intersection at a constant tuple is the cover member. -/
617	private lemma coverConst_iInf {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí Opens ‚Ü•X) (i : Œπ) :
618	    (‚®Ö k : Fin 1, U ((fun _ => i) k)) = U i := by simp
619	
620	/-- The double-index {\v C}ech intersection at `![i, j]` is the binary intersection. -/
621	private lemma coverPair_iInf {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí Opens ‚Ü•X) (i j : Œπ) :
622	    (‚®Ö k : Fin 2, U (![i, j] k)) = U i ‚äì U j := by
623	  apply le_antisymm
624	  ¬∑ exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (iInf_le _ 1)
625	  ¬∑ exact le_iInf (fun k => by fin_cases k <;> simp)
626	
627	/-- The `0`-th face of `![i, j]` is the constant tuple `j`. -/
628	private lemma pair_comp_Œ¥0 {Œπ : Type u} (i j : Œπ) :
629	    (![i, j] ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ (0 : Fin 2)).toOrderHom) = (fun _ => j) := by
630	  funext k; fin_cases k; simp [SimplexCategory.Œ¥, Fin.succAbove]
631	
632	/-- The `1`-st face of `![i, j]` is the constant tuple `i`. -/
633	private lemma pair_comp_Œ¥1 {Œπ : Type u} (i j : Œπ) :
634	    (![i, j] ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ (1 : Fin 2)).toOrderHom) = (fun _ => i) := by
635	  funext k; fin_cases k; simp [SimplexCategory.Œ¥, Fin.succAbove]
636	
637	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
638	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
639	/-- **Surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¬π` vanishing** (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`,
640	Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).
641	
642	For a short exact sequence of presheaves of modules `0 ‚Üí F ‚Üí G ‚Üí H ‚Üí 0` on a scheme `X`
643	‚Äî presented through the underlying `Ab`-presheaf maps `fŒπ`, `gœÄ`, with `fŒπ` a monomorphism
644	on sections (`hmono`), `gœÄ ‚àò fŒπ = 0` (`hœÄŒπ`), and every kernel section of `gœÄ` in the image
645	of `fŒπ` (`hker`) ‚Äî together with an open cover `U` of the open `‚®Ü·µ¢ U·µ¢` such that
646	`»û¬π(U, F) = 0` (`hH1`) and a family of local lifts `sLoc i ‚àà G(U·µ¢)` of `s ‚àà H(‚®Ü·µ¢ U·µ¢)`
647	(`hlift`), the section `s` lies in the image of `G(‚®Ü·µ¢ U·µ¢) ‚Üí H(‚®Ü·µ¢ U·µ¢)`.
648	
649	The differences `s‚±º|_{ij} ‚àí s·µ¢|_{ij}` of the local lifts map to `0` in `H`, hence lift to a
650	{\v C}ech `1`-cocycle in `F` (`hker`); the {\v C}ech-`H¬π` vanishing makes it a coboundary
651	(`sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology`); subtracting the coboundary makes the
652	corrected lifts agree on overlaps, and the sheaf condition on `G` glues them to a global
653	section of `G` over `‚®Ü·µ¢ U·µ¢` mapping to `s` (separatedness of `H` identifies its image with
654	`s`).  The "cofinal system of covers" of the Stacks statement is captured here by taking a
655	single cover satisfying both `»û¬π = 0` and the local-lift property as hypotheses.
656	
657	Project-local: the {\v C}ech surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is
658	new here (Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules). -/
659	theorem ses_cech_h1 {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí Opens ‚Ü•X)
660	    (F G H : X.PresheafOfModules)
661	    (fŒπ : F.presheaf ‚ü∂ G.presheaf) (gœÄ : G.presheaf ‚ü∂ H.presheaf)
662	    (hGsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf)
663	    (hHsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf)
664	    (hœÄŒπ : ‚àÄ (V : (Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ) (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj V)),
665	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fŒπ.app V) x) = 0)
666	    (hmono : ‚àÄ (V : (Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ), Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fŒπ.app V)))
667	    (hker : ‚àÄ (V : (Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ) (x : ToType (G.presheaf.obj V)),
668	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app V) x = 0 ‚Üí ‚àÉ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fŒπ.app V) y = x)
669	    (s : ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))))
670	    (hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
671	    (sLoc : ‚àÄ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))))
672	    (hlift : ‚àÄ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app (op (U i))) (sLoc i)
673	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s) :
674	    ‚àÉ g : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))),
675	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app (op (iSup U))) g = s := by
676	  classical
677	  -- single-index local sections, recast in ‚®Ö-coordinates
678	  set sLoc'coord : (œÉ : Fin 1 ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) :=
679	    fun œÉ => ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (iInf_le _ 0)).op) (sLoc (œÉ 0))
680	    with hsLoc'coord
681	  set sLoc' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U G).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) :=
682	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).symm sLoc'coord with hsLoc'
683	  have hpe0 : sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0 sLoc' = sLoc'coord :=
684	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).apply_symm_apply sLoc'coord
685	  -- the degree-1 cochain of G-differences
686	  set cyc1 := ConcreteCategory.hom
687	    (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 0) sLoc' with hcyc1
688	  set dGcoord : (œÉ : Fin 2 ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) :=
689	    fun œÉ => sectionCechProductEquiv U G 1 cyc1 œÉ with hdGcoord
690	  have hdG : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin 2 ‚Üí Œπ, dGcoord œÉ
691	      = ‚àë i : Fin 2, (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (i : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢
692	          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G œÉ i)
693	            (sLoc'coord (œÉ ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom)) := by
694	    intro œÉ
695	    have happ := sectionCech_objD_apply U G 0 sLoc' œÉ
696	    rw [hpe0] at happ
697	    exact happ
698	  -- `gœÄ` carries a single ‚®Ö-coordinate local section to the restriction of `s`
699	  have hgœÄsLoc' : ‚àÄ (œÅ : Fin 1 ‚Üí Œπ) (h : (‚®Ö k, U (œÅ k)) ‚â§ iSup U),
700	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app (op (‚®Ö k, U (œÅ k)))) (sLoc'coord œÅ)
701	        = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op) s := by
702	    intro œÅ h
703	    rw [hsLoc'coord, ‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, gœÄ.naturality,
704	      ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, hlift (œÅ 0)]
705	    exact restr_trans H.presheaf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U (œÅ 0)) s
706	  -- `gœÄ` kills the differences
707	  have hgœÄdG : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin 2 ‚Üí Œπ,
708	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app (op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) (dGcoord œÉ) = 0 := by
709	    intro œÉ
710	    rw [hdG, map_sum]
711	    have key : ‚àÄ i : Fin 2,
712	        ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app (op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
713	          ((-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (i : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢ ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G œÉ i)
714	            (sLoc'coord (œÉ ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom)))
715	        = (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (i : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢ ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map
716	            (homOfLE (le_trans (iInf_le (fun k => U (œÉ k)) (0 : Fin 2)) (le_iSup U (œÉ 0)))).op) s := by
717	      intro i
718	      rw [map_zsmul]; congr 1
719	      rw [‚Üê fŒπ_sectionCechFaceRestr U G H gœÄ œÉ i,
720	        hgœÄsLoc' (œÉ ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom) (le_trans (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U _))]

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FreePresheafComplex
9	
10	/-!
11	# Form-B absolute cohomology `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p(jShriekOU U, F)` (P5b input)
12	-/
13	
14	universe u
15	
16	open CategoryTheory Limits CategoryTheory.Abelian
17	
18	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
19	
20	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
21	
22	/-- The standard `HasExt` structure on `X.Modules`, made a section-local instance so the
23	`Ext`-based absolute cohomology below resolves it without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom`
24	typeclass search. -/
25	noncomputable local instance hasExtModules : HasExt.{u + 1, u, u + 1} X.Modules :=
26	  HasExt.standard _
27	
28	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî Form-B absolute cohomology -/
29	
30	noncomputable def jShriekOU (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.Modules :=
31	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (ùüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
32	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U))
33	
34	/-- The sheafification adjunction hom-bijection as an `AddEquiv` (additivity holds because
35	the right adjoint is an additive functor and composition is bilinear). -/
36	noncomputable def sheafificationHomAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
37	    (jShriekOU U ‚ü∂ F) ‚âÉ+
38	      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U) ‚ü∂
39	        (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) :=
40	  { (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ùüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv
41	      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U)) F with
42	    map_add' := by
43	      intro f g
44	      haveI : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ‚ãô
45	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (ùüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive := inferInstance
46	      simp only [Equiv.toFun_as_coe, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
47	      erw [Functor.map_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
48	      rfl }
49	
50	noncomputable def jShriekOU_homEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
51	    (jShriekOU U ‚ü∂ F) ‚âÉ+
52	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
53	  (sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F).trans (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv U _)
54	
55	/-- **Form-B absolute cohomology** `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{O_X}(jShriekOU U, F)`. -/
56	noncomputable def absoluteCohomology (p : ‚Ñï) (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
57	    (F : X.Modules) : AddCommGrpCat :=
58	  AddCommGrpCat.of (Ext (jShriekOU U) F p)
59	
60	/-- **`H‚Å∞(U, F) ‚âÖ Œì(U, F)`** as abelian groups: degree-zero `Ext` out of the
61	corepresenting object `jShriekOU U` recovers the sections of `F` over `U`. This is the
62	additive corepresentability of global sections specialised to `p = 0`. -/
63	noncomputable def absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
64	    (F : X.Modules) :
65	    Ext (jShriekOU U) F 0 ‚âÉ+
66	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
67	  (AddEquiv.mk' Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ (by
68	    intro a b
69	    refine Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ.symm.injective ?_
70	    simp only [Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ_symm_apply, Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_add, Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_homEquiv‚ÇÄ_apply])).trans
71	    (jShriekOU_homEquiv U F)
72	
73	/-- **Injective vanishing**: positive-degree absolute cohomology of an injective sheaf of
74	modules vanishes. Direct from `Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (the injective object is the
75	*second* `Ext` argument, so no restriction of the coefficient sheaf is needed). -/
76	theorem absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective (n : ‚Ñï) (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
77	    (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) I (n + 1)) : e = 0 :=
78	  Ext.eq_zero_of_injective e
79	
80	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, surjectivity at `H^{n‚ÇÅ}(U, F‚ÇÅ)`.**
81	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÅ` at fixed first argument
82	`jShriekOU U`, for a short exact sequence of `O_X`-modules. -/
83	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact‚ÇÅ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
84	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñï}
85	    (x‚ÇÅ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X‚ÇÅ n‚ÇÅ) (hx‚ÇÅ : x‚ÇÅ.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero n‚ÇÅ) = 0)
86	    {n‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï} (hn‚ÇÄ : n‚ÇÄ + 1 = n‚ÇÅ) :
87	    ‚àÉ x‚ÇÉ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X‚ÇÉ n‚ÇÄ, x‚ÇÉ.comp hS.extClass hn‚ÇÄ = x‚ÇÅ :=
88	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÅ (jShriekOU U) hS x‚ÇÅ hx‚ÇÅ hn‚ÇÄ
89	
90	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, exactness at `H^n(U, F‚ÇÇ)`.**
91	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÇ` at fixed first argument
92	`jShriekOU U`. -/
93	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact‚ÇÇ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
94	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n : ‚Ñï}
95	    (x‚ÇÇ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X‚ÇÇ n) (hx‚ÇÇ : x‚ÇÇ.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.g) (add_zero n) = 0) :
96	    ‚àÉ x‚ÇÅ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X‚ÇÅ n, x‚ÇÅ.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero n) = x‚ÇÇ :=
97	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÇ (jShriekOU U) hS x‚ÇÇ hx‚ÇÇ
98	
99	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, exactness at the connecting map.**
100	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÉ` at fixed first argument
101	`jShriekOU U`. -/
102	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact‚ÇÉ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
103	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï}
104	    (x‚ÇÉ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X‚ÇÉ n‚ÇÄ) {n‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñï} (hn‚ÇÅ : n‚ÇÄ + 1 = n‚ÇÅ)
105	    (hx‚ÇÉ : x‚ÇÉ.comp hS.extClass hn‚ÇÅ = 0) :
106	    ‚àÉ x‚ÇÇ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X‚ÇÇ n‚ÇÄ, x‚ÇÇ.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.g) (add_zero n‚ÇÄ) = x‚ÇÉ :=
107	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact‚ÇÉ (jShriekOU U) hS x‚ÇÉ hn‚ÇÅ hx‚ÇÉ
108	
109	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî naturality of `H‚Å∞ ‚âÖ Œì` -/
110	
111	/-- **Naturality of `Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ` in the second argument.** The degree-zero `Ext`
112	comparison intertwines composition with the degree-zero Ext class `Ext.mk‚ÇÄ g` and ordinary
113	post-composition with `g`. Project-local: Mathlib has the comparison `Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ` and the
114	composite formula `Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_comp_mk‚ÇÄ`, but not this intertwining as a packaged lemma. -/
115	private lemma homEquiv‚ÇÄ_comp_mk‚ÇÄ {A B Z : X.Modules} (e : Ext A B 0) (g : B ‚ü∂ Z) :
116	    Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ (e.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ g) (add_zero 0)) = Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ e ‚â´ g := by
117	  conv_lhs => rw [‚Üê Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_homEquiv‚ÇÄ_apply e, Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_comp_mk‚ÇÄ]
118	  rw [‚Üê Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ_symm_apply, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
119	
120	/-- **Naturality of `freeYonedaHomEquiv` in the coefficient presheaf.** Post-composition by a
121	morphism of presheaves of modules `q` corresponds, under the free‚ÄìYoneda evaluation bijection,
122	to applying `q` on sections over `V`. Project-local: the underlying bijection is project-local
123	(`freeYonedaHomEquiv`), so is its naturality. -/
124	private lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_naturality (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
125	    {F‚ÇÅ F‚ÇÇ : X.PresheafOfModules}
126	    (œà : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ‚ü∂ F‚ÇÅ) (q : F‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ F‚ÇÇ) :
127	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F‚ÇÇ (œà ‚â´ q)
128	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (q.app (Opposite.op V))) (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F‚ÇÅ œà) := by
129	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app,
130	    ModuleCat.comp_apply]
131	
132	/-- **Naturality of `sheafificationHomAddEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf.** This is the
133	naturality of the sheafification adjunction's hom-bijection in its right (codomain) argument;
134	the right adjoint is `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X` (definitionally the forget‚Äìrestrict
135	composite). Project-local because `sheafificationHomAddEquiv` is the project's additive
136	packaging of that bijection. -/
137	private lemma sheafificationHomAddEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
138	    {F‚ÇÅ F‚ÇÇ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU U ‚ü∂ F‚ÇÅ) (g : F‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ F‚ÇÇ) :
139	    sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F‚ÇÇ (h ‚â´ g)
140	      = sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F‚ÇÅ h ‚â´ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g :=
141	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ùüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv_naturality_right h g
142	
143	/-- **Naturality of `jShriekOU_homEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf.** Composing a map
144	`jShriekOU U ‚ü∂ F‚ÇÅ` with `g : F‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ F‚ÇÇ` corresponds, under corepresentability, to applying `g` on
145	sections over `U`. Project-local: assembled from the naturality of the two project-local halves
146	(`sheafificationHomAddEquiv` and `freeYonedaHomEquiv`). -/
147	private lemma jShriekOU_homEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
148	    {F‚ÇÅ F‚ÇÇ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU U ‚ü∂ F‚ÇÅ) (g : F‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ F‚ÇÇ) :
149	    jShriekOU_homEquiv U F‚ÇÇ (h ‚â´ g)
150	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (Opposite.op U)))
151	          (jShriekOU_homEquiv U F‚ÇÅ h) := by
152	  change freeYonedaHomEquiv U _ (sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F‚ÇÇ (h ‚â´ g)) = _
153	  rw [sheafificationHomAddEquiv_naturality, freeYonedaHomEquiv_naturality]
154	  rfl
155	
156	/-- **Naturality of `H‚Å∞(U,-) ‚âÖ Œì(U,-)` in the coefficient sheaf**
157	(blueprint `lem:absolute_cohomology_zero_natural`). For `g : F‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ F‚ÇÇ` in `X.Modules`, the
158	functorial action of `g` on `Ext‚Å∞(jShriekOU U, -)` (composition with the degree-zero Ext class
159	`Ext.mk‚ÇÄ g`) corresponds under the `H‚Å∞ ‚âÖ Œì` isomorphism to the sections map `g_U` over `U`.
160	In particular, when `g_U` is surjective so is `H‚Å∞(U, g)` ‚Äî the transfer used by the
161	surjectivity step of `lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing`. -/
162	theorem absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
163	    {F‚ÇÅ F‚ÇÇ : X.Modules} (g : F‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ F‚ÇÇ) (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F‚ÇÅ 0) :
164	    absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U F‚ÇÇ (e.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ g) (add_zero 0))
165	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (Opposite.op U)))
166	          (absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U F‚ÇÅ e) := by
167	  change jShriekOU_homEquiv U F‚ÇÇ (Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ (e.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ g) (add_zero 0))) = _
168	  rw [homEquiv‚ÇÄ_comp_mk‚ÇÄ, jShriekOU_homEquiv_naturality]
169	  rfl
170	
171	end AlgebraicGeometry

1190	/-- **The range identification (node N5)**: under the kernel equivalence
1191	`overlapKerEquiv`, the coboundary subspace `im (sectionDiff)` of the overlap sections
1192	corresponds exactly to the image of `d‚Å∞` inside the cocycles. -/
1193	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.map_range_pairDiff :
1194	    (LinearMap.range (S.pairDiff F).hom).map (S.overlapKerEquiv F).toLinearMap
1195	      = LinearMap.range (cechToCyclesD01 S.coverFamily F) := by
1196	  apply le_antisymm
1197	  ¬∑ rintro n hn
1198	    obtain ‚ü®q, ‚ü®p, rfl‚ü©, rfl‚ü© := Submodule.mem_map.mp hn
1199	    refine ‚ü®(S.pairLift F).hom (-p), Subtype.ext ?_‚ü©
1200	    have h := congrArg (fun t => t.hom p) (S.pairLift_comp_cechD01 F)
1201	    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, ModuleCat.hom_neg,
1202	      LinearMap.neg_apply] at h
1203	    rw [S.cechToCyclesD01_coe F, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, S.overlapKerEquiv_coe F,
1204	      map_neg, map_neg, h, neg_neg]
1205	  ¬∑ rintro n ‚ü®m, rfl‚ü©
1206	    refine Submodule.mem_map.mpr
1207	      ‚ü®(S.pairDiff F).hom (-((S.pairProj F).hom m)), ‚ü®-((S.pairProj F).hom m), rfl‚ü©,
1208	        Subtype.ext ?_‚ü©
1209	    have hfact := congrArg (fun t => t.hom m) (S.pairProj_comp_pairLift F)
1210	    have h := congrArg (fun t => t.hom ((S.pairProj F).hom m)) (S.pairLift_comp_cechD01 F)
1211	    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, ModuleCat.hom_neg,
1212	      LinearMap.neg_apply, ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq] at hfact h
1213	    rw [hfact] at h
1214	    rw [LinearEquiv.coe_coe, S.overlapKerEquiv_coe F, S.cechToCyclesD01_coe F,
1215	      map_neg, map_neg]
1216	    exact h.symm
1217	
1218	/-- The concrete `toCycles` is definitionally the `moduleCatToCycles` of the short
1219	complex `sc' 0 1 2` of the ƒåech cochain complex. -/
1220	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.cechToCyclesD01_eq :
1221	    cechToCyclesD01 S.coverFamily F
1222	      = ((Scheme.cechCochain C F S.coverFamily).sc' 0 1 2).moduleCatToCycles :=
1223	  rfl
1224	
1225	/-- **Node N5 ‚Äî the degree-`1` ƒåech cohomology of the 2-affine cover is the concrete
1226	cokernel `»û¬π = Œì(U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ, F) ‚ß∏ im (sectionDiff)`.**  Unconditional: this is a pure
1227	homological-algebra fact about the 2-cover complex ‚Äî no acyclicity or comparison
1228	hypothesis.  The chain is: homology of the complex at `1` ‚âÖ homology of the short
1229	complex `(d‚Å∞, d¬π)` ‚âÖ `ker d¬π ‚ß∏ im (toCycles)` (the concrete `ModuleCat` homology),
1230	and the kernel equivalence `overlapKerEquiv` matches `im (sectionDiff)` with
1231	`im (toCycles)` (`map_range_sectionDiff`), so the quotients agree. -/
1232	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok :
1233	    cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] S.H1Cok F :=
1234	  ((ShortComplex.homologyMapIso
1235	      ((Scheme.cechCochain C F S.coverFamily).isoSc' 0 1 2
1236	        ((ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï).prev_eq' rfl) ((ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï).next_eq' rfl))).trans
1237	    ((Scheme.cechCochain C F S.coverFamily).sc' 0 1 2).moduleCatHomologyIso).toLinearEquiv.trans
1238	  ((Submodule.Quotient.equiv (LinearMap.range (S.sectionDiff F))
1239	      (LinearMap.range
1240	        ((Scheme.cechCochain C F S.coverFamily).sc' 0 1 2).moduleCatToCycles)
1241	      (S.overlapKerEquiv F)
1242	      ((S.map_range_pairDiff F).trans
1243	        (congrArg LinearMap.range (S.cechToCyclesD01_eq F)))).symm)
1244	
1245	/-- **The lane's consumable corollary (nodes N5 + N6)**: under the ƒåech-to-derived
1246	comparison gate on the 2-affine cover, the genus-degree cohomology `H¬π(C, F)` is the
1247	concrete two-chart cokernel `»û¬π = Œì(U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ, F) ‚ß∏ (Œì(U‚ÇÅ, F) + Œì(U‚ÇÇ, F))`. -/
1248	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.hModuleOneEquivH1Cok
1249	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
1250	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
1251	    [HasCechToHModuleIso F S.coverFamily] :
1252	    HModule k F 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] S.H1Cok F :=
1253	  (S.hModuleOne_linearEquiv_cechCohomology_coverFamily F 1).trans
1254	    (S.cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok F)
1255

9	/-!
10	# ƒåech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis (Stacks 01EO) ‚Äî L1/L2 chain
11	
12	Project-local: builds the section-ƒåech short-exact-sequence (L1) and the
13	quotient-vanishing step (L2) of the 01EO dimension-shift argument.
14	-/
15	
16	universe u
17	
18	open CategoryTheory Limits CategoryTheory.Abelian
19	
20	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
21	
22	-- Re-activate the (file-local) `HasExt` instance from `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` so that the
23	-- `Ext`-based absolute cohomology resolves here without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom` search.
24	attribute [local instance] hasExtModules
25	
26	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
27	
28	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî functoriality of the section ƒåech complex -/
29	
30	/-- The cosimplicial morphism of section ƒåech objects induced by a morphism `œÜ` of
31	presheaves of modules, acting coordinatewise by the underlying presheaf morphism on each
32	basic-open section group. Project-local: `sectionCechCosimplicial` has no functoriality in
33	Mathlib. -/
34	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicialMap {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
35	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (œÜ : F ‚ü∂ G) :
36	    sectionCechCosimplicial U F ‚ü∂ sectionCechCosimplicial U G where
37	  app n := Limits.Pi.map (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
38	    ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œÜ).app (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
39	  naturality {m n} f := by
40	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
41	    intro œÉ
42	    simp only [sectionCechCosimplicial, Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.map_œÄ, Limits.Pi.lift_œÄ,
43	      Limits.Pi.map_œÄ_assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_œÄ_assoc]
44	    congr 1
45	    exact ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œÜ).naturality _
46	
47	/-- The section ƒåech cosimplicial object as a functor in the coefficient presheaf of modules.
48	Project-local: packages `sectionCechCosimplicialMap` with functoriality so the induced
49	short-complex maps compose and respect zero. -/
50	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor {Œπ : Type u}
51	    (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.PresheafOfModules ‚•§ CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
52	  obj F := sectionCechCosimplicial U F
53	  map œÜ := sectionCechCosimplicialMap U œÜ
54	  map_id F := by
55	    apply NatTrans.ext
56	    funext n
57	    show (Limits.Pi.map fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
58	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (ùüô F)).app
59	        (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) = ùüô _
60	    rw [show (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
61	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (ùüô F)).app
62	          (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
63	      = (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
64	        ùüô ((PresheafOfModules.presheaf F).obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))) from by
65	        funext œÉ; rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]; rfl]
66	    exact Limits.Pi.map_id
67	  map_comp œÜ œà := by
68	    apply NatTrans.ext
69	    funext n
70	    show (Limits.Pi.map fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
71	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (œÜ ‚â´ œà)).app
72	        (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) = _
73	    rw [show (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
74	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (œÜ ‚â´ œà)).app
75	          (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
76	      = (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
77	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œÜ).app (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))) ‚â´
78	          ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œà).app
79	            (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) from by funext œÉ; rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]; rfl]
80	    exact (Limits.Pi.map_comp_map _ _).symm
81	
82	/-- The section ƒåech cochain complex as a functor in the coefficient presheaf of modules.
83	Project-local: functoriality of `sectionCechComplex`, used to form the short exact sequence of
84	ƒåech complexes. -/
85	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexFunctor {Œπ : Type u}
86	    (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.PresheafOfModules ‚•§ CochainComplex Ab.{u} ‚Ñï :=
87	  sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor U ‚ãô AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}
88	
89	/-- The chain map of section ƒåech complexes induced by a morphism `œÜ` of presheaves of
90	modules. Project-local: functoriality of `sectionCechComplex` in the coefficient presheaf. -/
91	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexMap {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
92	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (œÜ : F ‚ü∂ G) :
93	    sectionCechComplex U F ‚ü∂ sectionCechComplex U G :=
94	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).map (sectionCechCosimplicialMap U œÜ)
95	
96	/-- **{\v C}ech cohomology accessor** `»û^p(ùí∞, F)`: the degree-`p` homology of the section
97	ƒåech complex, packaged as a named `Ab`-object. Project-local thin wrapper so the 01EO chain
98	can refer to `»û^p` uniformly. -/
99	noncomputable def cechCohomology {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
100	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ‚Ñï) : Ab.{u} :=
101	  (sectionCechComplex U F).homology p
102	
103	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî product of short exact sequences in `Ab` -/
104	
105	private lemma pi_œÄ_map_apply {J : Type u} {f g : J ‚Üí Ab.{u}} (œÜ : ‚àÄ j, f j ‚ü∂ g j)
106	    (x : ToType (‚àè·∂ú f)) (œÉ : J) :
107	    Limits.Pi.œÄ g œÉ (Limits.Pi.map œÜ x) = œÜ œÉ (Limits.Pi.œÄ f œÉ x) := by
108	  rw [‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, Limits.Pi.map_œÄ, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
109	
110	/-- **A product of short exact sequences of abelian groups is short exact.** This is the AB4*
111	content used in the section ƒåech short-exact-sequence: each ƒåech term is a product of section
112	groups over basic opens, so degreewise short-exactness of the ƒåech complexes reduces to this.
113	Project-local: Mathlib has no off-the-shelf "product of short exact sequences" lemma. -/
114	theorem shortExact_piMap {J : Type u} (S : J ‚Üí ShortComplex Ab.{u}) (h : ‚àÄ j, (S j).ShortExact) :
115	    (ShortComplex.mk (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).f)) (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).g))
116	      (by rw [Limits.Pi.map_comp_map]
117	          have hz : (fun j => (S j).f ‚â´ (S j).g) = (fun j => (0 : (S j).X‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ (S j).X‚ÇÉ)) := by
118	            funext j; exact (S j).zero
119	          rw [hz]; ext x; simp)).ShortExact := by
120	  haveI : ‚àÄ j, Mono (S j).f := fun j => (h j).mono_f
121	  -- Epi of the product map: surjectivity, chosen componentwise
122	  have hepi : Epi (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).g)) := by

130	    exact ((h œÉ).ab_surjective_g (Limits.Pi.œÄ (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÉ) œÉ y)).choose_spec

140	      intro œÉ

147	    refine (Concrete.productEquiv (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÇ)).injective (funext fun œÉ => ?_)

149	      pi_œÄ_map_apply, Concrete.productEquiv_symm_apply_œÄ]
150	    exact hw œÉ

153	    have hz : (fun j => (S j).f ‚â´ (S j).g) = (fun j => (0 : (S j).X‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ (S j).X‚ÇÉ)) := by
154	      funext j; exact (S j).zero

334	    ‚àÄ V ‚àà B, Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom

336	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map S.g)).app (Opposite.op V)))

352	-- The dimension-shift induction embeds modules into injectives, which needs

355	-- `analogies/p5a.md`), so ‚Äî following the project convention for the P5a derived-functor lane ‚Äî
356	-- it is carried as an explicit hypothesis on `injSES`/L4 rather than synthesized.

358	/-- The injective-embedding short exact sequence `0 ‚Üí F ‚Üí I ‚Üí I/F ‚Üí 0` of an `O_X`-module `F`,

360	the 01EO dimension-shift induction; carries `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` as a hypothesis. -/

362	    ShortComplex X.Modules :=
363	  ShortComplex.mk (Injective.Œπ F) (cokernel.œÄ (Injective.Œπ F)) (cokernel.condition _)

369	/-- **Dimension-shift induction `H·µñ(U, F) = 0` for `p > 0` (Stacks 01EO L4).** For a cover system

371	absolute cohomology `H·µñ(U, F) = Ext·µñ(jShriekOU U, F)` vanishes for every basic open `U ‚àà s.B` and
372	`p > 0`. Induction on `p`, quantified over all `F` in the inductive class: the quotient
373	`Q = I/F` re-enters the class (via the section ƒåech SES of L1 from the per-face SES, plus L2
374	quotient-vanishing and the cover system's injective acyclicity), the base case is L3, and the
375	inductive step is the covariant Ext long exact sequence. Project-local: the 01EO inductive core. -/
376	theorem absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (s : BasisCovSystem X)
377	    {F : X.Modules} (hF : HasVanishingHigherCech s F)
378	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X} (hU : U ‚àà s.B) {p : ‚Ñï} (hp : 0 < p)
379	    (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F p) : e = 0 := by
380	  have key : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (F : X.Modules), HasVanishingHigherCech s F ‚Üí
381	      ‚àÄ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X), U ‚àà s.B ‚Üí
382	        ‚àÄ e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F (n + 1), e = 0 := by
383	    intro n
384	    induction n with
385	    | zero =>
386	      intro F hF U hU e
387	      have hS := injSES_shortExact F
388	      haveI : Injective (injSES F).X‚ÇÇ := inferInstance
389	      exact absoluteCohomology_one_eq_zero_of_basis U hS
390	        (s.surj_of_vanishing (injSES F) hS hF U hU) e
391	    | succ m ih =>

915	The entire ƒåech bridge chain above is re-parameterized here from an `X.OpenCover` `ùí∞`
916	(via `coverOpen ùí∞`, `coverInterOpen ùí∞`, `cechFreeSimplicial ùí∞`, ‚Ä¶) to a **raw finite
917	family** `{Œπ : Type u} [Finite Œπ] (U : Œπ ‚Üí Opens ‚Ü•X)` with **no covering hypothesis**,
918	consuming Lane-A's `‚Ä¶Fam` chain in `FreePresheafComplex.lean`

923	`cechFree‚Ä¶ ùí∞ ‚Ü¶ cechFree‚Ä¶Fam U`.  The open-indexed building blocks

930	`injective_cech_acyclicFam`; the latter discharges `BasisCovSystem.injective_acyclic`

974	  simp only [Category.assoc]
975	  erw [Pi.map_œÄ, piComparison_comp_œÄ_assoc]
976	  rw [‚Üê Category.assoc]
977	  congr 1
978	  exact ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
979	    (congrArg _ (opCoproductIsoProduct_hom_comp_œÄ œÉ))
980	
981	/-- **The cosimplicial natural isomorphism `Hom(K(U)_‚Ä¢, F) ‚âÖ ƒå‚Ä¢(U, F)`** (family form). -/
982	noncomputable def homCechSectionCosimplicialIsoFam (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
983	    homCechCosimplicialFam U F ‚âÖ sectionCechCosimplicial U F :=
984	  NatIso.ofComponents (homCechSectionIsoAppFam U F) (by
985	    intro n m f
986	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
987	    intro œÉ
988	    dsimp only [sectionCechCosimplicial]
989	    rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, Pi.lift_œÄ]
990	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_œÄFam U F m œÉ]
991	    conv_rhs => rw [‚Üê Category.assoc]
992	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_œÄFam U F n (œÉ ‚àò ‚áë(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f))]
993	    erw [Category.assoc]
994	    erw [‚Üê freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality
995	        (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_leFam U ‚áë(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f) œÉ)) F]
996	    dsimp only [homCechCosimplicialFam, Functor.comp_map, Functor.rightOp_map]
997	    erw [‚Üê Category.assoc, ‚Üê Functor.map_comp, ‚Üê Category.assoc, ‚Üê Functor.map_comp]
998	    congr 1
999	    congr 1
1000	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
1001	    simp only [unop_comp]
1002	    dsimp only [cechFreeSimplicialFam]
1003	    erw [Limits.Sigma.Œπ_desc]
1004	    rfl
1005	    )
1006

1008	noncomputable def cechComplex_hom_identificationFam (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
1009	    homCechComplexFam U F ‚âÖ sectionCechComplex U F :=
1010	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).mapIso
1011	    (homCechSectionCosimplicialIsoFam U F)
1012	
1013	/-- The `i`-th coface of `homCechCosimplicialFam U F` is `Hom(-, F)` applied to the opposite of
1014	the `i`-th face of `cechFreeSimplicialFam U` (family form of `homCechCosimplicial_Œ¥`). -/
1015	private lemma homCechCosimplicial_Œ¥Fam (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {n : ‚Ñï} (i : Fin (n + 2)) :
1016	    (homCechCosimplicialFam U F).Œ¥ i
1017	      = (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicialFam U).Œ¥ i).op :=
1018	  rfl
1019	
1020	/-- **Degreewise differential identification** of the hom-complex with the mapped opposite of
1021	the free ƒåech complex (family form of `homCechComplex_d_eq`). -/
1022	private lemma homCechComplex_d_eqFam (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ‚Ñï) :
1023	    (homCechComplexFam U F).d p (p + 1)
1024	      = (((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï)).obj
1025	          (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplexFam U))).d p (p + 1) := by

1031	    ChainComplex.of_d (fun n => (cechFreeSimplicialFam U).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk n))) (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicialFam U)) p

1043	  erw [Functor.map_zsmul]
1044	  rfl

1048	    homCechComplexFam U F ‚âÖ

1050	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplexFam U)) :=
1051	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun _ => Iso.refl _) (by
1052	    rintro i j (rfl : i + 1 = j)
1053	    exact (Category.id_comp _).trans

1055

1057	of `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso`). -/
1058	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexMapOpIsoFam (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
1059	    ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï)).obj
1060	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplexFam U))
1061	      ‚âÖ sectionCechComplex U F :=
1062	  (homCechComplexMapOpIsoFam U F).symm ‚â™‚â´ cechComplex_hom_identificationFam U F
1063	
1064	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1065	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in

1067	-- coercions whose defeq checks exceed the default heartbeat budget (as in `injective_cech_acyclic`).
1068	/-- **Injective sheaves are ƒåech-acyclic** (family form of `injective_cech_acyclic`,
1069	blueprint `lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`).
1070	
1071	For an injective `O_X`-module `I` and any finite family `U : Œπ ‚Üí Opens ‚Ü•X` (no covering
1072	hypothesis), the section ƒåech cohomology of `I` vanishes in every positive degree `p > 0`:
1073	`»û·µñ(U, I) = 0`.  This is the cover-agnostic mirror of `injective_cech_acyclic`, consuming
1074	Lane-A's `cechFreeComplex_quasiIsoFam`; it discharges `BasisCovSystem.injective_acyclic`
1075	over covers of any open directly.

1077	Project-local: Mathlib has no ƒåech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. -/
1078	theorem injective_cech_acyclicFam (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] (p : ‚Ñï) (hp : 0 < p) :
1079	    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U
1080	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I)).homology p) := by
1081	  haveI : QuasiIso (cechFreeComplexAugFam U) := cechFreeComplex_quasiIsoFam U

1086	  set œà := ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.down ‚Ñï).symm).map
1087	      ((HomologicalComplex.opFunctor X.PresheafOfModules (ComplexShape.down ‚Ñï)).map
1088	        (cechFreeComplexAugFam U).op) with hœàdef
1089	  haveI : QuasiIso œà := quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective F _
1090	  -- transport the target onto the section ƒåech complex of `I`
1091	  set Œò := œà ‚â´ (sectionCechComplexMapOpIsoFam U F).hom with hŒòdef
1092	  haveI : QuasiIso Œò := inferInstance
1093	  haveI : QuasiIsoAt Œò p := QuasiIso.quasiIsoAt p
1094	  obtain ‚ü®n, rfl‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hp.ne'
1095	  -- the source complex is `Hom(-, I)` of a degree-`0`-concentrated complex: zero in degree `> 0`
1096	  have hX : IsZero ((((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex
1097	      (ComplexShape.down ‚Ñï).symm).obj ((HomologicalComplex.opFunctor X.PresheafOfModules
1098	        (ComplexShape.down ‚Ñï)).obj (Opposite.op
1099	          ((ChainComplex.single‚ÇÄ X.PresheafOfModules).obj (coverStructurePresheafFam U))))).X
1100	        (n + 1)) := by
1101	    rw [Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_X]
1102	    apply Functor.map_isZero
1103	    simp only [HomologicalComplex.opFunctor_obj, HomologicalComplex.op_X]
1104	    exact (HomologicalComplex.isZero_single_obj_X (ComplexShape.down ‚Ñï) 0
1105	      (coverStructurePresheafFam U) (n + 1) (Nat.succ_ne_zero n)).op
1106	  have hsrcZero : IsZero ((((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex
1107	      (ComplexShape.down ‚Ñï).symm).obj ((HomologicalComplex.opFunctor X.PresheafOfModules
1108	        (ComplexShape.down ‚Ñï)).obj (Opposite.op
1109	          ((ChainComplex.single‚ÇÄ X.PresheafOfModules).obj
1110	            (coverStructurePresheafFam U))))).homology (n + 1)) := by
1111	    rw [‚Üê HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology, HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff]
1112	    exact ShortComplex.exact_of_isZero_X‚ÇÇ _ hX
1113	  -- transfer vanishing across the quasi-isomorphism `Œò`
1114	  exact hsrcZero.of_iso (asIso (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap Œò (n + 1))).symm

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.Adelic.CechComparisonGate
7	
8	/-!
9	# Gate 4: the ƒåech-to-derived comparison on 2-affine covers, reduced to affine vanishing
10	
11	This file attacks **gate 4** of the unconditional genus-finiteness consumable
12	`Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`
13	(`CechComparisonGate.lean`), namely the instance
14	
15	```
16	‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily.
17	```
18	
19	`HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞` (`MayerVietorisCover.lean`) packages, for the cover `ùí∞`, a
20	degreewise `k`-linear comparison
21	`‚àÄ n, cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F n (‚®Ü ùí∞)` between unnormalised ƒåech
22	cohomology and the `Ext`-based derived cohomology `HModule'`.
23	
24	## The mathematical reduction (Leray on a 2-affine cover)
25	
26	For the 2-affine cover `S.coverFamily` of a separated `k`-curve `C`, with the three
27	pieces `U‚ÇÅ`, `U‚ÇÇ`, `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ` affine (the `AffineCoverMVSquare` fields), the comparison
28	is the classical statement "an acyclic cover computes derived cohomology", which for a
29	**two**-element cover is the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris long exact sequence
30	(`HModule'_sequence_exact`, in-tree) fed by **affine Serre vanishing in the constant-`k`
31	sheaf apparatus** `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`, i.e.
32	`Subsingleton (HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) i U) = 0` for every affine open `U` and
33	every `i > 0`.  Given that vanishing the comparison assembles degreewise:
34	
35	* `n = 0`: sheaf axiom (`H‚Å∞` on both sides is the group of global sections);
36	* `n = 1`: `AffineCoverMVSquare.cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok` (node N5, in-tree,
37	  unconditional) `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] H1Cok S F`, chained with the
38	  MV cokernel identification `H1Cok S F ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F 1 ‚ä§` (which uses the
39	  degree-`0`/degree-`1` slice of the MV LES together with the affine `H¬π`-vanishing on
40	  `U‚ÇÅ`, `U‚ÇÇ`);
41	* `n ‚â• 2`: both sides vanish ‚Äî the ƒåech complex of a 2-element cover is `3`-term, and the
42	  derived side vanishes by the MV LES together with the affine vanishing on
43	  `U‚ÇÅ`, `U‚ÇÇ`, `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ`.
44	
45	## What this file establishes
46	
47	* `AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_coverFamily` / `isAffineOpen_overlapOpen`: the three
48	  pieces of the cover, in their `coverFamily`/`overlapOpen` typed forms, are affine
49	  (bridging the structure fields to the family-indexed forms the degreewise assembly
50	  consumes).
51	* `AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_coverFamily` /
52	  `subsingleton_hModule'_overlapOpen`: the **per-piece derived vanishing** ‚Äî under
53	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F`, `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i (piece))` for every
54	  `i > 0`.  These are the affine-vanishing inputs the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris degreewise assembly
55	  reads off (the `H¬π` cokernel identification and the `n ‚â• 2` whole-space vanishing are
56	  both immediate consequences of the MV LES fed by exactly these).
57	* `module_finite_hModule_one_unconditional`: the capstone, stated under the honest gate
58	  set (finite map to ‚Ñô¬π, ‚Ñô¬π chart data, and the gate-4 comparison), delivering
59	  `Module.Finite k (H¬π(C, ùí™_C))`.  This is `module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1`
60	  with the `HasExt` gates synthesised (via `CechComparisonGate`); once the gate-4 instance
61	  is supplied ‚Äî i.e. once `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` is proved and
62	  the degreewise assembly built ‚Äî the genus of the curve is honest.
63	
64	## The precise residual
65	
66	The single named residual for the **unconditional** gate 4 is
67	
68	> `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` ‚Äî affine Serre vanishing
69	> `H‚Å±(U, ùí™_C) = 0` (`i > 0`, `U` affine) in the `Sheaf (Opens.gT ‚Ä¶) (ModuleCat k)`
70	> apparatus ‚Äî
71	
72	together with the degreewise assembly (the degree-`0`/degree-`1` MV cokernel
73	identification and the `n ‚â• 2` whole-space vanishing) built from it.  No `sorry`/axiom or
74	sorried instance is introduced here; the residual is the existing `Prop`-class
75	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`, whose producer for the structure sheaf is the genuine open
76	target (Mathlib supplies no scheme-cohomology Serre vanishing in this apparatus, and the
77	in-tree `X.Modules` vanishing is gated on the un-instantiable
78	`[EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules]`).
79	
80	**Universe note for the assembly (probe-confirmed here).**  The affine vanishing lives at
81	`HModule' = Abelian.Ext.{u}` (`Type u`), but the in-tree Mayer‚ÄìVietoris LES
82	`AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence` has its `HasExt` baked at `Abelian.Ext.{u+1}`
83	(`Type u+1`): the two carriers are *not* definitionally equal, so the degreewise assembly
84	must transport the per-piece vanishing across the universe bridge
85	`Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` (`MayerVietorisCore.lean`) before feeding it into the LES.
86	This is the same `Ext.{u}` ‚Üî `Ext.{u+1}` bridge the `HModule'`/`HModule` interface already
87	uses (`HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv`), and is the one non-obvious wrinkle a future
88	author of the assembly must account for.
89	-/
90	
91	set_option autoImplicit false
92	
93	universe u
94	
95	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
96	
97	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
98	
99	section Affineness
100	
101	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
102	
103	/-- Each piece of the 2-element cover family is affine: `S.coverFamily i` is `U‚ÇÅ` or
104	`U‚ÇÇ`, both affine by the `AffineCoverMVSquare` fields. -/
105	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_coverFamily (i : ULift.{u} (Fin 2)) :
106	    IsAffineOpen (S.coverFamily i) := by
107	  rcases i with ‚ü®i‚ü©
108	  fin_cases i
109	  ¬∑ exact S.isAffineOpen_U‚ÇÅ
110	  ¬∑ exact S.isAffineOpen_U‚ÇÇ
111	
112	/-- The overlap `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ` of the 2-element cover, in the `overlapOpen` typed form used by
113	the degreewise ƒåech assembly, is affine. -/
114	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_overlapOpen :
115	    IsAffineOpen S.overlapOpen :=
116	  S.isAffineOpen_inf
117	
118	end Affineness
119	
120	/-! ## Per-piece derived-cohomology vanishing (the acyclicity inputs of the assembly)
121	
122	Under affine Serre vanishing `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F`, each affine piece of the
123	2-affine cover has vanishing higher derived cohomology `HModule'`.  These are the exact
124	inputs the degreewise ƒåech comparison consumes (the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris LES on the cover reads
125	off `H¬π(‚ä§)` and the higher vanishing from precisely these). -/
126	
127	section PieceVanishing
128	
129	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
130	  (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
131	  (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
132	  [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
133	  [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]
134	
135	/-- Higher derived cohomology of `F` vanishes on each piece `S.coverFamily j` of the
136	2-affine cover (both pieces are affine). -/
137	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_coverFamily
138	    (j : ULift.{u} (Fin 2)) (i : ‚Ñï) (hi : 0 < i) :
139	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i (S.coverFamily j)) :=
140	  IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) (S.isAffineOpen_coverFamily j) i hi
141	
142	/-- Higher derived cohomology of `F` vanishes on the overlap `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ` of the 2-affine
143	cover (the overlap is affine). -/
144	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_overlapOpen
145	    (i : ‚Ñï) (hi : 0 < i) :
146	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i S.overlapOpen) :=
147	  IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) S.isAffineOpen_overlapOpen i hi
148	
149	end PieceVanishing
150	
151	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
152	
153	/-! ## The capstone: genus finiteness under the honest gate set
154	
155	Once the gate-4 comparison instance
156	`‚àÄ S, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily` is available (its unconditional
157	production is the residual `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` plus the
158	degreewise assembly), the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` is a finite `k`-module. -/
159	
160	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic
161	
162	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
163	
164	/-- **Genus finiteness of the curve, honest gate-set form.**  For a curve `C` over `k`
165	with a finite morphism to ‚Ñô¬π (`HasFiniteMapToP1`), the ‚Ñô¬π chart data
166	(`P1HasLaurentChartData`), and the ƒåech-to-derived comparison on 2-affine covers (gate 4),
167	the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C) = HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` is a finite `k`-module.
168	
169	This is `module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate` (the `HasExt`-free
170	restatement, `CechComparisonGate.lean`) named as the lane capstone.  It becomes
171	**unconditional** exactly when gate 2 (`P1HasLaurentChartData k`, lane A) and gate 4
172	(this file's target instance, whose residual is affine Serre vanishing
173	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`) are both discharged. -/
174	theorem module_finite_hModule_one_unconditional
175	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [HasFiniteMapToP1 C] [P1HasLaurentChartData k]
176	    [‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
177	      HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily] :
178	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
179	  module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate C
180	
181	end AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.Adelic.GenusFiniteness
7	
8	/-!
9	# The last genus-finiteness gates: scoping the ƒåech-to-derived comparison
10	
11	This file scopes the **final** gates of the adelic genus-finiteness consumable
12	`Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1` (`GenusFiniteness.lean`, node `N12`).
13	That consumable is stated under four gates:
14	
15	1. `HasFiniteMapToP1 C`               ‚Äî node `N9`  (curve ‚ü∂ ‚Ñô¬π, in progress elsewhere);
16	2. `P1HasLaurentChartData k`          ‚Äî node `N11b` (‚Ñô¬π chart rings, `P1ChartData.lean`);
17	3. `HasExt.{u}` **and** `HasExt.{u+1}` for the sheaf category
18	   `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`
19	   ‚Äî the ambient `Ext`-theory the `HModule` genus carrier runs on;
20	4. `‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
21	      HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily`
22	   ‚Äî the ƒåech-to-derived comparison (Leray/Cartan) on 2-affine covers.
23	
24	The question this file answers: **are gates 3 and 4 instantiable today?**
25	
26	## Gate 3 (the `HasExt` pair): INSTANTIABLE ‚Äî proved here
27	
28	The genus carrier lives in the sheaf category `ùíÆ := Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology
29	C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`, i.e. sheaves of `k`-modules for the *constant* base
30	field `k`.  This is the crucial point that dissolves the historical blocker: `ùíÆ` is a
31	**Grothendieck abelian category** (Mathlib `IsGrothendieckAbelian.{v} (Sheaf J A)` for a
32	small site `J` and Grothendieck-abelian `A = ModuleCat k`), so Mathlib supplies
33	`EnoughInjectives ùíÆ` (`079H`) and both `HasExt.{u} ùíÆ`, `HasExt.{u+1} ùíÆ` (`HasExt.standard`)
34	**for free**.  See `hasExt_moduleKSheaf` / `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf` /
35	`enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf` / `isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf` below.
36	
37	This is *not* the situation of the `SheafOfModules R = X.Modules` apparatus (`Ext` over the
38	structure sheaf `ùí™_X` as a sheaf of rings), which the historical ƒåech capstone
39	(`CechToCohomology.cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`, `AffineSerreVanishing.affine_serre_vanishing`)
40	runs on: there Mathlib v4.31 provides *no* Grothendieck-abelian / enough-injectives instance
41	for `SheafOfModules`, so every derived-functor statement in that lane carries
42	`[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` / `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` as an un-instantiable
43	hypothesis.  The genus carrier deliberately uses the constant-`k` sheaf category precisely to
44	avoid that trap ‚Äî and the trap is genuinely avoided.
45	
46	Consequence (`module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`): gate 3 can simply be
47	dropped from the `N12` consumable ‚Äî Mathlib synthesises it ‚Äî leaving only gates 1, 2, 4.
48	
49	## Gate 4 (the comparison `‚àÄ S, HasCechToHModuleIso ‚Ä¶`): NOT instantiable today
50	
51	`HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞` (`MayerVietorisCover.lean`) is the `Prop`-class packaging of the
52	comparison data `‚àÄ n, cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F n (‚®Ü ùí∞)`, i.e. "for the cover
53	`ùí∞`, unnormalised ƒåech cohomology equals derived (`Ext`) cohomology in every degree".
54	
55	For the 2-affine cover `S.coverFamily` this comparison is *mathematically* the Leray statement
56	"an affine cover is acyclic ‚üπ ƒåech computes cohomology", which for a **two**-element cover is
57	exactly the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris long exact sequence (`HModule'_sequence_exact`, in-tree) together
58	with **affine Serre vanishing in the constant-`k` sheaf apparatus**:
59	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`, i.e. `Subsingleton (HModule' k
60	(toModuleKSheaf C) i U) = 0` for every affine open `U` and every `i > 0`.  Given that vanishing,
61	the comparison assembles degreewise: `n = 0` (sheaf axiom), `n = 1` (`cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok`
62	= node `N5`, in-tree, plus the MV cokernel identification), `n ‚â• 2` (both sides vanish).
63	
64	The **precise gap** is therefore this single named input:
65	
66	> **MISSING:** `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` ‚Äî Serre vanishing
67	> `H‚Å±(U, ùí™_C) = 0` (`i > 0`, `U` affine) in the `Sheaf (Opens.gT ‚Ä¶) (ModuleCat k)` apparatus,
68	> **plus** the degreewise assembly of the 2-cover comparison from it.
69	
70	Neither piece is a one-line consequence of an existing lemma:
71	
72	* Mathlib has no scheme-cohomology Serre vanishing at all, in any apparatus.
73	* The in-tree Serre vanishing `AffineSerreVanishing.affine_serre_vanishing` lives in the *wrong*
74	  category (`X.Modules`, `Ext (jShriekOU U)`) and is itself gated on the un-instantiable
75	  `[EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules]`; bridging it to the constant-`k` apparatus would need a
76	  "cohomology is independent of the linear structure" comparison that is likewise absent.
77	* The ƒåech-to-derived carrier `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` is currently *produced* in-tree only
78	  from `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` ‚Äî which itself bundles `HasCechToHModuleIso` on the affine
79	  sub-covers, i.e. the very comparison we are trying to instantiate (a circular producer).
80	
81	So gate 4 is a genuine multi-lemma target, **but the historical infrastructure blocker is gone**:
82	because `EnoughInjectives ùíÆ` is now available (proved below), the honest way to close gate 4 is
83	to build affine Serre vanishing + the ƒåech comparison *directly in the constant-`k` sheaf
84	category* `ùíÆ` (where injective resolutions exist), rather than attempting to route through the
85	blocked `SheafOfModules` lane.  That is the recommended roadmap for the next wave.
86	
87	## Deliverable of this file
88	
89	* the gate-3 infrastructure, proved and named for citation
90	  (`isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf`, `enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf`,
91	  `hasExt_moduleKSheaf`, `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf`);
92	* the gate-3-free restatement of the `N12` consumable
93	  (`module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`): under gates 1, 2, 4 alone,
94	  `H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` is a finite `k`-module.  Once `P1ChartData` lands (gate 2) and the gate-4
95	  comparison is built, `Module.Finite k H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` ‚Äî hence the honesty of `genus C` ‚Äî is
96	  unconditional on a curve with a finite map to ‚Ñô¬π.
97	-/
98	
99	universe u
100	
101	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
102	
103	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic
104	
105	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
106	
107	/-! ## Gate 3: the `Ext` infrastructure of the constant-`k` sheaf category is present -/
108	
109	/-- The genus-carrier sheaf category `Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat k)` is **Grothendieck
110	abelian**: sheaves valued in the Grothendieck-abelian `ModuleCat.{u} k` on the small site of
111	opens of `X`.  Mathlib's `IsGrothendieckAbelian.{v} (Sheaf J A)` fires here.  This is the
112	structural fact that makes the derived-functor genus apparatus well-founded (contrast the
113	`SheafOfModules ùí™_X` apparatus, for which Mathlib v4.31 supplies no such instance). -/
114	theorem isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
115	    IsGrothendieckAbelian.{u}
116	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
117	  inferInstance
118	
119	/-- The genus-carrier sheaf category has **enough injectives** (Stacks `079H`, from
120	Grothendieck-abelianness).  This is the input the entire `Ext`/derived-functor comparison
121	needs, and the exact instance the `SheafOfModules ùí™_X` lane cannot supply.  Its availability
122	here is what dissolves the historical ƒåech-capstone blocker for the *constant-`k`* apparatus. -/
123	theorem enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
124	    EnoughInjectives
125	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
126	  inferInstance
127	
128	/-- Gate 3, lower universe: `HasExt.{u}` for the genus-carrier sheaf category is synthesisable
129	(from `HasExt.standard`, itself from Grothendieck-abelianness). -/
130	theorem hasExt_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
131	    HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
132	  inferInstance
133	
134	/-- Gate 3, upper universe: `HasExt.{u+1}` for the genus-carrier sheaf category is
135	synthesisable (the ambient `Ext`-theory the universe-bridged `HModule` genus carrier runs on). -/
136	theorem hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
137	    HasExt.{u+1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
138	  inferInstance
139	
140	/-! ## Gate-3-free restatement of the genus-finiteness consumable (node `N12`)
141	
142	Because both `HasExt` gates are now synthesised (gate 3 above), the `N12` consumable of
143	`GenusFiniteness.lean` can be restated without them: the only remaining gates are the geometric
144	inputs (`HasFiniteMapToP1 C`, `P1HasLaurentChartData k`) and the ƒåech-to-derived comparison
145	(gate 4).  This is the honest, tightest current form of adelic genus finiteness. -/
146	
147	/-- **Genus finiteness of the curve, gate-3-free form (node `N12`).**  For a curve `C` over `k`
148	with a finite morphism to ‚Ñô¬π and the ‚Ñô¬π chart data, and with the ƒåech-to-derived comparison on
149	2-affine covers (gate 4), the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C) = HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` is a
150	finite `k`-module.  Identical to `Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1` but with the
151	two `HasExt` hypotheses dropped ‚Äî Mathlib synthesises them from the Grothendieck-abelianness of
152	the constant-`k` sheaf category (`hasExt_moduleKSheaf`, `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf`).  Once gates
153	2 and 4 are discharged, `Module.Finite k H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` ‚Äî and hence the honesty of `genus C` ‚Äî is
154	unconditional on any curve with a finite map to ‚Ñô¬π. -/
155	theorem module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate
156	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [HasFiniteMapToP1 C] [P1HasLaurentChartData k]
157	    [‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
158	      HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily] :
159	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
160	  module_finite_hModule_one_of_finite_map C
161	    (fun S => ‚ü®S.hModuleOneEquivH1Cok (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)‚ü©)
162	
163	end AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.Adelic.FinitenessP1
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus finiteness of the curve (node `N12`, the adelic lane's consumable)
10	
11	This file is part of the **adelic Riemann‚ÄìRoch lane**.  It composes the two halves
12	of the lane that are now proved:
13	
14	* the **keystone** `Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finite_map`
15	  (`FinitenessP1.lean`, node `N11`): under the gates `HasFiniteMapToP1 C` and
16	  `P1HasLaurentChartData k`, *some* 2-affine cover `S` of `C` (the pullback of the
17	  standard ‚Ñô¬π charts along the finite map) has finite-dimensional ƒåech cokernel
18	  `»û¬π = S.H1Cok`, so any comparison `H¬π(C, ùí™_C) ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] S.H1Cok` transports the
19	  finiteness to the genus carrier;
20	* the **comparison** `AffineCoverMVSquare.hModuleOneEquivH1Cok`
21	  (`Cokernel.lean`, nodes `N5` + `N6`): for *every* 2-affine cover `S`, under the
22	  ƒåech-to-derived gate `HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily`,
23	  `HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] S.H1Cok (toModuleKSheaf C)` ‚Äî the N5 half
24	  (`cechCohomology 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó H1Cok`) is unconditional, only the N6 half is gated.
25	
26	## The honest gate set
27	
28	The keystone produces an *existential* cover (`D.pullbackSquare œÄ` for a chosen
29	finite `œÄ` and chart data `D`), which cannot be named in a theorem signature; the
30	comparison must therefore be available for **all** 2-affine covers, i.e. the honest
31	ƒåech-to-derived hypothesis is the `‚àÄ`-quantified instance gate
32	`[‚àÄ S, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily]` (a `Prop`-class
33	hypothesis with no sorried instance, in the `HasPicScheme`/`HasFiniteMapToP1`
34	gating pattern).  The full gate set of the consumable is
35	
36	* `HasFiniteMapToP1 C` ‚Äî node `N9`; derived in `FiniteMapToP1.lean` from the single
37	  strictly weaker gate `ExistsNonconstantMapToP1 C` under the ambient curve
38	  hypotheses;
39	* `P1HasLaurentChartData k` ‚Äî node `N11b`, the ‚Ñô¬π chart-ring computation
40	  (`P1ChartData.lean` is building towards its instance);
41	* `HasExt.{u}`/`HasExt.{u+1}` ‚Äî the ambient `Ext`-theory instances the `HModule`
42	  machinery runs on;
43	* `‚àÄ S, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily` ‚Äî the ƒåech-to-derived
44	  comparison (Leray/Cartan) for 2-affine covers of the curve.
45	
46	Under these, the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C) = HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` is a
47	finite-dimensional `k`-vector space, so `genus C = finrank k H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` is an
48	honest natural number.
49	-/
50	
51	universe u
52	
53	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
54	
55	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic
56	
57	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
58	
59	/-- **Genus finiteness of the curve (node `N12`, gated).**  For a curve `C` over `k`
60	with a finite morphism to ‚Ñô¬π and the ‚Ñô¬π chart data, and with the ƒåech-to-derived
61	comparison available on 2-affine covers, the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` is a finite
62	`k`-module.  Composes the `N11` keystone `module_finite_hModule_one_of_finite_map`
63	with the `N5`+`N6` comparison `hModuleOneEquivH1Cok` of `Cokernel.lean`. -/
64	theorem module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1
65	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [HasFiniteMapToP1 C] [P1HasLaurentChartData k]
66	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
67	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
68	    [‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
69	      HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily] :
70	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
71	  module_finite_hModule_one_of_finite_map C
72	    (fun S => ‚ü®S.hModuleOneEquivH1Cok (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)‚ü©)
73	
74	end AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic
