8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: finite-length carriers and producer instances
10	
11	Sub-file (3/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	hosts the downstream finite-length carriers (`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`,
13	`IsHModuleHomFinite`), their algebraic bridges (the `HModule` / `HModule'`
14	abbrevs, the H‚Å∞ `LinearEquiv` transport companions, the constant-sheaf-Œì adjunction
15	upgrade), and the curve-side producer instances. Upstream sub-files
16	`Presheaf.lean` and `SheafProperty.lean` provide the structure-sheaf object the
17	carriers depend on.
18	
19	Downstream finite-length carriers in this file:
20	
21	* H>0 affine cohomology vanishing is carried by iter-040's
22	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` (the producer instance for the curve case
23	  lives at iter-051).
24	* H‚Å∞ Hom-finiteness is carried by iter-043's **wholespace**
25	  `IsHModuleHomFinite`; the producer instance on a proper geometrically
26	  integral $k$-curve is supplied at iter-046 from Stein finiteness
27	  (`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper`).
28	* The iter-041 per-affine-open variant `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite` was an
29	  abandoned attempt (dead scaffolding ‚Äî $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is not finite
30	  over $k$ on a proper affine open of a non-trivial curve) and has been
31	  removed from this file.
32	
33	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
34	-/
35	
36	set_option autoImplicit false
37	
38	universe u v
39	
40	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
41	
42	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
43	
44	/-- Phase A step 5/6 bridge (iter-009 scaffold): the parallel `Sheaf.H` for
45	`ModuleCat k`-valued sheaves. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is
46	parameterised over `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` only, so closing `genus` honestly
47	requires this `ModuleCat k`-flavoured version. The result carries `Module k`
48	automatically via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and
49	`Module.finrank k` is therefore well-defined on it. The declaration is a
50	`noncomputable abbrev` (rather than `def`) so that instance synthesis sees
51	through the wrapper to find `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances. -/
52	noncomputable abbrev HModule
53	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
54	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
55	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
56	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ‚Ñï) : Type (u+1) :=
57	  Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n
58	
59	/-- Phase A step 6 algebraic bridge (iter-010 scaffold): the $k$-linear
60	identification of `HModule k F 0` with the Hom group from the constant
61	sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
62	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ : Ext X Y 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (X ‚ü∂ Y)` in any
63	`Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to the `Linear k`
64	enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
65	`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
66	`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ`;
67	probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
68	identify `H‚Å∞(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Œì(C, O_C)` viewed as a
69	`k`-module on a connected proper `k`-curve. -/
70	noncomputable def HModule_zero_linearEquiv
71	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
72	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
73	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
74	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
75	    HModule k F 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
76	      ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F) :=
77	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ
78	
79	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-013 scaffold): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
80	cohomology of an open `X : C` with values in a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`.
81	Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X = (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (op X)`
82	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L105) for
83	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, with `AddCommGrpCat.free ‚Üí ModuleCat.free k`.
84	
85	The codomain is `Type u` (not `ModuleCat.{u} k`): `Abelian.Ext` returns a bare
86	`Type u` carrying `Module k` via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` through the `Linear k`
87	enrichment. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) is required so
88	instance synthesis sees through the wrapper to find `Module k (HModule' k F n X)`
89	and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` ‚Äî under `def`, `Module.finrank` would fail
90	to typecheck (cf. iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`).
91	
92	This is the prerequisite for the iter-014+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris
93	LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`).
94	The iter-014+ work will state and prove the LES on a `MayerVietorisSquare`,
95	specialising to a 2-affine cover of a proper `k`-curve in iter-015+. The
96	comparison theorem `cechCohomology_OC C ùí∞ n ‚âÖ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`
97	for an acyclic cover (the technical heart of Path-2) is also queued for
98	iter-015+. -/
99	noncomputable abbrev HModule' (k : Type u) [Field k]
100	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
101	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
102	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
103	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ‚Ñï) (X : C) : Type u :=
104	  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
105	    ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n
106	
107	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-015): the `H‚Å∞` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`,
108	mirroring iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 `HModule'`. The
109	$k$-linear identification of `HModule' k F 0 X` with the Hom group from the
110	sheafified representable `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k).obj X)`.
111	Mathlib provides `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ : Ext X Y 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (X ‚ü∂ Y)`
112	in any `Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to `R = k` and to the
113	`Linear k`-enriched abelian category `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (the `Linear k`
114	enrichment is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's
115	`Sheaf.linear`), this collapses `HModule' k F 0 X` to a `k`-linear Hom group. The
116	closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ`; probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-015
117	plan-agent). Used downstream as the algebraic prerequisite for Stein-factorization-
118	derived `H‚Å∞(C, O_C) ‚âÉ k` on a connected proper `k`-curve (multi-iteration; queued
119	iter-017+ alongside the Mayer-Vietoris LES of iter-016+). -/
120	noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv
121	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
122	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
123	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
124	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
125	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) :
126	    HModule' k F 0 X ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
127	      ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
128	        ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ‚ü∂ F) :=
129	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ
130	
131	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H‚Å∞ transport companion for `HModule k F 0`. The iter-010
132	H‚Å∞ bridge `HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] ((constantSheaf J _).obj
133	(ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F)` identifies the degree-zero cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom
134	group; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across.
135	The `.symm` is required: iter-010's bridge has `HModule k F 0` on the LHS and the
136	Hom group on the RHS, but we want the Hom hypothesis on the LHS and the
137	`HModule` conclusion on the RHS, so we apply `.symm` first. Mirrors iter-037's
138	`module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X‚ÇÑ` pattern at degree $0$, with no Mayer-Vietoris
139	machinery required. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k
140	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness
141	input is supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves. -/
142	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero
143	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
144	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
145	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
146	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
147	    [Module.Finite k ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F)] :
148	    Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0) :=
149	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule_zero_linearEquiv k F).symm
150	
151	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H‚Å∞ transport companion for `HModule' k F 0 X`. Parallel
152	of `Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised carrier
153	`HModule'`. The iter-015 H‚Å∞ bridge `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv : HModule' k F 0 X ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
154	((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k).obj X) ‚ü∂ F)` identifies the
155	degree-zero open-evaluation cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom group from the sheafified
156	representable; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness
157	across. The `.symm` is required for the same orientation reason as in
158	`Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero`. -/
159	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero
160	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
161	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
162	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
163	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
164	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C)
165	    [Module.Finite k ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
166	        ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ‚ü∂ F)] :
167	    Module.Finite k (HModule' k F 0 X) :=
168	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule'_zero_linearEquiv k F X).symm
169	
170	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero` to
171	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` of a `Spec k`-scheme `C`. The
172	Grothendieck topology `Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat` is auto-inferred
173	via the iter-005 instances `instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK` and
174	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK`. The sheaf argument is inferred from the
175	result type. Mirrors iter-030 / iter-035 / iter-036 / iter-037's `_curve`
176	patterns. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k (HModule k
177	(toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness input is
178	supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves (typically the morally
179	trivial `H^0(C, O_C) ‚âÉ k` from Stein factorization on a connected proper
180	curve). -/
181	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_curve
182	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
183	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
184	    [Module.Finite k
185	      ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
186	        (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
187	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) :=
188	  Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero k _
189	
190	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule'_zero` to
191	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` of a `Spec k`-scheme `C`,
192	evaluated at an arbitrary open `U` of the underlying topological space. Parallel
193	of `module_finite_HModule_zero_curve` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised
194	carrier `HModule'`.  Same auto-inferred topology / instance setup. The open `U`
195	is an explicit parameter (unlike the implicit topology); the sheaf argument is
196	inferred. Used downstream in the cover-evaluation chain for proper geometrically
197	integral $k$-curves: for each affine corner $X_i$ of an `AffineCoverMVSquare`,
198	the iter-039 transport propagates `Module.Finite k`-ness from the Hom group
199	`((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô free_k).obj X_i) ‚ü∂ toModuleKSheaf C)` ‚Äî
200	morally `Module.Finite k (Œì(X_i, O_C))` for affine $X_i$ ‚Äî to the H‚Å∞ cohomology
201	piece `HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0 X_i`. -/
202	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero_curve
203	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
204	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
205	    (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
206	    [Module.Finite k
207	      ((presheafToSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
208	        ((yoneda ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj U) ‚ü∂
209	          Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
210	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0 U) :=
211	  Scheme.module_finite_HModule'_zero k _ U
212	
213	/-- Iter-040 affine cohomology vanishing carrier predicate. Packages the
214	geometric statement that for every affine open `U` of `C.left.toTopCat` and
215	every degree `i > 0`, the open-evaluation cohomology `Scheme.HModule' k F i U`
216	is the zero `k`-module (formulated as `Subsingleton`, since `HModule'` returns
217	a `Type u` rather than a `ModuleCat` object ‚Äî see iter-014 abbrev). The class
218	is the affine-vanishing input the cover-evaluation chain consumes once the
219	producer instance is supplied (queued for iter-041+; multi-iteration
220	project-local construction expected since Mathlib does not yet provide
221	scheme-level Serre vanishing on affines for the `ModuleCat k`-flavour). -/
222	class IsAffineHModuleVanishing
223	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
224	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
225	    Prop where
226	  subsingleton_HModule' : ‚àÄ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat},
227	    AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U ‚Üí ‚àÄ i, 0 < i ‚Üí
228	      Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U)
229	
230	/-- Iter-040 immediate consumer of `IsAffineHModuleVanishing`: given the
231	class hypothesis, the open-evaluation cohomology `HModule' k F i U` is
232	`Module.Finite k` for any affine open `U` and any `i > 0`. The proof unfolds
233	the class field to `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i U)` and then invokes
234	Mathlib's auto-derived `Subsingleton M ‚Üí Module.Finite R M` instance (any
235	subsingleton module is finitely generated by the empty set, hence finite).
236	This consumer is the building block for the cover-evaluation chain: combined
237	with iter-039's `H^0` curve specialisations and iter-037's general-degree
238	corner-bridge transport, it closes the algebraic side of `Module.Finite k
239	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` once the producer instance for
240	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing` is supplied. -/
241	theorem module_finite_HModule'_of_isAffineHModuleVanishing
242	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
243	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
244	    [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]
245	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
246	    (hU : AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U) (i : ‚Ñï) (hi : 0 < i) :
247	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
248	  have : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
249	    IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) hU i hi
250	  inferInstance
251	
252	/-- Iter-043 wholespace H‚Å∞ Hom-finiteness carrier predicate. Packages the
253	algebraic statement that the global Hom group
254	`((constantSheaf _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F)` is finite over `k`. Morally
255	`Œì(C.left, F)` (the **global** sections of `F`) being finite over `k`. On a
256	proper geometrically integral $k$-curve $C$ with $F = O_C$, this is
257	$\Gamma(C, O_C) \simeq k$ (Stein factorization on a proper geometrically
258	connected curve), so this class admits a producer instance from the
259	geometric content of $C$ (supplied at iter-046).
260	
261	**Historical note on the abandoned per-affine-open variant.** An earlier
262	iter-041 attempt packaged Hom-finiteness of
263	`((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô free k).obj U) ‚ü∂ F)` **for every
264	affine open** `U`. By Yoneda + free-functor + sheafify adjunctions this
265	Hom group is `‚âÉ‚Çó[k] Œì(U, F)`, and on a proper smooth $k$-curve $C$ with
266	$F = O_C$, $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is **NOT finite over $k$** for $U$ a proper
267	affine open ‚Äî e.g. for the standard cover of $\mathbb{P}^1_k$ by
268	$U_0 = U_1 = \mathbb{A}^1_k$, $\Gamma(U_i, O_{\mathbb{P}^1}) = k[t]$ is
269	infinite over $k$. That per-open class therefore admits no producer
270	instance on a non-trivial proper curve and has been deleted as dead
271	scaffolding.
272	
273	This wholespace version captures only the **global** Hom group, which on
274	a proper curve is finite via Stein. Iter-044+ LES finite-length transport
275	uses this wholespace class (in conjunction with iter-040's H>0 affine
276	vanishing). -/
277	class IsHModuleHomFinite
278	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
279	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) : Prop where
280	  module_finite_hom : Module.Finite k
281	    ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
282	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ‚ü∂ F)
283	
284	/-- Iter-043 immediate consumer: with the wholespace H‚Å∞ Hom-finiteness class
285	hypothesis in scope, transport via iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero`
286	(which transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across iter-010's H‚Å∞ algebraic bridge
287	`HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] ((constantSheaf _).obj _ ‚ü∂ F)`)
288	to obtain `Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0)`. Works at the global level
289	(one Hom-finiteness instance, not one per affine open). -/
290	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite
291	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
292	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
293	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C F] :
294	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k F 0) :=
295	  have := IsHModuleHomFinite.module_finite_hom (k := k) (C := C) (F := F)
296	  module_finite_HModule_zero k F
297	
298	/-- Iter-043 curve specialisation: direct dot-notation wrapper for
299	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`. Saves call sites in the curve setting from
300	re-typing the sheaf argument when chaining into the LES finite-length
301	transport (queued for iter-044+). Mirrors the iter-039/iter-042 `_curve`
302	patterns. -/
303	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve
304	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
305	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
306	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) :=
307	  module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite k C _
308	
309	/-- Iter-044 geometric Stein input for the iter-043 wholespace H‚Å∞
310	Hom-finiteness carrier. For `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` an
311	integral $k$-scheme with proper structure morphism, the global sections
312	$\Gamma(C, O_C)$ form a finite-dimensional $k$-vector space.
313	
314	This is Stein's classical statement, packaged via Mathlib's
315	`AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`
316	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean`):
317	for $X$ integral and $f \colon X \to \Spec K$ universally closed and
318	locally of finite type, the structure-morphism ring map `f.appTop`
319	is module-finite. `IsProper f` packages both `UniversallyClosed f` and
320	`LocallyOfFiniteType f`.
321	
322	The bridge from `RingHom.Finite (C.hom.appTop.hom)` to
323	`Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ‚ä§))` (where the algebra
324	structure on `Œì(C, ‚ä§)` is iter-006's `kToSection`-derived) uses
325	`RingHom.finite_algebraMap` plus `Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv` to
326	transport the base ring from intermediate `Œì(Spec k, ‚ä§)` to `k` along
327	the ring iso `Scheme.ŒìSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)`. The compatibility
328	of algebra maps reduces to showing
329	`kToSection C (op ‚ä§).hom = (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp (Scheme.ŒìSpecIso _).inv.hom`,
330	which collapses via `Subsingleton.elim` on the `‚ä§ ‚ü∂ ‚ä§` hom-set +
331	`Functor.map_id`.
332	
333	Iter-045+ consumes this input to assemble the producer instance
334	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` via lifting Mathlib's
335	`constantSheafŒìAdj.homEquiv` to a `LinearEquiv` + identification of
336	`Sheaf.Œì.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)` with the underlying global sections
337	+ transport via `Module.Finite.equiv`. -/
338	theorem module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper
339	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
340	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
341	    Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§)) := by
342	  have hf : (C.hom.appTop.hom).Finite :=
343	    AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k C.hom
344	  letI alg2 : Algebra ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))

346	    := RingHom.toAlgebra C.hom.appTop.hom
347	  have hM_inter :
348	      Module.Finite ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ‚ä§))

351	  refine Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv
352	    (Scheme.ŒìSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
353	    (RingEquiv.refl _) ?_
354	  ext x
355	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, RingEquiv.refl_apply,
356	    Function.comp_apply, RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
357	  have h_kts : (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C (Opposite.op ‚ä§)).hom =
358	                (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp ((Scheme.ŒìSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom) := by
359	    ext y

361	      RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]

365	        ((Scheme.ŒìSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv x)

367	          ((Scheme.ŒìSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv x) :=
368	              congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe h_kts) _

376	/-- Iter-045: LinearEquiv between the global-sections module
377	`(Sheaf.Œì J _).obj F` (an object of `ModuleCat k`) and the underlying carrier
378	of `F.obj.obj (op ‚ä§)` for any sheaf `F` on a topological space `X`.
379	
380	The underlying iso comes from `Sheaf.ŒìNatIsoSheafSections` (Mathlib
381	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/GlobalSections.lean`): on a site with terminal
382	`T`, the global-sections functor is naturally iso to evaluation at `T`. For
383	the topology of opens `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`, the terminal in
384	`TopologicalSpace.Opens X` is the top open `‚ä§` (this is `Preorder.isTerminalTop`
385	for any preorder with a top element). The categorical iso in `ModuleCat k` is
386	converted to a `LinearEquiv` via `Iso.toLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's standard
387	upgrading of `ModuleCat`-isos to LinearEquivs).
388	
389	Iter-046+ uses this `LinearEquiv` together with the linearised constant-sheaf
390	/ global-sections adjunction (multi-iteration; project-local lift of
391	Mathlib's `Adjunction.homAddEquiv` to `‚âÉ‚Çó[k]`) to construct the producer
392	instance `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. -/
393	noncomputable def SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top
394	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
395	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
396	    (Sheaf.Œì (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
397	      ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (‚ä§ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
398	  ((Sheaf.ŒìNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
399	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ‚ä§) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F).toLinearEquiv
400	
401	/-- Iter-045 immediate consumer: combining iter-044's geometric Stein input
402	`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper` with `SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top`
403	yields `Module.Finite k ((Sheaf.Œì).obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` for a proper
404	integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`.
405	
406	The `haveI` is necessary because `Module.Finite.equiv` does not
407	auto-synthesise the `[Module.Finite k]` hypothesis on the source: the iter-044
408	declaration's conclusion is `Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ‚ä§))`,
409	but the source of `(SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top _ _).symm` is
410	`(toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (op ‚ä§)` ‚Äî these are *the same Module* (via the
411	iter-006 `toModuleKPresheaf_obj` simp lemma) but Lean needs the `haveI` to
412	register the typeclass under the new spelling.
413	
414	This is the algebraic input for the iter-046+ producer-instance assembly:
415	bridging from `Sheaf.Œì.obj` to the Hom group `((constantSheaf @ unit) ‚ü∂ -)`
416	requires the linearised constant-sheaf-Œì adjunction, which is multi-iteration
417	project-local infrastructure. -/
418	theorem module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper
419	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
420	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
421	    Module.Finite k
422	      ((Sheaf.Œì (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
423	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)) := by
424	  haveI : Module.Finite k
425	      ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj
426	        (Opposite.op (‚ä§ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) : ModuleCat k) :=
427	    module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper k C
428	  exact Module.Finite.equiv
429	    (SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)).symm
430	
431	/-- Iter-046: applied LinearEquiv from the constant-sheaf-Œì adjunction. For any
432	sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` and `X : ModuleCat k`, gives a `k`-LinearEquiv
433	between the Hom group `((constantSheaf).obj X ‚ü∂ F)` (sheaf morphisms from the
434	constant sheaf at `X`) and `(X ‚ü∂ (Sheaf.Œì).obj F)` (module morphisms into the
435	global sections of `F`).
436	
437	Built from `(constantSheafŒìAdj).homLinearEquiv` (iter-046 Mathlib gap-fill).
438	The five `haveI` lines establish the typeclass scaffolding required to invoke
439	the gap-fill: `presheafToSheaf` Linear (via `sheafificationAdjunction`),
440	`constantSheaf` Additive + Linear (via composition), and `Sheaf.Œì` Additive +
441	Linear (via the `right_adjoint_*` propagators along `constantSheafŒìAdj`).
442	
443	Used in iter-046's producer instance `instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf`
444	to bridge from `Sheaf.Œì.obj`-finiteness (iter-045) to Hom-from-constantSheaf-
445	finiteness (the `IsHModuleHomFinite` carrier). -/
446	noncomputable def constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv
447	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C]
448	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
449	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
450	    (X : ModuleCat.{u} k) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
451	    ((constantSheaf J _).obj X ‚ü∂ F) ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] (X ‚ü∂ (Sheaf.Œì J _).obj F) :=
452	  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
453	    (sheafificationAdjunction J _).left_adjoint_linear k
454	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive := by
455	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
456	  haveI : (Sheaf.Œì J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive :=
457	    (constantSheafŒìAdj J _).right_adjoint_additive
458	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k := by
459	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
460	  haveI : (Sheaf.Œì J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
461	    (constantSheafŒìAdj J _).right_adjoint_linear k
462	  (constantSheafŒìAdj J _).homLinearEquiv k X F
463	
464	/-- Iter-046: Hom-from-`k` upgrade. The Hom group `(ModuleCat.of k k ‚ü∂ M)` for
465	`M : ModuleCat k` is canonically `k`-LinearEquivalent to `M` via `f ‚Ü¶ f 1`.
466	Direct one-liner combining `ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's
467	LinearEquiv-version of the underlying-LinearMap correspondence) with
468	`LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf` (the standard `(R ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M) ‚âÉ‚Çó[S] M` evaluation). -/
469	noncomputable def homFromOne_linearEquiv (k : Type u) [Field k] (M : ModuleCat.{u} k) :
470	    (ModuleCat.of k k ‚ü∂ M) ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] M :=
471	  (ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv (M := ModuleCat.of k k) (N := M) (S := k)).trans
472	    (LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M)
473	
474	/-- Iter-046: **the producer instance** for `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
475	on a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`. Closes the four-step chain:
476	
477	  (1) `constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 1) bridges the Hom group
478	      `((constantSheaf).obj k ‚ü∂ toModuleKSheaf C)` to `(k ‚ü∂ Sheaf.Œì.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))`.
479	  (2) `homFromOne_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 3) identifies `(k ‚ü∂ M)` with `M`
480	      as `k`-modules.
481	  (3) Combined LinearEquiv from (1)+(2): `((constantSheaf).obj k ‚ü∂ toModuleKSheaf C)
482	      ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] Sheaf.Œì.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
483	  (4) `module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper` (iter-045 step 2) provides
484	      `Module.Finite k (Sheaf.Œì.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` from `[IsIntegral C.left]
485	      [IsProper C.hom]` (iter-044 geometric Stein input).
486	  (5) Transport via `Module.Finite.equiv (.symm)` of the combined LinearEquiv.
487	
488	Once landed, iter-043's curve consumer `module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve`
489	fires automatically on `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` queries,
490	closing the H‚Å∞ side of the genus-finrank Module.Finite ladder.
491	
492	Marked `instance` (not `theorem`) ‚Äî this is a *producer* of a typeclass instance,
493	to be picked up by typeclass synthesis when the consumer asks for
494	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. Hypotheses `[IsIntegral C.left]`,
495	`[IsProper C.hom]` are class arguments propagated via instance synthesis from
496	the use site (where `C` is concretely a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme, e.g.\ a
497	smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve). -/
498	noncomputable instance instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf
499	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
500	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
501	    IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) where
502	  module_finite_hom := by
503	    haveI := Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper k C
504	    let LE1 := constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv k
505	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of k k)
506	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
507	    let LE2 := homFromOne_linearEquiv k
508	      ((Sheaf.Œì (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
509	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
510	    exact Module.Finite.equiv (LE1.trans LE2).symm
511	
512	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

516	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the ƒåech cochain complex of
517	the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`,
518	with respect to an arbitrary indexed family of opens `ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí Opens C.left.toTopCat`.
519	Built from Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (file
520	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
521	underlying presheaf of `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (iter-006). The result is a
522	cochain complex valued in `ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `‚Ñï`.
523	
524	The cohomology of this complex is `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` below. The
525	downstream comparison theorem (ƒåech cohomology = derived-functor cohomology
526	= `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` for an acyclic cover) is
527	queued for iter-013+; iter-012 only establishes the ƒåech-side carrier. -/
528	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain_OC
529	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
530	    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
531	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ‚Ñï :=
532	  (cechComplexFunctor ùí∞).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
533	
534	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the `n`-th ƒåech cohomology
535	of the structure sheaf for an arbitrary indexed open cover. Defined as the
536	`n`-th homology of the ƒåech cochain complex `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`. The
537	result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k` and therefore carries a `Module k`
538	structure for free; the iter-013+ comparison theorem will identify it
539	with `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n` when the cover is
540	acyclic. -/
541	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC
542	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
543	    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ‚Ñï) :
544	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
545	  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C ùí∞).homology n
546	
547	/-- Iter-047: parameterised ƒåech cochain complex generalising iter-012's
548	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC` to any sheaf of `k`-modules `F`, not just the structure
549	sheaf. Built from the same Mathlib `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor`
550	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
551	underlying presheaf of `F`. The result is a cochain complex valued in
552	`ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `‚Ñï`. Iter-012's specialisation
553	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC C ùí∞` is recovered by setting `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`
554	(see `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` below).
555	
556	This generalisation is the foundational scaffolding the iter-048+ ƒåech-vs-derived
557	comparison theorem will build on: the comparison map
558	`Scheme.cechCohomology k C F ùí∞ n ‚Üí‚Çó[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (‚®Ü·µ¢ ùí∞ i)`
559	(queued for iter-048) is naturally parameterised over the sheaf `F`, not just
560	the structure sheaf, so the parameterised carrier is the right level of
561	generality. -/
562	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain
563	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
564	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
565	    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
566	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ‚Ñï :=
567	  (cechComplexFunctor ùí∞).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)
568	
569	/-- Iter-047: parameterised ƒåech cohomology generalising iter-012's
570	`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`. The `n`-th cohomology of the parameterised ƒåech
571	cochain complex. The result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k`. Iter-012's
572	specialisation is recovered by `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` below. -/
573	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology
574	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
575	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
576	    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ‚Ñï) :
577	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
578	  (Scheme.cechCochain C F ùí∞).homology n
579	
580	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCochain_OC` is definitionally the
581	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCochain`. The
582	proof is `rfl` since iter-012's body is the same `(cechComplexFunctor ùí∞).obj
583	((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))` term. Used by downstream
584	consumers (iter-048+) to switch between the iter-012 structure-sheaf-specific
585	form and the iter-047 parameterised form without semantic loss. -/
586	theorem Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq
587	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
588	    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
589	    Scheme.cechCochain_OC C ùí∞ = Scheme.cechCochain C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞ :=
590	  rfl
591	
592	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` is definitionally the
593	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCohomology`. -/
594	theorem Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq
595	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
596	    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ‚Ñï) :
597	    Scheme.cechCohomology_OC C ùí∞ n =
598	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞ n :=
599	  rfl
600	
601	/-- Iter-048: ƒåech-side acyclicity carrier predicate. The cover `ùí∞` is
602	*ƒåech-acyclic for the sheaf `F` of `k`-modules* on `C` if positive-degree ƒåech
603	cohomology vanishes (in the `Subsingleton` sense ‚Äî `Scheme.cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n`
604	has type `ModuleCat.{u} k`, but `Subsingleton` on the underlying type is the
605	natural and more chainable form). Mirrors the iter-040 / iter-043 carrier-
606	predicate pattern: a single-field `Prop` class capturing a combinatorial
607	vanishing condition that downstream consumers receive as an instance argument.
608	
609	This is the foundational ƒåech-side input that the iter-051 producer instance for
610	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` will consume, in conjunction
611	with the iter-049+ ƒåech-vs-derived comparison theorem and iter-048's consumer. -/
612	class Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover
613	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
614	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
615	    {Œπ : Type u} (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) : Prop where
616	  subsingleton_cechCohomology :
617	    ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï), 0 < n ‚Üí Subsingleton (Scheme.cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n)
618	
619	/-- Iter-048: subsingleton transport via ƒåech acyclicity + comparison.
620	
621	Given the iter-048 carrier hypothesis `[IsCechAcyclicCover F ùí∞]` AND an explicit
622	comparison iso `compIso n : cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F n (‚®Ü ùí∞ i)`
623	(the ƒåech-vs-derived comparison, queued for iter-049+ to construct as a
624	theorem), conclude that `Subsingleton (HModule' k F n (‚®Ü ùí∞ i))` for `n ‚â• 1`.
625	
626	The `compIso` is taken as an *explicit argument*, not a class field. The
627	comparison itself is a `LinearEquiv` (data), so it cannot be a field of a
628	`Prop`-valued class; more importantly, decoupling the ƒåech-side combinatorial
629	vanishing (iter-048) from the substantive comparison theorem (iter-049+) lets
630	each step land as a single iteration. Iter-049+ will provide the comparison as
631	a theorem, which iter-048's consumer accepts directly via this argument.
632	
633	The consumer body extracts the class field (the `Subsingleton` on
634	`cechCohomology n`) and transports along `(compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton`. -/
635	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover
636	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
637	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
638	    {Œπ : Type u} {ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
639	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover F ùí∞]
640	    (compIso : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï),
641	      Scheme.cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
642	        Scheme.HModule' k F n (‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i))
643	    (n : ‚Ñï) (hn : 0 < n) :
644	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F n (‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i)) := by
645	  haveI := Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover.subsingleton_cechCohomology
646	    (F := F) (ùí∞ := ùí∞) n hn
647	  exact (compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton
648	
649	/-- Iter-048: curve specialisation at `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.
650	
651	Mirrors the iter-039 / iter-042 / iter-043 `_curve` pattern: a thin dot-notation
652	wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is the structure
653	sheaf) from re-typing `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-048 consumer
654	is chained through. Used by the iter-051 `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
655	producer instance. -/
656	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover_curve
657	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
658	    {Œπ : Type u} {ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
659	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞]
660	    (compIso : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï),
661	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
662	        Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i))
663	    (n : ‚Ñï) (hn : 0 < n) :
664	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (‚®Ü i, ùí∞ i)) :=
665	  Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover (ùí∞ := ùí∞) compIso n hn
666

8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the structure presheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (1/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	ships the categorical gap-fills, the sheafification / `Ext` typeclass prerequisites,
13	and the per-open ring-map helpers (1)‚Äì(5) that culminate in the presheaf-of-
14	`k`-modules `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6). Its sibling `SheafProperty.lean`
15	discharges the sheaf condition and bundles `toModuleKSheaf` (helpers 7‚Äì8); the
16	sibling `Carriers.lean` carries the downstream finite-length carriers and producer
17	instances.
18	
19	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
20	-/
21	
22	set_option autoImplicit false
23	
24	universe u v
25	
26	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	namespace CategoryTheory
29	
30	universe u‚ÇÇ v‚ÇÇ
31	
32	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ‚•§ C ‚•§ D` is additive when `D`
33	is preadditive. The `map_add` field reduces to `f + g = f + g` componentwise after
34	`ext; rfl`. -/
35	instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u‚ÇÇ}
36	    [Category.{v‚ÇÇ} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ‚•§ C ‚•§ D).Additive where
37	  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl
38	
39	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ‚•§ C ‚•§ D` is `R`-linear when
40	`D` is `Linear R`-enriched. The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is
41	load-bearing ‚Äî bare `[Linear R D]` would be parsed as `Linear` of a functor. -/
42	instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u‚ÇÇ}
43	    [Category.{v‚ÇÇ} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
44	    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
45	    (Functor.const C : D ‚•§ C ‚•§ D).Linear R where
46	  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl
47	
48	namespace Adjunction
49	
50	variable {C‚ÇÅ : Type u} {D‚ÇÅ : Type u‚ÇÇ} [Category.{v} C‚ÇÅ] [Category.{v‚ÇÇ} D‚ÇÅ]
51	    [Preadditive C‚ÇÅ] [Preadditive D‚ÇÅ] {F : C‚ÇÅ ‚•§ D‚ÇÅ} {G : D‚ÇÅ ‚•§ C‚ÇÅ} (adj : F ‚ä£ G)
52	    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C‚ÇÅ] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D‚ÇÅ]
53	
54	include adj
55	
56	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `G : D ‚•§ C` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ‚ä£ G`,
57	then `F : C ‚•§ D` is `R`-linear. Mirrors Mathlib's `left_adjoint_additive`; the
58	`simp` call closes via unit naturality + the `Linear.smul_comp` / `Linear.comp_smul`
59	default simp lemmas. -/
60	lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
61	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
62	    -- v4.31.0: the old `simp [adj.homEquiv_unit]` no longer closes; the categorical
63	    -- `Linear.comp_smul`/`smul_comp` only match via `erw` (the `‚âÉ‚Çó` homModule smul is defeq but
64	    -- not syntactically the `Linear.smul` instance), with unit-naturality in between.
65	    simp only [adj.homEquiv_unit, Functor.map_smul, ‚Üê Functor.comp_map]
66	    erw [‚Üê adj.unit.naturality, Linear.comp_smul, ‚Üê adj.unit.naturality, Linear.smul_comp]
67	    rfl)
68	
69	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `F : C ‚•§ D` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ‚ä£ G`,
70	then `G : D ‚•§ C` is `R`-linear. The dual of `left_adjoint_linear`. -/
71	lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
72	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
73	    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])
74	
75	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): linear lifting of `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`. Under
76	`[F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]`, the additive equivalence
77	`(F.obj X ‚ü∂ Y) ‚âÉ+ (X ‚ü∂ G.obj Y)` upgrades to a `LinearEquiv` over `R`. The
78	`change` is load-bearing: it exposes the underlying `homEquiv` form, allowing
79	`simp [homEquiv_unit]` to close via the same naturality argument as in
80	`left_adjoint_linear`. -/
81	noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
82	    (X : C‚ÇÅ) (Y : D‚ÇÅ) : (F.obj X ‚ü∂ Y) ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (X ‚ü∂ G.obj Y) :=
83	  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
84	  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
85	    map_smul' := fun r f => by
86	      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r ‚Ä¢ f) = r ‚Ä¢ adj.homEquiv _ _ f
87	      simp only [adj.homEquiv_unit, Functor.map_smul]
88	      erw [Linear.comp_smul] }
89	
90	end Adjunction
91	
92	end CategoryTheory
93	
94	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
95	
96	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (a): sheafification on the topology of opens of
97	any topological space, valued in `ModuleCat k`. Inferable from Mathlib's
98	small-site / concrete-category sheafification API; the prover's task is the
99	universe pinning, mirroring the iter-004 `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
100	instance instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK
101	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
102	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
103	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
104	  inferInstance
105	
106	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (b): `Ext` on the sheaf category. The universe
107	annotation `HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
108	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`; mirrors the iter-004
109	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
110	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK
111	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
112	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
113	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
114	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
115	  CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _
116	
117	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
118	
119	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
120	
121	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
122	
123	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k ‚Üí Œì(C, U)` induced by the
124	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ‚ü∂ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
125	restriction along `U ‚â§ ‚ä§`. -/
126	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
127	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ) :
128	    (CommRingCat.of k) ‚ü∂ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
129	  (Scheme.ŒìSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ‚â´
130	    C.hom.app ‚ä§ ‚â´ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
131	
132	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Œì(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
133	map of `kToSection`. -/
134	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
135	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ) :
136	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
137	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
140	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
141	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ) :
142	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
143	  rfl
144	
145	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
146	natural in `U`. -/
147	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
148	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ} (f : U ‚ü∂ V) :
149	    kToSection C U ‚â´ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
150	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ‚Üê C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
151	  rfl
152	
153	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
154	`algebraMap`. -/
155	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
156	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ} (f : U ‚ü∂ V) (r : k) :
157	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
158	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
159	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
160	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ‚ü∂ _) => g.hom r)
161	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
162	
163	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
164	
165	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
166	
167	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
168	
169	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
170	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
171	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
172	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ ‚•§ ModuleCat.{u} k where
173	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
174	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
175	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
176	      map_add' := fun x y => by
177	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
178	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
179	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
180	        congr 1
181	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
182	          (C := C) f r }
183	  map_id U := by
184	    ext x
185	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
186	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
187	    exact congrFun (congrArg (¬∑.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
188	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
189	    ext x
190	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
191	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
192	    exact congrFun (congrArg (¬∑.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
193	
194	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
195	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
196	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
197	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
198	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ) :
199	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
200	  rfl
201	
202	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
203

4	Authors: Christian Merten

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK.Presheaf
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the sheaf property and bundled structure sheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (2/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	discharges the sheaf condition for `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 7) and bundles the
13	result as `toModuleKSheaf` (helper 8); the forget-and-recover natural iso to the
14	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf is also packaged here. The downstream
15	finite-length carriers live in the sibling `Carriers.lean`.
16	
17	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
18	-/
19	
20	set_option autoImplicit false
21	
22	universe u v
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
27	
28	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
29	
30	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (7): the presheaf of (6) is a sheaf for the
31	Grothendieck topology of opens of `C.left.toTopCat`. -/
32	lemma toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
33	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
34	      (toModuleKPresheaf C) := by
35	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
36	  convert (Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
37	      (CategoryTheory.forget CommRingCat.{u})).mp C.left.sheaf.property using 1 <;> rfl
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 5 main: the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme as a sheaf
40	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
41	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/
42	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
43	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
44	  ‚ü®toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C‚ü©
45	
46	/-- Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007): forget-and-recover natural iso between
47	the iter-006 `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf and the iter-004
48	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf. The two sheaves agree on the nose at
49	the underlying-presheaf level: forgetting from `ModuleCat k` to `AddCommGrpCat`
50	via `forget‚ÇÇ` recovers the iter-004 `toAbSheaf C.left`. Closure body
51	`Iso.refl _`; probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`, iter-007 plan-agent). -/
52	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
53	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
54	    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
55	        (forget‚ÇÇ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
56	      ‚âÖ toAbSheaf C.left :=
57	  Iso.refl _
58	
59	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

27	
28	The genus carrier lives in the sheaf category `ùíÆ := Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology
29	C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`, i.e. sheaves of `k`-modules for the *constant* base
30	field `k`.  This is the crucial point that dissolves the historical blocker: `ùíÆ` is a
31	**Grothendieck abelian category** (Mathlib `IsGrothendieckAbelian.{v} (Sheaf J A)` for a
32	small site `J` and Grothendieck-abelian `A = ModuleCat k`), so Mathlib supplies
33	`EnoughInjectives ùíÆ` (`079H`) and both `HasExt.{u} ùíÆ`, `HasExt.{u+1} ùíÆ` (`HasExt.standard`)
34	**for free**.  See `hasExt_moduleKSheaf` / `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf` /
35	`enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf` / `isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf` below.
36	
37	This is *not* the situation of the `SheafOfModules R = X.Modules` apparatus (`Ext` over the
38	structure sheaf `ùí™_X` as a sheaf of rings), which the historical ƒåech capstone
39	(`CechToCohomology.cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`, `AffineSerreVanishing.affine_serre_vanishing`)
40	runs on: there Mathlib v4.31 provides *no* Grothendieck-abelian / enough-injectives instance
41	for `SheafOfModules`, so every derived-functor statement in that lane carries
42	`[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` / `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` as an un-instantiable
43	hypothesis.  The genus carrier deliberately uses the constant-`k` sheaf category precisely to
44	avoid that trap ‚Äî and the trap is genuinely avoided.
45	
46	Consequence (`module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`): gate 3 can simply be
47	dropped from the `N12` consumable ‚Äî Mathlib synthesises it ‚Äî leaving only gates 1, 2, 4.
48	
49	## Gate 4 (the comparison `‚àÄ S, HasCechToHModuleIso ‚Ä¶`): NOT instantiable today
50	
51	`HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞` (`MayerVietorisCover.lean`) is the `Prop`-class packaging of the
52	comparison data `‚àÄ n, cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F n (‚®Ü ùí∞)`, i.e. "for the cover
53	`ùí∞`, unnormalised ƒåech cohomology equals derived (`Ext`) cohomology in every degree".
54	
55	For the 2-affine cover `S.coverFamily` this comparison is *mathematically* the Leray statement
56	"an affine cover is acyclic ‚üπ ƒåech computes cohomology", which for a **two**-element cover is
57	exactly the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris long exact sequence (`HModule'_sequence_exact`, in-tree) together
58	with **affine Serre vanishing in the constant-`k` sheaf apparatus**:
59	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`, i.e. `Subsingleton (HModule' k
60	(toModuleKSheaf C) i U) = 0` for every affine open `U` and every `i > 0`.  Given that vanishing,
61	the comparison assembles degreewise: `n = 0` (sheaf axiom), `n = 1` (`cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok`
62	= node `N5`, in-tree, plus the MV cokernel identification), `n ‚â• 2` (both sides vanish).
63	
64	The **precise gap** is therefore this single named input:
65	
66	> **MISSING:** `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` ‚Äî Serre vanishing
67	> `H‚Å±(U, ùí™_C) = 0` (`i > 0`, `U` affine) in the `Sheaf (Opens.gT ‚Ä¶) (ModuleCat k)` apparatus,
68	> **plus** the degreewise assembly of the 2-cover comparison from it.
69	
70	Neither piece is a one-line consequence of an existing lemma:
71	
72	* Mathlib has no scheme-cohomology Serre vanishing at all, in any apparatus.
73	* The in-tree Serre vanishing `AffineSerreVanishing.affine_serre_vanishing` lives in the *wrong*
74	  category (`X.Modules`, `Ext (jShriekOU U)`) and is itself gated on the un-instantiable
75	  `[EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules]`; bridging it to the constant-`k` apparatus would need a
76	  "cohomology is independent of the linear structure" comparison that is likewise absent.
77	* The ƒåech-to-derived carrier `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` is currently *produced* in-tree only
78	  from `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` ‚Äî which itself bundles `HasCechToHModuleIso` on the affine
79	  sub-covers, i.e. the very comparison we are trying to instantiate (a circular producer).
80	
81	So gate 4 is a genuine multi-lemma target, **but the historical infrastructure blocker is gone**:
82	because `EnoughInjectives ùíÆ` is now available (proved below), the honest way to close gate 4 is
83	to build affine Serre vanishing + the ƒåech comparison *directly in the constant-`k` sheaf
84	category* `ùíÆ` (where injective resolutions exist), rather than attempting to route through the
85	blocked `SheafOfModules` lane.  That is the recommended roadmap for the next wave.
86	
87	## Deliverable of this file
88	
89	* the gate-3 infrastructure, proved and named for citation
90	  (`isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf`, `enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf`,
91	  `hasExt_moduleKSheaf`, `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf`);
92	* the gate-3-free restatement of the `N12` consumable
93	  (`module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`): under gates 1, 2, 4 alone,
94	  `H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` is a finite `k`-module.  Once `P1ChartData` lands (gate 2) and the gate-4
95	  comparison is built, `Module.Finite k H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` ‚Äî hence the honesty of `genus C` ‚Äî is
96	  unconditional on a curve with a finite map to ‚Ñô¬π.
97	-/
98	
99	universe u
100	
101	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
102	
103	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic
104	
105	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
106	
107	/-! ## Gate 3: the `Ext` infrastructure of the constant-`k` sheaf category is present -/
108	
109	/-- The genus-carrier sheaf category `Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat k)` is **Grothendieck
110	abelian**: sheaves valued in the Grothendieck-abelian `ModuleCat.{u} k` on the small site of
111	opens of `X`.  Mathlib's `IsGrothendieckAbelian.{v} (Sheaf J A)` fires here.  This is the
112	structural fact that makes the derived-functor genus apparatus well-founded (contrast the
113	`SheafOfModules ùí™_X` apparatus, for which Mathlib v4.31 supplies no such instance). -/
114	theorem isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
115	    IsGrothendieckAbelian.{u}
116	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
117	  inferInstance
118	
119	/-- The genus-carrier sheaf category has **enough injectives** (Stacks `079H`, from
120	Grothendieck-abelianness).  This is the input the entire `Ext`/derived-functor comparison
121	needs, and the exact instance the `SheafOfModules ùí™_X` lane cannot supply.  Its availability
122	here is what dissolves the historical ƒåech-capstone blocker for the *constant-`k`* apparatus. -/
123	theorem enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
124	    EnoughInjectives
125	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
126	  inferInstance
127	
128	/-- Gate 3, lower universe: `HasExt.{u}` for the genus-carrier sheaf category is synthesisable
129	(from `HasExt.standard`, itself from Grothendieck-abelianness). -/
130	theorem hasExt_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
131	    HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
132	  inferInstance
133	
134	/-- Gate 3, upper universe: `HasExt.{u+1}` for the genus-carrier sheaf category is
135	synthesisable (the ambient `Ext`-theory the universe-bridged `HModule` genus carrier runs on). -/
136	theorem hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
137	    HasExt.{u+1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
138	  inferInstance
139	
140	/-! ## Gate-3-free restatement of the genus-finiteness consumable (node `N12`)
141	
142	Because both `HasExt` gates are now synthesised (gate 3 above), the `N12` consumable of
143	`GenusFiniteness.lean` can be restated without them: the only remaining gates are the geometric
144	inputs (`HasFiniteMapToP1 C`, `P1HasLaurentChartData k`) and the ƒåech-to-derived comparison
145	(gate 4).  This is the honest, tightest current form of adelic genus finiteness. -/
146	
147	/-- **Genus finiteness of the curve, gate-3-free form (node `N12`).**  For a curve `C` over `k`
148	with a finite morphism to ‚Ñô¬π and the ‚Ñô¬π chart data, and with the ƒåech-to-derived comparison on
149	2-affine covers (gate 4), the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C) = HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` is a
150	finite `k`-module.  Identical to `Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1` but with the
151	two `HasExt` hypotheses dropped ‚Äî Mathlib synthesises them from the Grothendieck-abelianness of
152	the constant-`k` sheaf category (`hasExt_moduleKSheaf`, `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf`).  Once gates
153	2 and 4 are discharged, `Module.Finite k H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` ‚Äî and hence the honesty of `genus C` ‚Äî is
154	unconditional on any curve with a finite map to ‚Ñô¬π. -/
155	theorem module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate
156	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [HasFiniteMapToP1 C] [P1HasLaurentChartData k]
157	    [‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
158	      HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily] :
159	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
160	  module_finite_hModule_one_of_finite_map C
161	    (fun S => ‚ü®S.hModuleOneEquivH1Cok (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)‚ü©)
162	
163	end AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.Adelic.CechComparisonGate
7	
8	/-!
9	# Gate 4: the ƒåech-to-derived comparison on 2-affine covers, reduced to affine vanishing
10	
11	This file attacks **gate 4** of the unconditional genus-finiteness consumable
12	`Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`
13	(`CechComparisonGate.lean`), namely the instance
14	
15	```
16	‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily.
17	```
18	
19	`HasCechToHModuleIso F ùí∞` (`MayerVietorisCover.lean`) packages, for the cover `ùí∞`, a
20	degreewise `k`-linear comparison
21	`‚àÄ n, cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F n (‚®Ü ùí∞)` between unnormalised ƒåech
22	cohomology and the `Ext`-based derived cohomology `HModule'`.
23	
24	## The mathematical reduction (Leray on a 2-affine cover)
25	
26	For the 2-affine cover `S.coverFamily` of a separated `k`-curve `C`, with the three
27	pieces `U‚ÇÅ`, `U‚ÇÇ`, `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ` affine (the `AffineCoverMVSquare` fields), the comparison
28	is the classical statement "an acyclic cover computes derived cohomology", which for a
29	**two**-element cover is the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris long exact sequence
30	(`HModule'_sequence_exact`, in-tree) fed by **affine Serre vanishing in the constant-`k`
31	sheaf apparatus** `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`, i.e.
32	`Subsingleton (HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) i U) = 0` for every affine open `U` and
33	every `i > 0`.  Given that vanishing the comparison assembles degreewise:
34	
35	* `n = 0`: sheaf axiom (`H‚Å∞` on both sides is the group of global sections);
36	* `n = 1`: `AffineCoverMVSquare.cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok` (node N5, in-tree,
37	  unconditional) `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily 1 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] H1Cok S F`, chained with the
38	  MV cokernel identification `H1Cok S F ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F 1 ‚ä§` (which uses the
39	  degree-`0`/degree-`1` slice of the MV LES together with the affine `H¬π`-vanishing on
40	  `U‚ÇÅ`, `U‚ÇÇ`);
41	* `n ‚â• 2`: both sides vanish ‚Äî the ƒåech complex of a 2-element cover is `3`-term, and the
42	  derived side vanishes by the MV LES together with the affine vanishing on
43	  `U‚ÇÅ`, `U‚ÇÇ`, `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ`.
44	
45	## What this file establishes
46	
47	* `AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_coverFamily` / `isAffineOpen_overlapOpen`: the three
48	  pieces of the cover, in their `coverFamily`/`overlapOpen` typed forms, are affine
49	  (bridging the structure fields to the family-indexed forms the degreewise assembly
50	  consumes).
51	* `AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_coverFamily` /
52	  `subsingleton_hModule'_overlapOpen`: the **per-piece derived vanishing** ‚Äî under
53	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F`, `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i (piece))` for every
54	  `i > 0`.  These are the affine-vanishing inputs the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris degreewise assembly
55	  reads off (the `H¬π` cokernel identification and the `n ‚â• 2` whole-space vanishing are
56	  both immediate consequences of the MV LES fed by exactly these).
57	* `module_finite_hModule_one_unconditional`: the capstone, stated under the honest gate
58	  set (finite map to ‚Ñô¬π, ‚Ñô¬π chart data, and the gate-4 comparison), delivering
59	  `Module.Finite k (H¬π(C, ùí™_C))`.  This is `module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1`
60	  with the `HasExt` gates synthesised (via `CechComparisonGate`); once the gate-4 instance
61	  is supplied ‚Äî i.e. once `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` is proved and
62	  the degreewise assembly built ‚Äî the genus of the curve is honest.
63	
64	## The precise residual
65	
66	The single named residual for the **unconditional** gate 4 is
67	
68	> `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` ‚Äî affine Serre vanishing
69	> `H‚Å±(U, ùí™_C) = 0` (`i > 0`, `U` affine) in the `Sheaf (Opens.gT ‚Ä¶) (ModuleCat k)`
70	> apparatus ‚Äî
71	
72	together with the degreewise assembly (the degree-`0`/degree-`1` MV cokernel
73	identification and the `n ‚â• 2` whole-space vanishing) built from it.  No `sorry`/axiom or
74	sorried instance is introduced here; the residual is the existing `Prop`-class
75	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`, whose producer for the structure sheaf is the genuine open
76	target (Mathlib supplies no scheme-cohomology Serre vanishing in this apparatus, and the
77	in-tree `X.Modules` vanishing is gated on the un-instantiable
78	`[EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules]`).
79	
80	**Universe note for the assembly (probe-confirmed here).**  The affine vanishing lives at
81	`HModule' = Abelian.Ext.{u}` (`Type u`), but the in-tree Mayer‚ÄìVietoris LES
82	`AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence` has its `HasExt` baked at `Abelian.Ext.{u+1}`
83	(`Type u+1`): the two carriers are *not* definitionally equal, so the degreewise assembly
84	must transport the per-piece vanishing across the universe bridge
85	`Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` (`MayerVietorisCore.lean`) before feeding it into the LES.
86	This is the same `Ext.{u}` ‚Üî `Ext.{u+1}` bridge the `HModule'`/`HModule` interface already
87	uses (`HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv`), and is the one non-obvious wrinkle a future
88	author of the assembly must account for.
89	-/
90	
91	set_option autoImplicit false
92	
93	universe u
94	
95	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
96	
97	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
98	
99	section Affineness
100	
101	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
102	
103	/-- Each piece of the 2-element cover family is affine: `S.coverFamily i` is `U‚ÇÅ` or
104	`U‚ÇÇ`, both affine by the `AffineCoverMVSquare` fields. -/
105	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_coverFamily (i : ULift.{u} (Fin 2)) :
106	    IsAffineOpen (S.coverFamily i) := by
107	  rcases i with ‚ü®i‚ü©
108	  fin_cases i
109	  ¬∑ exact S.isAffineOpen_U‚ÇÅ
110	  ¬∑ exact S.isAffineOpen_U‚ÇÇ
111	
112	/-- The overlap `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ` of the 2-element cover, in the `overlapOpen` typed form used by
113	the degreewise ƒåech assembly, is affine. -/
114	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_overlapOpen :
115	    IsAffineOpen S.overlapOpen :=
116	  S.isAffineOpen_inf
117	
118	end Affineness
119	
120	/-! ## Per-piece derived-cohomology vanishing (the acyclicity inputs of the assembly)
121	
122	Under affine Serre vanishing `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F`, each affine piece of the
123	2-affine cover has vanishing higher derived cohomology `HModule'`.  These are the exact
124	inputs the degreewise ƒåech comparison consumes (the Mayer‚ÄìVietoris LES on the cover reads
125	off `H¬π(‚ä§)` and the higher vanishing from precisely these). -/
126	
127	section PieceVanishing
128	
129	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
130	  (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
131	  (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
132	  [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
133	  [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]
134	
135	/-- Higher derived cohomology of `F` vanishes on each piece `S.coverFamily j` of the
136	2-affine cover (both pieces are affine). -/
137	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_coverFamily
138	    (j : ULift.{u} (Fin 2)) (i : ‚Ñï) (hi : 0 < i) :
139	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i (S.coverFamily j)) :=
140	  IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) (S.isAffineOpen_coverFamily j) i hi
141	
142	/-- Higher derived cohomology of `F` vanishes on the overlap `U‚ÇÅ ‚äì U‚ÇÇ` of the 2-affine
143	cover (the overlap is affine). -/
144	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_overlapOpen
145	    (i : ‚Ñï) (hi : 0 < i) :
146	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i S.overlapOpen) :=
147	  IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) S.isAffineOpen_overlapOpen i hi
148	
149	end PieceVanishing
150	
151	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
152	
153	/-! ## The capstone: genus finiteness under the honest gate set
154	
155	Once the gate-4 comparison instance
156	`‚àÄ S, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily` is available (its unconditional
157	production is the residual `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` plus the
158	degreewise assembly), the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C)` is a finite `k`-module. -/
159	
160	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic
161	
162	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
163	
164	/-- **Genus finiteness of the curve, honest gate-set form.**  For a curve `C` over `k`
165	with a finite morphism to ‚Ñô¬π (`HasFiniteMapToP1`), the ‚Ñô¬π chart data
166	(`P1HasLaurentChartData`), and the ƒåech-to-derived comparison on 2-affine covers (gate 4),
167	the genus carrier `H¬π(C, ùí™_C) = HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` is a finite `k`-module.
168	
169	This is `module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate` (the `HasExt`-free
170	restatement, `CechComparisonGate.lean`) named as the lane capstone.  It becomes
171	**unconditional** exactly when gate 2 (`P1HasLaurentChartData k`, lane A) and gate 4
172	(this file's target instance, whose residual is affine Serre vanishing
173	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`) are both discharged. -/
174	theorem module_finite_hModule_one_unconditional
175	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [HasFiniteMapToP1 C] [P1HasLaurentChartData k]
176	    [‚àÄ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
177	      HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily] :
178	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
179	  module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate C
180	
181	end AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

29	## What `cechComplexFunctor` produces
30	
31	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean:65`
32	
33	```lean
34	noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï :=
35	  FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech
36	```
37	
38	Unfolding the constituents:
39	
40	- `FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor E = cosimplicialObjectFunctor E
41	  ‚ãô AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A`
42	  (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean:53`)
43	- `cosimplicialObjectFunctor E = evalOp C A ‚ãô (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj
44	  E.rightOp` (same file, L43)
45	- `(FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech.obj ‚¶ãn‚¶å = U.power (Fin (n+1))`, whose
46	  underlying object in `C` is `‚àè·∂ú (U ‚àò i)` indexed by
47	  `i : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/Cech.lean:34,186`)
48	
49	In our setting `C = (Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ`, `A = ModuleCat k`,
50	`P = (sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F`, finite products in `Opens` are infima.
51	The cochain at degree `n` is therefore exactly
52	
53	```
54	‚àè_{x : Fin (n+1) ‚Üí Œπ} F(‚®Ö k : Fin (n+1), ùí∞ (x k))
55	```
56	
57	‚Äî a `Module k` product (not a ŒÝ-type) inside `ModuleCat.{u} k`.
58	The differential `d^n` is the standard alternating sum
59	`‚àë_{j : Fin (n+2)} (-1)^j ¬∑ Œ¥_j` (Mathlib's `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`,
60	`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/AlternatingFaceMapComplex.lean:67`).
61	
62	**Crucial caveat**: this is the *unaugmented* ƒåech complex. There is no
63	`F(U) ‚Üí ‚àè_i F(ùí∞ i)` term sitting in degree `-1`. Standard ƒåech-acyclicity
64	for affine covers is usually stated about the *augmented* complex (Stacks
65	01EJ / 03B1), so the project either has to build the augmentation
66	explicitly, or carry the equivalent reasoning at the level of the
67	unaugmented homology (using the sheaf axiom for degree 0).

86	### Precedent: `CategoryTheory.SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
87	
88	`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:328`
89	
90	```lean
91	noncomputable def homotopyEquiv [Preadditive C] [HasZeroObject C]
92	    {X : SimplicialObject.Augmented C} (ed : ExtraDegeneracy X) :
93	    HomotopyEquiv (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.obj (drop.obj X))
94	      ((ChainComplex.single‚ÇÄ C).obj (point.obj X))
95	```
96	
97	Augmented-simplicial-with-extra-degeneracy ‚áí alternating face map complex
98	is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on the augmentation. This
99	is the **engine** for proving that a ƒåech complex over a "good" cover is
100	null-homotopic in positive degrees: the work that would otherwise be the
101	alternating-sum-cancellation argument is encapsulated here. Note this is
102	on `AlternatingFaceMapComplex` (chain complex) rather than
103	`alternatingCofaceMapComplex` (cochain complex) ‚Äî a dual will need to be
104	spelled out (or extracted by op).

106	### Precedent: `CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
107	
108	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:92`
109	
110	```lean
111	noncomputable def extraDegeneracyCech {i‚ÇÄ : U.I} (d : T ‚ü∂ U.obj i‚ÇÄ) :
112	    (U.cech.augmentOfIsTerminal (isTerminalIncl _ hT)).ExtraDegeneracy
113	```
114	
115	Specialises `homotopyEquiv` to the formal-coproduct ƒåech object: given a
116	"vertex" ‚Äî a section `T ‚ü∂ U.obj i‚ÇÄ` of one of the cover terms from the
117	terminal ‚Äî the augmented ƒåech object has an extra degeneracy. **This is
118	what fires after localizing at a single `f_i`**: in `D(f_i)`, the basic
119	open `D(f_i)` is a unit, so the affine `Spec(R[1/f_i]) = D(f_i)` is the
120	terminal of the cover-of-`D(f_i)` slice, and the inclusion `D(f_i) ‚äÇ
121	D(f_i)` (i.e. the cover's `i‚ÇÄ = i` member) supplies the section. Modulo
122	op-direction bookkeeping, this gives "ƒåech complex over `D(f_i)` is
123	null-homotopic in positive degrees" almost for free.

125	### Precedent: `exact_of_isLocalized_span`
126	
127	`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean:173`
128	
129	```lean
130	lemma exact_of_isLocalized_span (H : ‚àÄ r : s, Function.Exact
131	    (map (.powers r.1) (f r) (g r) F) (map (.powers r.1) (g r) (h r) G)) :
132	    Function.Exact F G
133	```
134	
135	(plus its sibling at L211, `exact_of_localized_span`, which directly
136	takes `f : M ‚Üí‚Çó N` without prequalifying localization data.)
137	
138	Hypothesis shape: for each generator `r ‚àà s` with `span s = ‚ä§`,
139	`map (.powers r) (f r) (g r) F` (the induced map between localizations)
140	is composed with `map (.powers r) (g r) (h r) G` to be exact. Conclusion:
141	`F` and `G` are exact unlocalized.
142	
143	**This is the spanning-set Koszul-style local exactness lemma**. The
144	iter-057 correction (noting the project's iter-056 docstring referenced a
145	non-existent `exact_of_localized_span` without the "is" prefix) was an
146	overcorrection: both `exact_of_localized_span` and
147	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` exist; the former is the user-facing direct
148	form, the latter is the explicit-localization-data form. The iter-058
149	report should clarify both forms exist and which one the iter-059+ work
150	will actually call.

248	## Cited references
249	
250	### Stacks Project ¬ß01ED (ƒåech cohomology)
251	
252	The acyclicity-on-affine proof in the Stacks Project (tag 01ED, sections
253	on ƒåech cohomology of quasicoherent sheaves on affine schemes) goes as
254	follows, *without* invoking Koszul:
255	
256	1. For an affine `U = Spec R`, a finite cover `ùí∞ = (D(f_i))_i` with
257	   `(f_i)` generating the unit ideal of `R`, and a quasi-coherent `F`
258	   with `M = F(U)`, the augmented ƒåech complex
259	   `0 ‚Üí M ‚Üí ‚àè_i M[1/f_i] ‚Üí ‚àè_{ij} M[1/f_i f_j] ‚Üí ...` is exact.
260	
261	2. **Proof**: exactness is local on `D(f_i)` for each `i`. Localising at
262	   `f_i`, the augmented ƒåech complex over `D(f_i)` has `D(f_i)` as a cover
263	   member (`f_i` is a unit in `R[1/f_i]`), giving an extra degeneracy
264	   via "inclusion of the vertex". By the standard extra-degeneracy
265	   homotopy, the localised augmented complex is contractible, hence
266	   exact.
267	
268	3. By "exact on each open of a spanning cover ‚áí exact globally" (which
269	   is `exact_of_isLocalized_span` in Mathlib), the original augmented
270	   complex is exact.
271	
272	This route is the most economical for our setting: it requires *no*
273	Koszul-complex machinery; Mathlib's `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
274	encapsulates the alternating-sum cancellation; and the local-to-global
275	step is exactly `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
276	
277	### Stacks Project ¬ß03OU (ƒåech-to-cohomology spectral sequence)
278	
279	The general comparison `H^p(C^‚Ä¢(ùí∞, F)) ‚Üí H^p(U, F)` (ƒåech-to-derived
280	edge map) extends to a spectral sequence
281	`E^{p,q}_2 = »û^p(ùí∞, H^q(F)) ‚áí H^{p+q}(U, F)`. When the cover is acyclic
282	(all `H^q(intersection, F) = 0` for `q > 0`), the spectral sequence
283	degenerates and the edge map is an isomorphism. Mathlib does not have
284	this spectral sequence in any form. **For the project, the most
285	economical substitute** is to construct the edge map directly (without
286	the spectral sequence) by:
287	
288	- Producing the augmented ƒåech complex `C^‚Ä¢ : 0 ‚Üí F(U) ‚Üí C^0 ‚Üí C^1 ‚Üí ...`
289	  on the affine. By step (1)‚Äì(3) above, this is acyclic, hence a
290	  resolution of `F(U)`.
291	- Sheaf-evaluating against an injective resolution of `F` (Mathlib does
292	  not provide injective sheaves in general, so this is the bottleneck).
293	- Identifying the resulting double-complex bipartite homology with
294	  `Ext`.
295	
296	Honest Lean blueprint estimate for the comparison: ~200-400 LOC.
