313	### What the project must provide
314	
315	#### Branch A ‚Äî ƒåech acyclicity (~150-250 LOC)
316	
317	For each affine `U` and spanning `s ‚äÜ Œì(C.left, U)`:
318	
319	1. **Augmentation lemma** (~30-50 LOC). Define the augmentation
320	   `F(U) ‚Üí ‚àè_i F(ùí∞ i)` as the natural sheaf-restriction morphism, and
321	   produce an *augmented* cochain complex extending iter-047's
322	   `Scheme.cechCochain`. This is structural; no Mathlib wrapper exists.
323	
324	2. **Localized identification** (~60-100 LOC). For each `f ‚àà s`, identify
325	   the localization (at `f`) of the degree-`n` ƒåech cochain
326	   `‚àè_x F(‚®Ö k, ùí∞ (x k))` with the degree-`n` cochain of the
327	   "cover-of-`D(f)`" ƒåech complex. The key inputs are
328	   `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (each intersection
329	   identifies as `Localization.Away` of `Œì(U)`) and
330	   `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (chain-level localization-preserves-exactness).
331	
332	3. **Extra-degeneracy invocation** (~30-60 LOC). The cover-of-`D(f)`
333	   ƒåech complex contains `D(f) = D(f ‚à© f)` as a member, so applying
334	   `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` (or its op-dual for cochain
335	   complexes) gives an extra degeneracy on the augmented complex. By
336	   `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`, the augmented cochain complex over
337	   `D(f)` is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on `F(D(f))`,
338	   hence the (un-augmented) ƒåech complex over `D(f)` is exact in
339	   positive degrees.
340	
341	4. **Local-to-global** (~30-40 LOC). Per-degree, apply
342	   `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ...` with the per-`f` local exactness
343	   from step 3 to lift to exactness of the unlocalized differential.
344	   Repackage as `IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)`.
345	
346	#### Branch B ‚Äî ƒåech-vs-derived comparison (~150-200 LOC)
347	
348	This is the substantial branch. Possibilities, ranked by economy:
349	
350	(i) **Direct comparison via augmented complex as resolution** (~150-200
351	LOC). After Branch A, the augmented ƒåech complex is exact, hence a
352	resolution of `F(U)`. Use `Abelian.Ext`'s functoriality (Mathlib has
353	`Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv‚ÇÄ` and adjacent API) to identify the homology of
354	`Hom(augmented ƒåech, F)` with `Ext^n(R, F)`.
355	
356	(ii) **Inductive Mayer-Vietoris** (~250-400 LOC). For finite covers,
357	unwind the cover one basic open at a time using the iter-014 LES.
358	Requires inductive structure and ad-hoc finite-cover reduction. **Not
359	recommended** ‚Äî does more work and produces a less reusable artefact.
360	
361	(iii) **Spectral sequence formalisation** (~600+ LOC). Build Stacks 03OU
362	in Lean. **Strongly not recommended** ‚Äî out of scope for the genus
363	proof.
364	
365	**Recommendation**: pursue (i). Total Branch B estimate ~150-200 LOC.

367	### Total project-local LOC estimate
368	
369	~300-450 LOC across Branches A + B, spanning iter-059 to iter-066+.
370	This is within the directive's "~350-650 LOC" upper bound ‚Äî the
371	mathlib-faithful design comes in at the low end of the plan-agent's
372	estimate.
373	
374	## Recommendation
375	
376	**Do not bypass the ƒåech-vs-derived comparison.** No Mathlib precedent
377	exists for "acyclic cover ‚áí ƒåech = Ext", but the constituent pieces ‚Äî
378	`ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`, `extraDegeneracyCech`,
379	`exact_of_isLocalized_span`, `isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` ‚Äî combine
380	into the standard Stacks 01ED proof of ƒåech acyclicity (Branch A) with
381	modest project-local glue (~150-250 LOC). The ƒåech-vs-derived comparison
382	(Branch B) is the real obligation; spending ~150-200 LOC on the direct
383	"augmented ƒåech complex is a resolution" route gives the cleanest
384	deliverable.
385	
386	**The alternating-sum-cancellation argument the directive feared having
387	to reproduce is NOT needed**: `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` encapsulates
388	it. The project work is the *bookkeeping* of mapping the localized ƒåech
389	complex to a ƒåech complex over `D(f)` (Branch A step 2), not the cancellation
390	itself.
391	
392	**Naming alignment**: all paths and names cited by the project (iter-047
393	import, iter-053‚Äì058 wrappers) remain valid. Recommend the iter-059
394	plan-agent update the iter-056 docstring at
395	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean:1441` to say
396	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (or `exact_of_localized_span`, depending on
397	which form the project ends up calling ‚Äî both exist).
398	
399	## Caveats
400	
401	- **Op direction**: Mathlib's `extraDegeneracyCech` builds on
402	  `SimplicialObject.Augmented`, giving an augmented *simplicial* object
403	  with extra degeneracy. The corresponding alternating face map complex
404	  is a *chain* complex. Our ƒåech complex is a *cochain* complex (built
405	  from an *augmented cosimplicial* object via
406	  `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). The op-duality is straightforward but
407	  takes ~20-30 LOC of routine repackaging.
408	- **Augmentation construction**: the augmentation `F(U) ‚Üí ‚àè_i F(ùí∞ i)` is
409	  natural in `F`, so it should be derivable from the universal property
410	  of the formal-coproduct construction. But the project will need to
411	  spell this out ‚Äî Mathlib does not currently package the augmentation
412	  alongside `cechComplexFunctor`.
413	- **Quasi-coherence assumption**: Stacks 01ED is for *quasi-coherent*
414	  sheaves on affine schemes, where the augmented ƒåech complex is exact
415	  *as a complex of `Œì(U)`-modules*. Our `toModuleKSheaf` is not the
416	  structure sheaf as an `O_C`-module ‚Äî it's the structure sheaf as a
417	  *constant `k`-module*. The Stacks proof carries over because the
418	  alternating-sum cancellation is purely *combinatorial* (lives at the
419	  level of the simplicial structure, not the algebra structure). But the
420	  blueprint should note this distinction so future readers don't
421	  conflate "quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_C`-modules" with "sheaves of
422	  `k`-modules underlying the structure sheaf".
423	- **`Subsingleton` vs `IsZero`**: project's `IsCechAcyclicCover` carries
424	  a `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` field. Mathlib's
425	  `subsingleton_H_of_isZero` (`SheafCohomology/Basic.lean:74`) reduces
426	  via `IsZero`. The project may want to expose both forms; the
427	  `Subsingleton` form is the chainable one (matches iter-040's
428	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` consumer).

44	### Decision Q1a: product-localisation commutation `IsLocalizedModule.pi`
45	
46	- **Mathlib idiom**: `IsLocalizedModule.pi`. Cite:
47	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi:93`.
48	
49	  ```lean
50	  instance pi {Œπ : Type*} [Finite Œπ]
51	      {M M' : Œπ ‚Üí Type*} [‚àÄ i, AddCommMonoid (M i)] [‚àÄ i, AddCommMonoid (M' i)]
52	      [‚àÄ i, Module R (M i)] [‚àÄ i, Module R (M' i)]
53	      (f : ‚àÄ i, M i ‚Üí‚Çó[R] M' i) [‚àÄ i, IsLocalizedModule S (f i)] :
54	      IsLocalizedModule S (.pi fun i ‚Ü¶ f i ‚àò‚Çó .proj i)
55	  ```
56	
57	  Plus the binary sibling `IsLocalizedModule.prodMap` at L77 of the
58	  same file. Both are derived from
59	  `IsBaseChange.pi` / `IsBaseChange.prodMap` via the
60	  `isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange` adapter, which means the
61	  underlying `IsLocalizedModule (LinearMap.pi ‚Ä¶)` instance is
62	  definitionally a base-change statement ‚Äî exactly the universal
63	  property "localisation commutes with finite products".
64	
65	  Why Mathlib chose this: localisation = base change against
66	  `Localization S`; base change of modules commutes with finite limits
67	  in `ModuleCat R` (a categorical fact); finite products in `ModuleCat
68	  R` are the dependent `‚àÄ i, M' i`-types via `ModuleCat.piIsoPi`. The
69	  instance is a typeclass so consumers don't have to invoke it by hand.
70	
71	- **Project's current path**: the iter-069 docstring at
72	  `BasicOpenCech.lean:1583` describes "the
73	  product-localisation commutation `LocalizedModule (powers f) (‚àè·∂ú_x M_x) ‚âÖ
74	  ‚àè·∂ú_x LocalizedModule (powers f) M_x` holds". The sorry at L1783
75	  (`h_loc_exact`) is supposed to be closed by this commutation plus
76	  `h_a‚ÇÄ_fun f`. As of iter-106 no call site has been written.
77	
78	- **Gap**: identical (modulo `LinearMap.pi`-vs-`Pi.lift` repackaging).
79	  The Mathlib instance covers the `LinearMap.pi`-flavoured product;
80	  bridging to `Limits.Pi.lift`-flavoured product in `ModuleCat k`
81	  proceeds via `ModuleCat.piIsoPi : (‚àè·∂ú Z) ‚âÖ ModuleCat.of k (‚àÄ i, Z i)`
82	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Products`), which the project
83	  already uses (the `e‚ÇÅ`, `e‚ÇÇ`, `e‚ÇÉ` `LinearEquiv` handles at
84	  `BasicOpenCech.lean:1615-1617`).
85	
86	- **Cost of divergence (if project rebuilds the pi-localisation instead
87	  of importing)**: redundant ~50-LOC infrastructure that Mathlib already

151	### Decision Q2a: post-hoc R-linearity certification
152	
153	- **Mathlib idiom**: the canonical idiom in Mathlib is to **build the
154	  complex in the right category in the first place** rather than
155	  certify R-linearity post hoc. `cechComplexFunctor : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§
156	  CochainComplex A ‚Ñï` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafCohomology.Cech:65`)
157	  works for any `A` with the requisite limits ‚Äî in particular for
158	  `A := ModuleCat R`, giving a ƒåech complex of **R-modules** whose
159	  differentials are R-linear by construction.
160	
161	  `CategoryTheory.Linear R (ModuleCat R)` is canonical: hom-sets in
162	  `ModuleCat R` ARE the R-modules of R-linear maps; composition and
163	  addition commute with the R-action by definition.
164	
165	  But `Linear R (ModuleCat k)` does **NOT** hold for `R ‚âÝ k` in
166	  general: a `k`-linear map between `R`-modules-over-`k` need not be
167	  R-linear. There is no `Linear R`-instance on `ModuleCat k`, nor any
168	  Mathlib lemma "alternating sum of R-linear maps in
169	  `Hom_{ModuleCat k}(M, N)` is R-linear", because the hom-set isn't an
170	  R-module to start with.
171	
172	  `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex` lives over
173	  `Preadditive C` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex`)
174	  and inherits nothing more ‚Äî it neither requires nor exposes a
175	  `Linear R`-structure.
176	
177	- **Project's current path**: build `cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) ùí∞`
178	  in `CochainComplex (ModuleCat k) ‚Ñï` and prove R-linearity of the
179	  differential post hoc via the iter-099+ alternating-sum scaffolding
180	  (`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux`, `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`,
181	  `alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`, the named family
182	  `cechCofaceMap_summand_family` + its R-linearity at iter-102/104).
183	
184	  The 7-iter-stuck blocker at L1120 is *bridging* the iter-104
185	  binder-level R-linearity proof to the call-site anonymous-closure
186	  form of the K‚ÇÄ differential.
187	
188	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. Mathlib's idiom is "build in
189	  `ModuleCat R` if you need R-linearity"; the project's `ModuleCat k`
190	  + post-hoc-certify path has produced 7 consecutive failed iters
191	  because the categorical infrastructure (`Pi.lift`, alternating sum,
192	  `eqToHom`) is precisely tuned to preserve `Preadditive`/`Linear k`
193	  structure but obstinately whnf-reduces anonymous closures in ways
194	  that defeat the elaborator when tactics try to extract per-summand
195	  R-linearity.
196	
197	  The cost is *exactly* the 7 stuck iters: post-hoc R-linearity
198	  certification on a `ModuleCat k`-valued ƒåech differential is a
199	  Mathlib gap **created by the project's design choice**, not a
200	  pre-existing Mathlib gap that needs filling.
201	
202	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB at the architectural level. Two
203	  feasible paths (see Recommendation below).

294	**Path A (architectural, ~150-250 LOC, FALLBACK)**: refactor the

296	complex.
297	
298	```
299	1. Define toModuleKPresheaf_R : (Opens.subType (‚â§ U))·µí·µñ ‚•§ ModuleCat R
300	   where R := Œì(C.left, U). The presheaf restricts toModuleKPresheaf
301	   to opens contained in U, with each section module the (R-module
302	   image of the) original Œì(C.left, V) under the restriction

306	   ModuleCat R) on the basic-open cover restricted to ‚â§ U.

308	3. Identify K‚ÇÄ = (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (algebraMap k R)).mapHomologicalComplex K‚ÇÄ_R
309	   (R is a k-algebra via the structure morphism k ‚Üí Œì(C.left, ‚ä§) ‚Üí
310	   Œì(C.left, U) = R; the restriction-scalars functor is k-linear).
311	
312	4. R-linearity of K‚ÇÄ.f is INTRINSIC to K‚ÇÄ_R.f's type (it's a
313	   morphism in ModuleCat R, so R-linear by definition). The
314	   restrictScalars functor preserves linearity, so K‚ÇÄ.f (which IS
315	   K‚ÇÄ_R.f with k-restricted view) is R-linear when viewed through
316	   the R-module structure transported back to K‚ÇÄ.X_i via the
317	   forgetful.

324	   restrictScalars + IsLocalizedModule.pi route directly on K‚ÇÄ_R,

326	```

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechToCohomology
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.QcohTildeSections
8	
9	/-!
10	# Serre vanishing on affines (Stacks 02KG) ‚Äî affine cover-system infrastructure
11	
12	Project-local: builds the *affine cover system* feeding the basis-comparison criterion
13	`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO). The basis `B` is the distinguished opens `D(f)`
14	of an affine scheme, and the admissible coverings `Cov` are the standard open covers.
15	
16	This file builds the cover-system infrastructure (Lane 1 of the 02KG decomposition) as far
17	as possible; the quasi-coherent seed `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh` and the top
18	`affine_serre_vanishing` are handed off to the assembly iteration (they consume Lane 2's
19	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`).
20	-/
21	
22	universe u
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	-- Re-activate the (file-local) `HasExt` instance from `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` so that the
29	-- `Ext`-based absolute cohomology resolves here without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom` search.
30	attribute [local instance] hasExtModules
31	
32	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
33	
34	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî distinguished opens closed under finite intersection -/
35	
36	/-- **Distinguished opens are closed under finite intersection** (Stacks 02KG, condition (1)).
37	For a family `s : Œπ ‚Üí R` of ring elements and a ƒåech multi-index `œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ`, the
38	`(p + 1)`-fold intersection of distinguished opens `‚®Ö‚Çñ D(s_{œÉ k})` is again a distinguished
39	open, namely `D(‚àè‚Çñ s_{œÉ k})`; hence it lies in the basis of distinguished opens. This discharges
40	the `faces_mem` field of the affine cover system. Project-local: re-export of `basicOpen_sprod`
41	in the membership shape the `BasisCovSystem.faces_mem` field consumes. -/
42	theorem affine_faces_mem {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {Œπ : Type u} (s : Œπ ‚Üí R)
43	    {p : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) :
44	    (‚®Ö k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (œÉ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
45	      ‚àà Set.range (fun f : R => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)) :=
46	  ‚ü®‚àè k, s (œÉ k), (basicOpen_sprod (p + 1) s œÉ).symm‚ü©
47	
48	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî covering-datum bridge to the open-cover ƒåech form -/
49	
50	/-- **The standard affine open cover realizes the distinguished-open family.** For a spanning
51	family `s : Œπ ‚Üí R`, the `i`-th member of the standard affine open cover
52	`affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs` of `Spec R` has open range exactly the distinguished open
53	`D(s_i)`, i.e. `coverOpen ùí∞ i = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)`. This is the open-level half of the
54	covering-datum bridge (`lem:cover_datum_bridge`): funext-rewriting the raw family `c.2 = fun i ‚Ü¶
55	D(s_i)` to `coverOpen ùí∞` identifies the two section ƒåech complexes, so the ƒåech cohomology computed
56	over the raw `CovDatum` and over the `X.OpenCover` agree. Project-local: needed to feed the
57	`X.OpenCover`-shaped `injective_cech_acyclic` into the raw-family `BasisCovSystem` fields. -/
58	theorem coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {Œπ : Type u} [Finite Œπ]
59	    (s : Œπ ‚Üí R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ‚ä§) (i : Œπ) :
60	    coverOpen (Scheme.AffineOpenCover.openCover (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs)) i
61	      = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i) := by
62	  unfold coverOpen
63	  change (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (s i))))).opensRange = _
64	  apply TopologicalSpace.Opens.ext
65	  change Set.range (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (s i))))).base = _
66	  rw [Spec.map_base]
67	  exact PrimeSpectrum.localization_away_comap_range (Localization.Away (s i)) (s i)
68	
69	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî injective acyclicity for the standard affine cover -/
70	
71	/-- **Injective ƒåech-acyclicity for the standard affine cover** (Stacks 02KG, `injective_acyclic`
72	field; Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`). For a spanning family `s : Œπ ‚Üí R` (so the
73	distinguished opens `D(s_i)` cover `Spec R`) and an injective `O_X`-module `I`, the positive-degree
74	section ƒåech cohomology over the standard cover vanishes:
75	`»û^q(ùí∞, I) = 0` for all `q > 0`. Reduces the raw-family `cechCohomology` to the `X.OpenCover` form
76	via `coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan` and applies `injective_cech_acyclic`. Project-local: this is
77	the affine instantiation of `injective_cech_acyclic` discharging the
78	`BasisCovSystem.injective_acyclic` field for standard covers of the whole affine. -/
79	theorem affine_injective_acyclic {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {Œπ : Type u} [Finite Œπ]
80	    (s : Œπ ‚Üí R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ‚ä§)
81	    (I : (Spec R).Modules) [Injective I] (q : ‚Ñï) (hq : 0 < q) :
82	    IsZero (cechCohomology (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
83	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj I) q) := by
84	  have hbridge : (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
85	      = coverOpen (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover := by
86	    funext i; exact (coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan s hs i).symm
87	  haveI : Finite (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover.I‚ÇÄ :=
88	    inferInstanceAs (Finite Œπ)
89	  rw [cechCohomology, hbridge]
90	  exact injective_cech_acyclic (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover I q hq
91	
92	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî the underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves colimits
93	
94	The gap-fill `toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits` / `toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms` (blueprint
95	`lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`, `lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`) ‚Äî the statement that
96	`SheafOfModules.toSheaf R` (the forgetful functor to abelian sheaves) preserves finite colimits,
97	hence epimorphisms ‚Äî is the foundational ingredient for `affine_surj_of_vanishing` (and hence for
98	the `surj_of_vanishing` field of `affineCoverSystem`).  Mathlib provides only the limit-side dual
99	`PreservesFiniteLimits (toSheaf R)` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Limits`); the
100	colimit side is genuinely absent because epis of sheaves of modules are only *locally* surjective
101	and no factorisation through the right adjoint `forget` can capture it.
102	
103	The build below mirrors Mathlib's `‚Ä¶/ModuleCat/Sheaf/Colimits.lean` construction but routes through
104	the **sheafification square** `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf (ùüô R.obj)` (not through
105	`forget`).  Write `L := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (ùüô R.obj)`, a left adjoint (counit iso).
106	Step 1: `L ‚ãô toSheaf R ‚âÖ toPresheaf R.obj ‚ãô presheafToSheaf J Ab` (the square), and the right-hand
107	composite preserves finite colimits (`toPresheaf` does objectwise; `presheafToSheaf` is a left
108	adjoint).  Step 2 (descent): for a finite diagram `F`, `F ‚âÖ (F ‚ãô forget R) ‚ãô L`; applying the
109	colimit-preserving `L` and then `L ‚ãô toSheaf R` to a colimit cocone of `F ‚ãô forget R` exhibits
110	`toSheaf R` as preserving the colimit of `F`.  Epi-preservation follows from `WalkingSpan` being
111	finite. -/
112	
113	/-- **The underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves finite colimits** (blueprint
114	`lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`).  The forgetful functor `SheafOfModules.toSheaf R` from
115	sheaves of `R`-modules to the underlying sheaves of abelian groups preserves finite colimits ‚Äî the
116	right-exact dual of the Mathlib-supplied `PreservesFiniteLimits (toSheaf R)`.  Project-local: the
117	missing colimit side, built through the sheafification square (never through the right adjoint
118	`forget`). -/
119	theorem toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits.{v', u', w'} {C : Type u'} [Category.{w'} C]
120	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} (R : Sheaf J RingCat.{v'})
121	    [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] :
122	    PreservesFiniteColimits (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R) := by
123	  have step1 : PreservesFiniteColimits
124	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (ùüô R.obj) ‚ãô SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R) :=
125	    preservesFiniteColimits_of_natIso
126	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf (ùüô R.obj)).symm
127	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfSize (PresheafOfModules.sheafification.{v'} (ùüô R.obj)) :=
128	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ùüô R.obj)).leftAdjoint_preservesColimits
129	  constructor
130	  intro K _ _
131	  constructor
132	  intro F
133	  set D := F ‚ãô SheafOfModules.forget R with hD
134	  have e : F ‚âÖ D ‚ãô PresheafOfModules.sheafification (ùüô R.obj) :=
135	    Functor.isoWhiskerLeft F
136	      (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ùüô R.obj)).counit).symm
137	  have hc : IsColimit (colimit.cocone D) := colimit.isColimit D
138	  have hLc := isColimitOfPreserves (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (ùüô R.obj)) hc
139	  have hF := isColimitOfPreserves
140	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (ùüô R.obj) ‚ãô SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) hc
141	  haveI hpres : PreservesColimit (D ‚ãô PresheafOfModules.sheafification (ùüô R.obj))
142	      (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) :=
143	    preservesColimit_of_preserves_colimit_cocone hLc hF
144	  exact preservesColimit_of_iso_diagram (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) e.symm
145	
146	/-- **The underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves epimorphisms** (blueprint
147	`lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`).  A one-line corollary of `toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`:
148	finite-colimit preservation implies preservation of pushouts (`WalkingSpan`), hence of
149	epimorphisms.  This is the instance unlocking the passage from a module epimorphism to local
150	surjectivity in `affine_surj_of_vanishing`. -/
151	theorem toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms.{v', u', w'} {C : Type u'} [Category.{w'} C]
152	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} (R : Sheaf J RingCat.{v'})
153	    [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] :
154	    (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R).PreservesEpimorphisms := by
155	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfShape WalkingSpan (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R) :=
156	    (toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits R).preservesFiniteColimits WalkingSpan
157	  exact preservesEpimorphisms_of_preservesColimitsOfShape _
158	
159	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî standard covers are cofinal -/
160	
161	/-- **Standard covers are cofinal among open covers of a distinguished open** (Stacks 02KG, Tag
162	009L; blueprint `lem:standard_cover_cofinal`).  Given a distinguished open `D(f)` of `Spec R` and an
163	arbitrary open cover `W : Œ± ‚Üí (Spec R).Opens` of it, there is a finite standard subcover refining
164	it: finitely many `g : Fin n ‚Üí R` with `D(f) = ‚®Ü·µ¢ D(g·µ¢)` and each `D(g·µ¢) ‚â§ W (œÜ i)`.  This is the
165	refinement step invoked in `affine_surj_of_vanishing`.  Project-local: combines quasi-compactness of
166	`D(f)` with the basic-open basis. -/
167	theorem standard_cover_cofinal {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (f : R) {Œ± : Type u}
168	    (W : Œ± ‚Üí (Spec R).Opens)
169	    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) ‚â§ ‚®Ü a, W a) :
170	    ‚àÉ (n : ‚Ñï) (g : Fin n ‚Üí R) (œÜ : Fin n ‚Üí Œ±),
171	      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) = ‚®Ü i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ‚àß
172	      ‚àÄ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ‚â§ W (œÜ i) := by
173	  classical
174	  set Uf : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f with hUf
175	  have hK : IsCompact (Uf : Set ‚Ü•(Spec R)) := PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen f
176	  have hbasis := PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens (R := R)
177	  -- `B r` is the distinguished open `D(r)` typed as an open of `Spec R`.
178	  let B : R ‚Üí (Spec R).Opens := fun r => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
179	  -- Index type: basic opens contained in `Uf ‚äì W a`, carrying the witness `a`.
180	  let I := {p : R √ó Œ± // B p.1 ‚â§ Uf ‚äì W p.2}
181	  let cover : I ‚Üí Set ‚Ü•(Spec R) := fun p => (B p.1.1 : Set ‚Ü•(Spec R))
182	  have hopen : ‚àÄ i, IsOpen (cover i) := fun i => (B i.1.1).isOpen
183	  -- The chosen basic opens cover `Uf`.
184	  have hsub : (Uf : Set ‚Ü•(Spec R)) ‚äÜ ‚ãÉ i, cover i := by
185	    intro x hx
186	    have hxUf : x ‚àà Uf := hx
187	    obtain ‚ü®a, hxa‚ü© := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp (hcov hxUf)
188	    have hxinf : x ‚àà ((Uf ‚äì W a : (Spec R).Opens) : Set ‚Ü•(Spec R)) := by
189	      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inf]
190	      exact ‚ü®hxUf, hxa‚ü©
191	    obtain ‚ü®v, hvrange, hxv, hvsub‚ü© :=
192	      hbasis.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxinf (Uf ‚äì W a).isOpen
193	    obtain ‚ü®r, hr‚ü© := hvrange
194	    have hr' : (B r : Set ‚Ü•(Spec R)) = v := hr
195	    have hle : B r ‚â§ Uf ‚äì W a := by
196	      rw [‚Üê SetLike.coe_subset_coe, hr']; exact hvsub
197	    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ‚ü®‚ü®(r, a), hle‚ü©, ?_‚ü©
198	    change x ‚àà (B r : Set ‚Ü•(Spec R))
199	    rw [hr']; exact hxv
200	  -- Quasi-compactness: extract a finite subcover.

2047	so `Scheme.basicOpen_res` gives `V ‚äì (Spec R).basicOpen (a : Œì(‚ä§))` and `basicOpen_eq_of_affine`

2061	/-- **Cover of a general affine open: {\v C}ech vanishing for the tilde sheaf** (Stacks 02KG,
2062	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`, generalised from `D(f)` to an arbitrary affine open).
2063	Let `s : Œπ ‚Üí R` be a finite family whose distinguished opens `D(s·µ¢)` cover an **affine** open
2064	`V = ‚®Ü·µ¢ D(s·µ¢)` of `Spec R` (`hV : IsAffineOpen V`; `V` need NOT be a single `D(f)`).  Then the section
2065	{\v C}ech complex of the tilde sheaf `~M` over `{D(s·µ¢)}` has vanishing homology in all positive degrees.
2066	
2067	This is the residual `htilde` of the enlarged (general-affine) 02KG cone ‚Äî the seed
2068	`affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing` consumes exactly this shape.  Discharged by
2069	`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineCover` with `S := Œì(V)`: the spanning datum `hspan` is
2070	`IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff` (the `D(s·µ¢)` cover `V`), and the per-{\v C}ech-multi-index
2071	localisation datum `hloc` comes from `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (each `D(s_œÉ) ‚äÜ V`)
2072	transported across the `R`-algebra iso `Œì(D(s_œÉ)) ‚âÉ‚Çê[R] Localization (powers (sÃÑ_œÉ))`. -/
2073	theorem sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
2074	    {Œπ : Type u} [Finite Œπ] (s : Œπ ‚Üí R)
2075	    (hV : IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (‚®Ü i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)))
2076	    (p : ‚Ñï) (hp : 1 ‚â§ p) :
2077	    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
2078	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M))).homology p) := by
2079	  set V := (‚®Ü i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i) : (Spec R).Opens) with hVdef
2080	  -- the restricted family `sÃÑ·µ¢ = algebraMap R Œì(V) s·µ¢` spans the unit ideal of `S = Œì(V)`.
2081	  have hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range
2082	      (fun i => algebraMap ‚Ü•R ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) (s i))) = ‚ä§ := by
2083	    rw [‚Üê hV.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff, iSup_range']
2084	    simp_rw [basicOpen_algMap_section V]
2085	    rw [‚Üê inf_iSup_eq, ‚Üê hVdef, inf_idem]
2086	  -- per ƒåech multi-index `œÉ`: the `S`-localisation at `sÃÑ_œÉ` is an `R`-localisation at `powers (s_œÉ)`.
2087	  have hloc : ‚àÄ {n : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ),
2088	      IsLocalization (Submonoid.powers (CechLocalized.sprod s œÉ))
2089	        (Localization (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap ‚Ü•R
2090	          ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) (CechLocalized.sprod s œÉ)))) := by
2091	    intro n œÉ
2092	    have hDaV : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (CechLocalized.sprod s œÉ) : (Spec R).Opens) ‚â§ V := by
2093	      have hsp : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (CechLocalized.sprod s œÉ) : (Spec R).Opens)
2094	          = ‚®Ö k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (œÉ k)) := (basicOpen_sprod (n + 1) s œÉ).symm
2095	      rw [hsp]
2096	      exact le_trans (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)) (œÉ 0))
2097	    set a := CechLocalized.sprod s œÉ with ha
2098	    have e : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a : (Spec R).Opens)
2099	        = (Spec R).basicOpen (algebraMap ‚Ü•R ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) a) := by
2100	      rw [basicOpen_algMap_section V a]; exact (inf_of_le_right hDaV).symm
2101	    -- pin the section-restriction algebra `Œì(V) ‚Üí Œì(D a)` (the ad-hoc `.toAlgebra` that
2102	    -- `isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` constructs from the inclusion `D a ‚ü∂ V`) so the algEquiv
2103	    -- and `restrictScalars` below resolve it consistently.
2104	    letI algVDa : Algebra ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V))
2105	        ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a))) :=
2106	      ((Spec R).presheaf.map (homOfLE hDaV).op).hom.toAlgebra
2107	    haveI towerRVDa : IsScalarTower ‚Ü•R ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V))
2108	        ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a))) :=
2109	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (fun _ => rfl)
2110	    haveI hS : IsLocalization.Away
2111	        (algebraMap ‚Ü•R ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) a)
2112	        ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a))) :=
2113	      hV.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen
2114	        (algebraMap ‚Ü•R ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) a) (homOfLE hDaV) e
2115	    let eAlg : ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)))
2116	        ‚âÉ‚Çê[‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V))]
2117	          Localization (Submonoid.powers
2118	            (algebraMap ‚Ü•R ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) a)) :=
2119	      IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers
2120	        (algebraMap ‚Ü•R ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) a)) _ _
2121	    exact IsLocalization.isLocalization_of_algEquiv (Submonoid.powers a) (eAlg.restrictScalars ‚Ü•R)
2122	  exact sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineCover M s
2123	    ‚Ü•((Spec R).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) hspan hloc p hp

/-!
# Smallness of Ext-groups from the existence of enough injectives

Let `C : Type u` be an abelian category (`Category.{v} C`) that has enough injectives.
If `C` is locally `w`-small, i.e. the type of morphisms in `C` are `Small.{w}`,
then we show that the condition `HasExt.{w}` holds, which means that for `X` and `Y` in `C`,
and `n : ‚Ñï`, we may define `Ext X Y n : Type w`. In particular, this holds for `w = v`.

However, the main lemma `hasExt_of_enoughInjectives` is not made an instance:
for a given category `C`, there may be different reasonable choices for the universe `w`,
and if we have two `HasExt.{w‚ÇÅ}` and `HasExt.{w‚ÇÇ}` instances, we would have
to specify the universe explicitly almost everywhere, which would be an inconvenience.
Then, we must be very selective regarding `HasExt` instances.

Note: this file dualizes the results in `HasEnoughProjectives.lean`.

-/

public section

universe w v u

open CategoryTheory Category

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] [Abelian C]

namespace CochainComplex

open HomologicalComplex

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma isSplitMono_from_singleFunctor_obj_of_injective
    {I : C} [Injective I] {L : CochainComplex C ‚Ñ§} {i : ‚Ñ§}
    (Œπ : (CochainComplex.singleFunctor C i).obj I ‚ü∂ L) [L.IsStrictlyGE i] [QuasiIsoAt Œπ i] :
    IsSplitMono Œπ := by
  let e := L.pOpcyclesIso (i - 1) i (by simp)
    ((L.isZero_of_isStrictlyGE i (i - 1) (by simp)).eq_of_src _ _)
  let Œ± := (singleObjHomologySelfIso _ _ _).inv ‚â´ homologyMap Œπ i ‚â´ L.homologyŒπ i ‚â´ e.inv
  have : Œπ.f i = (singleObjXSelf (ComplexShape.up ‚Ñ§) i I).hom ‚â´ Œ± := by
    rw [‚Üê cancel_mono e.hom]
---eq_zero---
  rw [h‚ÇÅ, ‚Üê Q.map_comp, h‚ÇÇ, Q.map_zero]

end DerivedCategory

namespace CategoryTheory

open Limits

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] [Abelian C]

namespace Abelian.Ext

open DerivedCategory

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma eq_zero_of_injective [HasExt.{w} C] {X I : C} {n : ‚Ñï} [Injective I]
    (e : Ext X I (n + 1)) : e = 0 := by
  let K := (CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).obj X
  have := K.isStrictlyGE_of_ge (-n) 0 (by lia)
  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
  apply homEquiv.injective
  simp only [‚Üê cancel_mono (((singleFunctors C).shiftIso (n + 1) (-(n + 1)) 0
    (by lia)).hom.app _), zero_hom, Limits.zero_comp]
  exact to_singleFunctor_obj_eq_zero_of_injective (K := K) (n := -n) _ (by lia)

lemma subsingleton_of_injective [HasExt.{w} C]
    (X I : C) [Injective I] (n : ‚Ñï) : Subsingleton (Ext.{w} X I (n + 1)) :=
  subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0 Ext.eq_zero_of_injective

end Abelian.Ext

771	‚Äî first `M ‚áù M_f` at `powers f`, then `M_f ‚áù N` at `powers (algebraMap R R_f a)` over the
772	localisation ring `R_f` ‚Äî presents `N` as the single away localisation `M_a`, provided `f` is
773	already inverted once `a` is (`a^j = f¬∑h`, i.e. `f ‚àà ‚àö(a)`).  This is the module analogue of
774	`Localization.Away f` then `Localization.Away a = Localization.Away a` when `f ‚à£ a‚Åø`. -/

779	docstring.  The `R`-linear composite `gN ‚àò mkf : M ‚Üí N` exhibits `N` as `M` localised at
780	`Submonoid.powers a`, given `a^j = f * h` (so `f` divides a power of `a`). -/
781	lemma isLocalizedModule_comp_away
782	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
783	    {Rf : Type u} [CommRing Rf] [Algebra R Rf]
784	    {Mf : Type u} [AddCommGroup Mf] [Module R Mf] [Module Rf Mf] [IsScalarTower R Rf Mf]
785	    {N : Type u} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module Rf N] [IsScalarTower R Rf N]
786	    (f a h : R) {j : ‚Ñï} (hfa : a ^ j = f * h)
787	    (mkf : M ‚Üí‚Çó[R] Mf) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) mkf]
788	    (gN : Mf ‚Üí‚Çó[Rf] N) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rf a)) gN] :
789	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) ((gN.restrictScalars R) ‚àò‚Çó mkf) := by
790	  have smulN : ‚àÄ (r : R) (y : N), r ‚Ä¢ y = (algebraMap R Rf r) ‚Ä¢ y :=
791	    fun r y => (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul Rf r y).symm
792	  have smulMf : ‚àÄ (r : R) (y : Mf), r ‚Ä¢ y = (algebraMap R Rf r) ‚Ä¢ y :=
793	    fun r y => (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul Rf r y).symm
794	  -- `a^j = f h` lifts to `a^{j n} = f^n h^n`
795	  have hfa' : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, a ^ (j * n) = f ^ n * h ^ n := fun n => by
796	    rw [pow_mul, hfa, mul_pow]
797	  refine ‚ü®?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©
798	  ¬∑ -- map_units: `a` acts invertibly on `N` (as `algebraMap R Rf a` does)
799	    rintro ‚ü®x, n, rfl‚ü©
800	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
801	    have hb : Function.Bijective
802	        (‚áë(algebraMap Rf (Module.End Rf N) ((algebraMap R Rf a) ^ n))) :=
803	      (Module.End.isUnit_iff _).1 (by
804	        rw [map_pow]
805	        exact (IsLocalizedModule.map_units gN
806	          ‚ü®algebraMap R Rf a, Submonoid.mem_powers _‚ü©).pow n)
807	    have hfun : ‚áë(algebraMap R (Module.End R N) (a ^ n))
808	        = ‚áë(algebraMap Rf (Module.End Rf N) ((algebraMap R Rf a) ^ n)) := by
809	      funext y
810	      simp only [Module.algebraMap_end_eq_smul_id, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.id_apply]
811	      rw [smulN, map_pow]
812	    rw [hfun]; exact hb
813	  ¬∑ -- surjectivity
814	    intro y
815	    obtain ‚ü®‚ü®mf, u‚ü©, hu‚ü© := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rf a)) gN y
816	    obtain ‚ü®k, hk‚ü© := u.2
817	    obtain ‚ü®‚ü®m0, v‚ü©, hv‚ü© := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers f) mkf mf
818	    obtain ‚ü®l, hl‚ü© := v.2
819	    simp only [] at hk hl
820	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hu hv
821	    dsimp only at hu hv
822	    have hay : (a ^ k : R) ‚Ä¢ y = gN mf := by
823	      rw [smulN, map_pow, hk]; exact hu
824	    have hfm : (f ^ l : R) ‚Ä¢ mf = mkf m0 := by rw [hl]; exact hv
825	    refine ‚ü®‚ü®h ^ l ‚Ä¢ m0, ‚ü®a ^ (j * l + k), j * l + k, rfl‚ü©‚ü©, ?_‚ü©
826	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def]
827	    change (a ^ (j * l + k) : R) ‚Ä¢ y = gN (mkf (h ^ l ‚Ä¢ m0))
828	    have lhs : (a ^ (j * l + k) : R) ‚Ä¢ y = (f ^ l * h ^ l) ‚Ä¢ gN mf := by
829	      rw [pow_add, hfa' l, mul_smul, hay]
830	    have rhs : gN (mkf (h ^ l ‚Ä¢ m0)) = (h ^ l * f ^ l) ‚Ä¢ gN mf := by
831	      rw [_root_.map_smul, ‚Üê hfm, LinearMap.map_smul_of_tower,
832	        LinearMap.map_smul_of_tower, ‚Üê mul_smul]
833	    rw [lhs, rhs, mul_comm (f ^ l) (h ^ l)]
834	  ¬∑ -- exists_of_eq
835	    intro x1 x2 hx
836	    simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars, Function.comp_apply] at hx
837	    obtain ‚ü®u, hu‚ü© := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq
838	      (S := Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rf a)) (f := gN) hx
839	    obtain ‚ü®k, hk‚ü© := u.2
840	    simp only [] at hk
841	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hu
842	    have h1 : mkf ((a ^ k : R) ‚Ä¢ x1) = mkf ((a ^ k : R) ‚Ä¢ x2) := by
843	      rw [_root_.map_smul, _root_.map_smul]
844	      simp only [smulMf, map_pow, hk]
845	      exact hu
846	    obtain ‚ü®w, hw‚ü© := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := mkf) h1
847	    obtain ‚ü®l, hl‚ü© := w.2
848	    simp only [] at hl
849	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hw
850	    have h2 : (f ^ l : R) ‚Ä¢ (a ^ k : R) ‚Ä¢ x1 = (f ^ l : R) ‚Ä¢ (a ^ k : R) ‚Ä¢ x2 := by
851	      rw [hl]; exact hw
852	    refine ‚ü®‚ü®a ^ (j * l + k), j * l + k, rfl‚ü©, ?_‚ü©
853	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def]
854	    change (a ^ (j * l + k) : R) ‚Ä¢ x1 = (a ^ (j * l + k) : R) ‚Ä¢ x2
855	    have hexp : a ^ (j * l + k) = h ^ l * (f ^ l * a ^ k) := by
856	      rw [pow_add, hfa' l]; ring
857	    simp only [hexp, mul_smul]
858	    rw [h2]
859	
860	end AwayComparison
861	
862	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî base-change composite localisation (route B1, change-of-ring)
863	
864	The general-affine-open seed (Stacks 02KG over an arbitrary affine `V = ‚®Ü·µ¢ D(g·µ¢)`, NOT a single
865	`D(f)`) is proved by change of base to `S = Œì(V)`, where the cover family `·∏°·µ¢ = algebraMap R S g·µ¢`
866	DOES span the unit ideal.  The transport back to `R` needs, per ƒåech multi-index `œÉ`, the fact that
867	the composite `M ‚Üí M ‚äó_R S ‚Üí (M ‚äó_R S)_{·∏°œÉ}` (base change to `S`, then localise at `·∏°œÉ`) presents the
868	localised tensor as the localisation `M_{gœÉ}` of `M` at `powers gœÉ` over `R`.  This is the algebraic
869	analogue of `AwayComparison.isLocalizedModule_comp_away`, but with the FIRST leg a base change
870	(`IsBaseChange S`) rather than a localisation: `M ‚Üí M_S` is not a localisation of `M` (S is not a
871	localisation of `R`).  The single geometric input ‚Äî that the localised ring is simultaneously an
872	`R`-localisation at `powers gœÉ` ‚Äî is supplied as the instance `[IsLocalization (powers a) Aloc]`. -/
873	
874	/-- **Base-change composite is a localisation.**  Let `bc : M ‚Üí‚Çó[R] MS` exhibit `MS` as the base
875	change of `M` to an `R`-algebra `S` (`IsBaseChange S bc`), and let `gN : MS ‚Üí‚Çó[S] N` localise `MS`
876	at `powers (algebraMap R S a)` over `S`, with localised ring `Aloc`.  If `Aloc` is moreover an
877	`R`-localisation at `powers a` (the geometric input, encoding `D(a) ‚äÜ Spec S` when `S = Œì(V)`), then
878	the `R`-linear composite `gN ‚àò bc : M ‚Üí‚Çó[R] N` exhibits `N` as the localisation of `M` at `powers a`.
879	
880	Pure base-change algebra: `IsBaseChange` is transitive (`IsBaseChange.comp`), and
881	`isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange` converts both directions.  This is the route-B1 replacement for
882	`AwayComparison.isLocalizedModule_comp_away` in the general-affine `dDiff_exact_of_affineCover`. -/
883	theorem isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away
884	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
885	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
886	    {MS : Type u} [AddCommGroup MS] [Module R MS] [Module S MS] [IsScalarTower R S MS]
887	    (bc : M ‚Üí‚Çó[R] MS) (hbc : IsBaseChange S bc)
888	    {a : R}
889	    {N : Type u} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module S N] [IsScalarTower R S N]
890	    (gN : MS ‚Üí‚Çó[S] N)
891	    (Aloc : Type u) [CommRing Aloc] [Algebra S Aloc]
892	    [IsLocalization (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R S a)) Aloc]
893	    [Module Aloc N] [IsScalarTower S Aloc N]
894	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R S a)) gN]
895	    [Algebra R Aloc] [IsScalarTower R S Aloc] [IsScalarTower R Aloc N]
896	    [IsLocalization (Submonoid.powers a) Aloc] :
897	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) ((gN.restrictScalars R) ‚àò‚Çó bc) := by
898	  have hg : IsBaseChange Aloc gN :=
899	    (isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R S a)) Aloc gN).mp
900	      inferInstance
901	  exact (isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers a) Aloc _).mpr (hbc.comp hg)
902	
903	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî the un-localised section ƒåech module complex `D‚Ä¢`
904	
905	`D^m` is the product `‚àè_{œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ} M_{s_œÉ}` of away-localisations of
906	`M = Œì(Spec R, F)` at the multi-index products `s_œÉ = ‚àè‚Çñ s_{œÉ k}`; its differential
907	is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation comparison maps (index deletion
908	drops a factor, so `s_{œÉ‚àòd‚±º} ‚à£ s_œÉ`).  This is the concrete `R`-module complex `D‚Ä¢`
909	of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` that the section ƒåech complex is identified
910	with.  Positive-degree exactness is reduced ‚Äî via `exact_of_isLocalized_span` ‚Äî to
911	the localised complexes already shown exact in `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`;
912	the bridge is the localisation-transitivity lemma
913	`AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule` (applied here as
914	`dToCech_isLocalizedModule`). -/
915	
916	namespace SectionCechModule
917	
918	open CechLocalized AwayComparison
919	
920	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {Œπ : Type*} (s : Œπ ‚Üí R)
921	variable (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
922	
923	/-- The un-localised section ƒåech coefficient `D_œÉ = M_{s_œÉ}`. -/
924	abbrev dCoeff {m : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ) : Type u :=

data.content:
/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.lake-packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean
---
/-
Copyright (c) 2026 Jo√´l Riou. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Jo√´l Riou
-/
module

public import Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproducts.Cech

/-!
# Cech cohomology

Given a family of objects `U : Œπ ‚Üí C` in a category `C` that has finite products,
we define a Cech complex functor
`cechComplexFunctor : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï` which sends a presheaf
`P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` in a preadditive category (where products exist) to the cochain
complex which in degree `n` consists of the product, indexed by `i : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ`,
of the value of `P` on the product of the objects `U (i a)` for `a : Fin (n + 1)`.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe w t v v' u u'

namespace CategoryTheory

open Limits

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A] [HasProducts.{w} A]

namespace Limits.FormalCoproduct

open Opposite

variable (E : SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C))

/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
functor `(C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CosimplicialObject A` which sends `P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` to the
cosimplicial object which sends `‚¶ãn‚¶å` to the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _‚¶ãn‚¶å`. -/
@[simps!]
noncomputable def cosimplicialObjectFunctor :
    (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CosimplicialObject A :=
  evalOp.{w} C A ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj E.rightOp

variable [Preadditive A]

/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
functor `(C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï` which sends `P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` to the
cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _‚¶ãn‚¶å`. -/
@[simps!]
noncomputable def cochainComplexFunctor : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï :=
  cosimplicialObjectFunctor E ‚ãô AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A

end Limits.FormalCoproduct

variable [HasFiniteProducts C] [Preadditive A] {Œπ : Type w} (U : Œπ ‚Üí C)

/-- Given a family of objects `U : Œπ ‚Üí C`, this is the Cech complex functor
`(C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï` which sends a presheaf `P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` to the
cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the product,
indexed by `x : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ`, of the value of `P` on the product of the
objects `U (x i)` for `i : Fin (n + 1)`. -/
noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï :=
  FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech

end CategoryTheory

924	abbrev dCoeff {m : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ) : Type u :=
925	  LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÉ)) M
926	
927	/-- The un-localised coface comparison `Œ¥ : M_{s_{œÉ‚àòd‚±º}} ‚Üí M_{s_œÉ}` (R-linear), the
928	canonical localisation map for the divisibility `s_{œÉ‚àòd‚±º} ‚à£ s_œÉ`. -/
929	noncomputable def dCoface (m : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (m + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
930	    dCoeff s M (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] dCoeff s M œÉ :=
931	  comparison
932	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove))) M)
933	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÉ)) M)
934	    (Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd s œÉ j)
935	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÉ)) M))
936	
937	/-- The un-localised differential `d : D^m ‚Üí D^{m+1}` as an `R`-linear map: the
938	alternating sum of cofaces, assembled with `LinearMap.pi` over the output tuple. -/
939	noncomputable def dDiff (m : ‚Ñï) :
940	    (‚àÄ œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ, dCoeff s M œÉ) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] (‚àÄ œÉ : Fin (m + 1) ‚Üí Œπ, dCoeff s M œÉ) :=
941	  LinearMap.pi fun œÉ => ‚àë j : Fin (m + 1),
942	    (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (j : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢ (dCoface s M m œÉ j ‚àò‚Çó LinearMap.proj (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove))
943	
944	/-- Apply form of `dDiff`: the alternating sum of cofaces of the deleted faces. -/
945	lemma dDiff_apply (m : ‚Ñï) (t : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ, dCoeff s M œÉ) (œÉ : Fin (m + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) :
946	    dDiff s M m t œÉ
947	      = ‚àë j : Fin (m + 1), (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (j : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢ dCoface s M m œÉ j (t (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove)) := by
948	  rw [dDiff, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.sum_apply]
949	  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun d _ => rfl
950	
951	variable (r : Œπ)
952	
953	/-- The per-index localisation comparison `M_{s_œÉ} ‚Üí M_{s_r ¬∑ s_œÉ}` carrying the
954	un-localised coefficient to the `cechCoeff` coefficient localised at `s_r`. -/
955	noncomputable def dToCech {m : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ) :
956	    dCoeff s M œÉ ‚Üí‚Çó[R] cechCoeff s M r œÉ :=
957	  comparison
958	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÉ)) M)
959	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s œÉ)) M)
960	    (Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_mul_left (sprod s œÉ) (s r))
961	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s œÉ)) M))
962	
963	/-- **Transitivity payoff**: `dToCech` exhibits the localised coefficient
964	`cechCoeff = M_{s_r ¬∑ s_œÉ}` as the localisation of the un-localised coefficient
965	`M_{s_œÉ}` away from the spanning element `s_r`.  Direct from
966	`AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule`; this is the per-coefficient input the
967	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` reduction of the section ƒåech complex consumes. -/
968	lemma dToCech_isLocalizedModule {m : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ) :
969	    IsLocalizedModule.Away (s r) (dToCech s M r œÉ) := by
970	  haveI inst : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÉ * s r))
971	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s œÉ)) M) := by
972	    rw [mul_comm]; infer_instance
973	  unfold dToCech
974	  exact comparison_isLocalizedModule (a := sprod s œÉ) (b := s r) _ _ _
975	
976	/-- **Per-coface localisation naturality**: the localised coface `cechCoface`
977	intertwines the per-index comparison `dToCech` with the un-localised coface
978	`dCoface`.  Both composites are away-comparison maps from `M_{s_{œÉ‚àòd‚±º}}` to
979	`M_{s_r ¬∑ s_œÉ}`, hence equal by `comparison_comp`.  This is the square that, summed
980	over the alternating signs, identifies the localised differential
981	`IsLocalizedModule.map (dDiff)` with `CombinatorialCech.depDiff (cechCoface)` ‚Äî
982	the final brick of the `exact_of_isLocalized_span` reduction. -/
983	lemma cechCoface_dToCech {m : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin (m + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) (j : Fin (m + 1))
984	    (x : dCoeff s M (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove)) :
985	    cechCoface s M r m œÉ j (dToCech s M r (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove) x)
986	      = dToCech s M r œÉ (dCoface s M m œÉ j x) := by
987	  simp only [cechCoface, dToCech, dCoface, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
988	  rw [comparison_comp_apply, comparison_comp_apply]
989	  exact Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_trans (sprod_succAbove_dvd s œÉ j) (dvd_mul_left (sprod s œÉ) (s r)))
990	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s œÉ)) M)
991	
992	/-- **Differential-naturality square** (the heart of the `exact_of_isLocalized_span`
993	reduction): localising the un-localised differential `dDiff` at a spanning element
994	`s_r` ‚Äî via the per-index comparisons `dToCech` ‚Äî yields the localised differential
995	`CombinatorialCech.depDiff (cechCoface)`.  Summing the per-coface squares
996	`cechCoface_dToCech` over the alternating signs.  Combined with
997	`dToCech_isLocalizedModule`, `IsLocalizedModule.pi`, and the uniqueness of
998	`IsLocalizedModule.map`, this identifies the localised `dDiff` with the
999	exact complex `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`. -/
1000	lemma dToCech_comm (m : ‚Ñï) (t : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ, dCoeff s M œÉ) :
1001	    CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ œÉ => cechCoeff s M r œÉ) (cechCoface s M r) (m := m)
1002	        (fun œÉ => dToCech s M r œÉ (t œÉ))
1003	      = fun œÉ => dToCech s M r œÉ (dDiff s M m t œÉ) := by
1004	  funext œÉ
1005	  simp only [CombinatorialCech.depDiff, dDiff_apply, map_sum]
1006	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
1007	  rw [map_zsmul, cechCoface_dToCech]

1009	/-! ### Assembly of step (a): positive-degree exactness of `D‚Ä¢`
1010	
1011	The localised differential at a fixed index `r`, bundled as an `R`-linear map
1012	(`locDiff`), so that `IsLocalizedModule.map` uniqueness identifies it with the
1013	localisation of `dDiff`.  Everything here is at a *fixed* index `r : Œπ` (no
1014	`Classical.choose`), hence friction-free; the spanning-element bookkeeping is
1015	confined to the final `dDiff_exact`. -/

1017	/-- R-linear underlying map of the localised coface `cechCoface` (the `comparison`
1018	before `.toAddMonoidHom`). -/
1019	noncomputable def cechCofaceLin (r : Œπ) (m : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (m + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
1020	    cechCoeff s M r (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] cechCoeff s M r œÉ :=
1021	  comparison
1022	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove))) M)
1023	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s œÉ)) M)
1024	    (Inverts.of_dvd (mul_dvd_mul_left (s r) (sprod_succAbove_dvd s œÉ j))
1025	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s œÉ)) M))
1026	
1027	lemma cechCoface_apply (r : Œπ) (m : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (m + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) (j : Fin (m + 1))
1028	    (x : cechCoeff s M r (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove)) :
1029	    cechCoface s M r m œÉ j x = cechCofaceLin s M r m œÉ j x := rfl
1030	
1031	/-- The localised section ƒåech differential at index `r`, as a bundled `R`-linear
1032	map (mirrors `dDiff` with the localised cofaces). -/
1033	noncomputable def locDiff (r : Œπ) (m : ‚Ñï) :
1034	    (‚àÄ œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ, cechCoeff s M r œÉ) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] (‚àÄ œÉ : Fin (m + 1) ‚Üí Œπ, cechCoeff s M r œÉ) :=
1035	  LinearMap.pi fun œÉ => ‚àë j : Fin (m + 1),
1036	    (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (j : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢ (cechCofaceLin s M r m œÉ j ‚àò‚Çó LinearMap.proj (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove))
1037	
1038	lemma locDiff_apply (r : Œπ) (m : ‚Ñï) (t : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ, cechCoeff s M r œÉ)
1039	    (œÉ : Fin (m + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) :
1040	    locDiff s M r m t œÉ
1041	      = ‚àë j : Fin (m + 1),
1042	          (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (j : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢ cechCofaceLin s M r m œÉ j (t (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove)) := by
1043	  rw [locDiff, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.sum_apply]
1044	  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun d _ => rfl

1046	/-- `locDiff` agrees, as a raw function, with the localised combinatorial
1047	differential `CombinatorialCech.depDiff (cechCoface)` already shown exact. -/
1048	lemma locDiff_eq_depDiff (r : Œπ) (m : ‚Ñï) :
1049	    ‚áë(locDiff s M r m)
1050	      = CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ œÉ => cechCoeff s M r œÉ)
1051	          (cechCoface s M r) (m := m) := by
1052	  funext t œÉ
1053	  rw [locDiff_apply]
1054	  simp only [CombinatorialCech.depDiff]
1055	  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => by rw [cechCoface_apply]

1057	/-- Positive-degree exactness of the bundled localised differential, transported
1058	from `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`. -/
1059	lemma locDiff_exact (r : Œπ) (m : ‚Ñï) :
1060	    Function.Exact (locDiff s M r (m + 1)) (locDiff s M r (m + 2)) := by
1061	  rw [locDiff_eq_depDiff, locDiff_eq_depDiff]
1062	  exact CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact s M r m
1063	
1064	/-- The product localisation map `D^m ‚Üí ‚àè_œÉ M_{s_r¬∑s_œÉ}` at index `r`. -/
1065	noncomputable def fLoc (r : Œπ) (m : ‚Ñï) :
1066	    (‚àÄ œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ, dCoeff s M œÉ) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] (‚àÄ œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ, cechCoeff s M r œÉ) :=
1067	  LinearMap.pi fun œÉ => dToCech s M r œÉ ‚àò‚Çó LinearMap.proj œÉ
1068	
1069	lemma fLoc_apply (r : Œπ) (m : ‚Ñï) (t : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ, dCoeff s M œÉ) (œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ) :
1070	    fLoc s M r m t œÉ = dToCech s M r œÉ (t œÉ) := by
1071	  simp only [fLoc, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.proj_apply]

1073	/-- `fLoc` exhibits the product localised coefficient as the localisation of `D^m`
1074	away from `s_r` (from `IsLocalizedModule.pi` + `dToCech_isLocalizedModule`). -/
1075	instance fLoc_isLocalizedModule [Finite Œπ] (r : Œπ) (m : ‚Ñï) :
1076	    IsLocalizedModule.Away (s r) (fLoc s M r m) := by
1077	  haveI : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (s r)) (dToCech s M r œÉ) :=

1098	lemma map_dDiff_eq_locDiff (r : Œπ) (m : ‚Ñï) {a : R}
1099	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m)]
1100	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r (m + 1))] :
1101	    IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m) (fLoc s M r (m + 1))

1103	  apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m)
1104	    (fun x => IsLocalizedModule.map_units (fLoc s M r (m + 1)) x)

1108	/-- A chosen `Œπ`-index realising a spanning-set element as `s i`, kept opaque so

1116	/-- **Step (a): positive-degree exactness of the un-localised section ƒåech module

1124	lemma dDiff_exact [Finite Œπ] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ‚ä§) (m : ‚Ñï) :

1126	  classical
1127	  haveI inst1 : ‚àÄ œÅ : ‚Üë(Set.range s),

1131	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (‚ÜëœÅ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s œÅ) (m + 2)) := fun œÅ => by
1132	    rw [‚Üê spanIdx_spec s œÅ]; infer_instance
1133	  haveI inst3 : ‚àÄ œÅ : ‚Üë(Set.range s),

1135	    rw [‚Üê spanIdx_spec s œÅ]; infer_instance

1153	/-- **Route-B positive-degree exactness of the un-localised section ƒåech module complex
1154	`D‚Ä¢` over a standard *sub*cover of `D(f)`** (`lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover`,
1155	module core).  Here `s = g` need NOT span the unit ideal of `R`; only its image `s/1`
1156	spans the unit ideal of the away ring `R_f` (`hspan`), and each `s i` lies in `‚àö(f)`
1157	(`hmem`).  The complex `‚àè_œÉ M_{s_œÉ}` is then exact in positive degrees.
1158	
1159	Route B (change-of-ring): instantiate `dDiff_exact` over `R_f = Localization.Away f` with
1160	the module `M_f = LocalizedModule (powers f) M` and the spanning family `s/1`, then transport
1161	the resulting positive-degree exactness back to the `R`-side along the degreewise additive
1162	isomorphisms `M_{s_œÉ} ‚âÖ (M_f)_{s_œÉ}` (`AwayComparison.isLocalizedModule_comp_away` + the
1163	universal `IsLocalizedModule.iso`), which intertwine the alternating-sum localisation
1164	differentials. -/
1165	lemma dDiff_exact_of_localizationAway [Finite Œπ] (f : R)
1166	    (hmem : ‚àÄ i, ‚àÉ k, f ‚à£ s i ^ k)
1167	    (hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range (fun i => algebraMap R (Localization.Away f) (s i))) = ‚ä§)
1168	    (m : ‚Ñï) :
1169	    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2)) := by
1170	  classical
1171	  set Rf := Localization.Away f with hRf
1172	  set Mf := LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M with hMf
1173	  set g' : Œπ ‚Üí Rf := fun i => algebraMap R Rf (s i) with hg'
1174	  -- `R_f`-side positive-degree exactness (the black-box reuse of `dDiff_exact`).
1175	  have Hf : Function.Exact (dDiff g' Mf (m + 1)) (dDiff g' Mf (m + 2)) :=
1176	    dDiff_exact g' Mf hspan m
1177	  -- `sprod g' œÉ` is the image of `sprod s œÉ`.
1178	  have hsprod : ‚àÄ {n : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin n ‚Üí Œπ), sprod g' œÉ = algebraMap R Rf (sprod s œÉ) := by
1179	    intro n œÉ; simp only [g', sprod, map_prod]
1180	  -- per (nonempty) `œÉ`: `f` divides a power of `sprod s œÉ`.
1181	  have hdvd : ‚àÄ {n : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ), ‚àÉ k, f ‚à£ sprod s œÉ ^ k := by
1182	    intro n œÉ
1183	    obtain ‚ü®k, hk‚ü© := hmem (œÉ 0)
1184	    refine ‚ü®k, ?_‚ü©
1185	    have he : sprod s œÉ ^ k = s (œÉ 0) ^ k * (‚àè x : Fin n, s (œÉ x.succ)) ^ k := by
1186	      rw [sprod, Fin.prod_univ_succ, mul_pow]
1187	    rw [he]; exact hk.mul_right _
1188	  -- the composite localisation structure map `M ‚Üí M_f ‚Üí (M_f)_{s_œÉ}` localises `M` at `s_œÉ`.
1189	  have inst_comp : ‚àÄ {n : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ),
1190	      IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÉ))
1191	        ((LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod g' œÉ)) Mf).restrictScalars R ‚àò‚Çó
1192	          LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
1193	    intro n œÉ
1194	    obtain ‚ü®k, h, hh‚ü© := hdvd œÉ
1195	    haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rf (sprod s œÉ)))
1196	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod g' œÉ)) Mf) := by
1197	      rw [‚Üê hsprod œÉ]; infer_instance
1198	    exact AwayComparison.isLocalizedModule_comp_away f (sprod s œÉ) h hh
1199	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) M)
1200	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod g' œÉ)) Mf)
1201	  -- the per-œÉ `R`-linear comparison `(M_f)_{s_œÉ} ‚âÖ M_{s_œÉ}` (Rf-side ‚Üí R-side).
1202	  let eœÉL : ‚àÄ {n : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ), dCoeff g' Mf œÉ ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] dCoeff s M œÉ :=
1203	    fun {n} œÉ =>
1204	      haveI := inst_comp œÉ
1205	      (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÉ))
1206	        ((LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod g' œÉ)) Mf).restrictScalars R ‚àò‚Çó
1207	          LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) M)).symm
1208	  -- `eœÉL œÑ` undoes the composite structure map: it sends `compMap œÑ mval ‚Ü¶ mk_{s_œÑ} mval`.
1209	  have heœÉL : ‚àÄ {n : ‚Ñï} (œÑ : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) (mval : M),
1210	      eœÉL œÑ ((LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod g' œÑ)) Mf)
1211	          (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) M mval))
1212	        = LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÑ)) M mval := by
1213	    intro n œÑ mval
1214	    haveI := inst_comp œÑ
1215	    exact DFunLike.congr_fun (IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_comp (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÑ))
1216	      ((LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod g' œÑ)) Mf).restrictScalars R ‚àò‚Çó
1217	        LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) M)) mval

1220	      (z : dCoeff g' Mf (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove)),

1223	    intro n œÉ j z
1224	    haveI := inst_comp (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove)

1228	      apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers (sprod s (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove)))
1229	        (LinearMap.restrictScalars R
1230	            (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod g' (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove))) Mf)
1231	          ‚àò‚Çó LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) M)
1232	        (fun x => (AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd s œÉ j)
1233	          (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÉ)) M)).isUnit_powers x)
1234	      apply LinearMap.ext; intro mval
1235	      simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars, Function.comp_apply,
1236	        LinearEquiv.coe_coe]
1237	      -- LHS
1238	      have hL : dCoface g' Mf (n + 1) œÉ j
1239	            ((LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod g' (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove))) Mf)
1240	              (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) M mval))

1242	              (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) M mval) := by
1243	        simp only [dCoface]

1245	      rw [hL, heœÉL œÉ mval, heœÉL (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove) mval]
1246	      -- RHS
1247	      simp only [dCoface]
1248	      exact (AwayComparison.comparison_apply _ _ _ mval).symm
1249	    exact DFunLike.congr_fun key z
1250	  -- bundle the per-œÉ comparisons into product `AddEquiv`s.
1251	  let E : (n : ‚Ñï) ‚Üí ((œÉ : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí dCoeff g' Mf œÉ) ‚âÉ+
1252	      ((œÉ : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí dCoeff s M œÉ) :=
1253	    fun n => AddEquiv.piCongrRight (fun œÉ => (eœÉL œÉ).toAddEquiv)

1258	    intro r

1265	    rw [dDiff_apply, dDiff_apply, map_sum]
1266	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun j _ => ?_)
1267	    rw [map_zsmul, nat]
1268	  exact Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact (E m) (E (m + 1)) (E (m + 2))
1269	    (sq m) (sq (m + 1)) Hf

module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.DerivedCategory.Ext.ExactSequences
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Biproducts
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.MayerVietorisSquare
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafCohomology.Basic

/-!
# The Mayer-Vietoris exact sequence in sheaf cohomology

Let `C` be a category equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J`.
Let `S : J.MayerVietorisSquare` be a Mayer-Vietoris square for `J`.
Let `F` be an abelian sheaf on `(C, J)`.

In this file, we obtain a long exact Mayer-Vietoris sequence:

`... ‚ü∂ H^n(S.X‚ÇÑ, F) ‚ü∂ H^n(S.X‚ÇÇ, F) ‚äû H^n(S.X‚ÇÉ, F) ‚ü∂ H^n(S.X‚ÇÅ, F) ‚ü∂ H^{n + 1}(S.X‚ÇÑ, F) ‚ü∂ ...`

-/

@[expose] public section

universe w v u

namespace CategoryTheory

open Category Opposite Limits Abelian

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
  [HasWeakSheafify J (Type v)] [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
  [HasExt.{w} (Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v})]

namespace GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare

variable (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v})

/-- The sum of two restriction maps in sheaf cohomology. -/
noncomputable def toBiprod (n : ‚Ñï) :
    F.H' n S.X‚ÇÑ ‚ü∂ F.H' n S.X‚ÇÇ ‚äû F.H' n S.X‚ÇÉ :=
  biprod.lift ((F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f‚ÇÇ‚ÇÑ.op)
      ((F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f‚ÇÉ‚ÇÑ.op)

lemma toBiprod_apply {n : ‚Ñï} (y : F.H' n S.X‚ÇÑ) :
    S.toBiprod F n y = (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv
      ‚ü®(F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f‚ÇÇ‚ÇÑ.op y,
        (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f‚ÇÉ‚ÇÑ.op y‚ü© := by
  apply (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.injective
  dsimp [toBiprod]
  ext
  ¬∑ rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
      Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
      ‚Üê AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_fst_apply,
      Iso.hom_inv_id_apply, ‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
      biprod.lift_fst, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
  ¬∑ rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
      Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
      ‚Üê AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_snd_apply,
      Iso.hom_inv_id_apply, ‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
      biprod.lift_snd, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]

/-- The difference of two restriction maps in sheaf cohomology. -/
noncomputable def fromBiprod (n : ‚Ñï) :
    F.H' n S.X‚ÇÇ ‚äû F.H' n S.X‚ÇÉ ‚ü∂ F.H' n S.X‚ÇÅ :=
  biprod.desc ((F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ.op)
      (-(F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f‚ÇÅ‚ÇÉ.op)

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma toBiprod_fromBiprod (n : ‚Ñï) : S.toBiprod F n ‚â´ S.fromBiprod F n = 0 := by
  simp only [toBiprod, fromBiprod, biprod.lift_desc, Preadditive.comp_neg,
    ‚Üê sub_eq_add_neg, sub_eq_zero, ‚Üê Functor.map_comp, ‚Üê op_comp, S.toSquare.fac]

lemma fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply {n : ‚Ñï}
    (y‚ÇÅ : F.H' n S.X‚ÇÇ) (y‚ÇÇ : F.H' n S.X‚ÇÉ) :
    S.fromBiprod F n ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv ‚ü®y‚ÇÅ, y‚ÇÇ‚ü©) =
      (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ.op y‚ÇÅ - (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f‚ÇÅ‚ÇÉ.op y‚ÇÇ := by
  dsimp [fromBiprod]
  rw [‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
  simp [AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_desc, sub_eq_add_neg]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
attribute [local simp] toBiprod_apply in
lemma biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply
    {n : ‚Ñï} (x : F.H' n S.X‚ÇÑ) :
    Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).hom (S.toBiprod F n x)) =
      (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.shortComplex.g).comp x (zero_add n) :=
  Ext.biprodAddEquiv.injective (by cat_disch)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
attribute [local simp] sub_eq_add_neg in
lemma mk‚ÇÄ_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom
    {n : ‚Ñï} (x : ‚Üë(F.H' n S.X‚ÇÇ ‚äû F.H' n S.X‚ÇÉ)) :
    (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.shortComplex.f).comp
      (Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).hom x)) (zero_add n) =
    (S.fromBiprod F n x) := by
  obtain ‚ü®‚ü®x‚ÇÇ, x‚ÇÉ‚ü©, rfl‚ü© :=
    (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.symm.surjective x
  dsimp
  rw [Ext.biprodAddEquiv_symm_apply,
    Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_symm_apply,
    fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply]
  cat_disch

variable (n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñï) (h : n‚ÇÄ + 1 = n‚ÇÅ)

/-- The connecting homomorphism of the Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence
in sheaf cohomology. -/
noncomputable def Œ¥ :
    F.H' n‚ÇÄ S.X‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ F.H' n‚ÇÅ S.X‚ÇÑ :=
  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (S.shortComplex_shortExact.extClass.precomp _ (by omega))

open ComposableArrows

/-- The Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence of an abelian sheaf `F : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat`
for a Mayer-Vietoris square `S : J.MayerVietorisSquare`. -/
noncomputable abbrev sequence : ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat.{w} 5 :=
  mk‚ÇÖ (S.toBiprod F n‚ÇÄ) (S.fromBiprod F n‚ÇÄ) (S.Œ¥ F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h)
    (S.toBiprod F n‚ÇÅ) (S.fromBiprod F n‚ÇÅ)

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- Comparison isomorphism from the Mayer-Vietoris sequence and the
contravariant sequence of `Ext`-groups. -/
noncomputable def sequenceIso : S.sequence F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h ‚âÖ
    Ext.contravariantSequence S.shortComplex_shortExact F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ (by omega) :=
  isoMk‚ÇÖ (Iso.refl _)
    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans (Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
    (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _)
    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans (Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
    (Iso.refl _)
    (by ext; apply biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
    (by ext; symm; apply mk‚ÇÄ_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
    (by dsimp; rw [comp_id, id_comp]; rfl)
    (by ext; apply biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
    (by ext; symm; apply mk‚ÇÄ_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)

lemma sequence_exact : (S.sequence F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h).Exact :=
  exact_of_iso (S.sequenceIso F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h).symm (Ext.contravariantSequence_exact _ _ _ _ _)

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma Œ¥_toBiprod : S.Œ¥ F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h ‚â´ S.toBiprod F n‚ÇÅ = 0 :=
  (S.sequence_exact F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h).zero 2

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma fromBiprod_Œ¥ : S.fromBiprod F n‚ÇÄ ‚â´ S.Œ¥ F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h = 0 :=
  (S.sequence_exact F n‚ÇÄ n‚ÇÅ h).zero 1

end GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare

end CategoryTheory

/-

module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.DerivedCategory.Ext.EnoughInjectives
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.GrothendieckCategory.EnoughInjectives

/-!
# Ext in Grothendieck abelian categories

Let `C : Type u` be an abelian category (with `Category.{v} C`).
If `C` is a Grothendieck abelian category relatively to a universe `w`,
the morphisms in `C` must be `w`-small, and as `C` has enough injectives,
the `Ext`-groups are also `w`-small. If `C` is a nonzero category,
it is possible to show that any `w`-small type `T` injects into a type
of morphisms in `C` (consider the various inclusions
`X ‚ü∂ ‚àê (fun (_ : T) ‚Ü¶ X)` for a nonzero object `X : C`). It follows that it
is reasonable to think that the universe `w` is unique, and is the best
choice for the universe where the `Ext`-groups in `C` should be defined.

In this situation, we make `HasExt.{w} C` an instance.
As a result, when `X` and `Y` are objects in `C` and `n : ‚Ñï`,
we have `Ext X Y n : Type w`.

-/

public section

namespace CategoryTheory

universe w v u

instance IsGrothendieckAbelian.hasExt
    (C : Type u) [Category.{v} C] [Abelian C] [IsGrothendieckAbelian.{w} C] :

30	/-- The cosimplicial morphism of section ƒåech objects induced by a morphism `œÜ` of
31	presheaves of modules, acting coordinatewise by the underlying presheaf morphism on each
32	basic-open section group. Project-local: `sectionCechCosimplicial` has no functoriality in
33	Mathlib. -/
34	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicialMap {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
35	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (œÜ : F ‚ü∂ G) :
36	    sectionCechCosimplicial U F ‚ü∂ sectionCechCosimplicial U G where
37	  app n := Limits.Pi.map (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
38	    ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œÜ).app (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
39	  naturality {m n} f := by
40	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
41	    intro œÉ
42	    simp only [sectionCechCosimplicial, Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.map_œÄ, Limits.Pi.lift_œÄ,
43	      Limits.Pi.map_œÄ_assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_œÄ_assoc]
44	    congr 1
45	    exact ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œÜ).naturality _

47	/-- The section ƒåech cosimplicial object as a functor in the coefficient presheaf of modules.
48	Project-local: packages `sectionCechCosimplicialMap` with functoriality so the induced
49	short-complex maps compose and respect zero. -/
50	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor {Œπ : Type u}
51	    (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.PresheafOfModules ‚•§ CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
52	  obj F := sectionCechCosimplicial U F
53	  map œÜ := sectionCechCosimplicialMap U œÜ
54	  map_id F := by
55	    apply NatTrans.ext
56	    funext n
57	    show (Limits.Pi.map fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
58	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (ùüô F)).app
59	        (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) = ùüô _
60	    rw [show (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
61	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (ùüô F)).app
62	          (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
63	      = (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
64	        ùüô ((PresheafOfModules.presheaf F).obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))) from by
65	        funext œÉ; rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]; rfl]
66	    exact Limits.Pi.map_id
67	  map_comp œÜ œà := by
68	    apply NatTrans.ext
69	    funext n
70	    show (Limits.Pi.map fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
71	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (œÜ ‚â´ œà)).app
72	        (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) = _
73	    rw [show (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
74	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (œÜ ‚â´ œà)).app
75	          (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
76	      = (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
77	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œÜ).app (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))) ‚â´
78	          ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œà).app
79	            (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) from by funext œÉ; rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]; rfl]
80	    exact (Limits.Pi.map_comp_map _ _).symm

82	/-- The section ƒåech cochain complex as a functor in the coefficient presheaf of modules.
83	Project-local: functoriality of `sectionCechComplex`, used to form the short exact sequence of
84	ƒåech complexes. -/
85	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexFunctor {Œπ : Type u}
86	    (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.PresheafOfModules ‚•§ CochainComplex Ab.{u} ‚Ñï :=
87	  sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor U ‚ãô AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}
88	
89	/-- The chain map of section ƒåech complexes induced by a morphism `œÜ` of presheaves of
90	modules. Project-local: functoriality of `sectionCechComplex` in the coefficient presheaf. -/
91	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexMap {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
92	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (œÜ : F ‚ü∂ G) :
93	    sectionCechComplex U F ‚ü∂ sectionCechComplex U G :=
94	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).map (sectionCechCosimplicialMap U œÜ)
95	
96	/-- **{\v C}ech cohomology accessor** `»û^p(ùí∞, F)`: the degree-`p` homology of the section
97	ƒåech complex, packaged as a named `Ab`-object. Project-local thin wrapper so the 01EO chain
98	can refer to `»û^p` uniformly. -/
99	noncomputable def cechCohomology {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
100	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ‚Ñï) : Ab.{u} :=
101	  (sectionCechComplex U F).homology p

103	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî product of short exact sequences in `Ab` -/
104	
105	private lemma pi_œÄ_map_apply {J : Type u} {f g : J ‚Üí Ab.{u}} (œÜ : ‚àÄ j, f j ‚ü∂ g j)
106	    (x : ToType (‚àè·∂ú f)) (œÉ : J) :
107	    Limits.Pi.œÄ g œÉ (Limits.Pi.map œÜ x) = œÜ œÉ (Limits.Pi.œÄ f œÉ x) := by
108	  rw [‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, Limits.Pi.map_œÄ, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
109	
110	/-- **A product of short exact sequences of abelian groups is short exact.** This is the AB4*
111	content used in the section ƒåech short-exact-sequence: each ƒåech term is a product of section
112	groups over basic opens, so degreewise short-exactness of the ƒåech complexes reduces to this.
113	Project-local: Mathlib has no off-the-shelf "product of short exact sequences" lemma. -/
114	theorem shortExact_piMap {J : Type u} (S : J ‚Üí ShortComplex Ab.{u}) (h : ‚àÄ j, (S j).ShortExact) :
115	    (ShortComplex.mk (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).f)) (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).g))
116	      (by rw [Limits.Pi.map_comp_map]
117	          have hz : (fun j => (S j).f ‚â´ (S j).g) = (fun j => (0 : (S j).X‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ (S j).X‚ÇÉ)) := by
118	            funext j; exact (S j).zero
119	          rw [hz]; ext x; simp)).ShortExact := by
120	  haveI : ‚àÄ j, Mono (S j).f := fun j => (h j).mono_f
121	  -- Epi of the product map: surjectivity, chosen componentwise
122	  have hepi : Epi (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).g)) := by
123	    rw [AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective]
124	    intro y
125	    refine ‚ü®(Concrete.productEquiv (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÇ)).symm
126	      (fun œÉ => ((h œÉ).ab_surjective_g (Limits.Pi.œÄ (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÉ) œÉ y)).choose), ?_‚ü©
127	    refine (Concrete.productEquiv (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÉ)).injective (funext fun œÉ => ?_)
128	    rw [Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply, Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply,
129	      pi_œÄ_map_apply, Concrete.productEquiv_symm_apply_œÄ]
130	    exact ((h œÉ).ab_surjective_g (Limits.Pi.œÄ (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÉ) œÉ y)).choose_spec
131	  haveI := hepi
132	  haveI hmono : Mono (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).f)) := inferInstance
133	  refine ShortComplex.ShortExact.mk ?_
134	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact]
135	  show Function.Exact (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).f)) (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).g))
136	  intro x
137	  constructor
138	  ¬∑ intro hx
139	    have hcomp : ‚àÄ œÉ, (S œÉ).g (Limits.Pi.œÄ (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÇ) œÉ x) = 0 := by
140	      intro œÉ
141	      have := congrArg (Limits.Pi.œÄ (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÉ) œÉ) hx
142	      rwa [pi_œÄ_map_apply, map_zero] at this
143	    choose w hw using fun œÉ => (((ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact (S œÉ)).mp
144	      (h œÉ).exact (Limits.Pi.œÄ (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÇ) œÉ x)).mp
145	      (hcomp œÉ) : Limits.Pi.œÄ (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÇ) œÉ x ‚àà Set.range (S œÉ).f)
146	    refine ‚ü®(Concrete.productEquiv (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÅ)).symm w, ?_‚ü©
147	    refine (Concrete.productEquiv (fun j => (S j).X‚ÇÇ)).injective (funext fun œÉ => ?_)
148	    rw [Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply, Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply,
149	      pi_œÄ_map_apply, Concrete.productEquiv_symm_apply_œÄ]
150	    exact hw œÉ
151	  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®w, rfl‚ü©
152	    rw [‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, Limits.Pi.map_comp_map]
153	    have hz : (fun j => (S j).f ‚â´ (S j).g) = (fun j => (0 : (S j).X‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ (S j).X‚ÇÉ)) := by
154	      funext j; exact (S j).zero
155	    rw [hz, show (Limits.Pi.map fun j => (0 : (S j).X‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂ (S j).X‚ÇÉ)) = 0 from by ext z; simp]
156	    rfl
