158	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî L1 short exact sequence of section ƒåech complexes -/
159	
160	/-- The degree-`p`, index-`œÉ` term short complex of section groups over the basic open
161	`‚®Ö‚Çñ U(œÉ k)` induced by a short complex `P` of presheaves of modules. Project-local: the
162	building block of the section ƒåech short exact sequence. -/
163	noncomputable def faceShortComplex {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
164	    (P : ShortComplex X.PresheafOfModules) {p : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) : ShortComplex Ab.{u} :=
165	  ShortComplex.mk
166	    (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map P.f).app (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
167	    (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map P.g).app (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
168	    (by rw [‚Üê NatTrans.comp_app, ‚Üê CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, P.zero,
169	          CategoryTheory.Functor.map_zero]; rfl)
170	
171	/-- The short complex of section ƒåech cochain complexes `0 ‚Üí ƒå(F) ‚Üí ƒå(I) ‚Üí ƒå(Q) ‚Üí 0` induced by
172	a short complex `P : 0 ‚Üí F ‚Üí I ‚Üí Q ‚Üí 0` of presheaves of modules. Project-local: Mathlib has no
173	section ƒåech complex, hence no short complex of them. -/
174	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexShortComplex {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
175	    (P : ShortComplex X.PresheafOfModules) : ShortComplex (CochainComplex Ab.{u} ‚Ñï) :=
176	  ShortComplex.mk (sectionCechComplexMap U P.f) (sectionCechComplexMap U P.g) (by
177	    apply HomologicalComplex.hom_ext
178	    intro i
179	    rw [HomologicalComplex.comp_f, HomologicalComplex.zero_f]
180	    show (Limits.Pi.map fun œÉ : Fin (i + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
181	        (faceShortComplex U P œÉ).f) ‚â´ (Limits.Pi.map fun œÉ : Fin (i + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
182	        (faceShortComplex U P œÉ).g) = 0
183	    rw [Limits.Pi.map_comp_map,
184	      show (fun œÉ : Fin (i + 1) ‚Üí Œπ => (faceShortComplex U P œÉ).f ‚â´ (faceShortComplex U P œÉ).g)
185	        = (fun œÉ : Fin (i + 1) ‚Üí Œπ => (0 : (faceShortComplex U P œÉ).X‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂
186	            (faceShortComplex U P œÉ).X‚ÇÉ)) from by funext œÉ; exact (faceShortComplex U P œÉ).zero,
187	      show (Limits.Pi.map fun œÉ : Fin (i + 1) ‚Üí Œπ => (0 : (faceShortComplex U P œÉ).X‚ÇÅ ‚ü∂
188	          (faceShortComplex U P œÉ).X‚ÇÉ)) = 0 from by ext z; simp])
189	
190	/-- **Short exact sequence of section ƒåech complexes from a basis (Stacks 01EO L1).** Given a
191	short complex `P` of presheaves of modules whose section sequences over every basic open
192	`‚®Ö‚Çñ U(œÉ k)` are short exact (left-exactness from the sheaf sequence plus the basis surjectivity
193	of `ses_cech_h1`), the induced section ƒåech complexes form a short exact sequence. Project-local:
194	this is the term-wise-product step of the 01EO proof; degreewise it is a product of the
195	basic-open section short exact sequences. -/
196	theorem cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
197	    (P : ShortComplex X.PresheafOfModules)
198	    (hface : ‚àÄ (p : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ), (faceShortComplex U P œÉ).ShortExact) :
199	    (sectionCechComplexShortComplex U P).ShortExact := by
200	  apply HomologicalComplex.shortExact_of_degreewise_shortExact
201	  intro i
202	  exact shortExact_piMap (fun œÉ : Fin (i + 1) ‚Üí Œπ => faceShortComplex U P œÉ) (hface i)

204	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî L2 homological core (quotient vanishing) -/
205	
206	/-- **Quotient preserves vanishing higher cohomology (homological core, Stacks 01EO L2).**
207	Given a short exact sequence of cochain complexes `0 ‚Üí X‚ÇÅ ‚Üí X‚ÇÇ ‚Üí X‚ÇÉ ‚Üí 0` in which the middle
208	term `X‚ÇÇ` has vanishing positive homology (the injective/acyclic term) and the left term `X‚ÇÅ`
209	has vanishing positive homology (condition (3)), the right term `X‚ÇÉ` (the quotient) again has
210	vanishing positive homology. Proved by the homology long exact sequence: the connecting map
211	gives `H·µñ(X‚ÇÉ) ‚âÖ H·µñ‚Å∫¬π(X‚ÇÅ) = 0`. Project-local: the 01EO dimension-shift needs this abstract
212	shape; it is then instantiated at the section ƒåech complexes. -/
213	theorem cechHomology_quotient_vanishing (T : ShortComplex (CochainComplex Ab.{u} ‚Ñï))
214	    (hT : T.ShortExact) (hI : ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (T.X‚ÇÇ.homology p))
215	    (hF : ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (T.X‚ÇÅ.homology p)) :
216	    ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (T.X‚ÇÉ.homology p) := by
217	  intro p hp
218	  have hrel : (ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï).Rel p (p + 1) := rfl
219	  have hiso : T.X‚ÇÉ.homology p ‚âÖ T.X‚ÇÅ.homology (p + 1) :=
220	    hT.Œ¥Iso p (p + 1) hrel (hI p hp) (hI (p + 1) (Nat.succ_pos p))
221	  exact (hF (p + 1) (Nat.succ_pos p)).of_iso hiso
222	
223	/-- **Quotient preserves vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology (Stacks 01EO L2).** Concrete form in
224	terms of `cechCohomology`: with the section ƒåech short exact sequence of `P` (from L1), if the
225	middle term `I` (injective/acyclic, condition from `injective_cech_acyclic`) and the left term
226	`F` (condition (3)) both have vanishing positive ƒåech cohomology, then so does the quotient `Q`.
227	Project-local: instantiates `cechHomology_quotient_vanishing` at the section ƒåech complexes. -/
228	theorem quotient_cech_vanishing_of_basis {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
229	    (P : ShortComplex X.PresheafOfModules)
230	    (hSES : (sectionCechComplexShortComplex U P).ShortExact)
231	    (hI : ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (cechCohomology U P.X‚ÇÇ p))
232	    (hF : ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (cechCohomology U P.X‚ÇÅ p)) :
233	    ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (cechCohomology U P.X‚ÇÉ p) :=
234	  cechHomology_quotient_vanishing (sectionCechComplexShortComplex U P) hSES hI hF

236	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî per-face short exact sequence (01EO) -/
237	
238	/-- The sections functor `Œì(V, -) : X.Modules ‚•§ Ab` over a fixed open `V`, as the composite of
239	the inclusion of sheaves of modules into presheaves of modules, the forgetful to presheaves of
240	abelian groups, and evaluation at `V`. Project-local: used only to package the left-exactness of
241	sections (each factor preserves finite limits) for the per-face short exact sequence. -/
242	noncomputable def sectionsFunctor (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.Modules ‚•§ Ab.{u} :=
243	  Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X ‚ãô PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ‚ãô
244	    (CategoryTheory.evaluation _ _).obj (Opposite.op V)
245	
246	/-- **Per-face short exact sequence from a sheaf short exact sequence (Stacks 01EO).** Given a
247	short exact sequence `S : 0 ‚Üí F ‚Üí I ‚Üí Q ‚Üí 0` of sheaves of `O_X`-modules and the assumption that
248	on every face `V_œÉ = ‚®Ö‚Çñ U(œÉ k)` the section map `I(V_œÉ) ‚Üí Q(V_œÉ)` is surjective, the per-face
249	short complex `faceShortComplex U (S.map toPresheafOfModules) œÉ` is short exact. Mono and middle
250	exactness come from left-exactness of the sections functor (`sectionsFunctor` preserves finite
251	limits); the epi is the surjectivity hypothesis (supplied downstream by `ses_cech_h1`).
252	Project-local: produces the per-face hypothesis consumed by `cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis`. -/
253	theorem faceShortComplex_shortExact_of_sheaf_ses {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
254	    (S : ShortComplex X.Modules) (hS : S.ShortExact)
255	    (hsurj : ‚àÄ (p : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ), Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
256	      (faceShortComplex U (S.map (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X)) œÉ).g))
257	    (p : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) :
258	    (faceShortComplex U (S.map (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X)) œÉ).ShortExact := by
259	  haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
260	    unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
261	  haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))) := by
262	    unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
263	  haveI : Mono S.f := hS.mono_f
264	  change (S.map (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))).ShortExact
265	  have hex : (S.map (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))).Exact :=
266	    ShortComplex.Exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel hS.exact
267	      (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))) hS.mono_f inferInstance
268	  haveI : Mono (S.map (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))).f :=
269	    inferInstanceAs (Mono ((sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))).map S.f))
270	  haveI : Epi (S.map (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))).g := by
271	    rw [AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective]
272	    exact hsurj p œÉ
273	  exact ShortComplex.ShortExact.mk hex

275	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî L3 base case `H¬π(U, F) = 0` -/
276	
277	/-- **Sheaf-cohomology base case `H¬π(U, F) = 0` (Stacks 01EO L3).** Given a short exact
278	sequence `S : 0 ‚Üí F ‚Üí I ‚Üí Q ‚Üí 0` in `X.Modules` with `I = S.X‚ÇÇ` injective, and assuming the
279	section map `I(U) ‚Üí Q(U)` is surjective, the first absolute cohomology `H¬π(U, F) = Ext¬π(jShriekOU
280	U, F)` vanishes. The proof runs the covariant Ext long exact sequence: `H¬π(U, I) = 0` by injective
281	vanishing, and surjectivity of the section map transfers (via the natural `H‚Å∞ ‚âÖ Œì`) to
282	surjectivity of `H‚Å∞(U, I) ‚Üí H‚Å∞(U, Q)`, killing the connecting map. Project-local: assembles the
283	`AbsoluteCohomology` Ext wrappers into the 01EO base case. -/
284	theorem absoluteCohomology_one_eq_zero_of_basis (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
285	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) [Injective S.X‚ÇÇ]
286	    (hsurj : Function.Surjective
287	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map S.g).app (Opposite.op U))))
288	    (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X‚ÇÅ 1) : e = 0 := by
289	  have hef : e.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero 1) = 0 :=
290	    absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective 0 U S.X‚ÇÇ _
291	  obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÉ, hx‚ÇÉ‚ü© := absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact‚ÇÅ U hS e hef (rfl : 0 + 1 = 1)
292	  have hgsurj : Function.Surjective
293	      (fun y : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X‚ÇÇ 0 => y.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.g) (add_zero 0)) := by
294	    intro z
295	    obtain ‚ü®i, hi‚ü© := hsurj (absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U S.X‚ÇÉ z)
296	    refine ‚ü®(absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U S.X‚ÇÇ).symm i, ?_‚ü©
297	    apply (absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U S.X‚ÇÉ).injective
298	    rw [absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv_naturality, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply, hi]
299	  obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÇ, hx‚ÇÇ‚ü© := hgsurj x‚ÇÉ
300	  rw [‚Üê hx‚ÇÉ, ‚Üê hx‚ÇÇ,
301	    Ext.comp_assoc x‚ÇÇ (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.g) hS.extClass (add_zero 0) (rfl : 0 + 1 = 1) rfl,
302	    hS.comp_extClass, Ext.comp_zero]

304	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî cover system on a basis (01EO conditions 1‚Äì2) -/
305	
306	/-- A **covering datum** on `X`: an index family of opens (the covered open is `‚®Ü i, c.2 i`).
307	Project-local: the lightweight cover representation matching the cover-local ƒåech lemmas
308	(`cechCohomology`, `faceShortComplex`), which are indexed by a family `Œπ ‚Üí Opens X`. -/
309	abbrev CovDatum (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type (u + 1) :=
310	  Œ£ Œπ : Type u, Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X
311	
312	/-- **Cover system on a basis** (Stacks 01EO conditions (1)‚Äì(2)). Records a basis `B`, a set of
313	admissible coverings `Cov`, the faces-in-basis datum (every finite intersection of a covering's
314	opens lies in `B`, condition (1)), and the cofinality datum in the form it is consumed ‚Äî
315	condition (2) packaged as the section-surjectivity it produces through `ses_cech_h1`: for every
316	short exact sequence `S` of `O_X`-modules whose left term has vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology,
317	the section map of `S.g` is surjective over every basic open. The auxiliary `injective_acyclic`
318	field records the standard-cover injective ƒåech-acyclicity (Stacks
319	`lemma-injective-trivial-cech`, i.e. `injective_cech_acyclic`) for the system's coverings.
320	Project-local: bespoke encoding; the two sheaf-theoretic fields are discharged at the affine
321	(02KG) instantiation by `ses_cech_h1` + standard-cover cofinality and by `injective_cech_acyclic`
322	respectively. -/
323	structure BasisCovSystem (X : Scheme.{u}) where
324	  /-- The basis of opens. -/
325	  B : Set (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
326	  /-- The set of admissible coverings. -/
327	  Cov : Set (CovDatum X)
328	  /-- Condition (1): every finite intersection of a covering's opens lies in the basis. -/
329	  faces_mem : ‚àÄ c ‚àà Cov, ‚àÄ (p : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí c.1), (‚®Ö k, c.2 (œÉ k)) ‚àà B
330	  /-- Condition (2), in the section-surjectivity shape produced by `ses_cech_h1` + cofinality. -/
331	  surj_of_vanishing : ‚àÄ (S : ShortComplex X.Modules), S.ShortExact ‚Üí
332	    (‚àÄ c ‚àà Cov, ‚àÄ q, 0 < q ‚Üí
333	      IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj S.X‚ÇÅ) q)) ‚Üí
334	    ‚àÄ V ‚àà B, Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
335	      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map
336	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map S.g)).app (Opposite.op V)))
337	  /-- Injective ƒåech-acyclicity for the system's coverings (`injective_cech_acyclic`). -/
338	  injective_acyclic : ‚àÄ (I : X.Modules), Injective I ‚Üí ‚àÄ c ‚àà Cov, ‚àÄ q, 0 < q ‚Üí
339	    IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I) q)
340	
341	/-- **Vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology for a cover system** (Stacks 01EO condition (3)). An
342	`O_X`-module `F` satisfies this when `»û·µñ(ùí∞, F) = 0` for every covering `ùí∞ ‚àà s.Cov` and every
343	`p > 0`. Stated for an arbitrary module (not necessarily quasi-coherent): the dimension-shift
344	induction feeds the quotient `Q = I/F` back as coefficient, and `Q` need not be quasi-coherent.
345	Project-local: the inductive predicate of the 01EO argument. -/
346	@[reducible] def HasVanishingHigherCech (s : BasisCovSystem X) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
347	  ‚àÄ c ‚àà s.Cov, ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí
348	    IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) p)

350	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî L4 dimension-shift induction -/
351	
352	-- The dimension-shift induction embeds modules into injectives, which needs
353	-- `EnoughInjectives X.Modules`. That instance is NOT available in Mathlib for sheaves of modules
354	-- (it would follow from `IsGrothendieckAbelian (SheafOfModules R)`, which is absent ‚Äî see
355	-- `analogies/p5a.md`), so ‚Äî following the project convention for the P5a derived-functor lane ‚Äî
356	-- it is carried as an explicit hypothesis on `injSES`/L4 rather than synthesized.
357	
358	/-- The injective-embedding short exact sequence `0 ‚Üí F ‚Üí I ‚Üí I/F ‚Üí 0` of an `O_X`-module `F`,
359	with `I = Injective.under F` the chosen injective and `I/F` its cokernel. Project-local helper for
360	the 01EO dimension-shift induction; carries `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` as a hypothesis. -/
361	@[reducible] private noncomputable def injSES [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (F : X.Modules) :
362	    ShortComplex X.Modules :=
363	  ShortComplex.mk (Injective.Œπ F) (cokernel.œÄ (Injective.Œπ F)) (cokernel.condition _)
364	
365	private lemma injSES_shortExact [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (F : X.Modules) :
366	    (injSES F).ShortExact :=
367	  ShortComplex.ShortExact.mk (ShortComplex.exact_cokernel _)
368	
369	/-- **Dimension-shift induction `H·µñ(U, F) = 0` for `p > 0` (Stacks 01EO L4).** For a cover system
370	`s` on a basis and any `O_X`-module `F` with vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology for `s`, the
371	absolute cohomology `H·µñ(U, F) = Ext·µñ(jShriekOU U, F)` vanishes for every basic open `U ‚àà s.B` and
372	`p > 0`. Induction on `p`, quantified over all `F` in the inductive class: the quotient
373	`Q = I/F` re-enters the class (via the section ƒåech SES of L1 from the per-face SES, plus L2
374	quotient-vanishing and the cover system's injective acyclicity), the base case is L3, and the
375	inductive step is the covariant Ext long exact sequence. Project-local: the 01EO inductive core. -/
376	theorem absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (s : BasisCovSystem X)
377	    {F : X.Modules} (hF : HasVanishingHigherCech s F)
378	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X} (hU : U ‚àà s.B) {p : ‚Ñï} (hp : 0 < p)
379	    (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F p) : e = 0 := by
380	  have key : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (F : X.Modules), HasVanishingHigherCech s F ‚Üí
381	      ‚àÄ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X), U ‚àà s.B ‚Üí
382	        ‚àÄ e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F (n + 1), e = 0 := by
383	    intro n
384	    induction n with
385	    | zero =>
386	      intro F hF U hU e
387	      have hS := injSES_shortExact F
388	      haveI : Injective (injSES F).X‚ÇÇ := inferInstance
389	      exact absoluteCohomology_one_eq_zero_of_basis U hS
390	        (s.surj_of_vanishing (injSES F) hS hF U hU) e
391	    | succ m ih =>
392	      intro F hF U hU e
393	      have hS := injSES_shortExact F
394	      haveI : Injective (injSES F).X‚ÇÇ := inferInstance
395	      -- The quotient `Q = (injSES F).X‚ÇÉ` again has vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology.
396	      have hQ : HasVanishingHigherCech s (injSES F).X‚ÇÉ := by
397	        intro c hc q hq
398	        set P := (injSES F).map (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X) with hP
399	        have hface : ‚àÄ (pp : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (pp + 1) ‚Üí c.1),
400	            (faceShortComplex c.2 P œÉ).ShortExact := by
401	          intro pp œÉ
402	          refine faceShortComplex_shortExact_of_sheaf_ses c.2 (injSES F) hS ?_ pp œÉ
403	          intro pp' œÉ'
404	          exact s.surj_of_vanishing (injSES F) hS hF _ (s.faces_mem c hc pp' œÉ')
405	        have hSES := cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis c.2 P hface
406	        exact quotient_cech_vanishing_of_basis c.2 P hSES
407	          (s.injective_acyclic (injSES F).X‚ÇÇ inferInstance c hc) (hF c hc) q hq
408	      -- Ext long exact sequence step: `H^{m+2}(F)` is squeezed by `H^{m+1}(Q) = 0` (IH) and
409	      -- `H^{m+2}(I) = 0` (injective vanishing).
410	      have hef : e.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ (injSES F).f) (add_zero (m + 1 + 1)) = 0 :=
411	        absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective (m + 1) U (injSES F).X‚ÇÇ _
412	      obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÉ, hx‚ÇÉ‚ü© :=
413	        absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact‚ÇÅ U hS e hef (rfl : (m + 1) + 1 = m + 1 + 1)
414	      have hx‚ÇÉzero : x‚ÇÉ = 0 := ih (injSES F).X‚ÇÉ hQ U hU x‚ÇÉ
415	      rw [‚Üê hx‚ÇÉ, hx‚ÇÉzero, Ext.zero_comp]
416	  obtain ‚ü®n, rfl‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hp.ne'
417	  exact key n F hF U hU e

419	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî ƒåech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis (01EO top) -/
420	
421	/-- **ƒåech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis (Stacks 01EO).** Let `s` be a cover system on a
422	basis for `X` and `F` an `O_X`-module with vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology for `s` (conditions
423	(1)‚Äì(3) of 01EO, packaged in `s` and `hF`). Then the absolute cohomology `H·µñ(U, F) =
424	Ext·µñ(jShriekOU U, F)` vanishes for every basic open `U ‚àà s.B` and every `p > 0`. The thin assembly
425	of the dimension-shift induction `absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis` (L4), which itself chains
426	the section-ƒåech short exact sequence (L1), quotient vanishing (L2), and the base case (L3).
427	Project-local: the named 01EO target, carrying `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` as a hypothesis (the
428	instance is absent in Mathlib for sheaves of modules; see `injSES`). -/
429	theorem cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (s : BasisCovSystem X)
430	    {F : X.Modules} (hF : HasVanishingHigherCech s F) :
431	    ‚àÄ U ‚àà s.B, ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí ‚àÄ e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F p, e = 0 :=
432	  fun _ hU _ hp e => absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis s hF hU hp e

635	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover

module

public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.GrothendieckAxioms.FunctorCategory
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.GrothendieckCategory.Basic
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Generator.Sheaf
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Abelian
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Equivalence

/-!

# AB axioms in sheaf categories

If `J` is a Grothendieck topology on a small category `C : Type v`,
and `A : Type u‚ÇÅ` (with `Category.{v} A`) is a Grothendieck abelian category,
then `Sheaf J A` is a Grothendieck abelian category.

-/

public section

universe v v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ u u‚ÇÅ u‚ÇÇ

namespace CategoryTheory

open Limits

namespace Sheaf

variable {C : Type u} {A : Type u‚ÇÅ} {K : Type u‚ÇÇ}
  [Category.{v} C] [Category.{v‚ÇÅ} A] [Category.{v‚ÇÇ} K]
  (J : GrothendieckTopology C)

section

/- The two instances in this section apply in very rare situations, as they assume
that the forgetful functor from sheaves to presheaves commutes with certain colimits.
This does apply for sheaves for the extensive topology --- condensed modules over a
ring are examples of such sheaves.  -/

variable [HasWeakSheafify J A]

instance [HasFiniteLimits A] [HasColimitsOfShape K A] [HasExactColimitsOfShape K A]
    [PreservesColimitsOfShape K (sheafToPresheaf J A)] : HasExactColimitsOfShape K (Sheaf J A) :=
  HasExactColimitsOfShape.domain_of_functor K (sheafToPresheaf J A)

instance [HasFiniteColimits A] [HasLimitsOfShape K A] [HasExactLimitsOfShape K A]
    [PreservesFiniteColimits (sheafToPresheaf J A)] : HasExactLimitsOfShape K (Sheaf J A) :=
  HasExactLimitsOfShape.domain_of_functor K (sheafToPresheaf J A)

end

instance hasFilteredColimitsOfSize
    [HasSheafify J A] [HasFilteredColimitsOfSize.{v‚ÇÇ, u‚ÇÇ} A] :
    HasFilteredColimitsOfSize.{v‚ÇÇ, u‚ÇÇ} (Sheaf J A) where
  HasColimitsOfShape K := by infer_instance

instance hasExactColimitsOfShape [HasFiniteLimits A] [HasSheafify J A]
    [HasColimitsOfShape K A] [HasExactColimitsOfShape K A] :
    HasExactColimitsOfShape K (Sheaf J A) :=
  (sheafificationAdjunction J A).hasExactColimitsOfShape K

instance ab5ofSize [HasFiniteLimits A] [HasSheafify J A]
    [HasFilteredColimitsOfSize.{v‚ÇÇ, u‚ÇÇ} A] [AB5OfSize.{v‚ÇÇ, u‚ÇÇ} A] :
    AB5OfSize.{v‚ÇÇ, u‚ÇÇ} (Sheaf J A) where
  ofShape K _ _ := by infer_instance

instance {C : Type v} [SmallCategory.{v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
    (A : Type u‚ÇÅ) [Category.{v‚ÇÅ} A] [Abelian A] [IsGrothendieckAbelian.{v} A]
    [HasSheafify J A] : IsGrothendieckAbelian.{v} (Sheaf J A) where

attribute [local instance] hasSheafifyEssentiallySmallSite in
lemma isGrothendieckAbelian_of_essentiallySmall
    {C : Type u‚ÇÇ} [Category.{v‚ÇÇ} C] [EssentiallySmall.{v} C]
    (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
    (A : Type u‚ÇÅ) [Category.{v‚ÇÅ} A] [Abelian A] [IsGrothendieckAbelian.{v} A]

262	/-- **Additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The free‚ÄìYoneda hom bijection
263	`Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda V), F) ‚âÉ F(V)` is an isomorphism of abelian groups: the source is
264	the (preadditive) hom-group of presheaves of modules and the target is the section group
265	`F(V)`. This is the per-multi-index additive core of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
266	(blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`); taking products over multi-indices and
267	intertwining with the differential upgrades it to the full complex isomorphism. Additivity
268	holds because, by `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, the bijection is evaluation of the
269	component `œà.app (op V)` (a module map, hence additive) on a fixed generator, and the
270	component of a sum of morphisms is the sum of components.
271	
272	Project-local because Mathlib packages the underlying bijection (`freeHomEquiv` ‚àò
273	`yonedaEquiv`) only at the level of types, not as an `AddEquiv`. -/
274	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
275	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
276	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ‚ü∂ F) ‚âÉ+
277	      (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) where
278	  toFun := freeYonedaHomEquiv V F
279	  invFun := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).symm
280	  left_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).left_inv
281	  right_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).right_inv
282	  map_add' f g := by
283	    rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply,
284	      PresheafOfModules.add_app]
285	    change (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f.app (Opposite.op V) + g.app (Opposite.op V))) _ = _
286	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add]; rfl

289	
290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : Œπ ‚Üí Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`‚àè_{œÉ : Fin (p+1) ‚Üí Œπ} F(‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ‚ü∂ [n]` acts at multi-index `œÉ : Fin (n+1) ‚Üí Œπ` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `œÉ ‚àò f` and then restricting along `‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k) ‚â§ ‚®Ö‚Çó U (œÉ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ‚•§ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d¬≤ = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ‚àè·∂ú (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
307	    Pi.œÄ _ (œÉ ‚àò f.toOrderHom) ‚â´
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    -- The map at `ùüô n` projects to index `œÉ ‚àò id = œÉ` and restricts along the thin
311	    -- (reflexive) `Opens` hom `homOfLE ‚ãØ : ‚®Ö U(œÉ k) ‚ü∂ ‚®Ö U(œÉ k)`, which is the identity.
312	    ext1 œÉ
313	    simp only [Pi.lift_œÄ]
314	    -- the restriction hom is an endomorphism in the thin `Opens·µí·µñ` category, hence `ùüô`
315	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (ùüô _)]
316	    -- `erw` is needed: the `Subsingleton.elim` rewrite leaves the term not type-correct
317	    -- under `instances` transparency, so plain `rw [Functor.map_id]` cannot match.
318	    erw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]

320	    apply Pi.hom_ext
321	    intro œÉ
322	    simp only [Pi.lift_œÄ, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
323	    rw [‚Üê Category.assoc, Pi.lift_œÄ, Category.assoc, ‚Üê Functor.map_comp]
324	    congr 1

40	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
41	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/

8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the structure presheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (1/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	ships the categorical gap-fills, the sheafification / `Ext` typeclass prerequisites,
13	and the per-open ring-map helpers (1)‚Äì(5) that culminate in the presheaf-of-
14	`k`-modules `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6). Its sibling `SheafProperty.lean`
15	discharges the sheaf condition and bundles `toModuleKSheaf` (helpers 7‚Äì8); the
16	sibling `Carriers.lean` carries the downstream finite-length carriers and producer
17	instances.
18	
19	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
20	-/
21	
22	set_option autoImplicit false
23	
24	universe u v
25	
26	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	namespace CategoryTheory
29	
30	universe u‚ÇÇ v‚ÇÇ
31	
32	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ‚•§ C ‚•§ D` is additive when `D`
33	is preadditive. The `map_add` field reduces to `f + g = f + g` componentwise after
34	`ext; rfl`. -/
35	instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u‚ÇÇ}
36	    [Category.{v‚ÇÇ} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ‚•§ C ‚•§ D).Additive where
37	  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl
38	
39	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ‚•§ C ‚•§ D` is `R`-linear when
40	`D` is `Linear R`-enriched. The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is
41	load-bearing ‚Äî bare `[Linear R D]` would be parsed as `Linear` of a functor. -/
42	instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u‚ÇÇ}
43	    [Category.{v‚ÇÇ} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
44	    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
45	    (Functor.const C : D ‚•§ C ‚•§ D).Linear R where
46	  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl
47	
48	namespace Adjunction
49	
50	variable {C‚ÇÅ : Type u} {D‚ÇÅ : Type u‚ÇÇ} [Category.{v} C‚ÇÅ] [Category.{v‚ÇÇ} D‚ÇÅ]
51	    [Preadditive C‚ÇÅ] [Preadditive D‚ÇÅ] {F : C‚ÇÅ ‚•§ D‚ÇÅ} {G : D‚ÇÅ ‚•§ C‚ÇÅ} (adj : F ‚ä£ G)
52	    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C‚ÇÅ] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D‚ÇÅ]
53	
54	include adj
55	
56	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `G : D ‚•§ C` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ‚ä£ G`,
57	then `F : C ‚•§ D` is `R`-linear. Mirrors Mathlib's `left_adjoint_additive`; the
58	`simp` call closes via unit naturality + the `Linear.smul_comp` / `Linear.comp_smul`
59	default simp lemmas. -/
60	lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
61	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
62	    -- v4.31.0: the old `simp [adj.homEquiv_unit]` no longer closes; the categorical
63	    -- `Linear.comp_smul`/`smul_comp` only match via `erw` (the `‚âÉ‚Çó` homModule smul is defeq but
64	    -- not syntactically the `Linear.smul` instance), with unit-naturality in between.
65	    simp only [adj.homEquiv_unit, Functor.map_smul, ‚Üê Functor.comp_map]
66	    erw [‚Üê adj.unit.naturality, Linear.comp_smul, ‚Üê adj.unit.naturality, Linear.smul_comp]
67	    rfl)
68	
69	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `F : C ‚•§ D` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ‚ä£ G`,
70	then `G : D ‚•§ C` is `R`-linear. The dual of `left_adjoint_linear`. -/
71	lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
72	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
73	    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])
74	
75	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): linear lifting of `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`. Under
76	`[F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]`, the additive equivalence
77	`(F.obj X ‚ü∂ Y) ‚âÉ+ (X ‚ü∂ G.obj Y)` upgrades to a `LinearEquiv` over `R`. The
78	`change` is load-bearing: it exposes the underlying `homEquiv` form, allowing
79	`simp [homEquiv_unit]` to close via the same naturality argument as in
80	`left_adjoint_linear`. -/
81	noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
82	    (X : C‚ÇÅ) (Y : D‚ÇÅ) : (F.obj X ‚ü∂ Y) ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] (X ‚ü∂ G.obj Y) :=
83	  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
84	  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
85	    map_smul' := fun r f => by
86	      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r ‚Ä¢ f) = r ‚Ä¢ adj.homEquiv _ _ f
87	      simp only [adj.homEquiv_unit, Functor.map_smul]
88	      erw [Linear.comp_smul] }
89	
90	end Adjunction
91	
92	end CategoryTheory

96	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (a): sheafification on the topology of opens of
97	any topological space, valued in `ModuleCat k`. Inferable from Mathlib's
98	small-site / concrete-category sheafification API; the prover's task is the
99	universe pinning, mirroring the iter-004 `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
100	instance instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK
101	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
102	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
103	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
104	  inferInstance

106	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (b): `Ext` on the sheaf category. The universe
107	annotation `HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
108	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`; mirrors the iter-004
109	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
110	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK
111	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
112	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
113	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
114	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
115	  CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _

119	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
120	
121	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
122	
123	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k ‚Üí Œì(C, U)` induced by the
124	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ‚ü∂ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
125	restriction along `U ‚â§ ‚ä§`. -/
126	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
127	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ) :
128	    (CommRingCat.of k) ‚ü∂ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
129	  (Scheme.ŒìSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ‚â´
130	    C.hom.app ‚ä§ ‚â´ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
131	
132	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Œì(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
133	map of `kToSection`. -/
134	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
135	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ) :
136	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
137	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
140	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
141	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ) :
142	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
143	  rfl
144	
145	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
146	natural in `U`. -/
147	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
148	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ} (f : U ‚ü∂ V) :
149	    kToSection C U ‚â´ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
150	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ‚Üê C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
151	  rfl
152	
153	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
154	`algebraMap`. -/
155	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
156	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ} (f : U ‚ü∂ V) (r : k) :
157	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
158	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
159	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
160	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ‚ü∂ _) => g.hom r)
161	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
162	
163	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf

165	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

167	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
168	
169	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
170	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
171	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
172	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ ‚•§ ModuleCat.{u} k where
173	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
174	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
175	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
176	      map_add' := fun x y => by
177	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
178	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
179	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
180	        congr 1
181	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
182	          (C := C) f r }
183	  map_id U := by
184	    ext x
185	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
186	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
187	    exact congrFun (congrArg (¬∑.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
188	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
189	    ext x
190	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
191	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
192	    exact congrFun (congrArg (¬∑.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
193	
194	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
195	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
196	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
197	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
198	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ) :
199	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
200	  rfl
201	
202	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproducts.Cech
10	
11	/-!
12	# Cech cohomology
13	
14	Given a family of objects `U : Œπ ‚Üí C` in a category `C` that has finite products,
15	we define a Cech complex functor
16	`cechComplexFunctor : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï` which sends a presheaf
17	`P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` in a preadditive category (where products exist) to the cochain
18	complex which in degree `n` consists of the product, indexed by `i : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ`,
19	of the value of `P` on the product of the objects `U (i a)` for `a : Fin (n + 1)`.
20	
21	-/
22	
23	@[expose] public section
24	
25	universe w t v v' u u'
26	
27	namespace CategoryTheory
28	
29	open Limits
30	
31	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A] [HasProducts.{w} A]
32	
33	namespace Limits.FormalCoproduct
34	
35	open Opposite
36	
37	variable (E : SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C))
38	
39	/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
40	functor `(C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CosimplicialObject A` which sends `P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` to the
41	cosimplicial object which sends `‚¶ãn‚¶å` to the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _‚¶ãn‚¶å`. -/
42	@[simps!]
43	noncomputable def cosimplicialObjectFunctor :
44	    (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CosimplicialObject A :=
45	  evalOp.{w} C A ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj E.rightOp
46	
47	variable [Preadditive A]
48	
49	/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
50	functor `(C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï` which sends `P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` to the
51	cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _‚¶ãn‚¶å`. -/
52	@[simps!]
53	noncomputable def cochainComplexFunctor : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï :=
54	  cosimplicialObjectFunctor E ‚ãô AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A
55	
56	end Limits.FormalCoproduct
57	
58	variable [HasFiniteProducts C] [Preadditive A] {Œπ : Type w} (U : Œπ ‚Üí C)
59	
60	/-- Given a family of objects `U : Œπ ‚Üí C`, this is the Cech complex functor
61	`(C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï` which sends a presheaf `P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` to the
62	cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the product,
63	indexed by `x : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ`, of the value of `P` on the product of the
64	objects `U (x i)` for `i : Fin (n + 1)`. -/
65	noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï :=
66	  FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech
67	
68	end CategoryTheory

11	/-!
12	# The Cech object for formal coproducts
13	
14	Let `C` be a category that has finite products. In this file, we define a
15	functor `cechFunctor : FormalCoproduct C ‚•§ SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct C)`
16	which sends a formal coproduct of objects `U j` (for `j : Œπ`) to the simplicial object
17	which sends `‚¶ãn‚¶å` to the formal coproduct, indexed by `i : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ`,
18	of the products of the objects `U (i a)` for all `a : Fin (n + 1)`.
19	
20	-/
21	
22	@[expose] public section
23	
24	universe w t v u
25	
26	namespace CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct
27	
28	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]
29	
30	/-- Given `U : FormalCoproduct C` and a type `Œ±`, this is the formal coproduct
31	indexed by all `i : Œ± ‚Üí U.I` of the products of the objects `U.obj (i a)`
32	for all `a : Œ±`. -/
33	@[simps]
34	noncomputable def power (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (Œ± : Type t)
35	    [HasProductsOfShape Œ± C] : FormalCoproduct.{max w t} C where
36	  I := Œ± ‚Üí U.I
37	  obj i := ‚àè·∂ú (U.obj ‚àò i)
38	
39	section
40	
41	variable (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (Œ± : Type) [HasProductsOfShape Œ± C]
42	
43	variable {Œ±} in
44	/-- The projection `U.power Œ± ‚ü∂ U` for each `a : Œ±`. -/
45	@[simps]
46	noncomputable def powerœÄ (a : Œ±) : U.power Œ± ‚ü∂ U where
47	  f i := i a
48	  œÜ _ := Pi.œÄ _ a
49	
50	/-- The (limit) fan expressing that `U.power Œ±` is a product of copies of
51	`U` indexed by `Œ±`. -/
52	noncomputable abbrev powerFan :
53	    Fan (fun (_ : Œ±) ‚Ü¶ U) :=
54	  Fan.mk (U.power Œ±) U.powerœÄ
55	
56	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
57	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
58	/-- `U.power Œ±` identifies to the product of copies of `U` indexed by `Œ±`. -/
59	noncomputable def isLimitPowerFan : IsLimit (U.powerFan Œ±) :=
60	  Fan.IsLimit.mk _
61	    (fun s ‚Ü¶
62	      { f i a := (s.proj a).f i
63	        œÜ i := Pi.lift (fun a ‚Ü¶ (s.proj a).œÜ i) })
64	    (fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ by ext <;> simp)
65	    (fun s m hm ‚Ü¶ by
66	      obtain ‚ü®f, œÜ‚ü© := m
67	      obtain rfl : f = fun i a ‚Ü¶ (s.proj a).f i := by
68	        ext i
69	        dsimp
70	        ext a
71	        exact congr_fun (congr_arg FormalCoproduct.Hom.f (hm a)) i
72	      ext i
73	      ¬∑ rfl
74	      ¬∑ dsimp
75	        ext a
76	        specialize hm a
77	        rw [hom_ext_iff] at hm
78	        obtain ‚ü®_, hm‚ü© := hm
79	        simpa using hm i)
80	
81	end
82	
83	/-- For any morphism `f : U ‚ü∂ V` in `FormalCoproduct C` and a type `Œ±`,
84	this is the induced map `U.power Œ± ‚ü∂ V.power Œ±`. -/
85	@[simps -fullyApplied]
86	noncomputable def powerMap {U V : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f : U ‚ü∂ V) (Œ± : Type t)
87	    [HasProductsOfShape Œ± C] :
88	    U.power Œ± ‚ü∂ V.power Œ± where
89	  f i := f.f ‚àò i
90	  œÜ i := Pi.map (fun a ‚Ü¶ f.œÜ (i a))
91	
92	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
93	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
94	@[simp]
95	lemma powerMap_id (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (Œ± : Type t) [HasProductsOfShape Œ± C] :
96	    powerMap (ùüô U) Œ± = ùüô _ := by
97	  cat_disch
98	
99	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
100	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
101	@[reassoc]
102	lemma powerMap_comp {U V W : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f : U ‚ü∂ V) (g : V ‚ü∂ W) (Œ± : Type t)
103	    [HasProductsOfShape Œ± C] :
104	    powerMap (f ‚â´ g) Œ± = powerMap f Œ± ‚â´ powerMap g Œ± := by
105	  ext
106	  ¬∑ cat_disch
107	  ¬∑ dsimp
108	    ext
109	    simp only [Category.comp_id, Category.assoc, Pi.map_œÄ, Function.comp_apply,
110	      Pi.map_œÄ_assoc]
111	    apply Pi.map_œÄ
112	
113	attribute [local simp] powerMap_comp
114	
115	/-- Given a type `Œ±`, this is the functor `FormalCoproduct C ‚•§ FormalCoproduct C`
116	which sends `U` to `U.power Œ±`. -/
117	@[simps]
118	noncomputable def powerFunctor (Œ± : Type t) [HasProductsOfShape Œ± C] :
119	    FormalCoproduct.{w} C ‚•§ FormalCoproduct.{max w t} C where
120	  obj U := U.power Œ±
121	  map f := powerMap f Œ±
122	
123	/-- The functoriality of `FormalCoproduct.power` with respect to the index type. -/
124	@[simps -fullyApplied]
125	noncomputable def mapPower (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) {Œ± Œ≤ : Type t}
126	    [HasProductsOfShape Œ± C] [HasProductsOfShape Œ≤ C] (f : Œ± ‚Üí Œ≤) :
127	    U.power Œ≤ ‚ü∂ U.power Œ± where
128	  f i := i ‚àò f
129	  œÜ _ := Pi.lift (fun _ ‚Ü¶ Pi.œÄ _ _)
130	
131	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
132	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
133	@[simp]
134	lemma mapPower_id (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (Œ± : Type t)
135	    [HasProductsOfShape Œ± C] :
136	    U.mapPower (id : Œ± ‚Üí Œ±) = ùüô _ := by
137	  cat_disch
138	
139	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
140	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
141	@[reassoc]
142	lemma mapPower_comp (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) {Œ± Œ≤ Œ≥ : Type t}
143	    [HasProductsOfShape Œ± C] [HasProductsOfShape Œ≤ C] [HasProductsOfShape Œ≥ C]
144	    (f : Œ± ‚Üí Œ≤) (g : Œ≤ ‚Üí Œ≥) :
145	    U.mapPower (g ‚àò f) = U.mapPower g ‚â´ U.mapPower f := by
146	  ext
147	  ¬∑ cat_disch
148	  ¬∑ dsimp
149	    ext
150	    simp [Function.comp_def]
151	
152	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
153	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
154	@[reassoc]
155	lemma mapPower_powerMap {U V : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f : U ‚ü∂ V)
156	    {Œ± Œ≤ : Type t} [HasProductsOfShape Œ± C] [HasProductsOfShape Œ≤ C] (g : Œ± ‚Üí Œ≤) :
157	    U.mapPower g ‚â´ powerMap f Œ± = powerMap f Œ≤ ‚â´ V.mapPower g := by
158	  ext
159	  ¬∑ cat_disch
160	  ¬∑ dsimp
161	    ext
162	    simp [Function.comp_def]
163	
164	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
165	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
166	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
167	lemma mapPower_œÄ (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) {Œ± Œ≤ : Type}
168	    [HasProductsOfShape Œ± C] [HasProductsOfShape Œ≤ C] (f : Œ± ‚Üí Œ≤) (a : Œ±) :
169	    mapPower U f ‚â´ U.powerœÄ a = U.powerœÄ (f a) := by
170	  ext <;> simp
171	
172	attribute [local simp] mapPower_comp mapPower_powerMap
173	
174	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
175	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
176	/-- The functor `(Type t)·µí·µñ ‚•§ FormalCoproduct.{w} C ‚•§ FormalCoproduct.{max w t} C`
177	which sends a type `Œ±` and `U : FormalCoproduct C` to `U.power Œ±`. -/
178	@[simps]
179	noncomputable def powerBifunctor [HasProducts.{t} C] :
180	    Type t·µí·µñ ‚•§ FormalCoproduct.{w} C ‚•§ FormalCoproduct.{max w t} C where
181	  obj Œ± := powerFunctor Œ±.unop
182	  map f := { app _ := mapPower _ f.unop }
183	  map_comp _ _ := by ext : 2; simp [types_comp]
184	
185	variable [HasFiniteProducts C]
186	
187	/-- Given `U : FormalCoproduct C`, this is the simplicial object
188	in `FormalCoproduct C` which sends `‚¶ãn‚¶å` to `U.power (Fin (n + 1))`. -/
189	@[simps]
190	noncomputable def cech (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) :
191	    SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) where
192	  obj n := U.power (ToType n.unop)
193	  map f := U.mapPower f.unop.toOrderHom.toFun
194	
195	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
196	/-- The functor `FormalCoproduct C ‚•§ SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct C)`
197	which sends a formal coproduct to its Cech object. -/
198	@[simps]
199	noncomputable def cechFunctor :
200	    FormalCoproduct.{w} C ‚•§ SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) where
201	  obj U := U.cech
202	  map f := { app _ := powerMap f _ }
203	  map_comp _ _ := by ext : 1; simp
204	
205	end CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct

15	/-!
16	# Formal Coproducts
17	
18	In this file we construct the category of formal coproducts given a category.
19	
20	## Main definitions
21	
22	* `FormalCoproduct`: the category of formal coproducts, which are indexed sets of objects in a
23	  category. A morphism `‚àê i : X.I, X.obj i ‚ü∂ ‚àê j : Y.I, Y.obj j` is given by a function
24	  `f : X.I ‚Üí Y.I` and maps `X.obj i ‚ü∂ Y.obj (f i)` for each `i : X.I`.
25	* `FormalCoproduct.eval : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ ((FormalCoproduct C)·µí·µñ ‚•§ A)`:
26	  the universal property that a presheaf on `C` where the target category has arbitrary coproducts,
27	  can be extended to a presheaf on `FormalCoproduct C`.
28	
29	## TODO
30	
31	* `FormalCoproduct.incl C : C ‚•§ FormalCoproduct.{w} C` probably preserves every limit?
32	
33	-/
34	
35	@[expose] public section
36	
37	universe w w‚ÇÅ w‚ÇÇ w‚ÇÉ v v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ v‚ÇÉ u u‚ÇÅ u‚ÇÇ u‚ÇÉ
38	
39	open Opposite CategoryTheory Functor
40	
41	namespace CategoryTheory
42	
43	namespace Limits
44	
45	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] (A : Type u‚ÇÅ) [Category.{v‚ÇÅ} A]
46	
47	variable (C) in
48	/-- A formal coproduct is an indexed set of objects, where ‚ü®I, f‚ü© corresponds to the "formal
49	coproduct" `‚®ø (i : I), f i`, where `f i : C` is the `i`·µó ∞ component. -/
50	structure FormalCoproduct where
51	  /-- The indexing type. -/
52	  I : Type w
53	  /-- The object in the original category indexed by `x : I`. -/
54	  obj (i : I) : C
55	
56	namespace FormalCoproduct
57	
58	/-- A morphism `(‚®ø (i : X.I), X.obj i) ‚ü∂ (‚®ø (j : Y.I), Y.obj i)` is given by first a function
59	on the indexing sets `f : X.I ‚Üí Y.I`, and then for each `i : X.I` a morphism
60	`X.obj i ‚ü∂ Y.obj (f i)`. -/
61	structure Hom (X Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) where
62	  /-- The function on the indexing sets. -/
63	  f : X.I ‚Üí Y.I
64	  /-- The map on each component. -/
65	  œÜ (i : X.I) : X.obj i ‚ü∂ Y.obj (f i)
66	
67	-- this category identifies to the full subcategory of the category of
68	-- presheaves of sets on `C` which are coproducts of representable presheaves
69	@[simps!] instance category : Category (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) where
70	  Hom := Hom
71	  id X := { f := id, œÜ := fun _ ‚Ü¶ ùüô _ }
72	  comp Œ± Œ≤ := { f := Œ≤.f ‚àò Œ±.f, œÜ := fun _ ‚Ü¶ Œ±.œÜ _ ‚â´ Œ≤.œÜ _ }
73	
74	@[ext (iff := false)]
75	lemma hom_ext {X Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} {f g : X ‚ü∂ Y} (h‚ÇÅ : f.f = g.f)
76	    (h‚ÇÇ : ‚àÄ (i : X.I), f.œÜ i ‚â´ eqToHom (by rw [h‚ÇÅ]) = g.œÜ i) : f = g := by
77	  obtain ‚ü®f, F‚ü© := f
78	  obtain ‚ü®g, G‚ü© := g
79	  obtain rfl : f = g := h‚ÇÅ
80	  obtain rfl : F = G := by ext i; simpa using h‚ÇÇ i
81	  rfl
82	
83	lemma hom_ext_iff {X Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f g : X ‚ü∂ Y) :
84	    f = g ‚Üî ‚àÉ h‚ÇÅ : f.f = g.f, ‚àÄ (i : X.I), f.œÜ i ‚â´ eqToHom (by rw [h‚ÇÅ]) = g.œÜ i :=
85	  ‚ü®(¬∑ ‚ñ∏ by simp), fun ‚ü®h‚ÇÅ, h‚ÇÇ‚ü© ‚Ü¶ hom_ext h‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÇ‚ü©
86	
87	lemma hom_ext_iff' {X Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f g : X ‚ü∂ Y) :
88	    f = g ‚Üî ‚àÄ i : X.I, ‚àÉ h‚ÇÅ : f.f i = g.f i, f.œÜ i ‚â´ eqToHom (by rw [h‚ÇÅ]) = g.œÜ i :=
89	  ‚ü®(¬∑ ‚ñ∏ by simp), fun h ‚Ü¶ hom_ext (funext fun i ‚Ü¶ (h i).fst) fun i ‚Ü¶ (h i).snd‚ü©
90	
91	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
92	/-- A way to create isomorphisms in the category of formal coproducts, by creating an `Equiv`
93	between the indexing sets, and then correspondingly isomorphisms of each component. -/
94	@[simps!] def isoOfComponents {X Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (e : X.I ‚âÉ Y.I)
95	    (h : ‚àÄ i, X.obj i ‚âÖ Y.obj (e i)) : X ‚âÖ Y where
96	  hom := { f := e, œÜ := fun i ‚Ü¶ (h i).hom }
97	  inv := { f := e.symm, œÜ := fun i ‚Ü¶ eqToHom (by simp) ‚â´ (h (e.symm i)).inv }
98	  hom_inv_id := by ext <;> aesop
99	  inv_hom_id := by ext <;> aesop
100	
101	variable (C) in
102	/-- An object of the original category produces a formal coproduct on that object only, so indexed
103	by `PUnit`, the type with one element. -/
104	@[simps!] def incl : C ‚•§ FormalCoproduct.{w} C where
105	  obj X := ‚ü®PUnit, fun _ ‚Ü¶ X‚ü©
106	  map f := ‚ü®fun _ ‚Ü¶ PUnit.unit, fun _ ‚Ü¶ f‚ü©
107	
108	section fromIncl
109	
110	variable {X : C} {Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C}
111	
112	/-- A map `incl(X) ‚ü∂ Y` is specified by an element of `Y`'s indexing set, and then a morphism
113	`X ‚ü∂ Y.obj i` in the original category. -/
114	@[simps!] def Hom.fromIncl (i : Y.I) (f : X ‚ü∂ Y.obj i) : (incl C).obj X ‚ü∂ Y :=
115	  ‚ü®fun _ ‚Ü¶ i, fun _ ‚Ü¶ f‚ü©
116	
117	/-- A map `incl(X) ‚ü∂ Y` is specified by an element of `Y`'s indexing set, and then a morphism
118	`X ‚ü∂ Y.obj i` in the original category. -/
119	def Hom.asSigma (f : (incl C).obj X ‚ü∂ Y) : Œ£ (i : Y.I), X ‚ü∂ Y.obj i :=
120	  ‚ü®f.f PUnit.unit, f.œÜ PUnit.unit‚ü©
121	
122	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
123	lemma Hom.fromIncl_asSigma (f : (incl C).obj X ‚ü∂ Y) :
124	    Hom.fromIncl f.asSigma.fst f.asSigma.snd = f := by
125	  ext <;> aesop
126	
127	end fromIncl
128	
129	-- This is probably some form of adjunction?
130	/-- A map `incl(X) ‚ü∂ Y` is specified by an element of `Y`'s indexing set, and then a morphism
131	`X ‚ü∂ Y.obj i` in the original category. -/
132	@[simps!] def inclHomEquiv (X : C) (Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) :
133	    ((incl C).obj X ‚ü∂ Y) ‚âÉ (i : Y.I) √ó (X ‚ü∂ Y.obj i) where
134	  toFun f := f.asSigma
135	  invFun f := .fromIncl f.1 f.2
136	  left_inv f := f.fromIncl_asSigma
137	  right_inv _ := rfl
138	
139	/-- `incl` is fully faithful, which means that `(X ‚ü∂ Y) ‚âÉ (incl(X) ‚ü∂ incl(Y))`. -/
140	@[simps!] def fullyFaithfulIncl : (incl C).FullyFaithful where
141	  preimage f := f.œÜ PUnit.unit
142	
143	instance : (incl C).Full :=
144	  fullyFaithfulIncl.full
145	
146	instance : (incl C).Faithful :=
147	  fullyFaithfulIncl.faithful
148	
149	/-- A family of maps with the same target can be turned into one arrow in the category of formal
150	coproducts. This is used in ƒåech cohomology. -/
151	@[simps!] def homOfPiHom (X : C) {J : Type w} (f : (j : J) ‚Üí C) (œÜ : (j : J) ‚Üí f j ‚ü∂ X) :
152	    FormalCoproduct.mk _ f ‚ü∂ (incl C).obj X :=
153	  ‚ü®fun _ ‚Ü¶ PUnit.unit, œÜ‚ü©
154	
155	section Coproduct
156	
157	variable (ùíú : Type w) (f : ùíú ‚Üí FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (t X : FormalCoproduct.{w} C)
158	
159	/-- We construct explicitly the data that specify the coproduct of a given family of formal
160	coproducts. -/
161	def cofan : Cofan f :=
162	  Cofan.mk ‚ü®(i : ùíú) √ó (f i).I, fun p ‚Ü¶ (f p.1).obj p.2‚ü©
163	    fun i ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®fun x ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®i, x‚ü©, fun x ‚Ü¶ ùüô ((f i).obj x)‚ü©
164	
165	section simp_lemmas
166	
167	variable {ùíú f}
168	
169	theorem cofan_inj (i : ùíú) : (cofan ùíú f).inj i = ‚ü®fun x ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®i, x‚ü©, fun x ‚Ü¶ ùüô ((f i).obj x)‚ü© := rfl
170	
171	@[simp] lemma cofan_inj_f_fst (i : ùíú) (x) : (((cofan ùíú f).inj i).f x).1 = i := rfl
172	
173	@[simp] lemma cofan_inj_f_snd (i : ùíú) (x) : (((cofan ùíú f).inj i).f x).2 = x := rfl
174	
175	@[simp] lemma cofan_inj_œÜ (i : ùíú) (x) : ((cofan ùíú f).inj i).œÜ x = ùüô ((f i).obj x) := rfl
176	
177	end simp_lemmas
178	
179	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
180	/-- The explicit `Equiv` between maps from the constructed coproduct `cofan ùíú f` and families of
181	maps from each component, which is the universal property of coproducts. -/
182	@[simps!] def cofanHomEquiv :
183	    ((cofan ùíú f).pt ‚ü∂ t) ‚âÉ ((i : ùíú) ‚Üí (f i ‚ü∂ t)) where
184	  toFun m i := (cofan ùíú f).inj i ‚â´ m
185	  invFun s := ‚ü®fun p ‚Ü¶ (s p.1).f p.2, fun p ‚Ü¶ (s p.1).œÜ p.2‚ü©
186	  left_inv m := hom_ext rfl (fun ‚ü®i, x‚ü© ‚Ü¶ by simp [cofan_inj])
187	  right_inv p := by ext <;> simp
188	
189	/-- `cofan ùíú f` is a coproduct of `f`. -/
190	@[simps!] def isColimitCofan : IsColimit (cofan ùíú f) :=
191	  Cofan.IsColimit.mk (cofan ùíú f) (fun t ‚Ü¶ (cofanHomEquiv _ _ _).symm t.inj)
192	    (fun t i ‚Ü¶ congrFun ((cofanHomEquiv _ _ _).right_inv t.inj) i)
193	    (fun _ _ h ‚Ü¶ (Equiv.eq_symm_apply _).2 (funext h))
194	
195	instance : HasCoproducts.{w} (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) :=
196	  hasCoproducts_of_colimit_cofans _ (isColimitCofan _)
197	
198	/-- The arbitrary choice of the coproduct is isomorphic to our constructed coproduct `cofan ùíú f`.
199	-/
200	noncomputable def coproductIsoCofanPt : ‚àê f ‚âÖ (cofan ùíú f).pt :=
201	  colimit.isoColimitCocone ‚ü®_, isColimitCofan _ _‚ü©
202	
203	variable {ùíú f} in
204	@[reassoc (attr := simp)] lemma Œπ_comp_coproductIsoCofanPt (i) :
205	    Sigma.Œπ f i ‚â´ (coproductIsoCofanPt ùíú f).hom = (cofan ùíú f).inj i :=
206	  colimit.isoColimitCocone_Œπ_hom _ _
207	
208	/-- Each `X : FormalCoproduct.{w} C` is actually itself a coproduct of objects of the original
209	category (after coercion using `incl C`). This is the function that specifies the family for which
210	`X` is a coproduct of. -/
211	def toFun (X : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) : X.I ‚Üí FormalCoproduct.{w} C :=
212	  (incl C).obj ‚àò X.obj
213	
214	/-- The witness that each `X : FormalCoproduct.{w} C` is itself a coproduct of objects of the
215	original category (after coercion using `incl C`), specified by `X.toFun`. -/
216	def cofanPtIsoSelf : (cofan X.I X.toFun).pt ‚âÖ X :=
217	  isoOfComponents (Equiv.sigmaPUnit X.I) fun i ‚Ü¶ Iso.refl (X.obj i.fst)
218	
219	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
220	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
221	lemma inj_comp_cofanPtIsoSelf_hom (i : X.I) :
222	    (cofan X.I X.toFun).inj i ‚â´ (cofanPtIsoSelf X).hom = .fromIncl i (ùüô (X.obj i)) :=
223	  hom_ext rfl (fun i => by aesop)
224	
225	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
226	lemma fromIncl_comp_cofanPtIsoSelf_inv (i : X.I) :
227	    Hom.fromIncl i (ùüô (X.obj i)) ‚â´ (cofanPtIsoSelf X).inv = (cofan X.I X.toFun).inj i :=
228	  (Iso.comp_inv_eq _).2 (inj_comp_cofanPtIsoSelf_hom _ _).symm
229	
230	/-- The isomorphism between the coproduct of `X.toFun` and the object `X` itself. -/
231	@[simps!] noncomputable def coproductIsoSelf :
232	    ‚àê X.toFun ‚âÖ X :=
233	  coproductIsoCofanPt _ _ ‚â™‚â´ cofanPtIsoSelf X
234	
235	@[reassoc (attr := simp)] lemma Œπ_comp_coproductIsoSelf_hom (i : X.I) :
236	    Sigma.Œπ _ i ‚â´ (coproductIsoSelf X).hom = .fromIncl i (ùüô (X.obj i)) := by
237	  simp [coproductIsoSelf]
238	
239	@[reassoc (attr := simp)] lemma fromIncl_comp_coproductIsoSelf_inv (i : X.I) :
240	    Hom.fromIncl i (ùüô (X.obj i)) ‚â´ (coproductIsoSelf X).inv = Sigma.Œπ X.toFun i :=
241	  (Iso.comp_inv_eq _).2 (Œπ_comp_coproductIsoSelf_hom _ _).symm
242	
243	end Coproduct
244	
245	section Terminal
246	
247	/-- Given a terminal object `T` in the original category, we show that `incl(T)` is a terminal
248	object in the category of formal coproducts. -/
249	def isTerminalIncl (T : C) (ht : IsTerminal T) : IsTerminal ((incl C).obj T) :=
250	  IsTerminal.ofUniqueHom (fun _ ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®fun _ ‚Ü¶ PUnit.unit, fun _ ‚Ü¶ ht.from _‚ü©)
251	    (fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ hom_ext (funext fun _ ‚Ü¶ rfl) (fun _ ‚Ü¶ ht.hom_ext _ _))
252	
253	instance [HasTerminal C] : HasTerminal (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) :=
254	  (isTerminalIncl (‚ä§_ C) terminalIsTerminal).hasTerminal
255	
256	end Terminal
257	
258	section Pullback
259	
260	variable {X Y Z : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f : X ‚ü∂ Z) (g : Y ‚ü∂ Z)
261	  (pb : ‚àÄ i : Function.Pullback f.f g.f,
262	    PullbackCone (f.œÜ i.1.1 ‚â´ eqToHom (by rw [i.2])) (g.œÜ i.1.2))
263	  (hpb : ‚àÄ i, IsLimit (pb i))
264	  (T : FormalCoproduct.{w} C)
265	
266	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
267	/-- Given two morphisms `f : X ‚ü∂ Z` and `g : Y ‚ü∂ Z`, given pullback in `C` over each component,
268	construct the pullback in `FormalCategory.{w} C`. -/
269	def pullbackCone : PullbackCone f g :=
270	  .mk (W := ‚ü®Function.Pullback f.f g.f, fun i ‚Ü¶ (pb i).pt‚ü©)

153	‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
154	Type: for an injective `I : X.Modules` and an open cover `ùí∞ : X.OpenCover`, the
155	positive-degree ƒåech cohomology `»û^p(ùí∞, I) = 0` for all `p > 0`.

157	Strategy (two independent parts; does NOT need decisions 4‚Äì5):
158	
159	Part (a) ‚Äî Injective sheaf ‚üπ injective presheaf:
160	- Apply `CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint` to the adjunction
161	  `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction Œ±` where `Œ± = ùüô` for the scheme `X`.
162	  This adjunction is `sheafification Œ± ‚ä£ (forget R) ‚ãô restrictScalars Œ±`, i.e.
163	  sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion `toPresheafOfModules`.
164	- The left adjoint `sheafification` is exact (it is exact as a colimit of
165	  sheafification-endofunctors over a Grothendieck topology), hence in particular
166	  `PreservesMonomorphisms`. So `Injective.injective_of_adjoint` gives:
167	  `I` injective in `X.Modules` ‚üπ `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules.obj I`
168	  is injective in `X.PresheafOfModules`.
169	- Relevant Mathlib names:
170	  `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction`,
171	  `CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint`,
172	  `Functor.preservesInjectiveObjects_of_adjunction_of_preservesMonomorphisms`,
173	  `Adjunction.map_injective`.
174	
175	Part (b) ‚Äî Vanishing of positive ƒåech cohomology:
176	- By step 4, `K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢ ‚Üí O_ùí∞[0]` is an exact augmented complex (quasi-iso from step 4).
177	- Since `I_pshf := toPresheafOfModules.obj I` is injective in `X.PresheafOfModules`
178	  (by part (a)), the functor `Hom_{PMod}(-, I_pshf)` is exact.
179	- Applying `Hom_{PMod}(-, I_pshf)` to the exact augmented complex `K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢ ‚Üí O_ùí∞` and
180	  using step 3 (`cechComplex_hom_identification`) to identify the resulting complex
181	  with `ƒå‚Ä¢(ùí∞, I, U)`, conclude that `ƒå‚Ä¢(ùí∞, I, U)` is exact in positive degrees, i.e.
182	  `»û^p(ùí∞, I) = 0` for `p > 0`.

184	  0BKP and surrounding; see `references/stacks-cohomology.md`).

186	‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ

194	  yoneda
195	‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
196	-/
197	
198	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî PresheafCech (injective transfer) -/
199	
200	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
201	
202	/-- **An injective sheaf of `O_X`-modules is injective as a presheaf of modules.**
203	
204	This is Part 1 of the {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injectives (blueprint
205	`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`): the inclusion
206	`Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X : X.Modules ‚•§ X.PresheafOfModules` is the right
207	adjoint of the (exact) sheafification functor, so it carries injective objects to
208	injective objects.
209	
210	Project-local because Mathlib has no packaged "injective sheaf of modules ‚üπ injective
211	presheaf of modules" statement; it is obtained here by feeding
212	`PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ùüô _)` (whose left adjoint
213	`sheafification` preserves monomorphisms) to `Injective.injective_of_adjoint`. The
214	right adjoint of that adjunction is *definitionally* `toPresheafOfModules X`
215	(`SheafOfModules.forget _ ‚ãô restrictScalars (ùüô _)`). -/
216	theorem injective_toPresheafOfModules (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] :
217	    Injective ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I) := by
218	  -- the left adjoint of `toPresheafOfModules X` is `sheafification (ùüô _)`, which is
219	  -- exact and in particular preserves monomorphisms
220	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (ùüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).PreservesMonomorphisms :=
221	    inferInstance
222	  -- realign the ambient category instance so the `Injective` instance is found cheaply
223	  haveI : Injective (C := SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf) I := ‚ÄπInjective I‚Ä∫
224	  exact Injective.injective_of_adjoint
225	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ùüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) I
226	
227	/-- **Free‚ÄìYoneda representability (per-term bijection).**
228	
229	For an open `V : Opens X` and a presheaf of `O_X`-modules `F`, the morphisms out of the
230	free presheaf of modules on the representable `yoneda V` are exactly the sections of
231	`F` over `V`:
232	```
233	  Hom_{PMod(O_X)}(free(yoneda V), F) ‚âÉ F(V).
234	```
235	This is the free‚Äìforgetful adjunction (`PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv`) composed with
236	the Yoneda bijection (`yonedaEquiv`). It is the single-multi-index core of the
237	{\v C}ech complex hom-identification (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`,
238	Stacks `lemma-cech-map-into`): taking the product over multi-indices turns this into
239	`Hom(K(ùí∞)_p, F) = ƒå^p(ùí∞, F)`.
240	
241	Project-local because Mathlib packages the two halves separately but not their
242	composite as the corepresentability of evaluation by `free(yoneda V)`. The target
243	`(F.presheaf ‚ãô forget Ab).obj (op V)` is the underlying set of the section group
244	`F(V)`. -/
245	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
246	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
247	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ‚ü∂ F) ‚âÉ
248	      (F.presheaf ‚ãô forget Ab).obj (Opposite.op V) :=
249	  PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv.trans yonedaEquiv
250	
251	/-- **Generator formula for `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The bijection
252	`Hom(free(yoneda V), F) ‚âÉ F(V)` is evaluation of the morphism's component at `V` on the
253	canonical free generator `freeMk (ùüô V)`. Recorded project-locally because this concrete
254	description is what makes the bijection visibly additive (`freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`) and is
255	unavailable as a packaged Mathlib lemma. -/
256	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
257	    (œà : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ‚ü∂ F) :
258	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F œà
259	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (œà.app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (ùüô V)) := by
260	  simp only [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2021 Adam Topaz. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Adam Topaz
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Whiskering
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	
13	Given categories `C D E`, functors `F : D ‚•§ E` and `G : E ‚•§ D` with an adjunction
14	`F ‚ä£ G`, we provide the induced adjunction between the functor categories `C ‚•§ D` and `C ‚•§ E`,
15	and the functor categories `E ‚•§ C` and `D ‚•§ C`.
16	
17	-/
18	
19	@[expose] public section
20	
21	
22	namespace CategoryTheory.Adjunction
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Functor
25	
26	variable (C : Type*) {D E : Type*} [Category* C] [Category* D] [Category* E] {F : D ‚•§ E} {G : E ‚•§ D}
27	
28	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
29	/-- Given an adjunction `F ‚ä£ G`, this provides the natural adjunction
30	  `(whiskeringRight C _ _).obj F ‚ä£ (whiskeringRight C _ _).obj G`. -/
31	@[simps! unit_app_app counit_app_app]
32	protected def whiskerRight (adj : F ‚ä£ G) :
33	    (whiskeringRight C D E).obj F ‚ä£ (whiskeringRight C E D).obj G where
34	  unit :=
35	    { app := fun X =>
36	        (rightUnitor _).inv ‚â´ whiskerLeft X adj.unit ‚â´ (associator _ _ _).inv
37	      naturality := by intros; ext; simp }
38	  counit :=
39	    { app := fun X =>
40	        (associator _ _ _).hom ‚â´ whiskerLeft X adj.counit ‚â´ (rightUnitor _).hom
41	      naturality := by intros; ext; simp }
42	
43	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
44	/-- Given an adjunction `F ‚ä£ G`, this provides the natural adjunction
45	  `(whiskeringLeft _ _ C).obj G ‚ä£ (whiskeringLeft _ _ C).obj F`. -/
46	@[simps! unit_app_app counit_app_app]
47	protected def whiskerLeft (adj : F ‚ä£ G) :
48	    (whiskeringLeft E D C).obj G ‚ä£ (whiskeringLeft D E C).obj F where
49	  unit :=
50	    { app := fun X =>
51	        (leftUnitor _).inv ‚â´ whiskerRight adj.unit X ‚â´ (associator _ _ _).hom }
52	  counit :=
53	    { app := fun X =>
54	        (associator _ _ _).inv ‚â´ whiskerRight adj.counit X ‚â´ (leftUnitor _).hom }
55	  left_triangle_components X := by ext; simp [‚Üê X.map_comp]
56	  right_triangle_components X := by ext; simp [‚Üê X.map_comp]
57	
58	end CategoryTheory.Adjunction
59

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2019 Kim Morrison. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Kim Morrison, Justus Springer
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Category.Preorder
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.FiniteLimits
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Products
11	public import Mathlib.Data.Finset.Lattice.Fold
12	
13	/-!
14	# Limits in lattice categories are given by infimums and supremums.
15	-/
16	
17	@[expose] public section
18	
19	
20	universe w w' u
21	
22	namespace CategoryTheory.Limits.CompleteLattice
23	
24	section Semilattice
25	
26	variable {Œ± : Type u} {J : Type w} [SmallCategory J] [FinCategory J]
27	
28	/-- The limit cone over any functor from a finite diagram into a `SemilatticeInf` with `OrderTop`.
29	-/
30	@[simps]
31	def finiteLimitCone [SemilatticeInf Œ±] [OrderTop Œ±] (F : J ‚•§ Œ±) : LimitCone F where
32	  cone :=
33	    { pt := Finset.univ.inf F.obj
34	      œÄ := { app := fun _ => homOfLE (Finset.inf_le (Fintype.complete _)) } }
35	  isLimit := { lift := fun s => homOfLE (Finset.le_inf fun j _ => (s.œÄ.app j).down.down) }
36	
37	/--
38	The colimit cocone over any functor from a finite diagram into a `SemilatticeSup` with `OrderBot`.
39	-/
40	@[simps]
41	def finiteColimitCocone [SemilatticeSup Œ±] [OrderBot Œ±] (F : J ‚•§ Œ±) : ColimitCocone F where
42	  cocone :=
43	    { pt := Finset.univ.sup F.obj
44	      Œπ := { app := fun _ => homOfLE (Finset.le_sup (Fintype.complete _)) } }
45	  isColimit := { desc := fun s => homOfLE (Finset.sup_le fun j _ => (s.Œπ.app j).down.down) }
46	
47	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
48	instance (priority := 100) hasFiniteLimits_of_semilatticeInf_orderTop [SemilatticeInf Œ±]
49	    [OrderTop Œ±] : HasFiniteLimits Œ± := ‚ü®by
50	  intro J ùí•‚ÇÅ ùí•‚ÇÇ
51	  exact { has_limit := fun F => HasLimit.mk (finiteLimitCone F) }‚ü©
52	
53	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
54	instance (priority := 100) hasFiniteColimits_of_semilatticeSup_orderBot [SemilatticeSup Œ±]
55	    [OrderBot Œ±] : HasFiniteColimits Œ± := ‚ü®by
56	  intro J ùí•‚ÇÅ ùí•‚ÇÇ
57	  exact { has_colimit := fun F => HasColimit.mk (finiteColimitCocone F) }‚ü©
58	
59	/-- The limit of a functor from a finite diagram into a `SemilatticeInf` with `OrderTop` is the
60	infimum of the objects in the image.
61	-/
62	theorem finite_limit_eq_finset_univ_inf [SemilatticeInf Œ±] [OrderTop Œ±] (F : J ‚•§ Œ±) :
63	    limit F = Finset.univ.inf F.obj :=
64	  (IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso (limit.isLimit F) (finiteLimitCone F).isLimit).to_eq
65	
66	/-- The colimit of a functor from a finite diagram into a `SemilatticeSup` with `OrderBot`
67	is the supremum of the objects in the image.
68	-/
69	theorem finite_colimit_eq_finset_univ_sup [SemilatticeSup Œ±] [OrderBot Œ±] (F : J ‚•§ Œ±) :
70	    colimit F = Finset.univ.sup F.obj :=
71	  (IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit F) (finiteColimitCocone F).isColimit).to_eq
72	
73	/--
74	A finite product in the category of a `SemilatticeInf` with `OrderTop` is the same as the infimum.
75	-/
76	theorem finite_product_eq_finset_inf [SemilatticeInf Œ±] [OrderTop Œ±] {Œπ : Type u} [Fintype Œπ]
77	    (f : Œπ ‚Üí Œ±) : ‚àè·∂ú f = Finset.univ.inf f := by
78	  trans
79	  ¬∑ exact
80	      (IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso (limit.isLimit _)
81	          (finiteLimitCone (Discrete.functor f)).isLimit).to_eq
82	  change Finset.univ.inf (f ‚àò discreteEquiv.toEmbedding) = Fintype.elems.inf f
83	  simp only [‚Üê Finset.inf_map, Finset.univ_map_equiv_to_embedding]
84	  rfl
85	
86	/-- A finite coproduct in the category of a `SemilatticeSup` with `OrderBot` is the same as the
87	supremum.
88	-/
89	theorem finite_coproduct_eq_finset_sup [SemilatticeSup Œ±] [OrderBot Œ±] {Œπ : Type u} [Fintype Œπ]
90	    (f : Œπ ‚Üí Œ±) : ‚àê f = Finset.univ.sup f := by
91	  trans
92	  ¬∑ exact
93	      (IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit _)
94	          (finiteColimitCocone (Discrete.functor f)).isColimit).to_eq
95	  change Finset.univ.sup (f ‚àò discreteEquiv.toEmbedding) = Fintype.elems.sup f
96	  simp only [‚Üê Finset.sup_map, Finset.univ_map_equiv_to_embedding]
97	  rfl
98	
99	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
100	instance (priority := 100) [SemilatticeInf Œ±] [OrderTop Œ±] : HasBinaryProducts Œ± := by
101	  have : ‚àÄ x y : Œ±, HasLimit (pair x y) := by
102	    letI := hasFiniteLimits_of_hasFiniteLimits_of_size.{u} Œ±
103	    infer_instance
104	  apply hasBinaryProducts_of_hasLimit_pair
105	
106	/-- The binary product in the category of a `SemilatticeInf` with `OrderTop` is the same as the
107	infimum.
108	-/
109	@[simp]
110	theorem prod_eq_inf [SemilatticeInf Œ±] [OrderTop Œ±] (x y : Œ±) : Limits.prod x y = x ‚äì y :=
111	  calc
112	    Limits.prod x y = limit (pair x y) := rfl
113	    _ = Finset.univ.inf (pair x y).obj := by rw [finite_limit_eq_finset_univ_inf (pair.{u} x y)]
114	    _ = x ‚äì (y ‚äì ‚ä§) := rfl
115	    -- Note: finset.inf is realized as a fold, hence the definitional equality
116	    _ = x ‚äì y := by rw [inf_top_eq]
117	
118	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
119	instance (priority := 100) [SemilatticeSup Œ±] [OrderBot Œ±] : HasBinaryCoproducts Œ± := by
120	  have : ‚àÄ x y : Œ±, HasColimit (pair x y) := by
121	    letI := hasFiniteColimits_of_hasFiniteColimits_of_size.{u} Œ±
122	    infer_instance
123	  apply hasBinaryCoproducts_of_hasColimit_pair
124	
125	/-- The binary coproduct in the category of a `SemilatticeSup` with `OrderBot` is the same as the
126	supremum.
127	-/
128	@[simp]
129	theorem coprod_eq_sup [SemilatticeSup Œ±] [OrderBot Œ±] (x y : Œ±) : Limits.coprod x y = x ‚äî y :=
130	  calc
131	    Limits.coprod x y = colimit (pair x y) := rfl
132	    _ = Finset.univ.sup (pair x y).obj := by rw [finite_colimit_eq_finset_univ_sup (pair x y)]
133	    _ = x ‚äî (y ‚äî ‚ä•) := rfl
134	    -- Note: Finset.sup is realized as a fold, hence the definitional equality
135	    _ = x ‚äî y := by rw [sup_bot_eq]
136	
137	/-- The pullback in the category of a `SemilatticeInf` with `OrderTop` is the same as the infimum
138	over the objects.
139	-/
140	@[simp]
141	theorem pullback_eq_inf [SemilatticeInf Œ±] [OrderTop Œ±] {x y z : Œ±} (f : x ‚ü∂ z) (g : y ‚ü∂ z) :
142	    pullback f g = x ‚äì y :=
143	  calc
144	    pullback f g = limit (cospan f g) := rfl
145	    _ = Finset.univ.inf (cospan f g).obj := by rw [finite_limit_eq_finset_univ_inf]
146	    _ = z ‚äì (x ‚äì (y ‚äì ‚ä§)) := rfl
147	    _ = z ‚äì (x ‚äì y) := by rw [inf_top_eq]
148	    _ = x ‚äì y := inf_eq_right.mpr (inf_le_of_left_le f.le)
149	
150	/-- The pushout in the category of a `SemilatticeSup` with `OrderBot` is the same as the supremum
151	over the objects.
152	-/
153	@[simp]
154	theorem pushout_eq_sup [SemilatticeSup Œ±] [OrderBot Œ±] (x y z : Œ±) (f : z ‚ü∂ x) (g : z ‚ü∂ y) :
155	    pushout f g = x ‚äî y :=
156	  calc
157	    pushout f g = colimit (span f g) := rfl
158	    _ = Finset.univ.sup (span f g).obj := by rw [finite_colimit_eq_finset_univ_sup]
159	    _ = z ‚äî (x ‚äî (y ‚äî ‚ä•)) := rfl
160	    _ = z ‚äî (x ‚äî y) := by rw [sup_bot_eq]
161	    _ = x ‚äî y := sup_eq_right.mpr (le_sup_of_le_left f.le)
162	
163	end Semilattice
164	
165	variable {Œ± : Type u} [CompleteLattice Œ±] {J : Type w} [Category.{w'} J]
166	
167	/-- The limit cone over any functor into a complete lattice.
168	-/
169	@[simps]
170	def limitCone (F : J ‚•§ Œ±) : LimitCone F where
171	  cone :=
172	    { pt := iInf F.obj
173	      œÄ := { app := fun _ => homOfLE (sInf_le (Set.mem_range_self _)) } }
174	  isLimit :=
175	    { lift := fun s =>
176	        homOfLE (le_sInf (by rintro _ ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü©; exact (s.œÄ.app j).le)) }
177	
178	/-- The colimit cocone over any functor into a complete lattice.
179	-/
180	@[simps]
181	def colimitCocone (F : J ‚•§ Œ±) : ColimitCocone F where
182	  cocone :=
183	    { pt := iSup F.obj
184	      Œπ := { app := fun _ => homOfLE (le_sSup (Set.mem_range_self _)) } }
185	  isColimit :=
186	    { desc := fun s =>
187	        homOfLE (sSup_le (by rintro _ ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü©; exact (s.Œπ.app j).le)) }
188	
189	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
190	instance (priority := 100) hasLimits_of_completeLattice : HasLimitsOfSize.{w, w'} Œ± where
191	  has_limits_of_shape _ := { has_limit := fun F => HasLimit.mk (limitCone F) }
192	
193	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
194	instance (priority := 100) hasColimits_of_completeLattice : HasColimitsOfSize.{w, w'} Œ± where
195	  has_colimits_of_shape _ := { has_colimit := fun F => HasColimit.mk (colimitCocone F) }
196	
197	/-- The limit of a functor into a complete lattice is the infimum of the objects in the image.
198	-/
199	theorem limit_eq_iInf (F : J ‚•§ Œ±) : limit F = iInf F.obj :=
200	  (IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso (limit.isLimit F) (limitCone F).isLimit).to_eq
201	
202	/-- The colimit of a functor into a complete lattice is the supremum of the objects in the image.
203	-/
204	theorem colimit_eq_iSup (F : J ‚•§ Œ±) : colimit F = iSup F.obj :=
205	  (IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit F) (colimitCocone F).isColimit).to_eq
206	
207	end CategoryTheory.Limits.CompleteLattice

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechHigherDirectImage
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open Scheme.Modules

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî `CombinatorialCech`

The constant-coefficient combinatorial core of standard-cover ƒåech acyclicity
(planner step **L3**, `analogies/p3-localisation.md`).  After localising the
extended ƒåech complex at a spanning element `s_r` (planner step L2, via
`exact_of_isLocalized_span`), the fixed index `r` becomes globally available, and
the alternating ƒåech coboundary admits the explicit contracting homotopy
`h(t)_{i‚ÇÄ‚Ä¶i_p} = t_{r i‚ÇÄ‚Ä¶i_p}` of Stacks
`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`.

This section formalises that homotopy and its consequences for the **constant
coefficient** complex `C·µñ = (Fin (p+1) ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M` with alternating coface
differential ‚Äî abstracted away from the localisation/geometry so the purely
combinatorial cancellation (`combHomotopy`) and the simplicial identity
(`combDifferential_comp`, `d¬≤ = 0`) are isolated and reusable.  The remaining
work to close `CechAcyclic.affine` is the **L1** categorical bridge identifying
the abstract `CechComplex` terms with the away-localisation modules `M_{s_œÉ}` and
its differential with this `combDifferential` (after localising at `s_r`); these
lemmas are then fed to `exact_of_isLocalized_span` node by node.

These declarations are `private`: they exist only to close `CechAcyclic.affine`
in this file.  The intended blueprint home is the `\lean{...}` bundle of
`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`. -/

namespace CombinatorialCech

variable {Œπ : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ‚Ñï}

/-- Alternating coface (ƒåech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
`C‚Åø = (Fin n ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M ‚ü∂ C‚Åø‚Å∫¬π`, `(d t)(œÉ) = ‚àë‚±º (-1) ≤ ‚Ä¢ t (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove)`.
The argument `œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove` is the `(n)`-tuple obtained from the `(n+1)`-tuple
`œÉ` by deleting the `j`-th index. -/
private def combDifferential (t : (Fin n ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M) : (Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M :=
  fun œÉ => ‚àë j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (j : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢ t (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove)

/-- The contracting homotopy of the localised complex: prepend the fixed
(globally invertible after localising at `s_r`) index `r`.
`(h u)(œÑ) = u (Fin.cons r œÑ)`. -/
private def combHomotopy (r : Œπ) (u : (Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M) : (Fin n ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M :=
  fun œÑ => u (Fin.cons r œÑ)

@[simp] private lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : Œπ) :
    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
  funext œÑ; simp [combHomotopy]

/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(j+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the
`j`-th coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : Œπ) (œÉ : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
    (Fin.cons r œÉ : Fin (n + 2) ‚Üí Œπ) ‚àò (k.succ).succAbove
      = Fin.cons r (œÉ ‚àò k.succAbove) := by
  funext l
  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
  ¬∑ simp
