261	
262	/-- **Additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The free‚ÄìYoneda hom bijection
263	`Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda V), F) ‚âÉ F(V)` is an isomorphism of abelian groups: the source is
264	the (preadditive) hom-group of presheaves of modules and the target is the section group
265	`F(V)`. This is the per-multi-index additive core of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
266	(blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`); taking products over multi-indices and
267	intertwining with the differential upgrades it to the full complex isomorphism. Additivity
268	holds because, by `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, the bijection is evaluation of the
269	component `œà.app (op V)` (a module map, hence additive) on a fixed generator, and the
270	component of a sum of morphisms is the sum of components.
271	
272	Project-local because Mathlib packages the underlying bijection (`freeHomEquiv` ‚àò
273	`yonedaEquiv`) only at the level of types, not as an `AddEquiv`. -/
274	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
275	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
276	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ‚ü∂ F) ‚âÉ+
277	      (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) where
278	  toFun := freeYonedaHomEquiv V F
279	  invFun := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).symm
280	  left_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).left_inv
281	  right_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).right_inv
282	  map_add' f g := by
283	    rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply,
284	      PresheafOfModules.add_app]
285	    change (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f.app (Opposite.op V) + g.app (Opposite.op V))) _ = _
286	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add]; rfl

288	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî section {\v C}ech complex -/
289	
290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : Œπ ‚Üí Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`‚àè_{œÉ : Fin (p+1) ‚Üí Œπ} F(‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ‚ü∂ [n]` acts at multi-index `œÉ : Fin (n+1) ‚Üí Œπ` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `œÉ ‚àò f` and then restricting along `‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k) ‚â§ ‚®Ö‚Çó U (œÉ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ‚•§ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d¬≤ = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ‚àè·∂ú (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
307	    Pi.œÄ _ (œÉ ‚àò f.toOrderHom) ‚â´
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    -- The map at `ùüô n` projects to index `œÉ ‚àò id = œÉ` and restricts along the thin
311	    -- (reflexive) `Opens` hom `homOfLE ‚ãØ : ‚®Ö U(œÉ k) ‚ü∂ ‚®Ö U(œÉ k)`, which is the identity.
312	    ext1 œÉ
313	    simp only [Pi.lift_œÄ]
314	    -- the restriction hom is an endomorphism in the thin `Opens·µí·µñ` category, hence `ùüô`
315	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (ùüô _)]
316	    -- `erw` is needed: the `Subsingleton.elim` rewrite leaves the term not type-correct
317	    -- under `instances` transparency, so plain `rw [Functor.map_id]` cannot match.
318	    erw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]
319	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
320	    apply Pi.hom_ext
321	    intro œÉ
322	    simp only [Pi.lift_œÄ, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
323	    rw [‚Üê Category.assoc, Pi.lift_œÄ, Category.assoc, ‚Üê Functor.map_comp]
324	    congr 1
325	
326	/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
327	(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).
328	
329	The cochain complex `ƒå‚Ä¢(ùí∞, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
330	`‚àè_{œÉ : Fin (p+1) ‚Üí Œπ} F(‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
331	obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
332	section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
333	it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
334	`Hom_{PMod}(K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢, F) ‚âÖ ƒå‚Ä¢(ùí∞, F)`.
335	
336	Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
337	noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
338	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ‚Ñï :=
339	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)
340	
341	end AlgebraicGeometry

1490	lemmas (c1)‚Äì(c3) move that abstract complex to the concrete localised-module complex
1491	`SectionCechModule.dDiff` (whose positive-degree exactness `dDiff_exact` is step (a)),
1492	and read off homology vanishing. -/

1494	section SectionCechBridge
1495	
1496	open CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicTopology
1497	
1498	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1499	
1500	/-- **(c1) Element-level product equivalence** (`lem:section_cech_product_equiv`):
1501	the underlying type of the degree-`p` object `‚àè·∂ú_œÉ F(‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k))` of the section
1502	{\v C}ech cosimplicial object is the dependent product `‚àè_œÉ ToType (F(‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k)))`.
1503	Supplied by `CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv` (the forgetful functor of
1504	`Ab` preserves discrete products). -/
1505	noncomputable def sectionCechProductEquiv {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1506	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ‚Ñï) :
1507	    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ‚âÉ
1508	      (‚àÄ œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ, ToType (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))) :=
1509	  Concrete.productEquiv
1510	    (fun œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
1511	
1512	/-- Coordinate projection of `sectionCechProductEquiv`: the `œÉ`-component is the
1513	underlying group map of the categorical projection `Pi.œÄ ‚Ä¶ œÉ`.  A definitional
1514	restatement of `Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply`, named so that downstream proofs can
1515	`rw` it without fighting the `Concrete.productEquiv` coercion. -/
1516	lemma sectionCechProductEquiv_apply {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1517	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ‚Ñï)
1518	    (y : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
1519	    (œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) :
1520	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F p y œÉ
1521	      = ConcreteCategory.hom
1522	          (Pi.œÄ (fun œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) œÉ) y :=
1523	  Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply _ y œÉ
1524	
1525	/-- **Homology-to-exactness reduction** (the `Ab`-side of `lem:section_cech_ab_exact`):
1526	the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the section {\v C}ech complex vanishes once the
1527	underlying group homomorphisms of the two consecutive coface differentials
1528	`objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence.  Pure homological algebra: combines
1529	`exactAt_iff_isZero_homology`, `exactAt_iff'`, and the abelian-group exactness
1530	criterion `ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact`. -/
1531	lemma sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact {Œπ : Type u}
1532	    (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ‚Ñï)
1533	    (h : Function.Exact
1534	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
1535	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
1536	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1)))) :
1537	    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1)) := by
1538	  rw [‚Üê HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
1539	      (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
1540	      ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact]
1541	  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
1542	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
1543	    CochainComplex.of_d (fun n => (sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk n))
1544	      (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)) q
1545	  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
1546	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
1547	    CochainComplex.of_d (fun n => (sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk n))
1548	      (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)) (q + 1)
1549	  change Function.Exact
1550	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
1551	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)))
1552	  rw [hf, hg]
1553	  exact h

1555	/-- Application of a finite sum of `Ab`-morphisms distributes over the sum. -/
1556	private lemma ab_hom_finsetSum_apply {A B : Ab.{u}} {Œ∫ : Type*}
1557	    (s : Finset Œ∫) (f : Œ∫ ‚Üí (A ‚ü∂ B)) (t : ToType A) :
1558	    ConcreteCategory.hom (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) t = ‚àë i ‚àà s, ConcreteCategory.hom (f i) t := by
1559	  classical
1560	  induction s using Finset.induction with
1561	  | empty => simp
1562	  | insert a s ha ih =>
1563	      rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, AddCommGrpCat.hom_add_apply, ih]
1564	
1565	/-- The `i`-th section {\v C}ech face restriction at a multi-index `œÉ` of the
1566	cosimplicial section object: the presheaf restriction of `F` from the deleted-face
1567	intersection `‚®Ö‚Çó U (œÉ (Œ¥ i l))` to the full intersection `‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k)`.  Factored as a
1568	named `def` so the cosimplicial differential (`sectionCech_objD_apply`) refers to it
1569	by name rather than re-elaborating the `homOfLE (le_iInf ‚Ä¶)` term. -/
1570	noncomputable def sectionCechFaceRestr {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1571	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {q : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin (q + 2) ‚Üí Œπ) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
1572	    F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö l, U ((œÉ ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom) l)))
1573	      ‚ü∂ F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))) :=
1574	  F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf
1575	    (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom l)))).op
1576	
1577	/-- **(c2, abstract) Coface match**: the underlying group action of the section
1578	{\v C}ech coface differential `objD q`, read through the product equivalence, is the
1579	alternating sum of the presheaf restriction maps applied to the deleted-face
1580	coordinates ‚Äî exactly the shape of `SectionCechModule.dDiff`.  This is purely about
1581	the cosimplicial structure of `sectionCechCosimplicial`; no sheaf identification yet. -/
1582	lemma sectionCech_objD_apply {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1583	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ‚Ñï)
1584	    (t : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk q)))
1585	    (œÉ : Fin (q + 2) ‚Üí Œπ) :
1586	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F (q + 1)
1587	        (ConcreteCategory.hom
1588	          (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t) œÉ
1589	      = ‚àë i : Fin (q + 2), (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (i : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢
1590	          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F œÉ i)
1591	            (sectionCechProductEquiv U F q t (œÉ ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom)) := by
1592	  classical
1593	  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply,
1594	      show ConcreteCategory.hom
1595	            (Pi.œÄ (fun œÉ : Fin (q + 2) ‚Üí Œπ => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) œÉ)
1596	            (ConcreteCategory.hom
1597	              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t)
1598	          = ConcreteCategory.hom
1599	              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q
1600	                ‚â´ Pi.œÄ (fun œÉ : Fin (q + 2) ‚Üí Œπ => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) œÉ) t
1601	          from rfl,
1602	      AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, Preadditive.sum_comp, ab_hom_finsetSum_apply]
1603	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
1604	  rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]
1605	  simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_zsmul, AddMonoidHom.smul_apply]
1606	  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]

37	For a family `s : Œπ ‚Üí R` of ring elements and a ƒåech multi-index `œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ`, the
38	`(p + 1)`-fold intersection of distinguished opens `‚®Ö‚Çñ D(s_{œÉ k})` is again a distinguished
39	open, namely `D(‚àè‚Çñ s_{œÉ k})`; hence it lies in the basis of distinguished opens. This discharges
40	the `faces_mem` field of the affine cover system. Project-local: re-export of `basicOpen_sprod`
41	in the membership shape the `BasisCovSystem.faces_mem` field consumes. -/
42	theorem affine_faces_mem {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {Œπ : Type u} (s : Œπ ‚Üí R)

45	      ‚àà Set.range (fun f : R => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)) :=
46	  ‚ü®‚àè k, s (œÉ k), (basicOpen_sprod (p + 1) s œÉ).symm‚ü©

51	family `s : Œπ ‚Üí R`, the `i`-th member of the standard affine open cover
52	`affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs` of `Spec R` has open range exactly the distinguished open
53	`D(s_i)`, i.e. `coverOpen ùí∞ i = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)`. This is the open-level half of the
54	covering-datum bridge (`lem:cover_datum_bridge`): funext-rewriting the raw family `c.2 = fun i ‚Ü¶
55	D(s_i)` to `coverOpen ùí∞` identifies the two section ƒåech complexes, so the ƒåech cohomology computed

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî `CombinatorialCech`

The constant-coefficient combinatorial core of standard-cover ƒåech acyclicity
(planner step **L3**, `analogies/p3-localisation.md`).  After localising the
extended ƒåech complex at a spanning element `s_r` (planner step L2, via
`exact_of_isLocalized_span`), the fixed index `r` becomes globally available, and
the alternating ƒåech coboundary admits the explicit contracting homotopy
`h(t)_{i‚ÇÄ‚Ä¶i_p} = t_{r i‚ÇÄ‚Ä¶i_p}` of Stacks
`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`.

This section formalises that homotopy and its consequences for the **constant
coefficient** complex `C·µñ = (Fin (p+1) ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M` with alternating coface
differential ‚Äî abstracted away from the localisation/geometry so the purely
combinatorial cancellation (`combHomotopy`) and the simplicial identity
(`combDifferential_comp`, `d¬≤ = 0`) are isolated and reusable.  The remaining
work to close `CechAcyclic.affine` is the **L1** categorical bridge identifying
the abstract `CechComplex` terms with the away-localisation modules `M_{s_œÉ}` and
its differential with this `combDifferential` (after localising at `s_r`); these
lemmas are then fed to `exact_of_isLocalized_span` node by node.

These declarations are `private`: they exist only to close `CechAcyclic.affine`
in this file.  The intended blueprint home is the `\lean{...}` bundle of
`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`. -/

namespace CombinatorialCech

variable {Œπ : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ‚Ñï}

/-- Alternating coface (ƒåech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
`C‚Åø = (Fin n ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M ‚ü∂ C‚Åø‚Å∫¬π`, `(d t)(œÉ) = ‚àë‚±º (-1) ≤ ‚Ä¢ t (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove)`.
The argument `œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove` is the `(n)`-tuple obtained from the `(n+1)`-tuple
`œÉ` by deleting the `j`-th index. -/
private def combDifferential (t : (Fin n ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M) : (Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M :=
  fun œÉ => ‚àë j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (j : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢ t (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove)

/-- The contracting homotopy of the localised complex: prepend the fixed
(globally invertible after localising at `s_r`) index `r`.
`(h u)(œÑ) = u (Fin.cons r œÑ)`. -/
private def combHomotopy (r : Œπ) (u : (Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M) : (Fin n ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí M :=
  fun œÑ => u (Fin.cons r œÑ)

@[simp] private lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : Œπ) :
    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî functoriality of the section ƒåech complex -/

/-- The cosimplicial morphism of section ƒåech objects induced by a morphism `œÜ` of
presheaves of modules, acting coordinatewise by the underlying presheaf morphism on each
basic-open section group. Project-local: `sectionCechCosimplicial` has no functoriality in
Mathlib. -/
noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicialMap {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (œÜ : F ‚ü∂ G) :
    sectionCechCosimplicial U F ‚ü∂ sectionCechCosimplicial U G where
  app n := Limits.Pi.map (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>
    ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œÜ).app (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))

    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext

      Limits.Pi.map_œÄ_assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_œÄ_assoc]
    congr 1
    exact ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map œÜ).naturality _

/-- The section ƒåech cosimplicial object as a functor in the coefficient presheaf of modules.
Project-local: packages `sectionCechCosimplicialMap` with functoriality so the induced
short-complex maps compose and respect zero. -/

        (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) = ùüô _
    rw [show (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len + 1) ‚Üí Œπ =>

163	arbitrary open cover `W : Œ± ‚Üí (Spec R).Opens` of it, there is a finite standard subcover refining
164	it: finitely many `g : Fin n ‚Üí R` with `D(f) = ‚®Ü·µ¢ D(g·µ¢)` and each `D(g·µ¢) ‚â§ W (œÜ i)`.  This is the
165	refinement step invoked in `affine_surj_of_vanishing`.  Project-local: combines quasi-compactness of
166	`D(f)` with the basic-open basis. -/

169	    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) ‚â§ ‚®Ü a, W a) :
170	    ‚àÉ (n : ‚Ñï) (g : Fin n ‚Üí R) (œÜ : Fin n ‚Üí Œ±),
171	      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) = ‚®Ü i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ‚àß
172	      ‚àÄ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ‚â§ W (œÜ i) := by

176	  have hbasis := PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens (R := R)
177	  -- `B r` is the distinguished open `D(r)` typed as an open of `Spec R`.
178	  let B : R ‚Üí (Spec R).Opens := fun r => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
179	  -- Index type: basic opens contained in `Uf ‚äì W a`, carrying the witness `a`.

182	  have hopen : ‚àÄ i, IsOpen (cover i) := fun i => (B i.1.1).isOpen
183	  -- The chosen basic opens cover `Uf`.

185	    intro x hx

193	    obtain ‚ü®r, hr‚ü© := hvrange
194	    have hr' : (B r : Set ‚Ü•(Spec R)) = v := hr
195	    have hle : B r ‚â§ Uf ‚äì W a := by
196	      rw [‚Üê SetLike.coe_subset_coe, hr']; exact hvsub
197	    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ‚ü®‚ü®(r, a), hle‚ü©, ?_‚ü©

200	  -- Quasi-compactness: extract a finite subcover.
201	  obtain ‚ü®t, ht‚ü© := hK.elim_finite_subcover cover hopen hsub
202	  -- Repackage the finite index set `t : Finset I` as `Fin n`.

222	/-- **Section surjectivity for the affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG, `surj_of_vanishing` field;
223	Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`). Let `S : 0 ‚Üí S‚ÇÅ ‚Üí S‚ÇÇ ‚Üí S‚ÇÉ ‚Üí 0` be a short exact sequence of
224	`O_X`-modules on `Spec R` whose left term `S‚ÇÅ` has vanishing positive ƒåech cohomology over every
225	standard cover `i ‚Ü¶ D(g·µ¢)` (`hvanish`). Then the section map `S‚ÇÇ(D f) ‚Üí S‚ÇÉ(D f)` is surjective over
226	every distinguished open `D f`. This discharges the `surj_of_vanishing` field of `affineCoverSystem`.
227	
228	Proof: `S.g` is an epi of `O_X`-modules, so (via `toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms` + the sheaf
229	local-surjectivity criterion) the underlying map of abelian presheaves is locally surjective. A
230	section `t ‚àà S‚ÇÉ(D f)` therefore lifts locally on an open cover of `D f`; refine it to a standard
231	cover `D(g·µ¢)` (`standard_cover_cofinal`) carrying local lifts, feed the cover, the lifts and the
232	vanishing `»û¬π(ùí∞, S‚ÇÅ) = 0` to the ƒåech `»û¬π`-surjectivity criterion `ses_cech_h1`, and glue to a
233	global lift. Project-local: the affine instantiation of the `ses_cech_h1` criterion. -/
234	theorem affine_surj_of_vanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
235	    (S : ShortComplex (Spec R).Modules) (hS : S.ShortExact)
236	    (hvanish : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (g : Fin n ‚Üí R) (f' : R),
237	      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f' : (Spec R).Opens)
238	        = ‚®Ü i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) ‚Üí
239	      ‚àÄ (q : ‚Ñï), 0 < q ‚Üí
240	        IsZero (cechCohomology (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
241	          ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X‚ÇÅ) q))
242	    (f : R) :
243	    Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
244	      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
245	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g)).app
246	          (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) := by
247	  classical
248	  set V‚ÇÄ : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f with hV‚ÇÄ
249	  set FX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X‚ÇÅ with hFX
250	  set GX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X‚ÇÇ with hGX
251	  set HX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X‚ÇÉ with hHX
252	  set fŒπ := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
253	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.f) with hfŒπ
254	  set gœÄ := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
255	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g) with hgœÄ
256	  -- Step 1: a module epi becomes a locally surjective map of abelian presheaves.
257	  haveI hgepi : Epi S.g := hS.epi_g
258	  have hepiTS : Epi ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g) :=
259	    @Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf)
260	      (toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) _ _ S.g hgepi
261	  have hls : TopCat.Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective gœÄ :=
262	    (Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi' AddCommGrpCat.{u}
263	      ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g)).mpr hepiTS
264	  rw [TopCat.Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff] at hls
265	  intro t
266	  -- Step 2: per-point local lifts of `t`.
267	  have hch : ‚àÄ p : ‚Ü•V‚ÇÄ, ‚àÉ (W : (Spec R).Opens) (hWle : W ‚â§ V‚ÇÄ)

366	/-- **The affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG, `def:affine_cover_system`). For `X = Spec R`, the
367	basis `B` is the distinguished opens `D f` (`f : R`) and the admissible coverings `Cov` are the
368	standard finite covers `i ‚Ü¶ D(g·µ¢)` (indexed by `ULift (Fin n)`, so as to land in `Type u`). The
369	three nontrivial fields are discharged by:
370	- `faces_mem` ‚Üê `affine_faces_mem` (finite intersections of distinguished opens are distinguished);
371	- `surj_of_vanishing` ‚Üê `affine_surj_of_vanishing` (the `ses_cech_h1` section-surjectivity criterion);
372	- `injective_acyclic` ‚Üê `injective_cech_acyclicFam` (cover-agnostic injective ƒåech-acyclicity, applied
373	  directly to the distinguished opens of each standard cover ‚Äî no `Spec R_f` restriction detour).
374	Project-local: the affine instantiation of `BasisCovSystem` consumed by `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`. -/
375	noncomputable def affineCoverSystem (R : CommRingCat.{u}) : BasisCovSystem (Spec R) where
376	  B := Set.range (fun f : R => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens))
377	  Cov := { c : CovDatum (Spec R) | ‚àÉ (n : ‚Ñï) (g : Fin n ‚Üí R) (f : R),
378	    c = ‚ü®ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)‚ü© ‚àß
379	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)

396	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî base change of the cover spanning condition -/
397	
398	/-- **A standard cover of `D(f)` spans the unit ideal of `R_f`.** If the distinguished opens
399	`D(g·µ¢)` cover `D(f)` in `Spec R`, then the images `g·µ¢ ‚Ü¶ R_f` of the cover family span the whole
400	ring `R_f = Localization.Away f`: pulled back along `Spec R_f ‚âÖ D(f) ‚Ü™ Spec R` the union of the
401	`D(g·µ¢)` becomes `D(f)` itself, which is all of `Spec R_f` since `f` is a unit there. Project-local:
402	the spanning hypothesis required to apply the standard-cover tilde {\v C}ech vanishing
403	`sectionCech_affine_vanishing` over `R_f` in the quasi-coherent seed's change-of-base leaf. -/
404	theorem affine_cover_span_localizationAway {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {Œπ : Type u} [Finite Œπ]
405	    (g : Œπ ‚Üí R) (f : R)
406	    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
407	      = ‚®Ü i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i)) :
408	    Ideal.span (Set.range (fun i => algebraMap R (Localization.Away f) (g i))) = ‚ä§ := by
409	  rw [‚Üê PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff]
410	  simp only [‚Üê PrimeSpectrum.comap_basicOpen]
411	  rw [‚Üê map_iSup, ‚Üê hcov, PrimeSpectrum.comap_basicOpen, eq_top_iff]
412	  rintro p -
413	  rw [PrimeSpectrum.mem_basicOpen]
414	  exact fun hmem => p.isPrime.ne_top
415	    (Ideal.eq_top_of_isUnit_mem _ hmem (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit f))

418	
419	/-- **{\v C}ech cohomology transports along an isomorphism of coefficient presheaves.** An
420	isomorphism `e : F ‚âÖ G` of presheaves of `ùí™_X`-modules induces an isomorphism of section {\v C}ech
421	complexes (`sectionCechComplexFunctor`), hence of their degree-`p` homologies; so vanishing of
422	`»û·µñ(ùí∞, F)` transfers to `»û·µñ(ùí∞, G)`. Project-local: the naturality used to reduce the
423	quasi-coherent {\v C}ech-vanishing seed to the tilde case via `F ‚âÖ ~(ŒìF)`. -/
424	theorem cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
425	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (e : F ‚âÖ G) (p : ‚Ñï)
426	    (h : IsZero (cechCohomology U F p)) : IsZero (cechCohomology U G p) :=
427	  h.of_iso ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor Ab.{u} (ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï) p).mapIso
428	    ((sectionCechComplexFunctor U).mapIso e)).symm

431	
432	/-- **Reduction of the quasi-coherent {\v C}ech-vanishing seed to the tilde case.**  For a
433	quasi-coherent `ùí™_{Spec R}`-module `F`, the affine structure theorem `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`
434	gives `F ‚âÖ ~M` with `M = Œì(Spec R, F)`; transporting along this isomorphism
435	(`cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso`) reduces the seed `HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystem R) F`
436	to the tilde-case standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing supplied in `htilde`.  Project-local: isolates
437	the single remaining geometric obligation (positive-degree {\v C}ech vanishing of `~M` over a
438	standard cover of a distinguished open `D(f)`), which is the change-of-base-to-`R_f` leaf. -/
439	theorem affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
440	    (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
441	    (htilde : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (g : Fin n ‚Üí R) (f : R),
442	        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
443	          = ‚®Ü i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) ‚Üí
444	        ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí
445	          IsZero (cechCohomology
446	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
447	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
448	              (tilde (moduleSpecŒìFunctor.obj F))) p)) :
449	    HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystem R) F := by
450	  intro c hc p hp
451	  obtain ‚ü®n, g, f, rfl, hcov‚ü© := hc
452	  refine cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso _
453	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).mapIso
454	      (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).symm) p ?_
455	  exact htilde n g f hcov p hp

459	/-- **Serre vanishing on affines, reduced to the tilde {\v C}ech-vanishing leaf** (Stacks 02KG).
460	Instantiates the basis-comparison criterion `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO) at the affine
461	cover system `affineCoverSystem R`, taking the whole affine `‚ä§ = D(1)` for the basic open: the
462	absolute cohomology `H·µñ(Spec R, F) = Ext·µñ(jShriekOU ‚ä§, F)` of a quasi-coherent `F` vanishes for
463	`p > 0`, modulo the standard-cover tilde {\v C}ech vanishing `htilde`.  Carries
464	`[EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules]` exactly as `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` does.  Project-local:
465	verifies the full Lane-1 assembly end-to-end, so that the blueprint targets `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh`
466	and `affine_serre_vanishing` both reduce to the single residual `htilde` (the change-of-base-to-`R_f`

469	    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
470	    (htilde : ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (g : Fin n ‚Üí R) (f : R),
471	        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
472	          = ‚®Ü i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) ‚Üí
473	        ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí
474	          IsZero (cechCohomology
475	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
476	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
477	              (tilde (moduleSpecŒìFunctor.obj F))) p))
478	    (p : ‚Ñï) (hp : 0 < p)
479	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU (‚ä§ : (Spec R).Opens)) F p) : e = 0 :=
480	  cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis (affineCoverSystem R)

486	/-- **The standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing residual `htilde`, discharged unconditionally.**  For a
487	quasi-coherent `ùí™_{Spec R}`-module `F` with global module `M = Œì(Spec R, F)`, the positive-degree
488	section {\v C}ech cohomology of the tilde sheaf `~M` over any standard cover `i ‚Ü¶ D(g·µ¢)` of a

492	Project-local: bundles the residual leaf in the precise shape both 02KG tops consume. -/
493	private theorem affine_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (F : (Spec R).Modules)
494	    (n : ‚Ñï) (g : Fin n ‚Üí R) (f : R)
495	    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
496	      = ‚®Ü i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
497	    (p : ‚Ñï) (hp : 0 < p) :
498	    IsZero (cechCohomology

501	        (tilde (moduleSpecŒìFunctor.obj F))) p) :=

508	`HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystem R) F`. Obtained by discharging the `htilde` hypothesis of

530	
531	The declarations below **enlarge** the affine cover system from the distinguished-open basis
532	`{D f}` to the basis of *all* affine opens of `Spec R`, so that the basis-comparison criterion
533	`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` yields `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, H) = 0` for any affine open `V` (not just a
534	distinguished `D f`), entirely inside `(Spec R).Modules` (no restriction functor). The route is the
535	ambient `enlarge-B` of `analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md`: the only structurally new pieces
536	are the generalisations of `standard_cover_cofinal` and `affine_surj_of_vanishing` from `D f` to a
537	general affine open (swapping `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen` for `IsAffineOpen.isCompact`); the
538	`faces_mem`/`injective_acyclic` fields are unchanged (faces are distinguished ‚äÜ affine; injective
539	acyclicity is cover-agnostic). The positive-degree quasi-coherent ƒåech-vanishing *seed* over covers
540	of a general affine open is the single remaining geometric residual; it is carried as an explicit
541	hypothesis `hseed` (the general analogue of `affine_tildeVanishing`, needing a change-of-base to
542	`Œì(V)` rather than to a single `R_f`). -/
543	
544	/-- **Distinguished opens of `Spec R` are affine opens.**  `D(r) ‚äÜ Spec R` is affine, being
545	isomorphic to `Spec R[1/r]` via `basicOpenIsoSpecAway`.  Project-local: places each distinguished
546	open into the enlarged affine-open basis, discharging `faces_mem` for `affineCoverSystemGeneral`. -/
547	theorem isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (r : R) :
548	    IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) :=
549	  IsAffine.of_isIso (basicOpenIsoSpecAway r).hom
550	
551	/-- **Standard covers are cofinal among open covers of a general affine open** (Stacks 02KG,
552	Tag 009L), generalising `standard_cover_cofinal` from a distinguished `D(f)` to an *arbitrary affine
553	open* `V` of `Spec R`.  Given an arbitrary open cover `W : Œ± ‚Üí (Spec R).Opens` of `V`, there is a
554	finite standard subcover refining it: finitely many `g : Fin n ‚Üí R` with `V = ‚®Ü·µ¢ D(g·µ¢)` and each
555	`D(g·µ¢) ‚â§ W (œÜ i)`.  The only change from `standard_cover_cofinal` is the source of

560	    ‚àÉ (n : ‚Ñï) (g : Fin n ‚Üí R) (œÜ : Fin n ‚Üí Œ±),

562	      ‚àÄ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ‚â§ W (œÜ i) := by

586	    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ‚ü®‚ü®(r, a), hle‚ü©, ?_‚ü©

590	  obtain ‚ü®t, ht‚ü© := hK.elim_finite_subcover cover hopen hsub

597	      refine TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ‚ü®e ‚ü®i, hit‚ü©, ?_‚ü©
598	      change x ‚àà B (e.symm (e ‚ü®i, hit‚ü©)).1.1.1

636	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.f) with hfŒπ

750	/-- **The enlarged affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG), with basis `B` the *all affine opens* of

752	`Cov` the standard finite covers `i ‚Ü¶ D(g·µ¢)` whose union `‚®Ü·µ¢ D(g·µ¢)` is affine.  The three fields:

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî L2 homological core (quotient vanishing) -/

/-- **Quotient preserves vanishing higher cohomology (homological core, Stacks 01EO L2).**
Given a short exact sequence of cochain complexes `0 ‚Üí X‚ÇÅ ‚Üí X‚ÇÇ ‚Üí X‚ÇÉ ‚Üí 0` in which the middle
term `X‚ÇÇ` has vanishing positive homology (the injective/acyclic term) and the left term `X‚ÇÅ`
has vanishing positive homology (condition (3)), the right term `X‚ÇÉ` (the quotient) again has
vanishing positive homology. Proved by the homology long exact sequence: the connecting map
gives `H·µñ(X‚ÇÉ) ‚âÖ H·µñ‚Å∫¬π(X‚ÇÅ) = 0`. Project-local: the 01EO dimension-shift needs this abstract
shape; it is then instantiated at the section ƒåech complexes. -/
theorem cechHomology_quotient_vanishing (T : ShortComplex (CochainComplex Ab.{u} ‚Ñï))
    (hT : T.ShortExact) (hI : ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (T.X‚ÇÇ.homology p))
    (hF : ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (T.X‚ÇÅ.homology p)) :
    ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (T.X‚ÇÉ.homology p) := by
  intro p hp
  have hrel : (ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï).Rel p (p + 1) := rfl
  have hiso : T.X‚ÇÉ.homology p ‚âÖ T.X‚ÇÅ.homology (p + 1) :=
    hT.Œ¥Iso p (p + 1) hrel (hI p hp) (hI (p + 1) (Nat.succ_pos p))
  exact (hF (p + 1) (Nat.succ_pos p)).of_iso hiso

/-- **Quotient preserves vanishing higher ƒåech cohomology (Stacks 01EO L2).** Concrete form in
terms of `cechCohomology`: with the section ƒåech short exact sequence of `P` (from L1), if the
middle term `I` (injective/acyclic, condition from `injective_cech_acyclic`) and the left term
`F` (condition (3)) both have vanishing positive ƒåech cohomology, then so does the quotient `Q`.
Project-local: instantiates `cechHomology_quotient_vanishing` at the section ƒåech complexes. -/
theorem quotient_cech_vanishing_of_basis {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (P : ShortComplex X.PresheafOfModules)
    (hSES : (sectionCechComplexShortComplex U P).ShortExact)
    (hI : ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (cechCohomology U P.X‚ÇÇ p))
    (hF : ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (cechCohomology U P.X‚ÇÅ p)) :
    ‚àÄ p, 0 < p ‚Üí IsZero (cechCohomology U P.X‚ÇÉ p) :=
  cechHomology_quotient_vanishing (sectionCechComplexShortComplex U P) hSES hI hF

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî per-face short exact sequence (01EO) -/

/-- The sections functor `Œì(V, -) : X.Modules ‚•§ Ab` over a fixed open `V`, as the composite of
the inclusion of sheaves of modules into presheaves of modules, the forgetful to presheaves of
abelian groups, and evaluation at `V`. Project-local: used only to package the left-exactness of
sections (each factor preserves finite limits) for the per-face short exact sequence. -/
noncomputable def sectionsFunctor (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.Modules ‚•§ Ab.{u} :=
  Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X ‚ãô PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ‚ãô
    (CategoryTheory.evaluation _ _).obj (Opposite.op V)

/-- **Per-face short exact sequence from a sheaf short exact sequence (Stacks 01EO).** Given a
short exact sequence `S : 0 ‚Üí F ‚Üí I ‚Üí Q ‚Üí 0` of sheaves of `O_X`-modules and the assumption that
on every face `V_œÉ = ‚®Ö‚Çñ U(œÉ k)` the section map `I(V_œÉ) ‚Üí Q(V_œÉ)` is surjective, the per-face
short complex `faceShortComplex U (S.map toPresheafOfModules) œÉ` is short exact. Mono and middle
exactness come from left-exactness of the sections functor (`sectionsFunctor` preserves finite
limits); the epi is the surjectivity hypothesis (supplied downstream by `ses_cech_h1`).
Project-local: produces the per-face hypothesis consumed by `cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis`. -/
theorem faceShortComplex_shortExact_of_sheaf_ses {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (S : ShortComplex X.Modules) (hS : S.ShortExact)
    (hsurj : ‚àÄ (p : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ), Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
      (faceShortComplex U (S.map (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X)) œÉ).g))
    (p : ‚Ñï) (œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) :
    (faceShortComplex U (S.map (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X)) œÉ).ShortExact := by
  haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
    unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
  haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))) := by
    unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
  haveI : Mono S.f := hS.mono_f
  change (S.map (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))).ShortExact
  have hex : (S.map (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))).Exact :=
    ShortComplex.Exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel hS.exact
      (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))) hS.mono_f inferInstance
  haveI : Mono (S.map (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))).f :=
    inferInstanceAs (Mono ((sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))).map S.f))
  haveI : Epi (S.map (sectionsFunctor (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))).g := by
    rw [AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective]
    exact hsurj p œÉ
  exact ShortComplex.ShortExact.mk hex

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî L3 base case `H¬π(U, F) = 0` -/

/-- **Sheaf-cohomology base case `H¬π(U, F) = 0` (Stacks 01EO L3).** Given a short exact
sequence `S : 0 ‚Üí F ‚Üí I ‚Üí Q ‚Üí 0` in `X.Modules` with `I = S.X‚ÇÇ` injective, and assuming the
section map `I(U) ‚Üí Q(U)` is surjective, the first absolute cohomology `H¬π(U, F) = Ext¬π(jShriekOU
U, F)` vanishes. The proof runs the covariant Ext long exact sequence: `H¬π(U, I) = 0` by injective
vanishing, and surjectivity of the section map transfers (via the natural `H‚Å∞ ‚âÖ Œì`) to
surjectivity of `H‚Å∞(U, I) ‚Üí H‚Å∞(U, Q)`, killing the connecting map. Project-local: assembles the
`AbsoluteCohomology` Ext wrappers into the 01EO base case. -/
theorem absoluteCohomology_one_eq_zero_of_basis (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) [Injective S.X‚ÇÇ]
    (hsurj : Function.Surjective
      (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map S.g).app (Opposite.op U))))
    (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X‚ÇÅ 1) : e = 0 := by
  have hef : e.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.f) (add_zero 1) = 0 :=
    absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective 0 U S.X‚ÇÇ _
  obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÉ, hx‚ÇÉ‚ü© := absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact‚ÇÅ U hS e hef (rfl : 0 + 1 = 1)
  have hgsurj : Function.Surjective
      (fun y : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X‚ÇÇ 0 => y.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ S.g) (add_zero 0)) := by
    intro z
    obtain ‚ü®i, hi‚ü© := hsurj (absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U S.X‚ÇÉ z)
    refine ‚ü®(absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U S.X‚ÇÇ).symm i, ?_‚ü©
    apply (absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U S.X‚ÇÉ).injective
    rw [absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv_naturality, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply, hi]
  obtain ‚ü®x‚ÇÇ, hx‚ÇÇ‚ü© := hgsurj x‚ÇÉ

/-!
# Mayer-Vietoris on a 2-affine cover and ƒåech-acyclic cover infrastructure

This file (iter-063 split, cohort 2) contains the 2-affine cover
Mayer-Vietoris theorems, the cover-totality bridges (universe-shift
`LinearEquiv`s, `finrank` / `Module.Finite` transports), the
`IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers, the comparison-iso typeclass
`HasCechToHModuleIso`, and the existence-bundled
`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` predicate together with the producer that
fires `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` from a `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover`
hypothesis.

This is cohort 2 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
* `MayerVietorisCover.lean` (this file): 2-affine cover MV +
  cover-totality bridges + `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers +
  `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.

See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
-/

module

public import Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproducts.Cech

/-!
# Cech cohomology

Given a family of objects `U : Œπ ‚Üí C` in a category `C` that has finite products,
we define a Cech complex functor
`cechComplexFunctor : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï` which sends a presheaf
`P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` in a preadditive category (where products exist) to the cochain
complex which in degree `n` consists of the product, indexed by `i : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ`,
of the value of `P` on the product of the objects `U (i a)` for `a : Fin (n + 1)`.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe w t v v' u u'

namespace CategoryTheory

open Limits

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A] [HasProducts.{w} A]

namespace Limits.FormalCoproduct

open Opposite

variable (E : SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C))

/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
functor `(C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CosimplicialObject A` which sends `P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` to the
cosimplicial object which sends `‚¶ãn‚¶å` to the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _‚¶ãn‚¶å`. -/
@[simps!]
noncomputable def cosimplicialObjectFunctor :
    (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CosimplicialObject A :=
  evalOp.{w} C A ‚ãô (Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj E.rightOp

variable [Preadditive A]

/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
functor `(C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï` which sends `P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` to the
cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _‚¶ãn‚¶å`. -/
@[simps!]
noncomputable def cochainComplexFunctor : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï :=
  cosimplicialObjectFunctor E ‚ãô AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A

end Limits.FormalCoproduct

variable [HasFiniteProducts C] [Preadditive A] {Œπ : Type w} (U : Œπ ‚Üí C)

/-- Given a family of objects `U : Œπ ‚Üí C`, this is the Cech complex functor
`(C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï` which sends a presheaf `P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A` to the
cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the product,
indexed by `x : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ`, of the value of `P` on the product of the
objects `U (x i)` for `i : Fin (n + 1)`. -/
noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï :=
  FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech

end CategoryTheory

module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.DerivedCategory.FullyFaithful
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.SmallShiftedHom

/-!
# Ext groups in abelian categories

Let `C` be an abelian category (with `C : Type u` and `Category.{v} C`).
In this file, we introduce the assumption `HasExt.{w} C` which asserts
that morphisms between single complexes in arbitrary degrees in
the derived category of `C` are `w`-small. Under this assumption,
we define `Ext.{w} X Y n : Type w` as shrunk versions of suitable
types of morphisms in the derived category. In particular, when `C` has
enough projectives or enough injectives, the property `HasExt.{v} C`
shall hold.

Note: in certain situations, `w := v` shall be the preferred
choice of universe (e.g. if `C := ModuleCat.{v} R` with `R : Type v`).
However, in the development of the API for Ext-groups, it is important
to keep a larger degree of generality for universes, as `w < v`
may happen in certain situations. Indeed, if `X : Scheme.{u}`,
then the underlying category of the √©tale site of `X` shall be a large
category. However, the category `Sheaf X.Etale AddCommGrpCat.{u}`
shall have good properties (because there is a small category of affine
schemes with the same category of sheaves), and even though the type of
morphisms in `Sheaf X.Etale AddCommGrpCat.{u}` shall be
in `Type (u + 1)`, these types are going to be `u`-small.
Then, for `C := Sheaf X.etale AddCommGrpCat.{u}`, we will have
`Category.{u + 1} C`, but `HasExt.{u} C` will hold
(as `C` has enough injectives). Then, the `Ext` groups between √©tale
sheaves over `X` shall be in `Type u`.

-/
(Bash completed with no output)

/-- **Surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¬π` vanishing** (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`,
Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).

For a short exact sequence of presheaves of modules `0 ‚Üí F ‚Üí G ‚Üí H ‚Üí 0` on a scheme `X`
‚Äî presented through the underlying `Ab`-presheaf maps `fŒπ`, `gœÄ`, with `fŒπ` a monomorphism
on sections (`hmono`), `gœÄ ‚àò fŒπ = 0` (`hœÄŒπ`), and every kernel section of `gœÄ` in the image
of `fŒπ` (`hker`) ‚Äî together with an open cover `U` of the open `‚®Ü·µ¢ U·µ¢` such that
`»û¬π(U, F) = 0` (`hH1`) and a family of local lifts `sLoc i ‚àà G(U·µ¢)` of `s ‚àà H(‚®Ü·µ¢ U·µ¢)`
(`hlift`), the section `s` lies in the image of `G(‚®Ü·µ¢ U·µ¢) ‚Üí H(‚®Ü·µ¢ U·µ¢)`.

The differences `s‚±º|_{ij} ‚àí s·µ¢|_{ij}` of the local lifts map to `0` in `H`, hence lift to a
{\v C}ech `1`-cocycle in `F` (`hker`); the {\v C}ech-`H¬π` vanishing makes it a coboundary
(`sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology`); subtracting the coboundary makes the
corrected lifts agree on overlaps, and the sheaf condition on `G` glues them to a global
section of `G` over `‚®Ü·µ¢ U·µ¢` mapping to `s` (separatedness of `H` identifies its image with
`s`).  The "cofinal system of covers" of the Stacks statement is captured here by taking a
single cover satisfying both `»û¬π = 0` and the local-lift property as hypotheses.

Project-local: the {\v C}ech surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is
new here (Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules). -/
theorem ses_cech_h1 {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí Opens ‚Ü•X)
    (F G H : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (fŒπ : F.presheaf ‚ü∂ G.presheaf) (gœÄ : G.presheaf ‚ü∂ H.presheaf)
    (hGsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf)
    (hHsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf)
    (hœÄŒπ : ‚àÄ (V : (Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ) (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj V)),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fŒπ.app V) x) = 0)
    (hmono : ‚àÄ (V : (Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ), Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fŒπ.app V)))
    (hker : ‚àÄ (V : (Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ) (x : ToType (G.presheaf.obj V)),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app V) x = 0 ‚Üí ‚àÉ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fŒπ.app V) y = x)
    (s : ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))))
    (hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
    (sLoc : ‚àÄ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))))
    (hlift : ‚àÄ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app (op (U i))) (sLoc i)
      = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s) :
    ‚àÉ g : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app (op (iSup U))) g = s := by
  classical
  -- single-index local sections, recast in ‚®Ö-coordinates
  set sLoc'coord : (œÉ : Fin 1 ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) :=
    fun œÉ => ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (iInf_le _ 0)).op) (sLoc (œÉ 0))
    with hsLoc'coord
  set sLoc' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U G).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) :=
    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).symm sLoc'coord with hsLoc'
  have hpe0 : sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0 sLoc' = sLoc'coord :=
    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).apply_symm_apply sLoc'coord
  -- the degree-1 cochain of G-differences
  set cyc1 := ConcreteCategory.hom
    (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 0) sLoc' with hcyc1
  set dGcoord : (œÉ : Fin 2 ‚Üí Œπ) ‚Üí ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) :=
    fun œÉ => sectionCechProductEquiv U G 1 cyc1 œÉ with hdGcoord
  have hdG : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin 2 ‚Üí Œπ, dGcoord œÉ
      = ‚àë i : Fin 2, (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (i : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢
          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G œÉ i)
            (sLoc'coord (œÉ ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom)) := by
    intro œÉ
    have happ := sectionCech_objD_apply U G 0 sLoc' œÉ
    rw [hpe0] at happ
    exact happ
  -- `gœÄ` carries a single ‚®Ö-coordinate local section to the restriction of `s`
  have hgœÄsLoc' : ‚àÄ (œÅ : Fin 1 ‚Üí Œπ) (h : (‚®Ö k, U (œÅ k)) ‚â§ iSup U),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app (op (‚®Ö k, U (œÅ k)))) (sLoc'coord œÅ)
        = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op) s := by
    intro œÅ h
    rw [hsLoc'coord, ‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, gœÄ.naturality,
      ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, hlift (œÅ 0)]
    exact restr_trans H.presheaf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U (œÅ 0)) s
  -- `gœÄ` kills the differences
  have hgœÄdG : ‚àÄ œÉ : Fin 2 ‚Üí Œπ,
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app (op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) (dGcoord œÉ) = 0 := by
    intro œÉ
    rw [hdG, map_sum]
    have key : ‚àÄ i : Fin 2,
        ConcreteCategory.hom (gœÄ.app (op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
          ((-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (i : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢ ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G œÉ i)
            (sLoc'coord (œÉ ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom)))
        = (-1 : ‚Ñ§) ^ (i : ‚Ñï) ‚Ä¢ ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map
            (homOfLE (le_trans (iInf_le (fun k => U (œÉ k)) (0 : Fin 2)) (le_iSup U (œÉ 0)))).op) s := by
      intro i
      rw [map_zsmul]; congr 1
      rw [‚Üê fŒπ_sectionCechFaceRestr U G H gœÄ œÉ i,
        hgœÄsLoc' (œÉ ‚àò (SimplexCategory.Œ¥ i).toOrderHom) (le_trans (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U _))]

679	    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (e) (r : Œì(Y, U)) :

694	    (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (h : IsAffineOpen (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ U)) (r : Œì(Y, U)) :
695	    haveI := hU.isLocalization_basicOpen r

706	    IsLocalization.map_comp, RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra,

713	`Œì(Y, Y.basicOpen r) ‚ü∂ Œì(X, X.basicOpen (f.app U r))` -/

lemma Spec_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat} (f : R) :
    IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) :=
  basicOpen_eq_of_affine f ‚ñ∏ (isAffineOpen_top (Spec <| .of R)).basicOpen _

instance [IsAffine X] (r : Œì(X, ‚ä§)) : IsAffine (X.basicOpen r) :=
  (isAffineOpen_top X).basicOpen _

include hU in
theorem Œπ_basicOpen_preimage (r : Œì(X, ‚ä§)) :
    IsAffineOpen ((X.basicOpen r).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ U) := by
  apply (X.basicOpen r).Œπ.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion.mp
  rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_Œπ, inf_comm,
    ‚Üê Scheme.basicOpen_res _ _ (homOfLE le_top).op]
  exact hU.basicOpen _

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
include hU in
theorem exists_basicOpen_le {V : X.Opens} (x : V) (h : ‚Üëx ‚àà U) :
    ‚àÉ f : Œì(X, U), X.basicOpen f ‚â§ V ‚àß ‚Üëx ‚àà X.basicOpen f := by
  have : IsAffine _ := hU
  obtain ‚ü®_, ‚ü®_, ‚ü®r, rfl‚ü©, rfl‚ü©, h‚ÇÅ, h‚ÇÇ : _ ‚â§ U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V‚ü© :=
    (isBasis_basicOpen U).exists_subset_of_mem_open (x.2 : (‚ü®x, h‚ü© : U) ‚àà _) (U.Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ V).isOpen
  replace h‚ÇÅ : x.1 ‚àà X.basicOpen r := by simpa [U.mem_basicOpen_toScheme] using! h‚ÇÅ
  replace h‚ÇÇ : X.basicOpen r ‚â§ V := by
    simpa [Scheme.image_basicOpen] using! (U.Œπ.image_mono h‚ÇÇ).trans (U.Œπ.image_preimage_le _)
  exact ‚ü®U.topIso.hom.hom r, by simp [Scheme.Opens.toScheme_presheaf_obj, h‚ÇÅ, h‚ÇÇ]‚ü©

noncomputable
instance {R : CommRingCat} {U} : Algebra R Œì(Spec R, U) :=
  inferInstanceAs (Algebra R ((Spec.structureSheaf R).presheaf.obj _))

@[simp]
lemma algebraMap_Spec_obj {R : CommRingCat} {U} : algebraMap R Œì(Spec R, U) =
    ((Scheme.ŒìSpecIso R).inv ‚â´ (Spec R).presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom := rfl

instance {R : CommRingCat} {f : R} :
    IsLocalization.Away f Œì(Spec R, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsLocalization.Away f
    ((Spec.structureSheaf R).obj.obj (op <| PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))

/-- Given an affine open U and some `f : U`,
this is the canonical map `Œì(ùí™‚Çì, D(f)) ‚ü∂ Œì(Spec ùí™‚Çì(U), D(f))`
This is an isomorphism, as witnessed by an `IsIso` instance. -/
def basicOpenSectionsToAffine :
    Œì(X, X.basicOpen f) ‚ü∂ Œì(Spec Œì(X, U), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) :=
  hU.fromSpec.app (X.basicOpen f) ‚â´
    (Spec Œì(X, U)).presheaf.map (eqToHom (hU.fromSpec_preimage_basicOpen f).symm).op

instance basicOpenSectionsToAffine_isIso :
    IsIso (basicOpenSectionsToAffine hU f) :=
  (hU.fromSpec.isIso_app _ (hU.opensRange_fromSpec.symm ‚ñ∏ X.basicOpen_le f)).comp_isIso'
    inferInstance

include hU in
theorem isLocalization_basicOpen :
    IsLocalization.Away f Œì(X, X.basicOpen f) := by

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
7	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Preorder
8	
9	/-!
10	# Mayer-Vietoris LES core for `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology
11	
12	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 1) contains the MV LES core for
13	`Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`-valued cohomology: the Mathlib gap-fill
14	`Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` plus the iter-016 ‚Üí iter-026 abstract
15	Mayer-Vietoris infrastructure (`HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor`,
16	`HModule'_toBiprod`, `HModule'_fromBiprod`, `HModule'_Œ¥`, `HModule'_sequence`,
17	`HModule'_sequence_exact`, and the `Œ¥`-zero simp companions).
18	
19	This is cohort 1 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean` (this file): MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	
24	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	
29	universe u v w w'
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
32	
33	/-! ## Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill: `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` as a `LinearEquiv`
34	
35	Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ‚âÉ Ext.{w'} X Y n`
36	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540) is a bare
37	`Equiv` only. The iter-034 universe-bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv`
38	(below in `section CoverTotality`) requires it as a `LinearEquiv` over the
39	`Linear R C`-enriched abelian category. The upgrade is mechanical: the
40	`AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` and `Module R (Ext X Y n)` instances are themselves
41	transferred from the standard derived category through `homEquiv`, and
42	Mathlib's load-bearing intermediate lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv` (Basic.lean
43	L543‚Äì545) shows that `chgUniv` preserves the underlying derived-category
44	morphism. So `chgUniv` automatically commutes with `+` and `‚Ä¢`. Two short
45	helper lemmas (`chgUniv_add` and `chgUniv_smul`) plus a 7-line `LinearEquiv`
46	structure literal give the upgrade.
47	
48	This cohort lives outside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` because the
49	helpers belong to the `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext` namespace. We use the
50	qualified-name pattern `Abelian.Ext.foo` rather than wrapping the entire file
51	in a second namespace block. -/
52	
53	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (a): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is additive. The
54	underlying `homEquiv` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean`
55	L162) is preserved by `chgUniv` (Mathlib lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv`,
56	L543‚Äì545), and the `AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` instance (L241‚Äì243) is defined by
57	transferring the `AddCommGroup` from the standard derived category through
58	`homEquiv`. Combining these via the `Ext.ext` extensionality (L177‚Äì179) gives
59	additivity. -/
60	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add
61	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
62	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ‚Ñï} (a b : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
63	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b) =
64	      Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a + Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b := by
65	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
66	  ext
67	  rw [Abelian.Ext.add_hom, ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
68	      ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply, ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
69	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b)) =
70	    Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a) +
71	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b)
72	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
73	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.add_hom]
74	
75	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (b): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is `R`-linear when `C`
76	is `Linear R`-enriched. Same proof shape as `chgUniv_add`, but using
77	`smul_hom` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Linear.lean` L52‚Äì55)
78	in place of `add_hom`. -/
79	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul
80	    {R : Type*} [Ring R] {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [Linear R C]
81	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ‚Ñï}
82	    (r : R) (a : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
83	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r ‚Ä¢ a) = r ‚Ä¢ Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a := by
84	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
85	  ext
86	  rw [Abelian.Ext.smul_hom, ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
87	      ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
88	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r ‚Ä¢ a)) =
89	    r ‚Ä¢ Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a)
90	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
91	      ‚Üê Abelian.Ext.smul_hom]
92	
93	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (c): the `R`-linear upgrade of Mathlib's bare-`Equiv`
94	`Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ‚âÉ Ext.{w'} X Y n` to a `LinearEquiv`,
95	when `C` is `Linear R`-enriched abelian. Combines (a) and (b). The
96	`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540 declaration is
97	universe-polymorphic but only proves the underlying bijection; this wrapper
98	adds the algebraic structure preservation needed for downstream `LinearEquiv`
99	chaining (specifically, the iter-034 universe bridge
100	`HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` below). -/
101	noncomputable def Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv
102	    {R : Type*} [Ring R] {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [Linear R C]
103	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ‚Ñï} :
104	    Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n ‚âÉ‚Çó[R] Abelian.Ext.{w'} X Y n where
105	  toFun a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a
106	  invFun a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w} a
107	  left_inv a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'}.left_inv a
108	  right_inv a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'}.right_inv a
109	  map_add' a b := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add a b
110	  map_smul' r a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul r a
111	
112	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
113	
114	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the substantive functor mirroring
115	Mathlib's `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheafFunctor` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Sends
116	a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` to the presheaf
117	`X ‚Ü¶ Ext^n((presheafToSheaf J _).obj ((yoneda ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k).obj X), F)`,
118	i.e.\ to a presheaf `C·µí·µñ ‚•§ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is
119	`HModule' k F n X` (definitionally, see `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` below).
120	
121	The codomain is `C·µí·µñ ‚•§ AddCommGrpCat` (not `C·µí·µñ ‚•§ ModuleCat k`) because Mathlib's
122	`Abelian.extFunctor n : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ C ‚•§ AddCommGrpCat` always lands in `AddCommGrpCat`
123	regardless of the source category's `Linear`-enrichment; the per-fiber `Module k`
124	structure on `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op X)` is preserved
125	through the iter-014 `HModule'` reducibility chain. Probe-confirmed body

  m2=$(stat -c %Y AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/Adelic/P1ChartData.lean)

  sleep 30
done

1390	end SectionCechModule
1391	
1392	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî quasi-coherent sections as away localisations (L1, step (b))
1393	
1394	The categorical‚Üímodule bridge needs the section-identification of
1395	`def:qcoh_sections_localized`: over a basic open `D(g)` the sections of a
1396	quasi-coherent sheaf are the away localisation `M_g`, and the restriction maps
1397	between basic opens are the canonical localisation comparison maps.  For the
1398	standard sheaf `tilde M` this is *verbatim* from Mathlib's `Tilde` development
1399	(`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen` carries `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g)` and the
1400	restriction compatibility is `tilde.toOpen_res`); the only project-local content is
1401	(i) the multi-index intersection `‚®Ö‚Çñ D(s_{œÉ k}) = D(s_œÉ)` identification, which lets
1402	the degree-`p` section group over the `(p+1)`-fold intersection be read as the
1403	localisation `M_{s_œÉ}` (this is what `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes
1404	degreewise), and (ii) the identification of the abstract presheaf restriction with
1405	`AwayComparison.comparison`, the differential brick.
1406	
1407	For an *arbitrary* quasi-coherent `F` the remaining input is the affine equivalence
1408	`F ‚âÖ tilde(ŒìF)` (Stacks 01I8); see `def:qcoh_sections_localized`.  The tilde case
1409	below is the gap-free part that lands the named target. -/
1410	
1411	/-- Splitting an indexed infimum over `Fin (n+1)` into the `0`-th term and the
1412	infimum over the tail.  Lattice-combinatorial helper behind `basicOpen_sprod`. -/
1413	private lemma iInf_fin_succ {Œ± : Type*} [CompleteLattice Œ±] (n : ‚Ñï) (f : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œ±) :
1414	    (‚®Ö i, f i) = f 0 ‚äì ‚®Ö i : Fin n, f i.succ := by
1415	  apply le_antisymm
1416	  ¬∑ exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iInf fun i => iInf_le _ i.succ)
1417	  ¬∑ refine le_iInf fun i => ?_
1418	    refine Fin.cases inf_le_left (fun j => le_trans inf_le_right (iInf_le _ j)) i
1419	
1420	/-- **Multi-index basic-open intersection** (geometric input to step (c)): the
1421	`(p+1)`-fold intersection of the basic opens `D(s_{œÉ k})` of a {\v C}ech multi-index
1422	`œÉ : Fin n ‚Üí Œπ` is the basic open `D(s_œÉ)` of the product `s_œÉ = ‚àè‚Çñ s_{œÉ k}`.  This
1423	identifies the section group `F(‚®Ö‚Çñ D(s_{œÉ k}))` of `sectionCechCosimplicial` with the
1424	away localisation `M_{s_œÉ}` of `SectionCechModule.dCoeff`. -/
1425	lemma basicOpen_sprod {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {Œπ : Type u} (n : ‚Ñï) (s : Œπ ‚Üí R) (œÉ : Fin n ‚Üí Œπ) :
1426	    (‚®Ö k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (œÉ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
1427	      = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (‚àè k, s (œÉ k)) := by
1428	  induction n with
1429	  | zero =>
1430	      rw [show (‚àè k, s (œÉ k)) = 1 from Finset.prod_of_isEmpty _]
1431	      simp only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one]
1432	      exact iInf_of_empty _
1433	  | succ m ih =>
1434	      rw [Fin.prod_univ_succ, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul, ‚Üê ih (fun i => œÉ i.succ),
1435	        iInf_fin_succ]
1436	      rfl
1437	
1438	/-- **Quasi-coherent sections over a {\v C}ech intersection are an away localisation**
1439	(`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, tilde case ‚Äî step (b) of the L1 bridge).  For the
1440	standard sheaf `tilde M` of an `R`-module `M`, the section-restriction map from `M`
1441	to the sections over the `(p+1)`-fold basic-open intersection
1442	`‚®Ö‚Çñ D(s_{œÉ k}) = D(s_œÉ)` exhibits that section group as the away localisation
1443	`M_{s_œÉ}` (`IsLocalizedModule` for `Submonoid.powers (‚àè‚Çñ s_{œÉ k})`).  Combines the
1444	Mathlib instance `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g) (tilde.toOpen M (D g)).hom` with the
1445	intersection identification `basicOpen_sprod`.  This is the degreewise section
1446	identification that `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes; the restriction
1447	compatibility (item (5) of the blueprint definition) is `tilde.toOpen_res` together
1448	with `qcohRestriction_eq_comparison` below. -/
1449	lemma qcohSectionsAwayLocalized {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {Œπ : Type u}
1450	    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {n : ‚Ñï} (s : Œπ ‚Üí R) (œÉ : Fin n ‚Üí Œπ) :
1451	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (‚àè k, s (œÉ k)))
1452	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (‚®Ö k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (œÉ k)))).hom := by
1453	  rw [basicOpen_sprod]
1454	  infer_instance
1455	
1456	/-- **Restriction = localisation comparison** (`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, item (5);
1457	the differential brick of step (c)).  For the standard sheaf `tilde M`, the presheaf
1458	restriction map between basic-open section groups `M_a ‚Üí M_b` (along an inclusion
1459	`D(b) ‚äÜ D(a)`) is, as an `R`-linear map, the canonical away-localisation comparison
1460	`AwayComparison.comparison` ‚Äî provided `a` acts invertibly on `M_b` (`Inverts a M_b`,
1461	which holds whenever `a ‚à£ b`, the {\v C}ech-face case).  Proved by the universal
1462	property `AwayComparison.comparison_unique`: both the restriction and the comparison
1463	are `R`-linear maps that recover `tilde.toOpen M (D b)` after precomposition with
1464	`tilde.toOpen M (D a)` (the restriction does so by `tilde.toOpen_res`).  Summed over
1465	the alternating signs, this identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with the
1466	module differential `SectionCechModule.dDiff`. -/
1467	lemma qcohRestriction_eq_comparison {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {a b : R}
1468	    (i : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b : (Spec R).Opens) ‚ü∂ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1469	    (hb : AwayComparison.Inverts a
1470	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
1471	        (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)))) :
1472	    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op).hom
1473	      = AwayComparison.comparison (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1474	          (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb := by
1475	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
1476	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom := inferInstance
1477	  refine (AwayComparison.comparison_unique
1478	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1479	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb _ ?_).symm
1480	  have h := AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen_res M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1481	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b) i
1482	  exact congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom h
1483	
1484	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî section {\v C}ech homology bridge (L1 steps c, d)
1485	
1486	The categorical‚Üímodule bridge of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact`: the
1487	`Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (of `PresheafCech.lean`)
1488	has its degree-`p` object a *categorical product* `‚àè·∂ú_œÉ F(‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k))` in `Ab`, and
1489	its differential the alternating sum of the {\v C}ech coface restrictions.  These
1490	lemmas (c1)‚Äì(c3) move that abstract complex to the concrete localised-module complex
1491	`SectionCechModule.dDiff` (whose positive-degree exactness `dDiff_exact` is step (a)),
1492	and read off homology vanishing. -/
1493	
1494	section SectionCechBridge
1495	
1496	open CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicTopology
1497	
1498	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1499	
1500	/-- **(c1) Element-level product equivalence** (`lem:section_cech_product_equiv`):
1501	the underlying type of the degree-`p` object `‚àè·∂ú_œÉ F(‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k))` of the section
1502	{\v C}ech cosimplicial object is the dependent product `‚àè_œÉ ToType (F(‚®Ö‚Çñ U (œÉ k)))`.
1503	Supplied by `CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv` (the forgetful functor of
1504	`Ab` preserves discrete products). -/
1505	noncomputable def sectionCechProductEquiv {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1506	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ‚Ñï) :
1507	    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ‚âÉ
1508	      (‚àÄ œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ, ToType (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))) :=
1509	  Concrete.productEquiv
1510	    (fun œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))
1511	
1512	/-- Coordinate projection of `sectionCechProductEquiv`: the `œÉ`-component is the
1513	underlying group map of the categorical projection `Pi.œÄ ‚Ä¶ œÉ`.  A definitional
1514	restatement of `Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply`, named so that downstream proofs can
1515	`rw` it without fighting the `Concrete.productEquiv` coercion. -/
1516	lemma sectionCechProductEquiv_apply {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1517	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ‚Ñï)
1518	    (y : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
1519	    (œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ) :
1520	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F p y œÉ
1521	      = ConcreteCategory.hom
1522	          (Pi.œÄ (fun œÉ : Fin (p + 1) ‚Üí Œπ => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)))) œÉ) y :=
1523	  Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply _ y œÉ
1524	
1525	/-- **Homology-to-exactness reduction** (the `Ab`-side of `lem:section_cech_ab_exact`):
1526	the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the section {\v C}ech complex vanishes once the
1527	underlying group homomorphisms of the two consecutive coface differentials
1528	`objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence.  Pure homological algebra: combines
1529	`exactAt_iff_isZero_homology`, `exactAt_iff'`, and the abelian-group exactness
1530	criterion `ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact`. -/
1531	lemma sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact {Œπ : Type u}
1532	    (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ‚Ñï)
1533	    (h : Function.Exact
1534	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
1535	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
1536	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1)))) :
1537	    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1)) := by
1538	  rw [‚Üê HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
1539	      (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),

# Interface map: building `HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily` from `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F`

## 5. Degree ‚â• 2

**No** in-project lemma asserts vanishing (or any other identification) of `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily n` for `n ‚â• 2`. Searched `Cokernel.lean` (declaration list lines 136‚Äì1257 fully enumerated: nothing past the degree-1 `cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok`/`hModuleOneEquivH1Cok`), `CechAcyclicInstance.lean`, `CechComparisonGate.lean` ‚Äî the only occurrences of "`n ‚â• 2`"/"vanish" are in **docstring prose** describing the intended (unbuilt) argument:
> *"`n ‚â• 2`: both sides vanish ‚Äî the ƒåech complex of a 2-element cover is `3`-term, and the derived side vanishes by the MV LES together with the affine vanishing on `U‚ÇÅ`, `U‚ÇÇ`, `U‚ÇÅ‚äìU‚ÇÇ`."* (`CechAcyclicInstance.lean:41-43`, `CechComparisonGate.lean:62`)

No lemma backs this claim. As the task notes, the terms are **not** structurally zero (they're products over `Fin(n+1) ‚Üí ULift(Fin 2)`, nonzero in every degree); the vanishing would have to come from an actual homology computation of the (degenerate-index-heavy) `n`-truncation, mirroring the degree-1 `sc' 0 1 2`/`moduleCatHomologyIso` argument one level up, or from a genuinely different route (e.g. showing the augmented ƒåech complex is split-exact past degree 1 using the degenerate/repeated-index structure ‚Äî Mathlib's `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex`/`FormalCoproduct` machinery has no such "acyclic cover ‚üπ complex quasi-iso to sheaf" theorem at all, confirmed by reading `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean` in full ‚Äî it only *defines* `cechComplexFunctor`, no comparison theorem). This is the single largest structural gap.

`Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover` (`Carriers.lean:612`) is the `Prop`-class that would package exactly "`Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n)` for `n>0`" (both degree 1 and degree ‚â•2 combined) but **no instance producer for it exists** for `ùí∞ = S.coverFamily` anywhere; it is consumed abstractly by `subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover` (line 635) but never instantiated.

## 6. Shape of the MV LES across all degrees

`HModule'_sequence_exact` (item 1) gives, for **each fixed consecutive pair** `(n‚ÇÄ,n‚ÇÅ = n‚ÇÄ+1)`, exactness of the 6-object window
```
H‚Åø‚Å∞(X‚ÇÑ) ‚Üí H‚Åø‚Å∞(X‚ÇÇ)‚äûH‚Åø‚Å∞(X‚ÇÉ) ‚Üí H‚Åø‚Å∞(X‚ÇÅ) ‚Üí H‚Åø¬π(X‚ÇÑ) ‚Üí H‚Åø¬π(X‚ÇÇ)‚äûH‚Åø¬π(X‚ÇÉ) ‚Üí H‚Åø¬π(X‚ÇÅ)
```
at its 4 internal vertices. There is **no single in-project theorem packaging the full infinite LES** `... ‚Üí H‚Åø(X‚ÇÑ) ‚Üí H‚Åø(X‚ÇÇ)‚äûH‚Åø(X‚ÇÉ) ‚Üí H‚Åø(X‚ÇÅ) ‚Üí H‚Åø‚Å∫¬π(X‚ÇÑ) ‚Üí ...`; it must be assembled by **chaining `HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n n' h` over all consecutive `n`** (each call re-proves the exactness at `H‚Åø‚Å∫¬π(X‚ÇÑ)`/`H‚Åø‚Å∫¬π(X‚ÇÇ)‚äûH‚Åø‚Å∫¬π(X‚ÇÉ)` that the *next* call also proves at its left end ‚Äî the individual windows overlap by one vertex, so the family covers every consecutive triple). This chaining is not itself written anywhere in-project (no `HModule'_sequence_exact_all` / induction wrapper).

Specialized to `S : AffineCoverMVSquare` via `AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence`/`_exact` (`MayerVietorisCover.lean:111`/`121`) and `_curve` (lines 135/146), with corners `X‚ÇÅ=U‚ÇÅ‚äìU‚ÇÇ, X‚ÇÇ=U‚ÇÅ, X‚ÇÉ=U‚ÇÇ, X‚ÇÑ=‚ä§` (item 2). This is the biproduct-shaped LES on `U‚ÇÅ,U‚ÇÇ` directly ‚Äî it is **not indexed by `S.coverFamily`'s `ULift(Fin 2)`**, so bridging it degree-by-degree to `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily n` (the actual quantity `HasCechToHModuleIso` compares) is itself unaddressed work (beyond the degree-1 case, which sidesteps this by computing `cechCohomology...1` directly as `H1Cok`, a quantity built from `U‚ÇÅ,U‚ÇÇ` sections without reference to the abstract biproduct sequence at all).

1. **Degree 0**: no identification `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily 0 ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F 0 ‚ä§` (sheaf-axiom step). Even the pure algebraic sub-step `Hom(sheafify(free‚àòyoneda X), F) ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] F(X)` (needed to finish `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv`) is unbuilt in the constant-`k` apparatus (Mathlib pieces identified: `sheafificationAdjunction`, `Adjunction.whiskerRight (ModuleCat.adj k)`, `yonedaEquiv` ‚Äî none chained together here).
2. **Degree 1**: `H1Cok S F ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule' k F 1 ‚ä§` via the degree-0/1 slice of `HModule'_sequence_exact` fed by `subsingleton_hModule'_coverFamily` on `U‚ÇÅ,U‚ÇÇ` ‚Äî described in docstrings, **not proved**. (`cechCohomology...1 ‚âÉ H1Cok` itself, `cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok`, *is* available unconditionally.)
3. **Degree ‚â• 2**: no vanishing statement for `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily n`, `n‚â•2`, exists at all (neither the ƒåech side nor an `HModule'` side); Mathlib's `Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean` supplies no acyclic-cover-computes-cohomology comparison theorem to lean on generically either.
4. **Structural bridge**: `S.coverFamily : ULift(Fin 2) ‚Üí Opens` (feeding `cechCohomology`/`Scheme.cechCochain`) and `S.toMayerVietorisSquare` (feeding `HModule'_sequence`, biproduct-shaped on `U‚ÇÅ,U‚ÇÇ` directly) are two independent representations; no degreewise comparison map/iso between the `Fin(n+1)‚ÜíULift(Fin 2)`-indexed ƒåech complex and the abstract MV biproduct sequence is constructed beyond the ad hoc degree-1 `sc' 0 1 2` computation.
5. **Full LES packaging**: `HModule'_sequence_exact` only proves one consecutive-pair window at a time; no chained/inductive full-LES theorem exists (needed to reach `HModule'¬π(‚ä§)` from vanishing at all corners, or degree ‚â•2 vanishing which needs two consecutive windows: `(1,2)` for the `X‚ÇÑ` vertex at degree 2, using vanishing at `X‚ÇÅ,X‚ÇÇ,X‚ÇÉ` degree 1 *and* 2).
6. No instance producer anywhere for `Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily` (the class that would directly package "positive-degree ƒåech cohomology of this cover vanishes"), which ‚Äî combined with the (missing) `HasCechToHModuleIso`-independent degree-0 sheaf-axiom identification ‚Äî is the other route to the target class via `subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover`.
