F)\). Specialising \(F = \toModuleKSheaf C\) gives
  \(\mathrm{cechCohomology}\, C\, (\toModuleKSheaf C)\, S.\cover\, 1
  \;\simeq_k\; \check{H}^1 = A_{01}/(A_0 + A_1)\).
\end{proposition}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  \uses{lem:adelic_cech_d01_d12_formula, lem:adelic_cech_coface_restriction}
  Unfold \(\mathrm{cechCohomology}\, C\, F\, \mathcal U\, 1\) as the
  homology at the middle term of the short complex
  \(C^0 \xrightarrow{d^0} C^1 \xrightarrow{d^1} C^2\) of the ƒåech
  cochain complex, i.e.\ \(\ker d^1 / \operatorname{im}(C^0 \to \ker
  d^1)\). For the two-element index set \(\mathcal U = \{U_0, U_1\}\)
  every triple of indices \(x \colon \mathrm{Fin}\,3 \to \{0,1\}\)
  repeats a value, so by \Cref{lem:adelic_cech_d01_d12_formula} the
  degree-\(1\) differential \(d^1\), evaluated in each of the eight
  index components of \(C^2 = \prod_x \Gamma(\bigsqcap_a
  U_{x(a)}, F)\), collapses by the coface-restriction identities of
  \Cref{lem:adelic_cech_coface_restriction} (parallel restrictions
  cancel and diagonal restrictions round-trip). Consequently
  \(\ker d^1 \subseteq C^1 = \Gamma(U_0, F) \times \Gamma(U_1, F) \times
  \Gamma(U_1, F) \times \Gamma(U_0, F)\) (indexed by the four functions
  \(\mathrm{Fin}\,2 \to \{0,1\}\)) consists exactly of the tuples
  \((0, q, -q, 0)\) for \(q \in \Gamma(U_0 \cap U_1, F)\), giving a
  \(k\)-linear isomorphism \(\Gamma(U_0 \cap U_1, F) \xrightarrow{\ \sim\
  } \ker d^1\), \(q \mapsto (0, q, -q, 0)\). Under this identification
  the image of \(d^0 \colon \Gamma(U_0, F) \oplus \Gamma(U_1, F) \to
  \ker d^1\), \((f_0, f_1) \mapsto (0,\, f_0|_{01} - f_1|_{01},\,
  f_1|_{01} - f_0|_{01},\, 0)\), corresponds to \(\{ f_0|_{01} -
  f_1|_{01} : f_i \in \Gamma(U_i, F)\} = \Gamma(U_0, F) + \Gamma(U_1,
  F)\) inside \(\Gamma(U_0 \cap U_1, F)\) (each \(\Gamma(U_i, F)\) being
  closed under negation). Composing the two identifications gives the
  claimed \(k\)-linear isomorphism
  \(\mathrm{cechCohomology}\, C\, F\, \mathcal U\, 1 \simeq_k
  \Gamma(U_0 \cap U_1, F)/(\Gamma(U_0, F) + \Gamma(U_1, F))\).
\end{proof}

\begin{proposition}
[\(\mathrm{IsCechAcyclicCover}\) for the affine square]
  \label{thm:adelic_instIsCechAcyclicCover}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic.instIsCechAcyclicCover}
  \uses{def:Scheme_IsCechAcyclicCover, lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:affine_injective_acyclic}
  The two-element affine cover \(S.\cover = \{U_0, U_1\}\) satisfies
  \(\mathrm{IsCechAcyclicCover}\, (\toModuleKSheaf C)\, S.\cover\): the
  higher ƒåech cohomology of the structure sheaf along the cover
  vanishes.
\end{proposition}
\begin{proof}
  Acyclicity of an affine cover for a quasi-coherent sheaf is the
  Serre-vanishing seed of \Cref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}: on an affine
  open the higher cohomology of \(\toModuleKSheaf C\) vanishes, and the
  injective-acyclic reduction \Cref{lem:affine_injective_acyclic}
  propagates this to the ƒåech complex of the cover whose members and all
  of whose finite intersections are affine. For the two-element square
  the only nonempty intersections are \(U_0, U_1, U_0 \cap U_1\), all
  affine by construction of \(\AffineCoverMVSquare\)
  (\Cref{def:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare}); the vanishing seed applies to
  each, giving \(\mathrm{IsCechAcyclicCover}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{proposition}
[ƒåech-to-derived comparison for the affine square]
  \label{thm:adelic_instHasCechToHModuleIso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic.instHasCechToHModuleIso}
  \uses{def:Scheme_HasCechToHModuleIso, thm:adelic_instIsCechAcyclicCover, thm:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare_HModule_prime_sequence_exact}
  The affine square satisfies
  \(\HasCechToHModuleIso\, (\toModuleKSheaf C)\, S.\cover\): the ƒåech
  cohomology of the cover computes the sheaf cohomology of
  \(\toModuleKSheaf C\).
\end{proposition}
\begin{proof}
  The comparison is furnished by the Mayer--Vietoris presentation of the
  affine square. The exact sequence
  \Cref{thm:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare_HModule_prime_sequence_exact}
  expresses \(\HModulePrime\) at the join \(U_0 \sqcup U_1 = \top\) as the
  cohomology of the two-term complex
  \(\HModulePrime(U_0) \oplus \HModulePrime(U_1) \to
  \HModulePrime(U_0 \cap U_1)\); since the cover is ƒåech-acyclic
  (\Cref{thm:adelic_instIsCechAcyclicCover}) the higher terms
  \(H^{>0}(U_i)\) vanish, so this two-term complex is exactly the ƒåech
  complex, and its cohomology agrees with the derived-functor cohomology
  \(\HModulePrime\) degreewise. Packaging the degreewise isomorphisms is
  \(\HasCechToHModuleIso\).
  \paragraph{Gate.} This is the substantive ƒåech-to-derived comparison.
  Where its Lean discharge is not yet available it is threaded as the
  existing carrier class \(\HasCechToHModuleIso\), supplied at the use
  site and later closed by \(\texttt{inferInstance}\); every downstream consumer
  verifies against the hypothesis meanwhile.
\end{proof}

\begin{theorem}
[Bridge: \(H^1(C, \struct{C})\) is the cover cokernel (N6)]
  \label{thm:adelic_HModuleOne_linearEquiv_cokernel}
  \notready  % gate class AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HasCechToHModuleIso
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.AffineCoverMVSquare.hModuleOneEquivH1Cok}
  \uses{thm:adelic_cechH1_eq_cokernel, def:Scheme_HasCechToHModuleIso, def:Scheme_HModule_prime_eq_HModule_linearEquiv, def:Scheme_cechToHModuleIso, def:Scheme_HModule}
  Under the ƒåech-to-derived comparison gate
  \(\HasCechToHModuleIso\, (\toModuleKSheaf C)\, S.\cover\)
  (\Cref{def:Scheme_HasCechToHModuleIso}), there is a \(k\)-linear
  isomorphism
  \[
    \HModule\, k\, (\toModuleKSheaf C)\, 1
    \;\simeq_k\; \check{H}^1 \;=\; A_{01}/(A_0 + A_1).
  \]
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
  Chain two isomorphisms.
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item Under the gate \(\HasCechToHModuleIso\)
      (\Cref{def:Scheme_HasCechToHModuleIso}), the comparison
      \(\texttt{cechToHModuleIso}\) (\Cref{def:Scheme_cechToHModuleIso})
      together with the universe bridge
      \Cref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_eq_HModule_linearEquiv} identifies
      \(\HModule\, k\, (\toModuleKSheaf C)\, 1\) with
      \(\mathrm{cechCohomology}\, C\, (\toModuleKSheaf C)\, S.\cover\,
      1\) (using that the cover family's join is \(\top\)).
    \item The two-element identification
      \Cref{thm:adelic_cechH1_eq_cokernel} --- proved
      \emph{unconditionally}, with no acyclicity hypothesis on the
      cover --- rewrites the ƒåech \(H^1\) as the cokernel
      \(A_{01}/(A_0 + A_1)\).
  \end{enumerate}

30:noncomputable def jShriekOU (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.Modules :=
50:noncomputable def jShriekOU_homEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
63:noncomputable def absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
164:    absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U F‚ÇÇ (e.comp (Ext.mk‚ÇÄ g) (add_zero 0))
/-
Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FreePresheafComplex

/-!
# Form-B absolute cohomology `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p(jShriekOU U, F)` (P5b input)
-/

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits CategoryTheory.Abelian

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The standard `HasExt` structure on `X.Modules`, made a section-local instance so the
`Ext`-based absolute cohomology below resolves it without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom`
typeclass search. -/
noncomputable local instance hasExtModules : HasExt.{u + 1, u, u + 1} X.Modules :=
  HasExt.standard _

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement ‚Äî Form-B absolute cohomology -/

noncomputable def jShriekOU (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.Modules :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (ùüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U))

/-- The sheafification adjunction hom-bijection as an `AddEquiv` (additivity holds because
the right adjoint is an additive functor and composition is bilinear). -/
noncomputable def sheafificationHomAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
    (jShriekOU U ‚ü∂ F) ‚âÉ+
      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U) ‚ü∂
        (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) :=
  { (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (ùüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv
      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U)) F with
    map_add' := by
      intro f g
      haveI : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ‚ãô
          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (ùüô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive := inferInstance
      simp only [Equiv.toFun_as_coe, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
      erw [Functor.map_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
      rfl }

noncomputable def jShriekOU_homEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
    (jShriekOU U ‚ü∂ F) ‚âÉ+
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
  (sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F).trans (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv U _)

/-- **Form-B absolute cohomology** `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{O_X}(jShriekOU U, F)`. -/
noncomputable def absoluteCohomology (p : ‚Ñï) (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.Modules) : AddCommGrpCat :=
  AddCommGrpCat.of (Ext (jShriekOU U) F p)

/-- **`H‚Å∞(U, F) ‚âÖ Œì(U, F)`** as abelian groups: degree-zero `Ext` out of the
corepresenting object `jShriekOU U` recovers the sections of `F` over `U`. This is the
additive corepresentability of global sections specialised to `p = 0`. -/
noncomputable def absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    (F : X.Modules) :
    Ext (jShriekOU U) F 0 ‚âÉ+
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
  (AddEquiv.mk' Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ (by
    intro a b
    refine Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ.symm.injective ?_
    simp only [Ext.homEquiv‚ÇÄ_symm_apply, Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_add, Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_homEquiv‚ÇÄ_apply])).trans
    (jShriekOU_homEquiv U F)

/-- **Injective vanishing**: positive-degree absolute cohomology of an injective sheaf of
modules vanishes. Direct from `Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (the injective object is the
*second* `Ext` argument, so no restriction of the coefficient sheaf is needed). -/

text:
# Scouting Report: Porting the Stacks-01EO ƒåech-Vanishing Seed to a General Scheme's Affine Opens

## 1. The algebraic heart ‚Äî pure ring/module-level statement

**Home file: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean`** (NOT `TildeExactness.lean`/`AffineSerreVanishing.lean`/`QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean`/`QcohTildeSections.lean` ‚Äî those are downstream consumers/identification glue). The reusable algebraic core lives in `namespace SectionCechModule` (opens `CechLocalized AwayComparison`), variables `{R : Type u} [CommRing R] {Œπ : Type*} (s : Œπ ‚Üí R) (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]`.

**Terms** (pure `R`-module data, no scheme/sheaf in sight):
- `CechAcyclic.lean:590` `CechLocalized.sprod {m} (œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ) : R := ‚àè k, s (œÉ k)` ‚Äî the localizing element for a ƒåech multi-index.
- `CechAcyclic.lean:924` `abbrev SectionCechModule.dCoeff {m} (œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ) : Type u := LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÉ)) M` ‚Äî degree-`m` coefficient at multi-index `œÉ`.
- `CechAcyclic.lean:939` `noncomputable def dDiff (m : ‚Ñï) : (‚àÄ œÉ : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ, dCoeff s M œÉ) ‚Üí‚Çó[R] (‚àÄ œÉ : Fin (m+1) ‚Üí Œπ, dCoeff s M œÉ)` ‚Äî the alternating-sum-of-cofaces differential (`LinearMap.pi` of alternating sums of the away-localisation comparison maps `dCoface`).
- `CechAcyclic.lean:945` `dDiff_apply` unfolds it as `‚àë j, (-1)^j ‚Ä¢ dCoface s M m œÉ j (t (œÉ ‚àò j.succAbove))`.

**The three exactness theorems (module-level, no scheme):**

```
-- CechAcyclic.lean:1124
lemma SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact [Finite Œπ] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ‚ä§) (m : ‚Ñï) :
    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2))
```
This is *the* algebraic core: for a spanning family `s : Œπ ‚Üí R`, the complex `‚àè_œÉ M_{s_œÉ}` (over `œÉ : Fin(m+1)‚ÜíŒπ`) is exact in positive degrees, proved by `exact_of_isLocalized_span` + the localised combinatorial core `CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact` (localise at each spanning `s r`, reduce to `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`, `CechAcyclic.lean:755`).

```
-- CechAcyclic.lean:1165
lemma SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact_of_localizationAway [Finite Œπ] (f : R)
    (hmem : ‚àÄ i, ‚àÉ k, f ‚à£ s i ^ k)
    (hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range (fun i => algebraMap R (Localization.Away f) (s i))) = ‚ä§)
    (m : ‚Ñï) :
    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2))
```
Route B (change of ring to `Localization.Away f`): `s` need only span the unit ideal after localising at `f` (each `s·µ¢ ‚àà ‚àöf`); instantiates `dDiff_exact` over `R_f` and ladder-transports along `AwayComparison.isLocalizedModule_comp_away` (`CechAcyclic.lean:781`).

```
-- CechAcyclic.lean:1290  ‚Üê THE ONE TO REUSE for a general affine open
lemma SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact_of_affineCover [Finite Œπ]
    (S : Type u) [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    (hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range (fun i => algebraMap R S (s i))) = ‚ä§)
    (hloc : ‚àÄ {n : ‚Ñï} (œÉ : Fin (n + 1) ‚Üí Œπ),
      IsLocalization (Submonoid.powers (sprod s œÉ))
        (Localization (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R S (sprod s œÉ)))))
    (m : ‚Ñï) :
    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2))
```
**This is exactly the level of generality the curve-side seed needs.** It is a statement about an abstract ring `R`, an `R`-module `M`, a family `s : Œπ ‚Üí R`, and *any* `R`-algebra `S` (in practice `S := Œì(V, ùí™)` for an affine open `V`) such that (a) the images `s i ‚Ü¶ S` span the unit ideal of `S`, and (b) for every ƒåech multi-index `œÉ`, the `S`-localisation of `M ‚äó_R S` at `sprod s œÉ`'s image is *simultaneously* an `R`-localisation of `M` at `powers (sprod s œÉ)` (`hloc`, encoding `D(s_œÉ) ‚äÜ V`). Route B1: instantiates `dDiff_exact` over `MS := TensorProduct R S M` with base change `bc := TensorProduct.mk R S M 1`, using `isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away` (`CechAcyclic.lean:883`, itself pure base-change algebra: `IsBaseChange.comp` + `isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange`).

**Geometric wrappers** (`R := Œì(Spec R)`-level, still Spec-specific but consuming the above verbatim):
```
-- CechAcyclic.lean:1940
theorem sectionCech_homology_exact {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
    {Œπ : Type u} [Finite Œπ] (s : Œπ ‚Üí R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ‚ä§)
    (p : ‚Ñï) (hp : 1 ‚â§ p) :
    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M))).homology p)

-- CechAcyclic.lean:1973
theorem sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway ... (f : R)
    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) = ‚®Ü i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
    (p : ‚Ñï) (hp : 1 ‚â§ p) : IsZero (... .homology p)

-- CechAcyclic.lean:2014
theorem sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineCover ...
    (S : Type u) [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] (hspan) (hloc) (p : ‚Ñï) (hp : 1 ‚â§ p) :
    IsZero (... .homology p)

-- CechAcyclic.lean:2073  ‚Üê the FULL Spec-side analogue of what you need on X
theorem sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
    {Œπ : Type u} [Finite Œπ] (s : Œπ ‚Üí R)
    (hV : IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (‚®Ü i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)))
    (p : ‚Ñï) (hp : 1 ‚â§ p) :
    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M))).homology p)
```
`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen` (`CechAcyclic.lean:2073‚Äì2124`) is *exactly* the pattern to replicate on a general `X`: it sets `S := Œì(V, ùí™_{Spec R})` where `V = ‚®Ü·µ¢ D(s·µ¢)`, discharges `hspan` from `hV.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff` and `hloc` per-œÉ from `hV.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` transported along an `AlgEquiv`.

**Bottom line for reuse on `X`:** you do *not* need to re-derive any ƒåech combinatorics. `SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact_of_affineCover` is 100% ring/module-theoretic and scheme-independent; the only new work is a geometric wrapper analogous to `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen` that (i) identifies `Œì(X, X.basicOpen f)` (`f ‚àà Œì(X,U)`, `U` affine ‚äÜ `X`) as an `R`-localisation of `M := Œì(U, ùí™_X)` via `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`/`isLocalization_basicOpen`, and (ii) bridges to the *shape* of ƒåech complex you actually need (see ¬ß2/¬ß3 ‚Äî this is where care is needed because there are two non-defeq ƒåech complex flavours in the project).

---

## 2. The two ƒåech complex shapes ‚Äî NOT literally the same terms

### (a) `sectionCechComplex`/`cechCohomology` ‚Äî Ab-valued, `X.PresheafOfModules`

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/PresheafCech.lean:303` `sectionCechCosimplicial {Œπ} (U : Œπ ‚Üí Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u}` with
  `obj n := ‚àè·∂ú (fun œÉ : Fin (n.len+1) ‚Üí Œπ => F.presheaf.obj (op (‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k))))` ‚Äî a *literal* order-theoretic infimum `‚®Ö k, U (œÉ k)` inside `F.presheaf.obj`.
- `PresheafCech.lean:337` `sectionCechComplex U F := (alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ‚Ñï`.
- `CechToCohomology.lean:99` `cechCohomology U F p := (sectionCechComplex U F).homology p : Ab.{u}`.

### (b) `Scheme.cechCochain`/`Scheme.cechCohomology` ‚Äî `ModuleCat k`-valued, via Mathlib's `cechComplexFunctor`

- `StructureSheafModuleK/Carriers.lean:562` `Scheme.cechCochain (C) (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (ùí∞ : Œπ ‚Üí Opens C.left.toTopCat) := (cechComplexFunctor ùí∞).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F) : CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ‚Ñï`.
- `:573` `Scheme.cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n := (Scheme.cechCochain C F ùí∞).homology n : ModuleCat.{u} k`.
- `:528`/`:541` the original (iter-012) structure-sheaf-specific `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`/`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` with `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`; `:586‚Äì599` `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq`/`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` are `rfl`-provable definitional-unfolding bridges (they are just the `F := toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation, not a comparison to flavour (a)).
- `:612` `class Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover F ùí∞` ‚Äî `Subsingleton (Scheme.cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n)` for `n > 0` ‚Äî the **target predicate** your seed presumably needs to instantiate.

### Mathlib's `cechComplexFunctor` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`, read in full)

```
-- FormalCoproducts/Basic.lean, FormalCoproducts/Cech.lean, Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean
cechComplexFunctor U : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï
  := FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech
  := cosimplicialObjectFunctor E ‚ãô alternatingCofaceMapComplex A     -- E = (mk _ U).cech
```
Unwinding `evalOp`/`power`/`cech` (all in `FormalCoproducts/Basic.lean`, `FormalCoproducts/Cech.lean`): the degree-`n` term is
```
‚àè·∂ú (œÉ : Fin (n+1) ‚Üí Œπ), P.obj (op (‚àè·∂ú k, U (œÉ k)))
```
where **both** `‚àè·∂ú` are *categorical* products (via `HasFiniteProducts C`), **not** literal `‚®Ö`/`Pi.lift` over an order-theoretic meet the way `sectionCechCosimplicial` is. For `C = TopologicalSpace.Opens X` (a `CompleteLattice`), the categorical finite product coincides with `‚®Ö` only up to the *propositional* (not `rfl`) equality `CategoryTheory.Limits.CompleteLattice.limit_eq_iInf` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/Lattice.lean:199`) ‚Äî a `le_antisymm` proof, since `Opens X` is a thin/partial-order category (iso ‚áí equal by antisymmetry, but not definitionally).

**Confirmed empirically**: `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/Adelic/Cokernel.lean:346` proves exactly this comparison by hand:
```
theorem prodOpens_eq_iInf {m : ‚Ñï} (j : Fin m ‚Üí Œπ) :
    (‚àè·∂ú ((FormalCoproduct.mk _ ùí∞).obj ‚àò j) : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) = ‚®Ö i, ùí∞ (j i) :=
  le_antisymm (le_iInf fun a => (Limits.Pi.œÄ ((FormalCoproduct.mk _ ùí∞).obj ‚àò j) a).le) ...
```
‚Äî a real `le_antisymm` lemma, i.e. genuinely not `rfl`. This is the only place in the project comparing the two ƒåech-term shapes, and it is done bespoke inside `Cokernel.lean` for a specific `ULift (Fin 2)` two-element cover (`AffineCoverMVSquare`), not as a reusable general lemma.

**Index-universe (`ULift`) note:** `cechComplexFunctor` requires `U : Œπ ‚Üí C` with `Œπ : Type w` and `HasFiniteProducts C`; the project instantiates it with `Œπ : Type u` directly in `Scheme.cechCochain`. Elsewhere (`AffineSerreVanishing.lean`, e.g. `:238,:377,:493`) the flavour-(a) Spec-side lemmas systematically use `Œπ := ULift.{u} (Fin n)` to land finite index families in `Type u` (universe-pinning discipline for `Ideal.span`/`Finite Œπ` arguments living in `Type u`); `Cokernel.lean`'s `AffineCoverMVSquare.coverFamily` uses `ULift.{u} (Fin 2)` for the same reason feeding `cechComplexFunctor`.

**Conclusion for ¬ß2**: **No comparison lemma between the two flavours exists in the project** beyond the ad-hoc `prodOpens_eq_iInf`/`prodOpens_fin_one`/`prodOpens_fin_two` family in `Cokernel.lean` (grep confirms `cechComplexFunctor` appears only in `Cokernel.lean` and `Carriers.lean`; `sectionCechCosimplicial`/`sectionCechComplex` appear only in `CechBridge.lean`/`PresheafCech.lean`/`CechAcyclic.lean`/`CechToCohomology.lean`/`CechSectionIdentification*.lean` ‚Äî **zero file imports/references both families**). Building the seed for `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf`/`Scheme.cechCohomology` (flavour b) will require either (i) reproving the algebraic-heart exactness directly against flavour-(b)'s term shape (degreewise `‚àè·∂ú` in `ModuleCat k`, via `Concrete.productEquiv`/`Pi.map`, mirroring `sectionCechProductEquiv` at `CechAcyclic.lean:1505`, but for the categorical `‚àè·∂ú` rather than `‚®Ö`), or (ii) building a general term-by-term iso `Scheme.cechCochain C F ùí∞ ‚âÖ (Ab-or-ModuleK-valued sectionCechComplex-analogue)` using `prodOpens_eq_iInf`-style lemmas degreewise, then transporting exactness/vanishing across that iso (¬ß3).

---

## 3. Vanishing transport tools

Two already-built patterns, both in-project, plus one core Mathlib fact:

1. **Ab-flavour, same-category iso transport** ‚Äî `AffineSerreVanishing.lean:424`:
```
theorem cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso {Œπ : Type u} (U : Œπ ‚Üí TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (e : F ‚âÖ G) (p : ‚Ñï)
    (h : IsZero (cechCohomology U F p)) : IsZero (cechCohomology U G p) :=
  h.of_iso ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor Ab.{u} (ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï) p).mapIso
    ((sectionCechComplexFunctor U).mapIso e)).symm
```
This is the template: coefficient-presheaf iso ‚áí `sectionCechComplexFunctor`/`sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor` (`CechToCohomology.lean:85`/`:50`) mapIso ‚áí `HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor _ _ p` mapIso ‚áí `IsZero.of_iso`. The analogous route for `ModuleCat k`-valued complexes/`Scheme.cechCohomology` would need a `ModuleCat`-valued `cechComplexFunctor`-mapIso from a sheaf-of-`k`-modules iso (Mathlib's `cechComplexFunctor` is already functorial in the coefficient presheaf `P : C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A`, so `(cechComplexFunctor ùí∞).mapIso` is directly available ‚Äî no project-local reconstruction needed here, unlike flavour (a)).

2. **`IsZero ‚Üî Subsingleton` bridge for `ModuleCat`** ‚Äî Mathlib, `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Basic.lean:253,377,384`:
```
theorem ModuleCat.isZero_of_subsingleton (M : ModuleCat R) [Subsingleton M] : IsZero M
theorem ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (h : IsZero M) : Subsingleton M
ModuleCat.isZero_iff_subsingleton : IsZero M ‚Üî Subsingleton M
```
This is exactly the bridge needed since `Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover` (`Carriers.lean:612`) is phrased with `Subsingleton (Scheme.cechCohomology C F ùí∞ n)` rather than `IsZero`. Project precedent: `FreePresheafComplex.lean:607` already uses `ModuleCat.isZero_of_subsingleton` in exactly this direction.

3. **Generic `IsZero.of_iso`** (`CategoryTheory.Limits.IsZero.of_iso`, standard Mathlib) transports `IsZero` across any iso in any category ‚Äî this is what both patterns above bottom out in; no forget‚ÇÇ-specific machinery was needed (the homology objects being compared always live in the *same* target category `Ab`/`ModuleCat k`, so no forget‚ÇÇ-reflects-zero argument shows up anywhere in the project). If you *do* need to cross categories (e.g. compare an `Ab`-valued homology object with a `ModuleCat k`-valued one via `forget‚ÇÇ`), the project's only precedent for `forget‚ÇÇ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat` faithfulness/iso-reflection is `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean:772` (`isLimitOfReflectsOfMapIsLimit (forget‚ÇÇ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat) ...`) and `FreePresheafComplex.lean:408-410` (`Functor.map_isIso (forget‚ÇÇ (ModuleCat _) Ab) _`) ‚Äî i.e. `forget‚ÇÇ` reflects isos/limits (it's an equivalence onto its (additive, exact) essential image for a fixed ring), no bespoke "reflects zero objects" lemma exists but is derivable trivially from `forget‚ÇÇ`'s exactness (`isZero_of_subsingleton`/`subsingleton_of_isZero` transported along the forget‚ÇÇ's underlying-type identification).

---

## 4. Basis/cofinality material and Mathlib generalisation lemmas for a general `X`

### `standard_cover_cofinal` (`AffineSerreVanishing.lean:167`)
```
theorem standard_cover_cofinal {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (f : R) {Œ± : Type u}
    (W : Œ± ‚Üí (Spec R).Opens)
    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) ‚â§ ‚®Ü a, W a) :
    ‚àÉ (n : ‚Ñï) (g : Fin n ‚Üí R) (œÜ : Fin n ‚Üí Œ±),
      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) = ‚®Ü i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ‚àß
      ‚àÄ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ‚â§ W (œÜ i)
```
Spec-specific inputs used: `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen f`, `PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens`.

### `standard_cover_cofinal_affine` (`AffineSerreVanishing.lean:558`) ‚Äî the D(f)‚Üígeneral-affine-open generalisation *already done* (Spec-side)
```
theorem standard_cover_cofinal_affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (V : (Spec R).Opens)
    (hV : IsAffineOpen V) {Œ± : Type u} (W : Œ± ‚Üí (Spec R).Opens) (hcov : V ‚â§ ‚®Ü a, W a) :
    ‚àÉ (n : ‚Ñï) (g : Fin n ‚Üí R) (œÜ : Fin n ‚Üí Œ±),
      V = ‚®Ü i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ‚àß
      ‚àÄ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ‚â§ W (œÜ i)
```
Only change from `standard_cover_cofinal`: `hK := hV.isCompact` (`IsAffineOpen.isCompact`) in place of `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen f`; same `isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens` + finite-subcover argument (identical proof shape, `V ‚äì W a` in place of `Uf ‚äì W a`). **This is the exact template to port to a general scheme `X`, affine open `U`**: the analogue needs `PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens` replaced by the basis-of-basic-opens-of-`U` statement `IsAffineOpen.isBasis_basicOpen` (used at `AffineScheme.lean:622`, `(isBasis_basicOpen U)`), and `PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen` replaced by `X.basicOpen (¬∑ : Œì(X,U))`.

### Mathlib v4.31 lemmas available for the port to a general `X`/affine `U` (all read in full from `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` and `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Scheme.lean`)

- **`Scheme.basicOpen`** (`Scheme.lean:655`, section `BasicOpen`, `variable (X : Scheme) {V U : X.Opens} (f g : Œì(X, U))`): `X.basicOpen (f : Œì(X,U)) : X.Opens`, keyed on sections over an **arbitrary** `U` (not just `‚ä§`) ‚Äî exactly matches what's needed.
  - `Scheme.lean:687` `X.basicOpen_le : X.basicOpen f ‚â§ U` (tagged `@[sheaf_restrict]`).
  - `Scheme.lean:677` `X.basicOpen_res (i : op U ‚ü∂ op V) : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map i f) = V ‚äì X.basicOpen f`.
  - `Scheme.lean:718` `X.basicOpen_mul : X.basicOpen (f * g) = X.basicOpen f ‚äì X.basicOpen g` ‚Äî the "same-open sections" product formula (used exactly as `PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul` is used in `CechAcyclic.lean:1434`/`basicOpen_sprod` on the Spec side; ports verbatim to `sprod` for `X.basicOpen`).
  - `Scheme.lean:736` `X.basicOpen_one : X.basicOpen (1 : Œì(X,U)) = U`.
  - `Scheme.lean:739` `instance algebra_section_section_basicOpen (f : Œì(X,U)) : Algebra Œì(X,U) Œì(X, X.basicOpen f) := (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (basicOpen_le f)).op).hom.toAlgebra` ‚Äî **already a synthesizable instance** for the restriction algebra (note: `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen`'s docstring, `CechAcyclic.lean:2035`, claims "NOT a synthesizable instance" for the analogous `Algebra Œì(V) Œì(D a)` on `Spec R` ‚Äî but the general-`X` `Scheme.basicOpen` API already provides it as an instance keyed on `X.basicOpen_le`; worth double-checking whether it fires automatically before reconstructing the ad-hoc `.toAlgebra` pattern).

- **`IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`** (`AffineScheme.lean:597`, `include hU`): `IsAffineOpen (X.basicOpen f)` for `hU : IsAffineOpen U`, `f : Œì(X,U)`.

- **`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen`** (`AffineScheme.lean:659`, `include hU`): `IsLocalization.Away f Œì(X, X.basicOpen f)` ‚Äî the direct algebraic content: sections over `X.basicOpen f` are `Œì(X,U)_f`.

- **`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`** (`AffineScheme.lean:726`): `{V : X.Opens} (i : V ‚ü∂ U) (e : V = X.basicOpen f) : @IsLocalization.Away _ _ f Œì(X,V) _ (X.presheaf.map i.op).hom.toAlgebra` ‚Äî the exact lemma the Spec-side `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen` (`CechAcyclic.lean:2073`) invokes for `hloc`.

- **`IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`** (`AffineScheme.lean:909`, `include hU`): `{s : Set Œì(X,U)} : ‚®Ü f : s, X.basicOpen (f : Œì(X,U)) = U ‚Üî Ideal.span s = ‚ä§` ‚Äî **THE** "covering ‚áî spans unit ideal" criterion (name confirmed exactly). Companion: `IsAffineOpen.self_le_iSup_basicOpen_iff` (`:936`) is the `‚â§` form.

- **`IsAffineOpen.isCompact`** (`AffineScheme.lean:501`, `protected theorem`, `include hU`): `IsCompact (U : Set X)` ‚Äî the quasi-compactness input `standard_cover_cofinal_affine` uses.

- **`IsAffineOpen.isBasis_basicOpen`** (used at `AffineScheme.lean:622`) ‚Äî the "basic opens of `U` form a basis of `U`" fact, the general-`X` analogue of `PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens`, needed for the `exists_subset_of_mem_open` step of the cofinality proof.

- Instance `IsAffine.of_isIso`, `IsAffine X.basicOpen r` for `[IsAffine X]` (`AffineScheme.lean:608`, only for `‚ä§`-sections) vs. the general `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen` (works for any affine `U`, any `f : Œì(X,U)`) ‚Äî use the latter for the curve case since `U` is a proper affine open, not all of `X`.

**Summary porting recipe for ¬ß4**: replace every `PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen`/`Spec R` occurrence in `standard_cover_cofinal_affine` with `X.basicOpen (¬∑ : Œì(X,U))`/`X`, `IsAffineOpen.isCompact` for quasi-compactness (already what `standard_cover_cofinal_affine` uses ‚Äî no change needed there), and `IsAffineOpen.isBasis_basicOpen` in place of `PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens`. This is a mechanical, already-modeled port.

---

## 5. `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` ‚Äî definition and restriction-map relation to `O_C`

Split across three files (`StructureSheafModuleK/{Presheaf,SheafProperty,Carriers}.lean`).

**`Presheaf.lean:171` ‚Äî the presheaf, `toModuleKPresheaf`:**
```
noncomputable def Scheme.toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
    (Opens C.left.toTopCat)·µí·µñ ‚•§ ModuleCat.{u} k where
  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
      map_add' := ...
      map_smul' := ... }   -- uses toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
```
So **as a type/underlying group, `toModuleKPresheaf.obj U` is literally `Œì(C.left, U) = O_C(U)`**; the restriction map is `O_C`'s own presheaf restriction map `(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom`, merely re-packaged as a `k`-linear map (`ModuleCat.ofHom` wraps the *same* function, `map_smul'` proved via naturality of the algebra map, `map_add'` trivial). There is no separate "sheaf of `k`-modules" data beyond `O_C` ‚Äî the `k`-module structure comes from the algebra map below.

**`Presheaf.lean:126,134` ‚Äî the `k`-algebra structure on each `Œì(C,U)`:**
```
noncomputable def kToSection (C) (U) : CommRingCat.of k ‚ü∂ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
  (Scheme.ŒìSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ‚â´ C.hom.app ‚ä§ ‚â´ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
noncomputable instance algebraSection (C) (U) : Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
```
i.e. `algebraMap k Œì(C,U)` is the structure-morphism's global ring map `C.hom.app ‚ä§ : k ‚Üí Œì(C,‚ä§)` restricted to `U`; `kToSection_naturality` (`:147`) and `algebraMap_naturality` (`:155`) show this is compatible with `O_C`'s own restriction maps, i.e. `toModuleKPresheaf`'s restriction maps *are* `O_C`'s restriction maps and they are `k`-algebra (hence `k`-linear) maps.

**`SheafProperty.lean:42` ‚Äî the sheaf itself, `toModuleKSheaf`:**
```
noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
  ‚ü®toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C‚ü©
```
with `toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare` (`:52`, `Iso.refl _`!) confirming `forget‚ÇÇ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat` applied to `toModuleKSheaf C` is **literally equal** (not just isomorphic) to the pre-existing `AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf `toAbSheaf C.left`.

**No existing lemma computes `toModuleKSheaf` sections on basic opens or relates to localization** (grep for `toModuleKPresheaf.*basicOpen`/`toModuleKSheaf.*[Ll]ocaliz` across the whole project returns nothing). This is a genuine gap: to run the algebraic heart (¬ß1) against `toModuleKSheaf`, you will need to establish, for `f ‚àà Œì(U, O_C)` (`U` affine open of `C.left`), that `(toModuleKSheaf C).presheaf.obj (op (C.left.basicOpen f))` is (as a `k`-module) the localisation `(Œì(U,O_C))_f`, via `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen` composed with the definitional identity `toModuleKPresheaf.obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)` (`Presheaf.lean:197`, `@[simp] toModuleKPresheaf_obj`, `rfl`) ‚Äî i.e. the `k`-module carrier of the sheaf is definitionally `O_C`'s carrier, so `IsLocalization.Away` transports for free at the type level; only the `k`-linearity of the localisation-comparison maps (`AwayComparison.comparison`) needs re-establishing in the `ModuleCat k` bundling.

# Port Plan: Stacks-01EO ƒåech‚ÜîDerived Comparison, `X.Modules` ‚Üí `ùíÆ := Sheaf(Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat)(ModuleCat k)`

All paths relative to `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge` unless marked `MATHLIB:`. Mathlib root: `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.lake-packages/mathlib/Mathlib`.

## A. Template inventory (dependency order)

### A.0 Upstream dependencies of `CechBridge.lean` (must be ported first ‚Äî the "hard" 2357+730 LOC substrate)

| `coverOpen`, `coverInterOpen` | `FreePresheafComplex.lean:129,135` | cover-member / multi-index intersection opens | `X.OpenCover`, `ùí∞.f i` |

| `cechFreePresheafComplex` | `FreePresheafComplex.lean:207` | the free ƒåech **chain** complex `K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢` | ditto |
| `freeYonedaAug`, `cechFreeAug` | `FreePresheafComplex.lean:230,276` | augmentation `K(ùí∞)_0 ‚Üí O_ùí∞` | ditto |

| `cechFreeComplexAug` | `FreePresheafComplex.lean:346` | augmented chain complex `K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢ ‚Üí O_ùí∞[0]` | ditto |
| `combDifferential`/`combHomotopy`/`combDifferential_exact` etc. | `FreePresheafComplex.lean:458-580` | **pure combinatorics** ‚Äî contracting homotopy for the free ƒåech resolution | none (already coefficient-agnostic; ports verbatim) |
| `cechFreeEval_X`,`cechEngineD`,`cechEngineComplexAug`,‚Ä¶ (the bulk, 580-1439) | `FreePresheafComplex.lean:580-1439` | sectionwise (objectwise) evaluation of `K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢` at an open `V`, contraction, `cechEngineComplexAug_quasiIso` | `X.PresheafOfModules` colimits computed objectwise, `PresheafOfModules.evaluation` |
| `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` | `FreePresheafComplex.lean:1439` | **the resolution theorem**: `K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢ ‚Üí O_ùí∞[0]` is a quasi-iso | assembles all of the above |

  - `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf` (`HasExt.{u+1}`) ‚Äî `CechComparisonGate.lean:136-138`.
  - Also `AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology.instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK` (via `HasExt.standard`) ‚Äî `StructureSheafModuleK/Presheaf.lean:110-115`, and `instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK` ‚Äî `Presheaf.lean:100-104`.
  - All **confirmed instantiable today** ‚Äî no project-local proof obligation remains for gate 3.

**(4) `presheafToSheaf` preserves finite limits/monomorphisms ‚Äî generic in Mathlib, no project-local proof needed (contrast the `X.Modules` `toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`/`toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms`):**
- `instance [HasSheafify J A] : PreservesFiniteLimits (presheafToSheaf J A)` ‚Äî `MATHLIB CategoryTheory/Sites/Sheafification.lean:76`. Fully generic over any small site `J : GrothendieckTopology C` and any `A` (with `HasSheafify J A`) ‚Äî **no** restriction to sheaves-of-modules-over-a-sheaf-of-rings, unlike the project's `toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`/`_preservesEpimorphisms` (`AffineSerreVanishing.lean:119-157`), whose signature is `{R : Sheaf J RingCat} [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] : PreservesFiniteColimits (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R)` ‚Äî that pair is about the **colimit**-side forgetful functor `SheafOfModules ‚Üí Sheaf` (needed because `X.Modules = SheafOfModules O_X` sits over a *sheaf of rings*, not over a fixed field). **The port does not need this pair at all** ‚Äî `ùíÆ`'s objects already *are* `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`, there is no intermediate "sheaf of modules over a sheaf of rings" forgetful functor to push finite-colimit-preservation through. The relevant fact for the port (injective transport, ¬ßB row 4) needs only the *finite-limit* side, which Mathlib gives unconditionally at `Sheafification.lean:76`.
- Companion: `sheafificationAdjunction J A : presheafToSheaf J A ‚ä£ sheafToPresheaf J A` ‚Äî `Sheafification.lean:81`, `def sheafToPresheaf` L.‚âà32, generic.
- `Injective.injective_of_adjoint {L ‚ä£ R} (J : D) [Injective J] : Injective (R.obj J)` ‚Äî `MATHLIB CategoryTheory/Preadditive/Injective/Basic.lean:195`, category-generic (this is exactly the lemma the project's `injective_toPresheafOfModules` already calls, `PresheafCech.lean:224`).

**(5) `cechComplexFunctor` and its constituents.**
- `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor {Œπ} (U : Œπ ‚Üí C) : (C·µí·µñ ‚•§ A) ‚•§ CochainComplex A ‚Ñï := FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech` ‚Äî `MATHLIB CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean:65`, requires `[HasFiniteProducts C] [Preadditive A]` (and `[HasProducts.{w} A]` ambient).
- `FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor E := cosimplicialObjectFunctor E ‚ãô AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A` ‚Äî `Cech.lean:53`.
- `FormalCoproduct.cosimplicialObjectFunctor E := evalOp C A ‚ãô (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj E.rightOp` ‚Äî `Cech.lean:43`.
- Extra-degeneracy precedent (relevant only for the *affine* Serre-vanishing branch, not directly for 01EO): `CategoryTheory.SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` ‚Äî `MATHLIB AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:328`; `Limits.FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` ‚Äî `MATHLIB CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:92`.

**(6) Homology-LES machinery for the SES-of-complexes step (L2), generic in `CochainComplex C ‚Ñï` for any abelian `C`:**
- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.Œ¥Iso (hi : IsZero (S.X‚ÇÇ.homology i)) (hj : IsZero (S.X‚ÇÇ.homology j)) : S.X‚ÇÉ.homology i ‚âÖ S.X‚ÇÅ.homology j` ‚Äî `MATHLIB Algebra/Homology/HomologySequence.lean:362`.
- `HomologicalComplex.shortExact_of_degreewise_shortExact` ‚Äî `MATHLIB Algebra/Homology/HomologicalComplexAbelian.lean:56`.
- `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology`, `.exactAt_iff'`, `ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact` ‚Äî same generic-abelian-category API the project already uses at `sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology` (`CechBridge.lean:459-481`).

---

## D. Structure recommendation

### D.1 Proposed file split (constant-`k` port, new directory `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK/Cech/` or siblings of `StructureSheafModuleK/`)

| New file | Mirrors | Est. LOC | Contents |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FreeSheafCorep.lean` | `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` ¬ß1 + extraction from `Carriers.lean:99-129` | 80-150 | Named `freeSheafOn (X : C) : ùíÆ`; the un-Ext'd corepresentability `Hom_ùíÆ(freeSheafOn X, F) ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] F.obj.obj (op X)` (Yoneda + `sheafificationAdjunction`); redefine `HModule'` in terms of `freeSheafOn` (or leave `Carriers.lean` as-is and just add the missing corepresentability lemma there). |
| `FreeCechResolution.lean` | `FreePresheafComplex.lean` (2357 LOC) | **900-1400** | The dominant cost. `K(U)_‚Ä¢ : ChainComplex ((Opens X)·µí·µñ ‚•§ ModuleCat k) ‚Ñï` built from `‚®Å_œÉ (yoneda ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k).obj (V_œÉ)`; augmentation to a "cover structure presheaf"; the contracting-homotopy combinatorics (`combDifferential`/`combHomotopy`/`combDifferential_exact`, `FreePresheafComplex.lean:458-580`, ports **almost verbatim** ‚Äî coefficient-agnostic) driving `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`-analogue. |
| `InjectiveCechAcyclic.lean` | `PresheafCech.lean` + `CechBridge.lean` ¬ß"Hom(-,I) exact" + ¬ß"injective_cech_acyclic" | 250-400 | `injective_sheafToPresheaf` (one-liner via ¬ßC.4/¬ßB row-4 ‚Äî *much shorter* than `injective_toPresheafOfModules`); `Hom(-,F)`-exactness-for-injective instance (reuse `preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective`, `CechBridge.lean:401`, verbatim, it's already category-generic); the ƒåech-hom-identification iso (mirrors `cechComplex_hom_identification`/`homCechComplexMapOpIso`/`sectionCechComplexMapOpIso`, `CechBridge.lean:136-368`) against `cechComplexFunctor`'s cosimplicial object instead of hand-rolled `sectionCechCosimplicial`; the top theorem `injective_cech_acyclic`. |
| `CechCoordinates.lean` | `PresheafCech.lean:303-339` + `CechAcyclic.lean:1505-1613` | 150-250 | `sectionCechProductEquiv`/`sectionCechFaceRestr`/`sectionCech_objD_apply`/`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact`/`sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology`, rebuilt against `Scheme.cechCochain`/`(cechComplexFunctor ùí∞).obj`'s cosimplicial object (i.e. `FormalCoproduct.cosimplicialObjectFunctor`) rather than a hand-rolled `sectionCechCosimplicial`. |
| `SesCechH1.lean` | `CechBridge.lean:542-828` (`ses_cech_h1` + restriction bookkeeping) | 250-350 | Direct structural port; swap `X.PresheafOfModules`/`TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf` for `ùíÆ`'s sheaf-gluing API (generic `Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` after composing with `sheafToPresheaf`/`forget‚ÇÇ`). |
| `CechToCohomologyK.lean` | `CechToCohomology.lean` (434 LOC) | 250-350 | **Shorter than the original** ‚Äî drop `sectionCechCosimplicialMap`/`Functor`/`sectionCechComplexFunctor`/`Map` (¬ßB row 3: free via `cechComplexFunctor`'s built-in functoriality); keep `faceShortComplex`, `sectionCechComplexShortComplex`/`cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis` (L1), `cechHomology_quotient_vanishing`/`quotient_cech_vanishing_of_basis` (L2, near-verbatim reuse), `absoluteCohomology_one_eq_zero_of_basis`-analogue (L3), `BasisCovSystem`/`HasVanishingHigherCech`/`absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis`/`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`-analogue (L4) ‚Äî **and here `injSES`/`injSES_shortExact` become *unconditional* instances, dropping `[EnoughInjectives ùíÆ]` from every downstream signature**, since it's an `inferInstance`. |
| **Total port estimate** | | **~1900-2900 LOC** | vs. ~4400 LOC of the `X.Modules` original (`CechBridge.lean` 1118 + `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` 171 + `CechToCohomology.lean` 434 + `FreePresheafComplex.lean` 2357 + `PresheafCech.lean` 342 + `CechAcyclic.lean`'s relevant 1505-1613 slice ‚âà 4300+, minus large savings from ¬ßB rows 3-4 and ¬ßC.4). |

This is consistent with the independent estimate in `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md` (~300-450 LOC) for the **affine-only, spanning-family** route via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`/extra-degeneracy ‚Äî that is a *cheaper alternative* to Branch A/B above but only closes the *affine* case (needed for gate 4 / Serre vanishing on `D(f)`), not the fully general basis-comparison `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`. If the near-term goal is only **gate 4** (`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`, per `CechComparisonGate.lean:64-78`), the localization route is materially cheaper than porting the whole 01EO chain; if the goal is the **general** Stacks-01EO statement (arbitrary basis/cover system, matching the `X.Modules` capstone), the full port above is required.

### D.2 The 3 hardest lemmas to port

**1. `injective_cech_acyclic` (`CechBridge.lean:874`, base-case ingredient of L3/via `BasisCovSystem.injective_acyclic`).**
Proof strategy to mirror (10 lines):
1. Build the free ƒåech chain complex of presheaves `K(U)_p = ‚®Å_{œÉ:Fin(p+1)‚ÜíŒπ} free(yoneda(V_œÉ))` (analogue: `‚®Å_œÉ (yoneda ‚ãô ModuleCat.free k).obj V_œÉ`), with differential the alternating sum of face maps induced by inclusions `V_{œÉ} ‚â§ V_{œÉ‚àòŒ¥·µ¢}`.
2. Show the augmentation `K(U)_‚Ä¢ ‚Üí O_U[0]` (`O_U` = "presheaf that is `‚äï_{i:V_i‚äáV} k`-ish structure of the cover", concretely `coverStructurePresheaf`-analogue) is a quasi-iso, via the *sectionwise* contracting homotopy: fix `i_fix` with `V ‚äÜ U_{i_fix}`, homotopy `h(s)_{i‚ÇÄ,‚Ä¶,i‚Çö} = [i‚ÇÄ = i_fix]¬∑s_{i‚ÇÅ,‚Ä¶,i‚Çö}`, check `dh+hd=id` (`combHomotopy_spec`/`combDifferential_exact`, `FreePresheafComplex.lean:483-580`, coefficient-agnostic, ports verbatim).
3. Show `(sheafToPresheaf J _).obj I` is injective in the presheaf category, for `I` injective in `ùíÆ` ‚Äî **one-liner** via `Injective.injective_of_adjoint (sheafificationAdjunction J (ModuleCat k)) I`, using Mathlib's free `PreservesFiniteLimits (presheafToSheaf J A)` instance (¬ßC.4) ‚Äî this step is **much shorter** than the `X.Modules` analogue `injective_toPresheafOfModules`.
4. `Hom(-,I_pshf)` is exact (`preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective`, `CechBridge.lean:401`, category-generic, reuse verbatim).
5. Apply `Hom(-,I_pshf)` to the quasi-iso augmentation ‚üπ quasi-iso of `Hom`-complexes; identify `Hom(K(U)_‚Ä¢,I_pshf)` with the section ƒåech complex `ƒå‚Ä¢(U,I)` (analogue of `cechComplex_hom_identification`/`homCechComplexMapOpIso`) ‚Äî this identification against Mathlib's `cechComplexFunctor` cosimplicial object (rather than a hand-rolled one) is genuinely new plumbing (~150-250 LOC) but structurally the same shape as `CechBridge.lean:121-369`.
6. Conclude: source complex is `Hom(-,I)` of a degree-0-concentrated complex ‚üπ zero in positive degree ‚üπ transport across the quasi-iso to `ƒå‚Ä¢(U,I)`'s positive-degree homology (exact tail of `CechBridge.lean:874-911`, structurally verbatim).

**2. `ses_cech_h1` (`CechBridge.lean:659-826`, L1's surjectivity ingredient ‚Äî Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).**
Proof strategy (10 lines): given SES `0‚ÜíF‚ÜíG‚ÜíH‚Üí0` and local lifts `sLoc i ‚àà G(U·µ¢)` of `s‚ààH(‚®ÜU·µ¢)`, form the degree-1 cochain of pairwise differences `dGcoord œÉ = sLoc(œÉ1)|_œÉ - sLoc(œÉ0)|_œÉ`; show `gœÄ` kills it (LES exactness on sections, from `hœÄŒπ`/`hker`); lift the killed cochain into `F` pointwise (`hker`); show the lift is a genuine 1-cocycle in `F` (transport the `d¬≤=0` identity of `G`'s cochain through the mono `fŒπ`); apply `»û¬π(U,F)=0` to extract a 0-cochain `t` with `dt` = the lift (`sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology`); correct `sLoc` by `fŒπ(t)` to get a genuinely compatible family; glue via `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` on `G`; check the glued section maps to `s` by separatedness of `H`. **Port cost**: mechanical (swap `X.PresheafOfModules`/its restriction API for `ùíÆ`'s `sheafToPresheaf`/`forget‚ÇÇ`-composed presheaf and Mathlib's generic sheaf-gluing lemma), but the sheer bookkeeping volume (7 private helper lemmas, `CechBridge.lean:552-635`) means this is a **large, low-conceptual-risk, high-LOC** port item ‚Äî budget ~250-350 LOC.

**3. The ƒåech-hom-identification `cechComplex_hom_identification`/`homCechComplexMapOpIso`/`sectionCechComplexMapOpIso` (`CechBridge.lean:121-369`).**
This is the piece with the **least direct Mathlib precedent for the new setting**, because it must reconcile two different presentations of "the ƒåech complex": (a) Mathlib's `cechComplexFunctor`'s `FormalCoproduct`-based cosimplicial object (used to define `Scheme.cechCochain`/`Scheme.cechCohomology`, `Carriers.lean:562-578`, the target the whole port must land on) versus (b) the free-presheaf-resolution's own natural cosimplicial presentation `Hom(K(U)_‚Ä¢,F)` (built from `Sigma.Œπ`/`opCoproductIsoProduct`/`piComparison`, mirroring `homCechSectionIsoApp`, `CechBridge.lean:162-254`). The `X.Modules` proof needed this because `sectionCechComplex` was **hand-rolled** to match `K(ùí∞)_‚Ä¢`'s shape by construction; in the port, the target complex `Scheme.cechCochain` is fixed externally by `cechComplexFunctor`, so the identification must instead show `Hom(K(U)_‚Ä¢, F) ‚âÖ (cechComplexFunctor U).obj (sheafToPresheaf.obj F)` degreewise (`Hom(‚äï_œÉ free(yoneda V_œÉ), F) ‚âÖ ‚àè_œÉ F(V_œÉ)`, matching `FormalCoproduct.mk _ U`.cech's degree-`p` object `U.power (Fin(p+1))`), then check differential-intertwining against `FormalCoproduct`'s coface maps rather than the project's own `homOfLE`-based ones. Budget ~150-250 LOC of genuinely new work; recommend prototyping the degree-0 and degree-1 cases by hand first (`lean_multi_attempt`/`lean_goal` against `FormalCoproduct.mk`'s unfolding) before committing to the general-`n` proof, since the `FormalCoproduct`/`SimplicialObject` API's defeq behavior around `U.power`/`E.rightOp` is unfamiliar territory for this codebase (no prior project usage found).

pre-composition maps via the iter-031 iso's hom/inv. The round-trip
equations close by the same four-step rewrite chain. Lean's universe
inference adapts automatically because the LHS `HModule' k F n T`
forces `Ext.{u}` everywhere. -/
noncomputable def HModule'_top_linearEquiv
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ‚Ñï) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
    HModule' k F n T
      ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
              (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n :=
  let Œ± := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ Œ±.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ Œ±.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    (by
      ext x
      change (Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ Œ±.inv).comp
        ((Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ Œ±.hom).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
      rw [‚Üê Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_comp_mk‚ÇÄ,
          Œ±.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_id_comp])
    (by
      ext y
      change (Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ Œ±.hom).comp
        ((Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ Œ±.inv).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
      rw [‚Üê Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_comp_mk‚ÇÄ,
          Œ±.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk‚ÇÄ_id_comp])

/-- Iter-034 universe-bump bridge. Composes iter-033's
`HModule'_top_linearEquiv` (universe-`u` cover-totality between `HModule' k F n T`
and `Ext.{u} ((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n`) with the
universe shift `Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` (Mathlib gap-fill at the top
of this file) lifting the result to `Ext.{u+1} ((constantSheaf J _).obj
(ModuleCat.of k k)) F n = HModule k F n` (definitional unfold of the iter-009
abbrev). The composition gives the full bridge `HModule' k F n T ‚âÉ‚Çó[k]
HModule k F n` for terminal `T`, closing Step 3 of the Serre-finiteness
scaffold. Iter-035+ specialises this to `AffineCoverMVSquare` using
iter-029's `toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X‚ÇÑ : ... = ‚ä§` simp lemma. -/
noncomputable def HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ‚Ñï) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
    HModule' k F n T ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule k F n :=
  (HModule'_top_linearEquiv k F n hT).trans Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv

/-- Iter-035 corner-bridge specialisation, abstract sheaf form. With iter-034's
universal bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` in scope, the natural
specialisation lands the bridge on the `X‚ÇÑ` corner of an `AffineCoverMVSquare`,
where iter-029's simp lemma `toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X‚ÇÑ` identifies the
corner with `‚ä§ : Opens X.toTopCat`. Mathlib's `Preorder.isTerminalTop`
(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/Preorder.lean`) supplies the
`IsTerminal ‚ä§` witness; transporting this back via the simp lemma's `.symm`
gives the required `IsTerminal X‚ÇÑ` to feed into the universal bridge. The
qualified `TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat` form is required to sidestep the
iter-029 sub-namespace shadowing trap (bare `Opens X.toTopCat` inside `def
AffineCoverMVSquare.foo` shadows to `Scheme.Opens` which expects a `Scheme`). -/
noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_X‚ÇÑ_linearEquiv
    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ‚Ñï) :
    HModule' k F n S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X‚ÇÑ ‚âÉ‚Çó[k] HModule k F n :=
  HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv k F n
    (S.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X‚ÇÑ.symm ‚ñ∏
      (Preorder.isTerminalTop (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)))
